````ocaml
 18:  *  3. Neither the name of the author nor the names of his contributors
 19:  *     may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this software
 20:  *     without specific prior written permission.
 21:  *
 22:  *  THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE CONTRIBUTORS ``AS IS'' AND ANY EXPRESS
 23:  *  OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED
 24:  *  WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE
 25:  *  DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR
 26:  *  ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL
 27:  *  DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS
 28:  *  OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
 29:  *  HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT,
 30:  *  STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN
 31:  *  ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE
 32:  *  POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
 33:  *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*)
 34: 
 35: module Writer = Serialize.Writer
 36: 
 37: type error =
 38:   [ `Bad_request
 39:   | `Internal_server_error
 40:   | `Exn of exn
 41:   ]
 42: 
 43: type error_handler =
 44:   ?request:Request.t -> error -> (Headers.t -> Body.Writer.t) -> unit
 45: 
 46: type response_state =
 47:   | Waiting
 48:   | Fixed of
 49:       { response : Response.t
 50:       ; mutable iovec :
 51:           [ `String of string | `Bigstring of Bigstringaf.t ]
 52:             Httpun_types.IOVec.t
 53:       }
 54:   | Streaming of
 55:       { response : Response.t
 56:       ; response_body : Body.Writer.t
 57:       ; trailers : Headers.t
 58:       }
 59:   | Complete of Response.t
 60: 
 61: type request_info =
 62:   { request : Request.t
 63:   ; request_body : Body.Reader.t
 64:   ; mutable request_body_bytes : int64
 65:   }
 66: 
 67: type active_state = (request_info, request_info) Stream.active_state
 68: 
 69: type active_stream =
 70:   { body_buffer_size : int
 71:   ; encoder : Hpack.Encoder.t
 72:   ; mutable response_state : response_state
 73:         (* We're not doing anything with these yet, we could probably have a
 74:          * `Reqd.schedule_read_trailers` function that would be called once
 75:          * trailer headers are emitted. *)
 76:   ; mutable trailers_parser : Stream.partial_headers option
 77:   ; mutable trailers : Headers.t option
 78:   ; create_push_stream :
 79:       Stream_identifier.t
 80:       -> (t, [ `Push_disabled | `Stream_ids_exhausted ]) result
 81:   }
 82: 
 83: and state =
 84:   (active_state, active_stream, request_info * active_stream) Stream.state
 85: 
 86: and t = (state, error, error_handler) Stream.t
 87: 
 88: let create_active_request request request_body =
 89:   { request; request_body; request_body_bytes = Int64.zero }
 90: 
 91: let create_active_stream encoder body_buffer_size create_push_stream =
 92:   { body_buffer_size
 93:   ; encoder
 94:   ; response_state = Waiting
 95:   ; trailers_parser = None
 96:   ; trailers = None
 97:   ; create_push_stream
 98:   }
 99: 
100: let request (t : t) =
101:   match t.state with
102:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _ | FullHeaders), _) ->
103:     assert false
104:   | Active ((Open (ActiveMessage { request; _ }) | HalfClosed { request; _ }), _)
105:   | Reserved ({ request; _ }, _) ->
106:     request
107:   | Closed _ -> assert false
108: 
109: let request_body (t : t) =
110:   match t.state with
111:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _ | FullHeaders), _) ->
112:     assert false
113:   | Active
114:       ( ( Open (ActiveMessage { request_body; _ })
115:         | HalfClosed { request_body; _ } )
116:       , _ ) ->
117:     request_body
118:   | Reserved _ ->
119:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
120:      *   Promised requests MUST NOT include a request body. *)
121:     failwith
122:       "h2.Reqd.request_body: Promised requests must not include a request body"
123:   | Closed _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.request_body: Stream already closed"
124: 
125: let response (t : t) =
126:   match t.state with
127:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> None
128:   | Active
129:       ( (Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _)
130:       , { response_state; _ } )
131:   | Reserved (_, { response_state; _ }) ->
132:     (match response_state with
133:     | Waiting -> None
134:     | Streaming { response; _ } | Fixed { response; _ } | Complete response ->
135:       Some response)
136:   | Closed _ -> None
137: 
138: let response_exn (t : t) =
139:   match t.state with
140:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) ->
141:     failwith "h2.Reqd.response_exn: response has not started"
142:   | Active
143:       ( (Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _)
144:       , { response_state; _ } )
145:   | Reserved (_, { response_state; _ }) ->
146:     (match response_state with
147:     | Waiting -> failwith "h2.Reqd.response_exn: response has not started"
148:     | Streaming { response; _ } | Fixed { response; _ } | Complete response ->
149:       response)
150:   | Closed _ -> assert false
151: 
152: let send_fixed_response (t : t) s response data =
153:   match s.response_state with
154:   | Waiting ->
155:     let iovec, length =
156:       match data with
157:       | `String s ->
158:         let len = String.length s in
159:         let iovec = { Httpun_types.IOVec.buffer = `String s; off = 0; len } in
160:         iovec, len
161:       | `Bigstring b ->
162:         let len = Bigstringaf.length b in
163:         let iovec =
164:           { Httpun_types.IOVec.buffer = `Bigstring b; off = 0; len }
165:         in
166:         iovec, len
167:     in
168:     let should_send_data = length <> 0 in
169:     let frame_info =
170:       Writer.make_frame_info
171:         ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size
172:         ~flags:
173:           (if should_send_data
174:            then Flags.default_flags
175:            else Flags.(set_end_stream default_flags))
176:         t.id
177:     in
178:     Writer.write_response_headers t.writer s.encoder frame_info response;
179:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
180:      *   An HTTP request/response exchange fully consumes a single stream.
181:      *   [...] A response starts with a HEADERS frame and ends with a frame
182:      *   bearing END_STREAM, which places the stream in the "closed" state. *)
183:     if should_send_data
184:     then s.response_state <- Fixed { response; iovec }
185:     else s.response_state <- Complete response;
186:     Writer.wakeup t.writer
187:   | Streaming _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.respond_with_*: response already started"
188:   | Fixed _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.respond_with_*: response already sent"
189:   | Complete _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.respond_with_*: response already complete"
190: 
191: let schedule_trailers (t : t) new_trailers =
192:   match t.state with
193:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> assert false
194:   | Closed _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.schedule_trailers: stream already closed"
195:   | Reserved _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.schedule_trailers: response not started"
196:   | Active ((Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
197:     (match stream.response_state with
198:     | Streaming { response; response_body; trailers = old_trailers } ->
199:       if old_trailers <> Headers.empty
200:       then failwith "h2.Reqd.schedule_trailers: trailers already scheduled";
201:       stream.response_state <-
202:         Streaming { response; response_body; trailers = new_trailers }
203:     | _ ->
204:       failwith
205:         "h2.Reqd.schedule_trailers: can only send trailers in Streaming mode")
206: 
207: let unsafe_respond_with_data (t : t) response data =
208:   match t.state with
209:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> assert false
210:   | Active ((Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
211:     send_fixed_response t stream response data
212:   | Reserved (request_info, stream) ->
213:     send_fixed_response t stream response data;
214:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
215:      *   reserved (local): [...] In this state, only the following transitions
216:      *   are possible: The endpoint can send a HEADERS frame. This causes the
217:      *   stream to open in a "half-closed (remote)" state. *)
218:     Writer.flush t.writer (fun _reason ->
219:       (* TODO(anmonteiro): different if closed? *)
220:       t.state <- Active (HalfClosed request_info, stream))
221:   | Closed _ -> assert false
222: 
223: let respond_with_string (t : t) response str =
224:   match t.error_code with
225:   | No_error -> unsafe_respond_with_data t response (`String str)
226:   | _ ->
227:     failwith
228:       "h2.Reqd.respond_with_string: invalid state, currently handling error"
229: 
230: let respond_with_bigstring (t : t) response bstr =
231:   match t.error_code with
232:   | No_error -> unsafe_respond_with_data t response (`Bigstring bstr)
233:   | _ ->
234:     failwith
235:       "h2.Reqd.respond_with_bigstring: invalid state, currently handling error"
236: 
237: let send_streaming_response ~flush_headers_immediately (t : t) s response =
238:   let wait_for_first_flush = not flush_headers_immediately in
239:   match s.response_state with
240:   | Waiting ->
241:     let frame_info =
242:       Writer.make_frame_info ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size t.id
243:     in
244:     let response_body_buffer = Bigstringaf.create s.body_buffer_size in
245:     let response_body =
246:       Body.Writer.create response_body_buffer ~writer:t.writer
247:     in
248:     Writer.write_response_headers t.writer s.encoder frame_info response;
249:     if wait_for_first_flush then Writer.yield t.writer;
250:     s.response_state <-
251:       Streaming { response; response_body; trailers = Headers.empty };
252:     Writer.wakeup t.writer;
253:     response_body
254:   | Streaming _ ->
255:     failwith "h2.Reqd.respond_with_streaming: response already started"
256:   | Fixed _ | Complete _ ->
257:     failwith "h2.Reqd.respond_with_streaming: response already complete"
258: 
259: let unsafe_respond_with_streaming (t : t) ~flush_headers_immediately response =
260:   match t.state with
261:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> assert false
262:   | Active ((Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
263:     send_streaming_response ~flush_headers_immediately t stream response
264:   | Reserved (request_info, stream) ->
265:     let response_body =
266:       send_streaming_response ~flush_headers_immediately t stream response
267:     in
268:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
269:      *   reserved (local): [...] In this state, only the following transitions
270:      *   are possible: The endpoint can send a HEADERS frame. This causes the
271:      *   stream to open in a "half-closed (remote)" state. *)
272:     Writer.flush t.writer (fun _reason ->
273:       (* TODO(anmonteiro): different if closed? *)
274:       t.state <- Active (HalfClosed request_info, stream));
275:     response_body
276:   | Closed _ -> assert false
277: 
278: let respond_with_streaming (t : t) ?(flush_headers_immediately = false) response
279:   =
280:   match t.error_code with
281:   | No_error ->
282:     unsafe_respond_with_streaming ~flush_headers_immediately t response
283:   | _ ->
284:     failwith
285:       "h2.Reqd.respond_with_streaming: invalid state, currently handling error"
286: 
287: let start_push_stream (t : t) s request =
288:   match s.create_push_stream t.id with
289:   | Ok promised_reqd ->
290:     let frame_info =
291:       Writer.make_frame_info ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size t.id
292:     in
293:     Writer.write_push_promise
294:       t.writer
295:       s.encoder
296:       frame_info
297:       ~promised_id:promised_reqd.id
298:       request;
299:     let { encoder; body_buffer_size; create_push_stream; _ } = s in
300:     (* From RFC7540§8.2:
301:      *   Promised requests [...] MUST NOT include a request body. *)
302:     let request_info = create_active_request request Body.Reader.empty in
303:     let active_stream =
304:       create_active_stream encoder body_buffer_size create_push_stream
305:     in
306:     (* From RFC7540§8.2.1:
307:      *   Sending a PUSH_PROMISE frame creates a new stream and puts the stream
308:      *   into the "reserved (local)" state for the server and the "reserved
309:      *   (remote)" state for the client.
310:      *
311:      * Note: we do this before flushing the writer because request handlers
312:      * might immediately call one of the `respond_with` functions and expect
313:      * the stream to be in the `Reserved` state. *)
314:     promised_reqd.state <- Reserved (request_info, active_stream);
315:     Writer.wakeup t.writer;
316:     Ok promised_reqd
317:   | Error e ->
318:     Error (e :> [ `Push_disabled | `Stream_cant_push | `Stream_ids_exhausted ])
319: 
320: (* TODO: We could easily allow the priority of the PUSH request to be
321:  * configurable. We should allow users of this API to define the weight (maybe
322:  * not strictly), dependency on the current Reqd, and exclusivity *)
323: let unsafe_push (t : t) request =
324:   match t.state with
325:   | Idle | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> assert false
326:   | Active ((Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
327:     start_push_stream t stream request
328:   (* Already checked in `push` *)
329:   | Reserved _ | Closed _ -> assert false
330: 
331: let push (t : t) request =
332:   match t.error_code with
333:   | No_error ->
334:     if Stream_identifier.is_pushed t.id
335:     then
336:       (* From RFC7540§6.6:
337:        *   PUSH_PROMISE frames MUST only be sent on a peer-initiated stream that
338:        *   is in either the "open" or "half-closed (remote)" state. *)
339:       Error `Stream_cant_push
340:     else unsafe_push t request
341:   | _ -> failwith "h2.Reqd.push: invalid state, currently handling error"
342: 
343: let _report_error ?request (t : t) s (error : error) error_code =
344:   match s.response_state, t.error_code with
345:   | Waiting, No_error ->
346:     t.error_code <- Stream.error_to_code error error_code;
347:     let status =
348:       match (error :> [ error | Status.standard ]) with
349:       | `Exn _ -> `Internal_server_error
350:       | #Status.standard as status -> status
351:     in
352:     t.error_handler ?request error (fun headers ->
353:       let response = Response.create ~headers status in
354:       unsafe_respond_with_streaming ~flush_headers_immediately:true t response)
355:   | Streaming { response_body; _ }, No_error ->
356:     Body.Writer.close response_body;
357:     t.error_code <- Stream.error_to_code error error_code;
358:     Stream.reset_stream t error_code
359:   | Fixed _, No_error ->
360:     (* Still need to send an RST_STREAM frame. Set t.error_code with
361:      * `error_code` and `flush_response_body` below will reset the stream after
362:      * flushing any remaining body bytes. *)
363:     t.error_code <- Stream.error_to_code error error_code;
364:     Stream.reset_stream t error_code
365:   | (Waiting | Fixed _ | Streaming _), Exn _ ->
366:     (* XXX(seliopou): Decide what to do in this unlikely case. There is an
367:      * outstanding call to the [error_handler], but an intervening exception
368:      * has been reported as well. *)
369:     failwith "h2.Reqd.report_exn: NYI"
370:   | (Waiting | Streaming _ | Fixed _ | Complete _), _ -> ()
371: 
372: let report_error (t : t) exn error_code =
373:   match t.state with
374:   | Idle | Reserved _ | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) ->
375:     assert false
376:   | Active (Open FullHeaders, stream) -> _report_error t stream exn error_code
377:   | Active
378:       ( ( Open (ActiveMessage { request; request_body; _ })
379:         | HalfClosed { request; request_body; _ } )
380:       , stream ) ->
381:     Body.Reader.close request_body;
382:     _report_error t stream ~request exn error_code
383:   | Closed _ -> ()
384: 
385: let report_exn t exn = report_error t (`Exn exn) Error_code.InternalError
386: 
387: let try_with t f : (unit, exn) result =
388:   try
389:     f ();
390:     Ok ()
391:   with
392:   | exn ->
393:     report_exn t exn;
394:     Error exn
395: 
396: let error_code = Stream.error_code
397: (* Private API, not exposed to the user through h2.mli *)
398: 
399: let requires_output (t : t) =
400:   match t.state with
401:   | Idle -> false
402:   | Reserved _ -> true
403:   | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) -> false
404:   | Active
405:       ( (Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _)
406:       , { response_state; _ } ) ->
407:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
408:      *   A server can send a complete response prior to the client sending an
409:      *   entire request if the response does not depend on any portion of the
410:      *   request that has not been sent and received. *)
411:     (match response_state with
412:     | Complete _ -> false
413:     | Fixed { iovec = { len; _ }; _ } -> len > 0
414:     | Streaming _ -> true
415:     | Waiting -> true)
416:   | Closed _ -> false
417: 
418: let flush_request_body (t : t) =
419:   match t.state with
420:   | Active
421:       ( ( Open (ActiveMessage { request_body; _ })
422:         | HalfClosed { request_body; _ } )
423:       , _ ) ->
424:     if Body.Reader.has_pending_output request_body
425:     then (
426:       try Body.Reader.execute_read request_body with exn -> report_exn t exn)
427:   | _ -> ()
428: 
429: let write_buffer_data writer ~off ~len frame_info buffer =
430:   match buffer with
431:   | `String str -> Writer.write_data writer ~off ~len frame_info str
432:   | `Bigstring bstr -> Writer.schedule_data writer ~off ~len frame_info bstr
433: 
434: let close_stream (t : t) =
435:   match t.error_code with
436:   | No_error ->
437:     (match t.state with
438:     | Active (Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _), _) ->
439:       (* From RFC7540§8.1:
440:        *   A server can send a complete response prior to the client sending an
441:        *   entire request if the response does not depend on any portion of the
442:        *   request that has not been sent and received. When this is true, a
443:        *   server MAY request that the client abort transmission of a request
444:        *   without error by sending a RST_STREAM with an error code of NO_ERROR
445:        *   after sending a complete response (i.e., a frame with the END_STREAM
446:        *   flag). *)
447:       Stream.reset_stream t Error_code.NoError
448:     | Active (HalfClosed _, _) ->
449:       Writer.flush t.writer (fun _reason -> Stream.finish_stream t Finished)
450:     | _ -> assert false)
451:   | Exn _ -> Stream.reset_stream t InternalError
452:   | Other { code; _ } -> Stream.reset_stream t code
453: 
454: let flush_response_body (t : t) ~max_bytes =
455:   match t.state with
456:   | Active ((Open _ | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
457:     (match stream.response_state with
458:     | Streaming { response; response_body; trailers } ->
459:       if Body.Writer.has_pending_output response_body && max_bytes > 0
460:       then
461:         Body.Writer.transfer_to_writer
462:           response_body
463:           t.writer
464:           ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size
465:           ~max_bytes
466:           t.id
467:       else if Body.Writer.is_closed response_body
468:       then (
469:         (* no pending output and closed, we can finalize the message and close
470:            the stream *)
471:         let frame_info =
472:           Writer.make_frame_info
473:             ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size
474:             ~flags:Flags.(set_end_stream default_flags)
475:             t.id
476:         in
477:         match trailers with
478:         | _ :: _ ->
479:           Writer.write_response_trailers
480:             t.writer
481:             stream.encoder
482:             frame_info
483:             trailers;
484:           close_stream t;
485:           stream.response_state <- Complete response;
486:           0
487:         | [] ->
488:           (* From RFC7540§6.9.1:
489:            *   Frames with zero length with the END_STREAM flag set (that is,
490:            *   an empty DATA frame) MAY be sent if there is no available space
491:            *   in either flow-control window. *)
492:           Writer.schedule_data t.writer frame_info ~len:0 Bigstringaf.empty;
493:           close_stream t;
494:           stream.response_state <- Complete response;
495:           0)
496:       else (* no pending output but Body is still open *)
497:         0
498:     | Fixed ({ iovec = { buffer; off; len } as iovec; _ } as r)
499:       when max_bytes > 0 ->
500:       let is_partial_flush = max_bytes < len in
501:       let frame_info =
502:         let flags =
503:           if is_partial_flush
504:           then Flags.default_flags
505:           else Flags.(set_end_stream default_flags)
506:         in
507:         Writer.make_frame_info ~max_frame_size:t.max_frame_size ~flags t.id
508:       in
509:       let len_to_write = if is_partial_flush then max_bytes else len in
510:       write_buffer_data t.writer ~off ~len:len_to_write frame_info buffer;
511:       r.iovec <- Httpun_types.IOVec.shift iovec len_to_write;
512:       if not is_partial_flush then close_stream t;
513:       len_to_write
514:     | Fixed _ | Waiting | Complete _ -> 0)
515:   | _ -> 0
516: 
517: let deliver_trailer_headers (t : t) headers =
518:   match t.state with
519:   | Active (Open (PartialHeaders _ | FullHeaders), _) -> assert false
520:   | Active ((Open (ActiveMessage _) | HalfClosed _), stream) ->
521:     (* TODO: call the schedule_trailers callback *)
522:     stream.trailers <- Some headers
523:   | _ -> assert false
````

## File: ../../../../.cache/opam/5.2.0+ox/lib/h2/scheduler.ml
````ocaml
  1: (*----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  2:  *  Copyright (c) 2019 António Nuno Monteiro

 32: 
 33: module StreamsTbl = struct
 34:   include Hashtbl.MakeSeeded (struct
 35:       type t = Stream_identifier.t
 36: 
 37:       let equal = Stream_identifier.( === )
 38:       let hash i k = Hashtbl.seeded_hash i k
 39: 
 40:       (* Required for OCaml >= 5.0.0, but causes errors for older compilers
 41:          because it is an unused value declaration. *)
 42:       let[@warning "-32"] seeded_hash = hash
 43:     end)
 44: 
 45:   let[@inline] find_opt h key = try Some (find h key) with Not_found -> None
 46: end
 47: 
 48: module type StreamDescriptor = sig
 49:   type t
 50: 
 51:   val id : t -> Stream_identifier.t
 52:   val requires_output : t -> bool
 53:   val flush_write_body : t -> max_bytes:int -> int
 54:   val finish_stream : t -> Stream.closed_reason -> unit
 55:   val is_idle : t -> bool
 56: end
 57: 
 58: module Make (Streamd : StreamDescriptor) = struct
 59:   module rec PriorityTreeNode : sig
 60:     type root = Root
 61:     type nonroot = NonRoot
 62: 
 63:     type stream = nonroot node
 64:     and parent = Parent : _ node -> parent
 65: 
 66:     and _ node =
 67:       (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
 68:        *   A stream that is not dependent on any other stream is given a stream
 69:        *   dependency of 0x0. In other words, the non-existent stream 0 forms
 70:        *   the root of the tree.
 71:        *
 72:        * Note:
 73:        *   We use a GADT because the root of the tree doesn't have an
 74:        *   associated request descriptor. It has the added advantage of
 75:        *   allowing us to enforce that all (other) streams in the tree are
 76:        *   associated with a request descriptor. *)
 77:       | Connection :
 78:           { all_streams : stream StreamsTbl.t
 79:           ; mutable t_last : int
 80:           ; mutable children : PriorityQueue.t
 81:           ; (* Connection-level flow control window.
 82:              * outbound flow control, what we're allowed to send.
 83:              *
 84:              * From RFC7540§6.9.1:
 85:              *   Two flow-control windows are applicable: the stream
 86:              * flow-control window and the connection flow-control window. *)
 87:             mutable flow : Settings.WindowSize.t
 88:           ; (* inbound flow control, what the client is allowed to send. *)
 89:             mutable inflow : Settings.WindowSize.t
 90:           ; mutable marked_for_removal : Stream.closed StreamsTbl.t
 91:           }
 92:           -> root node
 93:       | Stream :
 94:           { descriptor : Streamd.t
 95:           ; mutable t_last : int
 96:           ; mutable t : int
 97:           ; mutable priority : Priority.t
 98:           ; mutable parent : parent
 99:           ; mutable children : PriorityQueue.t
100:           ; (* Stream-level flow control window. See connection-level above.
101:              *
102:              * From RFC7540§6.9.1:
103:              *   Two flow-control windows are applicable: the stream
104:              * flow-control window and the connection flow-control window. *)
105:             mutable flow : Settings.WindowSize.t
106:           ; mutable inflow : Settings.WindowSize.t
107:           }
108:           -> nonroot node
109:   end =
110:     PriorityTreeNode
111: 
112:   and PriorityQueue :
113:     (Psq.S with type k = Int32.t and type p = PriorityTreeNode.stream) =
114:     Psq.Make
115:       (Int32)
116:       (struct
117:         include PriorityTreeNode
118: 
119:         type t = stream
120: 
121:         let compare (Stream { t = t1; _ }) (Stream { t = t2; _ }) =
122:           compare t1 t2
123:       end)
124: 
125:   include PriorityTreeNode
126: 
127:   type t = root node
128: 
129:   (* TODO(anmonteiro): change according to SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS? *)
130:   let make_root ?(capacity = 65536) () =
131:     Connection
132:       { t_last = 0
133:       ; children = PriorityQueue.empty
134:       ; all_streams = StreamsTbl.create ~random:true capacity
135:       ; flow = Settings.WindowSize.default_initial_window_size
136:       ; inflow = Settings.WindowSize.default_initial_window_size
137:       ; marked_for_removal = StreamsTbl.create ~random:true 256
138:       }
139: 
140:   let create
141:       ~parent
142:       ~initial_send_window_size
143:       ~initial_recv_window_size
144:       descriptor
145:     =
146:     Stream
147:       { descriptor
148:       ; t_last = 0
149:       ; t = 0
150:       ; (* From RFC7540§5.3.5:
151:          *   All streams are initially assigned a non-exclusive dependency on
152:          *   stream 0x0. Pushed streams (Section 8.2) initially depend on their
153:          *   associated stream. In both cases, streams are assigned a default
154:          *   weight of 16. *)
155:         priority = Priority.default_priority
156:       ; parent
157:       ; children = PriorityQueue.empty
158:       ; flow = initial_send_window_size
159:       ; inflow = initial_recv_window_size
160:       }
161: 
162:   let stream_id : type a. a node -> int32 = function
163:     | Connection _ -> Stream_identifier.connection
164:     | Stream { descriptor; _ } -> Streamd.id descriptor
165: 
166:   let children : type a. a node -> PriorityQueue.t = function
167:     | Stream { children; _ } -> children
168:     | Connection { children; _ } -> children
169: 
170:   let remove_child : type a. a node -> int32 -> unit =
171:    fun parent id ->
172:     match parent with
173:     | Connection ({ children; _ } as node) ->
174:       (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
175:        *   A stream that is not dependent on any other stream is given a stream
176:        *   dependency of 0x0. In other words, the non-existent stream 0 forms
177:        *   the root of the tree. *)
178:       node.children <- PriorityQueue.remove id children
179:     | Stream ({ children; _ } as node) ->
180:       node.children <- PriorityQueue.remove id children
181: 
182:   let update_children : type a. a node -> PriorityQueue.t -> unit =
183:    fun parent updated_children ->
184:     match parent with
185:     | Connection s -> s.children <- updated_children
186:     | Stream s -> s.children <- updated_children
187: 
188:   let set_parent stream_node ~exclusive (Parent new_parent_node as new_parent) =
189:     let (Stream ({ descriptor; parent = Parent old_parent_node; _ } as stream)) =
190:       stream_node
191:     in
192:     let stream_id = Streamd.id descriptor in
193:     remove_child old_parent_node stream_id;
194:     stream.parent <- new_parent;
195:     let new_children =
196:       let new_children = children new_parent_node in
197:       if exclusive
198:       then (
199:         (* From RFC7540§5.3.3:
200:          *   Dependent streams move with their parent stream if the parent is
201:          *   reprioritized. Setting a dependency with the exclusive flag for a
202:          *   reprioritized stream causes all the dependencies of the new parent
203:          *   stream to become dependent on the reprioritized stream. *)
204:         stream.children <-
205:           PriorityQueue.fold
206:             (fun k (Stream p as p_node) pq ->
207:                p.parent <- Parent stream_node;
208:                PriorityQueue.add k p_node pq)
209:             stream.children
210:             new_children;
211:         (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
212:          *   An exclusive flag allows for the insertion of a new level of
213:          *   dependencies. The exclusive flag causes the stream to become the
214:          *   sole dependency of its parent stream, causing other dependencies
215:          *   to become dependent on the exclusive stream. *)
216:         PriorityQueue.sg stream_id stream_node)
217:       else PriorityQueue.add stream_id stream_node new_children
218:     in
219:     update_children new_parent_node new_children
220: 
221:   let would_create_cycle ~new_parent (Stream { descriptor; _ }) =
222:     let rec inner : type a. a node -> bool = function
223:       | Connection _ -> false
224:       | Stream { parent = Parent parent; _ }
225:         when Stream_identifier.(stream_id parent === Streamd.id descriptor) ->
226:         true
227:       | Stream { parent = Parent parent; _ } -> inner parent
228:     in
229:     let (Parent parent_node) = new_parent in
230:     inner parent_node
231: 
232:   let reprioritize_stream (Connection root as t) ~priority stream_node =
233:     let (Stream stream) = stream_node in
234:     let new_parent, new_priority =
235:       if Stream_identifier.is_connection priority.Priority.stream_dependency
236:       then Parent t, priority
237:       else
238:         match
239:           StreamsTbl.find_opt root.all_streams priority.stream_dependency
240:         with
241:         | Some parent_stream ->
242:           (match
243:              StreamsTbl.mem root.marked_for_removal priority.stream_dependency
244:            with
245:           | true ->
246:             (* A stream that is marked for removal is also not present in the
247:                tree *)
248:             Parent t, Priority.default_priority
249:           | false -> Parent parent_stream, priority)
250:         | None ->
251:           (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
252:            *   A dependency on a stream that is not currently in the tree —
253:            *   such as a stream in the "idle" state — results in that stream
254:            *   being given a default priority (Section 5.3.5). *)
255:           Parent t, Priority.default_priority
256:     in
257:     (* bail early if trying to set the same priority *)
258:     if not (Priority.equal stream.priority new_priority)
259:     then (
260:       let { Priority.stream_dependency; exclusive; _ } = new_priority in
261:       let (Parent current_parent_node) = stream.parent in
262:       let current_parent_id = stream_id current_parent_node in
263:       (* only need to set a different parent if the parent or exclusive status
264:        * changed *)
265:       if (not Stream_identifier.(stream_dependency === current_parent_id))
266:          || exclusive <> stream.priority.exclusive
267:       then (
268:         let (Parent new_parent_node) = new_parent in
269:         (match new_parent_node with
270:         | Stream new_parent_stream ->
271:           if would_create_cycle ~new_parent stream_node
272:           then (
273:             (* From RFC7540§5.3.3:
274:              *   If a stream is made dependent on one of its own dependencies,
275:              *   the formerly dependent stream is first moved to be dependent
276:              *   on the reprioritized stream's previous parent. The moved
277:              *   dependency retains its weight. *)
278:             set_parent new_parent_node ~exclusive:false stream.parent;
279:             new_parent_stream.priority <-
280:               { new_parent_stream.priority with
281:                 stream_dependency = current_parent_id
282:               })
283:         | Connection _ ->
284:           (* The root node cannot be dependent on any other streams, so we
285:            * don't need to worry about it creating cycles. *)
286:           ());
287:         (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
288:          *   When assigning a dependency on another stream, the stream is added
289:          *   as a new dependency of the parent stream. *)
290:         set_parent stream_node ~exclusive new_parent);
291:       stream.priority <- new_priority)
292: 
293:   let update_t node n =
294:     let (Stream ({ parent = Parent parent; descriptor; _ } as stream)) = node in
295:     let tlast_p =
296:       match parent with
297:       | Connection { t_last; _ } -> t_last
298:       | Stream { t_last; _ } -> t_last
299:     in
300:     stream.t <- tlast_p + (n * 256 / stream.priority.weight);
301:     let id = Streamd.id descriptor in
302:     remove_child parent id;
303:     let updated_children = PriorityQueue.add id node (children parent) in
304:     update_children parent updated_children
305: 
306:   let update_t_last : type a. a node -> int -> unit =
307:    fun p_node t_last ->
308:     match p_node with
309:     | Connection p -> p.t_last <- t_last
310:     | Stream p -> p.t_last <- t_last
311: 
312:   let add
313:       (Connection root as t)
314:       ~priority
315:       ~initial_send_window_size
316:       ~initial_recv_window_size
317:       descriptor
318:     =
319:     let stream =
320:       create
321:         ~parent:(Parent t)
322:         ~initial_send_window_size
323:         ~initial_recv_window_size
324:         descriptor
325:     in
326:     let stream_id = Streamd.id descriptor in
327:     StreamsTbl.add root.all_streams stream_id stream;
328:     root.children <- PriorityQueue.add stream_id stream root.children;
329:     if priority != Priority.default_priority
330:     then reprioritize_stream t ~priority stream;
331:     update_t stream 0;
332:     stream
333: 
334:   let get_node (Connection root) stream_id =
335:     StreamsTbl.find_opt root.all_streams stream_id
336: 
337:   let find t stream_id =
338:     match get_node t stream_id with
339:     | Some (Stream { descriptor; _ }) -> Some descriptor
340:     | None -> None
341: 
342:   let iter (Connection { all_streams; _ }) ~f =
343:     StreamsTbl.iter (fun _id stream -> f stream) all_streams
344: 
345:   let allowed_to_transmit (Connection root) (Stream stream) =
346:     Int32.compare root.flow 0l > 0 && Int32.compare stream.flow 0l > 0
347: 
348:   let allowed_to_receive (Connection root) (Stream stream) size =
349:     size <= root.inflow && size <= stream.inflow
350: 
351:   let write (Connection root as t) stream_node =
352:     let (Stream ({ descriptor; _ } as stream)) = stream_node in
353:     (* From RFC7540§6.9.1:
354:      *   Two flow-control windows are applicable: the stream flow-control
355:      *   window and the connection flow-control window. The sender MUST NOT
356:      *   send a flow-controlled frame with a length that exceeds the space
357:      *   available in either of the flow-control windows advertised by the
358:      *   receiver. *)
359:     let allowed_bytes =
360:       if allowed_to_transmit t stream_node
361:       then min root.flow stream.flow
362:       else
363:         (* There might be a zero-length DATA frame (with the end stream flag
364:            set) waiting to be sent. *)
365:         0l
366:     in
367:     let written =
368:       Streamd.flush_write_body
369:         ~max_bytes:(Int32.to_int allowed_bytes)
370:         descriptor
371:     in
372:     let written32 = Int32.of_int written in
373:     (* From RFC7540§6.9.1:
374:      *   After sending a flow-controlled frame, the sender reduces the space
375:      *   available in both windows by the length of the transmitted frame. *)
376:     root.flow <- Int32.sub root.flow written32;
377:     stream.flow <- Int32.sub stream.flow written32;
378:     written
379: 
380:   let mark_for_removal (Connection root) id closed =
381:     StreamsTbl.replace root.marked_for_removal id closed
382: 
383:   let implicitly_close_idle_stream descriptor max_seen_ids =
384:     let implicitly_close_stream descriptor =
385:       if Streamd.is_idle descriptor
386:       then
387:         (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
388:          *   The first use of a new stream identifier implicitly closes all
389:          *   streams in the "idle" state that might have been initiated by
390:          *   that peer with a lower-valued stream identifier. *)
391:         Streamd.finish_stream descriptor Finished
392:     in
393:     let max_client_stream_id, max_pushed_stream_id = max_seen_ids in
394:     let stream_id = Streamd.id descriptor in
395:     if Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id
396:     then (
397:       if stream_id < max_client_stream_id
398:       then implicitly_close_stream descriptor)
399:     else if stream_id < max_pushed_stream_id
400:     then implicitly_close_stream descriptor
401: 
402:   (* Scheduling algorithm from https://goo.gl/3sSHXJ (based on nghttp2):
403:    *
404:    * 1  def schedule(p):
405:    * 2    if stream #p has data to send:
406:    * 3      send data for #p, update nsent[p]
407:    * 4      return
408:    * 5    if #p's queue is empty:
409:    * 6      return
410:    * 7    pop #i from queue
411:    * 8    update t_last[p] = t[i]
412:    * 9    schedule(i)
413:    * 10   if #i or its descendant is "active":
414:    * 11     update t[i] and push it into queue again
415:    * 12
416:    * 13 schedule(0)
417:    *)
418:   let flush t max_seen_ids =
419:     let rec schedule : type a. a node -> int * bool = function
420:       | Connection _ as p_node ->
421:         (* The root can never send data. *)
422:         traverse p_node
423:       | Stream ({ descriptor; _ } as stream) as p_node ->
424:         let written =
425:           if Streamd.requires_output descriptor
426:           then
427:             (* In this branch, flow-control has no bearing on activity, otherwise
428:              * a flow-controlled stream would be considered inactive (because it
429:              * can't make progress at the moment) and removed from the priority
430:              * tree altogether. *)
431:             write t p_node
432:           else 0
433:         in
434:         if written > 0
435:         then
436:           (* We check for activity again, because the stream may have gone
437:            * inactive after the call to `write` above. *)
438:           let subtree_is_active =
439:             Streamd.requires_output descriptor
440:             || not (PriorityQueue.is_empty stream.children)
441:           in
442:           written, subtree_is_active
443:         else
444:           (* If we haven't written anything, check if any of our children
445:              have. *)
446:           let written, subtree_is_active' = traverse p_node in
447:           let subtree_is_active =
448:             Streamd.requires_output descriptor || subtree_is_active'
449:           in
450:           (match written with
451:           | 0 -> written, subtree_is_active
452:           | written ->
453:             (* If there's still more to write, put the node back in the tree. *)
454:             if subtree_is_active then update_t p_node written;
455:             written, subtree_is_active)
456:     and traverse : type a. a node -> int * bool =
457:      fun p_node ->
458:       let rec loop remaining_children =
459:         match PriorityQueue.pop remaining_children with
460:         | Some ((id, (Stream i as i_node)), remaining_children') ->
461:           update_t_last p_node i.t;
462:           let written, subtree_is_active = schedule i_node in
463: 
464:           if not subtree_is_active
465:           then (
466:             implicitly_close_idle_stream i.descriptor max_seen_ids;
467:             (* XXX(anmonteiro): we may not want to remove from the tree right
468:              * away. *)
469:             remove_child p_node id);
470: 
471:           (match written with
472:           | 0 ->
473:             (* If this subtree didn't write anything, check the other children
474:                in the priority queue. *)
475:             loop remaining_children'
476:           | written ->
477:             (* If there's still more to write, put the node back in the tree. *)
478:             if subtree_is_active then update_t i_node written;
479:             written, subtree_is_active)
480:         | None ->
481:           (* No data written, but queue was not originally empty.
482:            * Therefore, we can't determine the subtree is inactive. *)
483:           0, true
484:       in
485:       let children = children p_node in
486:       match PriorityQueue.is_empty children with
487:       | true ->
488:         (* Queue is empty, see line 6 above. *)
489:         0, false
490:       | false -> loop children
491:     in
492: 
493:     let (Connection root) = t in
494:     ignore (schedule t);
495:     StreamsTbl.iter
496:       (fun id closed ->
497:          (* When a stream completes, i.e. doesn't require more output and
498:           * enters the `Closed` state, we set a TTL value which represents the
499:           * number of writer yields that the stream has before it is removed
500:           * from the connection Hash Table. By doing this we avoid losing some
501:           * potentially useful information regarding the stream's state at the
502:           * cost of keeping it around for a little while longer. *)
503:          if closed.Stream.ttl = 0
504:          then (
505:            StreamsTbl.remove root.marked_for_removal id;
506:            StreamsTbl.remove root.all_streams id)
507:          else closed.ttl <- closed.ttl - 1)
508:       root.marked_for_removal
509: 
510:   (* XXX(anmonteiro): Consider using `optint` for this?
511:    * https://github.com/mirage/optint
512:    *)
513:   let check_flow flow growth flow' =
514:     (* Check for overflow on 32-bit systems. *)
515:     Int32.compare flow' growth > 0 = (Int32.compare flow 0l > 0)
516:     && Int32.compare flow' Settings.WindowSize.max_window_size <= 0
517: 
518:   let add_flow : type a. a node -> int32 -> bool =
519:    fun t growth ->
520:     match t with
521:     | Connection ({ flow; _ } as root) ->
522:       let flow' = Int32.add flow growth in
523:       let valid_flow = check_flow flow growth flow' in
524:       if valid_flow then root.flow <- flow';
525:       valid_flow
526:     | Stream ({ flow; _ } as stream) ->
527:       let flow' = Int32.add flow growth in
528:       let valid_flow = check_flow flow growth flow' in
529:       if valid_flow then stream.flow <- flow';
530:       valid_flow
531: 
532:   let add_inflow : type a. a node -> int32 -> bool =
533:    fun t growth ->
534:     match t with
535:     | Connection ({ inflow; _ } as root) ->
536:       let inflow' = Int32.add inflow growth in
537:       let valid_inflow = check_flow inflow growth inflow' in
538:       if valid_inflow then root.inflow <- inflow';
539:       valid_inflow
540:     | Stream ({ inflow; _ } as stream) ->
541:       let inflow' = Int32.add inflow growth in
542:       let valid_inflow = check_flow inflow growth inflow' in
543:       if valid_inflow then stream.inflow <- inflow';
544:       valid_inflow
545: 
546:   let deduct_inflow : type a. a node -> int32 -> unit =
547:    fun t size ->
548:     match t with
549:     | Connection ({ inflow; _ } as root) ->
550:       (* no need to check, we verify that the peer is allowed to send. *)
551:       root.inflow <- Int32.sub inflow size
552:     | Stream ({ inflow; _ } as stream) -> stream.inflow <- Int32.sub inflow size
553: 
554:   let pp_hum fmt t =
555:     let rec pp_hum_inner level fmt t =
556:       let pp_binding fmt (id, Stream { children; t; _ }) =
557:         Format.fprintf
558:           fmt
559:           "\n%s%ld, %d -> [%a]"
560:           (String.make (level * 2) ' ')
561:           id
562:           t
563:           (pp_hum_inner (level + 1))
564:           children
565:       in
566:       PriorityQueue.pp pp_binding fmt t
567:     in
568:     pp_hum_inner 0 fmt t
569: 
570:   let pp_hum fmt (Connection { children; _ }) = pp_hum fmt children
571: end
````

````ocaml

 32:  *  POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.

 34: 
 35: open Faraday
 36: module IOVec = Httpun_types.IOVec
 37: 
 38: type frame_info =
 39:   { flags : Flags.t
 40:   ; stream_id : Stream_identifier.t
 41:   ; padding : Bigstringaf.t
 42:   ; max_frame_payload : int
 43:   }
 44: 
 45: let write_uint24 t n =
 46:   let write_octet t o = write_uint8 t (o land 0xff) in
 47:   write_octet t (n lsr 16);
 48:   write_octet t (n lsr 8);
 49:   write_octet t n
 50: 
 51: let write_frame_header t frame_header =
 52:   let { Frame.payload_length; flags; stream_id; frame_type } = frame_header in
 53:   write_uint24 t payload_length;
 54:   write_uint8 t (Frame.FrameType.serialize frame_type);
 55:   write_uint8 t flags;
 56:   BE.write_uint32 t stream_id
 57: 
 58: let write_frame_with_padding t info frame_type length writer =
 59:   let header, writer =
 60:     if Bigstringaf.length info.padding = 0
 61:     then
 62:       let header =
 63:         { Frame.payload_length = length
 64:         ; flags = info.flags
 65:         ; stream_id = info.stream_id
 66:         ; frame_type
 67:         }
 68:       in
 69:       header, writer
 70:     else
 71:       let pad_length = Bigstringaf.length info.padding in
 72:       let writer' t =
 73:         write_uint8 t pad_length;
 74:         writer t;
 75:         schedule_bigstring ~off:0 ~len:pad_length t info.padding
 76:       in
 77:       let header =
 78:         { Frame.payload_length = length + pad_length + 1
 79:         ; flags = Flags.set_padded info.flags
 80:         ; stream_id = info.stream_id
 81:         ; frame_type
 82:         }
 83:       in
 84:       header, writer'
 85:   in
 86:   write_frame_header t header;
 87:   writer t
 88: 
 89: let write_data_frame t ?off ?len info body =
 90:   let writer t = write_string t ?off ?len body in
 91:   let length = match len with Some len -> len | None -> String.length body in
 92:   write_frame_with_padding t info Data length writer
 93: 
 94: let schedule_data_frame t info ?off ?len bstr =
 95:   let writer t = schedule_bigstring t ?off ?len bstr in
 96:   let length =
 97:     match len with Some len -> len | None -> Bigstringaf.length bstr
 98:   in
 99:   write_frame_with_padding t info Data length writer
100: 
101: let write_priority t { Priority.exclusive; stream_dependency; weight } =
102:   let stream_dependency_id =
103:     if exclusive
104:     then Priority.set_exclusive stream_dependency
105:     else stream_dependency
106:   in
107:   BE.write_uint32 t stream_dependency_id;
108:   (* From RFC7540§6.3:
109:    *   An unsigned 8-bit integer representing a priority weight for the stream
110:    *   (see Section 5.3). Add one to the value to obtain a weight between 1 and
111:    *   256.
112:    *
113:    * Note: we store priority with values from 1 to 256, so decrement here. *)
114:   write_uint8 t (weight - 1)
115: 
116: let bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs =
117:   let rec loop t remaining iovecs =
118:     match remaining, iovecs with
119:     | 0, _ | _, [] -> ()
120:     | remaining, { IOVec.buffer; off; len } :: xs ->
121:       if remaining < len
122:       then schedule_bigstring t ~off ~len:remaining buffer
123:       else (
124:         schedule_bigstring t ~off ~len buffer;
125:         loop t (remaining - len) xs)
126:   in
127:   loop t len iovecs
128: 
129: let write_headers_frame t info ~priority ?len iovecs =
130:   let len = match len with Some len -> len | None -> IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
131:   if priority == Priority.default_priority
132:   then
133:     (* See RFC7540§6.3:
134:      *   Just the Header Block Fragment length if no priority. *)
135:     let writer t = bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs in
136:     write_frame_with_padding t info Headers len writer
137:   else
138:     (* See RFC7540§6.2:
139:      *   Exclusive Bit & Stream Dependency (4 octets) + Weight (1 octet) +
140:      *   Header Block Fragment length. *)
141:     let payload_length = len + 5 in
142:     let info' = { info with flags = Flags.set_priority info.flags } in
143:     let writer t =
144:       write_priority t priority;
145:       bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs
146:     in
147:     write_frame_with_padding t info' Headers payload_length writer
148: 
149: let write_priority_frame t info priority =
150:   let header =
151:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
152:     ; stream_id =
153:         info.stream_id
154:         (* See RFC7540§6.3:
155:          *   Stream Dependency (4 octets) + Weight (1 octet). *)
156:     ; payload_length = 5
157:     ; frame_type = Priority
158:     }
159:   in
160:   write_frame_header t header;
161:   write_priority t priority
162: 
163: let write_rst_stream_frame t info e =
164:   let header =
165:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
166:     ; stream_id =
167:         info.stream_id
168:         (* From RFC7540§6.4:
169:          *   The RST_STREAM frame contains a single unsigned, 32-bit integer
170:          *   identifying the error code (Section 7). *)
171:     ; payload_length = 4
172:     ; frame_type = RSTStream
173:     }
174:   in
175:   write_frame_header t header;
176:   BE.write_uint32 t (Error_code.serialize e)
177: 
178: let write_settings_frame t info settings =
179:   let header =
180:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
181:     ; stream_id =
182:         info.stream_id
183:         (* From RFC7540§6.5.1:
184:          *   The payload of a SETTINGS frame consists of zero or more
185:          *   parameters, each consisting of an unsigned 16-bit setting
186:          *   identifier and an unsigned 32-bit value. *)
187:     ; payload_length = List.length settings * 6
188:     ; frame_type = Settings
189:     }
190:   in
191:   write_frame_header t header;
192:   Settings.write_settings_payload t settings
193: 
194: let write_push_promise_frame t info ~promised_id ?len iovecs =
195:   let len = match len with Some len -> len | None -> IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
196:   let payload_length =
197:     (* From RFC7540§6.6:
198:      *   The PUSH_PROMISE frame includes the unsigned 31-bit identifier of the
199:      *   stream the endpoint plans to create along with a set of headers that
200:      *   provide additional context for the stream. *)
201:     4 + len
202:   in
203:   let writer t =
204:     BE.write_uint32 t promised_id;
205:     bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs
206:   in
207:   write_frame_with_padding t info PushPromise payload_length writer
208: 
209: let default_ping_payload =
210:   (* From RFC7540§6.7:
211:    *   In addition to the frame header, PING frames MUST contain 8 octets of
212:    *   opaque data in the payload. *)
213:   let bstr = Bigstringaf.create 8 in
214:   for i = 0 to 7 do
215:     Bigstringaf.unsafe_set bstr i '\000'
216:   done;
217:   bstr
218: 
219: let write_ping_frame t info ?(off = 0) payload =
220:   (* From RFC7540§6.7:
221:    *   In addition to the frame header, PING frames MUST contain 8 octets of
222:    *   opaque data in the payload. *)
223:   let payload_length = 8 in
224:   let header =
225:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
226:     ; stream_id = info.stream_id
227:     ; payload_length
228:     ; frame_type = Ping
229:     }
230:   in
231:   write_frame_header t header;
232:   schedule_bigstring ~off ~len:payload_length t payload
233: 
234: let write_go_away_frame t info stream_id error_code debug_data =
235:   let debug_data_len = Bigstringaf.length debug_data in
236:   let header =
237:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
238:     ; stream_id =
239:         info.stream_id
240:         (* See RFC7540§6.8:
241:          *   Last-Stream-ID (4 octets) + Error Code (4 octets) + Additional
242:          *   Debug Data (opaque) *)
243:     ; payload_length = 8 + debug_data_len
244:     ; frame_type = GoAway
245:     }
246:   in
247:   write_frame_header t header;
248:   BE.write_uint32 t stream_id;
249:   BE.write_uint32 t (Error_code.serialize error_code);
250:   schedule_bigstring t ~off:0 ~len:debug_data_len debug_data
251: 
252: let write_window_update_frame t info window_size =
253:   let header =
254:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
255:     ; stream_id =
256:         info.stream_id
257:         (* From RFC7540§6.9:
258:          *   The payload of a WINDOW_UPDATE frame is one reserved bit plus an
259:          *   unsigned 31-bit integer indicating the number of octets that the
260:          *   sender can transmit in addition to the existing flow-control
261:          *   window. *)
262:     ; payload_length = 4
263:     ; frame_type = WindowUpdate
264:     }
265:   in
266:   write_frame_header t header;
267:   BE.write_uint32 t window_size
268: 
269: let write_continuation_frame t info ?len iovecs =
270:   let len = match len with Some len -> len | None -> IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
271:   let header =
272:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
273:     ; stream_id = info.stream_id
274:     ; payload_length = len
275:     ; frame_type = Continuation
276:     }
277:   in
278:   write_frame_header t header;
279:   bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs
280: 
281: let write_unknown_frame t ~code info payload =
282:   let payload_length = Bigstringaf.length payload in
283:   let header =
284:     { Frame.flags = info.flags
285:     ; stream_id = info.stream_id
286:     ; payload_length
287:     ; frame_type = Unknown code
288:     }
289:   in
290:   write_frame_header t header;
291:   schedule_bigstring t ~off:0 ~len:payload_length payload
292: 
293: let write_connection_preface t =
294:   (* From RFC7540§3.5:
295:    *   In HTTP/2, each endpoint is required to send a connection preface as a
296:    *   final confirmation of the protocol in use and to establish the initial
297:    *   settings for the HTTP/2 connection. [...] The client connection preface
298:    *   starts with a sequence of 24 octets, [...] the string
299:    *   PRI * HTTP/2.0\r\n\r\nSM\r\n\r\n. *)
300:   write_string t Frame.connection_preface
301: 
302: module Writer = struct
303:   type t =
304:     { buffer : Bigstringaf.t
305:           (* The buffer that the encoder uses for buffered writes. Managed by
306:            * the control module for the encoder. *)
307:     ; encoder : Faraday.t
308:           (* The encoder that handles encoding for writes. Uses the [buffer]
309:            * referenced above internally. *)
310:     ; mutable drained_bytes : int
311:           (* The number of bytes that were not written due to the output stream
312:            * being closed before all buffered output could be written. Useful
313:            * for detecting error cases. *)
314:     ; mutable wakeup : Optional_thunk.t
315:     }
316: 
317:   let create buffer_size =
318:     let buffer = Bigstringaf.create buffer_size in
319:     let encoder = Faraday.of_bigstring buffer in
320:     { buffer; encoder; drained_bytes = 0; wakeup = Optional_thunk.none }
321: 
322:   let faraday t = t.encoder
323: 
324:   let make_frame_info
325:       ?(padding = Bigstringaf.empty)
326:       ?(flags = Flags.default_flags)
327:       ?(max_frame_size = Config.default.read_buffer_size)
328:       stream_id
329:     =
330:     { flags; stream_id; padding; max_frame_payload = max_frame_size }
331: 
332:   let write_connection_preface t settings_list =
333:     write_connection_preface t.encoder;
334:     let frame_info = make_frame_info Stream_identifier.connection in
335:     (* From RFC7540§3.5:
336:      *   This sequence MUST be followed by a SETTINGS frame (Section 6.5),
337:      *   which MAY be empty. *)
338:     write_settings_frame t.encoder frame_info settings_list
339: 
340:   let chunk_data_frames ?(off = 0) ~f frame_info total_length =
341:     let { max_frame_payload; _ } = frame_info in
342:     if max_frame_payload < total_length
343:     then
344:       let rec loop ~off remaining =
345:         if max_frame_payload < remaining
346:         then (
347:           (* Note: If we're splitting data into several frames, only the last
348:            * one should contain the END_STREAM flag, so unset it here if it's
349:            * set. *)
350:           let frame_info =
351:             { frame_info with flags = Flags.clear_end_stream frame_info.flags }
352:           in
353:           f ~off ~len:max_frame_payload frame_info;
354:           loop ~off:(off + max_frame_payload) (remaining - max_frame_payload))
355:         else f ~off ~len:remaining frame_info
356:       in
357:       loop ~off total_length
358:     else f ~off ~len:total_length frame_info
359: 
360:   let write_data t frame_info ?off ?len str =
361:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
362:     then
363:       let total_length =
364:         match len with Some len -> len | None -> String.length str
365:       in
366:       chunk_data_frames
367:         frame_info
368:         ?off
369:         total_length
370:         ~f:(fun ~off ~len frame_info ->
371:           write_data_frame t.encoder frame_info ~off ~len str)
372: 
373:   let schedule_data t frame_info ?off ?len bstr =
374:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
375:     then
376:       let total_length =
377:         match len with Some len -> len | None -> Bigstringaf.length bstr
378:       in
379:       chunk_data_frames
380:         frame_info
381:         ?off
382:         total_length
383:         ~f:(fun ~off ~len frame_info ->
384:           schedule_data_frame t.encoder frame_info ~off ~len bstr)
385: 
386:   (* Chunk header block fragments into HEADERS|PUSH_PROMISE + CONTINUATION
387:    * frames. *)
388:   let chunk_header_block_fragments
389:       t
390:       frame_info
391:       ?(has_priority = false)
392:       ~(write_frame :
393:          Faraday.t -> frame_info -> ?len:int -> Bigstringaf.t iovec list -> unit)
394:       faraday
395:     =
396:     let block_size = Faraday.pending_bytes faraday in
397:     let total_length =
398:       if has_priority
399:       then
400:         (* See RFC7540§6.2: Exclusive Bit & Stream Dependency (4 octets) +
401:            Weight (1 octet) + Header Block Fragment length. *)
402:         block_size + 5
403:       else block_size
404:     in
405:     let { max_frame_payload; _ } = frame_info in
406:     if max_frame_payload < total_length
407:     then (
408:       let headers_block_len =
409:         if has_priority then max_frame_payload - 5 else max_frame_payload
410:       in
411:       ignore
412:         (Faraday.serialize faraday (fun iovecs ->
413:            write_frame t.encoder frame_info ~len:headers_block_len iovecs;
414:            `Ok headers_block_len));
415:       let rec loop remaining =
416:         if max_frame_payload < remaining
417:         then (
418:           (* Note: Don't reuse flags from frame info as CONTINUATION frames
419:            * only define END_HEADERS.
420:            *
421:            * From RFC7540§6.10:
422:            *   The CONTINUATION frame defines the following flag:
423:            *
424:            *     END_HEADERS (0x4): When set, bit 2 indicates that this frame
425:            *     ends a header block (Section 4.3). *)
426:           let frame_info = { frame_info with flags = Flags.default_flags } in
427:           ignore
428:             (Faraday.serialize faraday (fun iovecs ->
429:                write_continuation_frame
430:                  t.encoder
431:                  frame_info
432:                  ~len:max_frame_payload
433:                  iovecs;
434:                `Ok max_frame_payload));
435:           loop (remaining - max_frame_payload))
436:         else
437:           let frame_info =
438:             { frame_info with flags = Flags.(set_end_header default_flags) }
439:           in
440:           ignore
441:             (Faraday.serialize faraday (fun iovecs ->
442:                write_continuation_frame
443:                  t.encoder
444:                  frame_info
445:                  ~len:remaining
446:                  iovecs;
447:                `Ok remaining))
448:       in
449:       loop (block_size - headers_block_len))
450:     else
451:       let frame_info =
452:         { frame_info with flags = Flags.set_end_header frame_info.flags }
453:       in
454:       ignore
455:         (Faraday.serialize faraday (fun iovecs ->
456:            let len = IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
457:            write_frame t.encoder frame_info ~len iovecs;
458:            `Ok len))
459: 
460:   let encode_headers hpack_encoder faraday headers =
461:     List.iter
462:       (fun header -> Hpack.Encoder.encode_header hpack_encoder faraday header)
463:       (Headers.to_hpack_list headers)
464: 
465:   let write_request_like_frame t hpack_encoder ~write_frame frame_info request =
466:     let { Request.meth; target; scheme; headers } = request in
467:     let faraday = Faraday.create 0x1000 in
468:     Hpack.Encoder.encode_header
469:       hpack_encoder
470:       faraday
471:       { Headers.name = ":method"
472:       ; value = Httpun_types.Method.to_string meth
473:       ; sensitive = false
474:       };
475:     if meth <> `CONNECT
476:     then (
477:       (* From RFC7540§8.3:
478:        *   The :scheme and :path pseudo-header fields MUST be omitted. *)
479:       Hpack.Encoder.encode_header
480:         hpack_encoder
481:         faraday
482:         { Headers.name = ":path"; value = target; sensitive = false };
483:       Hpack.Encoder.encode_header
484:         hpack_encoder
485:         faraday
486:         { Headers.name = ":scheme"; value = scheme; sensitive = false });
487:     encode_headers hpack_encoder faraday headers;
488:     chunk_header_block_fragments t frame_info ~write_frame faraday
489: 
490:   let write_request_headers t hpack_encoder ~priority frame_info request =
491:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
492:     then
493:       let write_frame = write_headers_frame ~priority in
494:       write_request_like_frame t hpack_encoder ~write_frame frame_info request
495: 
496:   let write_push_promise t hpack_encoder frame_info ~promised_id request =
497:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
498:     then
499:       let write_frame = write_push_promise_frame ~promised_id in
500:       write_request_like_frame t hpack_encoder ~write_frame frame_info request
501: 
502:   let write_response_headers t hpack_encoder frame_info response =
503:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
504:     then (
505:       let { Response.status; headers; _ } = response in
506:       let faraday = Faraday.create 0x1000 in
507:       (* From RFC7540§8.1.2.4:
508:        *   For HTTP/2 responses, a single :status pseudo-header field is defined
509:        *   that carries the HTTP status code field (see [RFC7231], Section 6).
510:        *   This pseudo-header field MUST be included in all responses; otherwise,
511:        *   the response is malformed (Section 8.1.2.6). *)
512:       Hpack.Encoder.encode_header
513:         hpack_encoder
514:         faraday
515:         { Headers.name = ":status"
516:         ; value = Status.to_string status
517:         ; sensitive = false
518:         };
519:       encode_headers hpack_encoder faraday headers;
520:       chunk_header_block_fragments
521:         t
522:         frame_info
523:         ~write_frame:(write_headers_frame ~priority:Priority.default_priority)
524:         ~has_priority:false
525:         faraday)
526: 
527:   let write_response_trailers t hpack_encoder frame_info trailers =
528:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
529:     then (
530:       let faraday = Faraday.create 0x1000 in
531:       (* From RFC7540§8.1:
532:        *  optionally, one HEADERS frame, followed by zero or more
533:        *  CONTINUATION frames containing the trailer-part, if present (see
534:        *  [RFC7230], Section 4.1.2). *)
535:       encode_headers hpack_encoder faraday trailers;
536:       chunk_header_block_fragments
537:         t
538:         frame_info
539:         ~write_frame:(write_headers_frame ~priority:Priority.default_priority)
540:         ~has_priority:false
541:         faraday)
542: 
543:   let write_rst_stream t frame_info e =
544:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
545:     then write_rst_stream_frame t.encoder frame_info e
546: 
547:   let write_window_update t frame_info n =
548:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
549:     then write_window_update_frame t.encoder frame_info n
550: 
551:   let schedule_iovecs t ~len frame_info iovecs =
552:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
553:     then
554:       let writer t ~len ~iovecs = bounded_schedule_iovecs t ~len iovecs in
555:       chunk_data_frames frame_info len ~f:(fun ~off ~len frame_info ->
556:         write_frame_with_padding
557:           t.encoder
558:           frame_info
559:           Data
560:           len
561:           (writer ~iovecs:(IOVec.shiftv iovecs off) ~len))
562: 
563:   let write_priority t frame_info priority =
564:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
565:     then write_priority_frame t.encoder frame_info priority
566: 
567:   let write_settings t frame_info settings =
568:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
569:     then write_settings_frame t.encoder frame_info settings
570: 
571:   let write_ping t frame_info ?off payload =
572:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
573:     then write_ping_frame t.encoder frame_info ?off payload
574: 
575:   let write_go_away t frame_info ~debug_data ~last_stream_id error =
576:     if not (is_closed t.encoder)
577:     then
578:       write_go_away_frame t.encoder frame_info last_stream_id error debug_data
579: 
580:   let on_wakeup_writer t k =
581:     if Faraday.is_closed t.encoder
582:     then failwith "on_wakeup_writer on closed conn"
583:     else if Optional_thunk.is_some t.wakeup
584:     then failwith "on_wakeup: only one callback can be registered at a time"
585:     else t.wakeup <- Optional_thunk.some k
586: 
587:   let wakeup t =
588:     let f = t.wakeup in
589:     t.wakeup <- Optional_thunk.none;
590:     Optional_thunk.call_if_some f
591: 
592:   let flush t f =
593:     flush_with_reason t.encoder (fun reason ->
594:       let result =
595:         match reason with
596:         | Nothing_pending | Shift -> `Written
597:         | Drain -> `Closed
598:       in
599:       f result)
600: 
601:   let unyield t =
602:     (* Faraday doesn't have a function to take the serializer out of a yield
603:        state. In the meantime, `flush` does it. *)
604:     flush t (fun _reason -> ())
605: 
606:   let yield t = Faraday.yield t.encoder
607:   let close t = Faraday.close t.encoder
608: 
609:   let close_and_drain t =
610:     Faraday.close t.encoder;
611:     let drained = Faraday.drain t.encoder in
612:     t.drained_bytes <- t.drained_bytes + drained
613: 
614:   let is_closed t = Faraday.is_closed t.encoder
615:   let drained_bytes t = t.drained_bytes
616: 
617:   let report_result t result =
618:     match result with
619:     | `Closed -> close_and_drain t
620:     | `Ok len -> shift t.encoder len
621: 
622:   let next t =
623:     match Faraday.operation t.encoder with
624:     | `Close -> `Close (drained_bytes t)
625:     | `Yield -> `Yield
626:     | `Writev iovecs -> `Write iovecs
627: end
````

## File: ../../../../.cache/opam/5.2.0+ox/lib/h2/server_connection.ml
````ocaml
   1: (*----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   2:  *  Copyright (c) 2017 Inhabited Type LLC.
   3:  *  Copyright (c) 2019 Antonio N. Monteiro.

   5:  *  All rights reserved.

  12:  *     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.

  14:  *  2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright
  15:  *     notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
  16:  *     documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.

  19:  *     may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this software
  20:  *     without specific prior written permission.
  21:  *

  23:  *  OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED
  24:  *  WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE
  25:  *  DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR
  26:  *  ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL
  27:  *  DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS
  28:  *  OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
  29:  *  HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT,
  30:  *  STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE) ARISING IN
  31:  *  ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE
  32:  *  POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
  33:  *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*)
  34: 
  35: module AB = Angstrom.Buffered
  36: module Reader = Parse.Reader
  37: module Writer = Serialize.Writer
  38: 
  39: module Scheduler = Scheduler.Make (struct
  40:     include Stream
  41: 
  42:     type t = Reqd.t
  43: 
  44:     let flush_write_body = Reqd.flush_response_body
  45:     let requires_output = Reqd.requires_output
  46:   end)
  47: 
  48: type request_handler = Reqd.t -> unit
  49: 
  50: type error =
  51:   [ `Bad_request
  52:   | `Internal_server_error
  53:   | `Exn of exn
  54:   ]
  55: 
  56: type error_handler =
  57:   ?request:Request.t -> error -> (Headers.t -> Body.Writer.t) -> unit
  58: 
  59: type t =
  60:   { mutable settings : Settings.t
  61:   ; reader : Reader.frame
  62:   ; writer : Writer.t
  63:   ; config : Config.t
  64:   ; request_handler : request_handler
  65:   ; error_handler : error_handler
  66:   ; streams : Scheduler.t
  67:         (* Number of currently open client streams. Used for
  68:          * MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS bookkeeping *)
  69:   ; mutable current_client_streams : int
  70:   ; mutable max_client_stream_id : Stream_identifier.t
  71:   ; mutable max_pushed_stream_id : Stream_identifier.t
  72:   ; mutable receiving_headers_for_stream : Stream_identifier.t option
  73:         (* Keep track of number of SETTINGS frames that we sent and for which
  74:          * we haven't eceived an acknowledgment from the client. *)
  75:   ; mutable unacked_settings : int
  76:   ; mutable did_send_go_away : bool
  77:         (* From RFC7540§4.3:
  78:          *   Header compression is stateful. One compression context and one
  79:          *   decompression context are used for the entire connection. *)
  80:   ; hpack_encoder : Hpack.Encoder.t
  81:   ; hpack_decoder : Hpack.Decoder.t
  82:   }
  83: 
  84: let is_closed t = Reader.is_closed t.reader && Writer.is_closed t.writer
  85: let wakeup_writer t = Writer.wakeup t.writer
  86: let shutdown_reader t = Reader.force_close t.reader
  87: 
  88: let shutdown_writer t =
  89:   Writer.close t.writer;
  90:   wakeup_writer t
  91: 
  92: let shutdown t =
  93:   shutdown_reader t;
  94:   shutdown_writer t
  95: 
  96: (* Handling frames against closed streams is hard. See:
  97:  * https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1iG_U2bKTc9CnKr0jPTrNfmxyLufx_cK2nNh9VjrKH6s
  98:  *)
  99: let was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id =
 100:   if Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id
 101:   then Stream_identifier.(stream_id <= t.max_client_stream_id)
 102:   else Stream_identifier.(stream_id <= t.max_pushed_stream_id)
 103: 
 104: (* TODO: currently connection-level errors are not reported to the error
 105:  * handler because it is assumed that an error handler will produce a response,
 106:  * and since HTTP/2 is multiplexed, there's no matching response for a
 107:  * connection error. We should do something about it. *)
 108: let report_error t = function
 109:   | Error.ConnectionError (error, data) ->
 110:     if not t.did_send_go_away
 111:     then (
 112:       (* From RFC7540§5.4.1:
 113:        *   An endpoint that encounters a connection error SHOULD first send a
 114:        *   GOAWAY frame (Section 6.8) with the stream identifier of the last
 115:        *   stream that it successfully received from its peer. The GOAWAY frame
 116:        *   includes an error code that indicates why the connection is
 117:        *   terminating. After sending the GOAWAY frame for an error condition,
 118:        *   the endpoint MUST close the TCP connection. *)
 119:       let debug_data =
 120:         if String.length data = 0
 121:         then Bigstringaf.empty
 122:         else Bigstringaf.of_string ~off:0 ~len:(String.length data) data
 123:       in
 124:       let frame_info = Writer.make_frame_info Stream_identifier.connection in
 125:       (* TODO: Only write if not already shutdown. *)
 126:       Writer.write_go_away
 127:         t.writer
 128:         frame_info
 129:         ~debug_data
 130:         ~last_stream_id:t.max_client_stream_id
 131:         error;
 132:       Writer.flush t.writer (fun _reason ->
 133:         (* XXX: We need to allow lower numbered streams to complete before
 134:          * shutting down. *)
 135:         shutdown t);
 136:       t.did_send_go_away <- true;
 137:       wakeup_writer t)
 138:   | StreamError (stream_id, error) ->
 139:     (match Scheduler.find t.streams stream_id with
 140:     | Some reqd -> Stream.reset_stream reqd error
 141:     | None ->
 142:       if not (was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id)
 143:       then
 144:         (* Possible if the stream was going to enter the Idle state (first time
 145:          * we saw e.g. a PRIORITY frame for it) but had e.g. a
 146:          * FRAME_SIZE_ERROR. *)
 147:         let frame_info = Writer.make_frame_info stream_id in
 148:         Writer.write_rst_stream t.writer frame_info error);
 149:     wakeup_writer t
 150: 
 151: let report_connection_error t ?(reason = "") error =
 152:   report_error t (ConnectionError (error, reason))
 153: 
 154: let report_stream_error t stream_id error =
 155:   report_error t (StreamError (stream_id, error))
 156: 
 157: let set_error_and_handle ?request t stream error error_code =
 158:   assert (request = None);
 159:   Reqd.report_error stream error error_code;
 160:   wakeup_writer t
 161: 
 162: let report_exn t exn =
 163:   if not (is_closed t)
 164:   then
 165:     let reason = Printexc.to_string exn in
 166:     report_connection_error t ~reason Error_code.InternalError
 167: 
 168: let on_close_stream t id ~active closed =
 169:   if active
 170:   then
 171:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.2:
 172:      *   Streams that are in the "open" state or in either of the "half-closed"
 173:      *   states count toward the maximum number of streams that an endpoint is
 174:      *   permitted to open. *)
 175:     t.current_client_streams <- t.current_client_streams - 1;
 176:   Scheduler.mark_for_removal t.streams id closed
 177: 
 178: let send_window_update :
 179:     type a. t -> a Scheduler.PriorityTreeNode.node -> int32 -> unit
 180:   =
 181:  fun t stream n ->
 182:   let send_window_update_frame stream_id n =
 183:     let valid_inflow = Scheduler.add_inflow stream n in
 184:     assert valid_inflow;
 185:     let frame_info = Writer.make_frame_info stream_id in
 186:     Writer.write_window_update t.writer frame_info n
 187:   in
 188:   if Int32.compare n 0l > 0
 189:   then (
 190:     let max_window_size = Settings.WindowSize.max_window_size in
 191:     let stream_id = Scheduler.stream_id stream in
 192:     let rec loop n =
 193:       if n > max_window_size
 194:       then (
 195:         send_window_update_frame stream_id max_window_size;
 196:         loop (Int32.sub n max_window_size))
 197:       else send_window_update_frame stream_id n
 198:     in
 199:     loop n;
 200:     wakeup_writer t)
 201: 
 202: let create_push_stream t parent_stream_id =
 203:   let candidate_push_stream_id = Int32.add t.max_pushed_stream_id 2l in
 204:   if not t.settings.enable_push
 205:   then
 206:     (* From RFC7540§6.6:
 207:      *   PUSH_PROMISE MUST NOT be sent if the SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH setting of
 208:      *   the peer endpoint is set to 0. *)
 209:     Error `Push_disabled
 210:   else if Stream_identifier.(candidate_push_stream_id > max_stream_id)
 211:   then (
 212:     (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 213:      *   Stream identifiers cannot be reused. Long-lived connections can result
 214:      *   in an endpoint exhausting the available range of stream identifiers.
 215:      *   [...] A server that is unable to establish a new stream identifier can
 216:      *   send a GOAWAY frame so that the client is forced to open a new
 217:      *   connection for new streams. *)
 218:     report_connection_error t Error_code.NoError;
 219:     Error `Stream_ids_exhausted)
 220:   else
 221:     let pushed_stream_id = candidate_push_stream_id in
 222:     t.max_pushed_stream_id <- pushed_stream_id;
 223:     let reqd =
 224:       Stream.create
 225:         pushed_stream_id
 226:         ~max_frame_size:t.settings.max_frame_size
 227:         t.writer
 228:         t.error_handler
 229:         (on_close_stream t pushed_stream_id)
 230:     in
 231:     (* From RFC7540§5.3.5:
 232:      *   All streams are initially assigned a non-exclusive dependency on
 233:      *   stream 0x0. Pushed streams (Section 8.2) initially depend on their
 234:      *   associated stream. In both cases, streams are assigned a default
 235:      *   weight of 16. *)
 236:     let _stream : Scheduler.nonroot Scheduler.node =
 237:       Scheduler.add
 238:         t.streams
 239:         ~priority:
 240:           { Priority.default_priority with
 241:             stream_dependency = parent_stream_id
 242:           }
 243:         ~initial_send_window_size:t.settings.initial_window_size
 244:         ~initial_recv_window_size:t.config.initial_window_size
 245:         reqd
 246:     in
 247:     Ok reqd
 248: 
 249: let handle_headers t ~end_stream stream active_stream headers =
 250:   let (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ }) = stream in
 251:   (* From RFC7540§5.1.2:
 252:    *   Endpoints MUST NOT exceed the limit set by their peer. An endpoint that
 253:    *   receives a HEADERS frame that causes its advertised concurrent stream
 254:    *   limit to be exceeded MUST treat this as a stream error (Section 5.4.2)
 255:    *   of type PROTOCOL_ERROR or REFUSED_STREAM. *)
 256:   if Int32.(
 257:        compare
 258:          (of_int (t.current_client_streams + 1))
 259:          t.config.max_concurrent_streams)
 260:      > 0
 261:   then
 262:     if t.unacked_settings > 0
 263:     then
 264:       (* From RFC7540§8.1.4:
````
