````ocaml
 265:        *   The REFUSED_STREAM error code can be included in a RST_STREAM frame
 266:        *   to indicate that the stream is being closed prior to any processing
 267:        *   having occurred. Any request that was sent on the reset stream can
 268:        *   be safely retried.
 269:        *
 270:        * Note: if there are pending SETTINGS to acknowledge, assume there was a
 271:        * race condition and let the client retry. *)
 272:       report_stream_error t reqd.Stream.id Error_code.RefusedStream
 273:     else report_stream_error t reqd.Stream.id Error_code.ProtocolError
 274:   else (
 275:     reqd.state <- Active (Open FullHeaders, active_stream);
 276:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.2:
 277:      *   Streams that are in the "open" state or in either of the "half-closed"
 278:      *   states count toward the maximum number of streams that an endpoint is
 279:      *   permitted to open. *)
 280:     t.current_client_streams <- t.current_client_streams + 1;
 281:     match Headers.method_path_and_scheme_or_malformed headers with
 282:     | `Malformed ->
 283:       (* From RFC7540§8.1.2.6:
 284:        *   For malformed requests, a server MAY send an HTTP response prior to
 285:        *   closing or resetting the stream. *)
 286:       set_error_and_handle t reqd `Bad_request ProtocolError
 287:     | `Valid (meth, path, scheme) ->
 288:       (* Note: we don't need to check for `end_stream` flag + a non-zero body
 289:        * length, as the spec allows for non-zero content-length headers and no
 290:        * DATA frames.
 291:        *
 292:        * From RFC7540§8.1.2.6:
 293:        *   A response that is defined to have no payload, as described in
 294:        *   [RFC7230], Section 3.3.2, can have a non-zero content-length header
 295:        *   field, even though no content is included in DATA frames. *)
 296:       (match Message.body_length headers with
 297:       | `Error e -> set_error_and_handle t reqd e ProtocolError
 298:       | `Fixed _ | `Unknown ->
 299:         let request =
 300:           Request.create
 301:             ~scheme
 302:             ~headers
 303:             (Httpun_types.Method.of_string meth)
 304:             path
 305:         in
 306:         let request_body =
 307:           if end_stream
 308:           then Body.Reader.empty
 309:           else
 310:             Body.Reader.create
 311:               (Bigstringaf.create t.config.request_body_buffer_size)
 312:               ~done_reading:(fun len ->
 313:                 let len = Int32.of_int len in
 314:                 (* From RFC7540§6.9.1:
 315:                  *   The receiver of a frame sends a WINDOW_UPDATE frame as it
 316:                  *   consumes data and frees up space in flow-control windows.
 317:                  *   Separate WINDOW_UPDATE frames are sent for the stream- and
 318:                  *   connection-level flow-control windows. *)
 319:                 match reqd.state with
 320:                 | Active _ ->
 321:                   send_window_update t t.streams len;
 322:                   send_window_update t stream len
 323:                 | Idle | Reserved _ | Closed _ -> ())
 324:         in
 325:         let request_info = Reqd.create_active_request request request_body in
 326:         if end_stream
 327:         then (
 328:           (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 329:            *   [...] an endpoint receiving an END_STREAM flag causes the stream
 330:            *   state to become "half-closed (remote)". *)
 331:           reqd.state <- Active (HalfClosed request_info, active_stream);
 332:           (* Deliver EOF to the request body, as the handler might be waiting
 333:            * on it to produce a response. *)
 334:           Body.Reader.close request_body)
 335:         else
 336:           reqd.state <- Active (Open (ActiveMessage request_info), active_stream);
 337:         t.request_handler reqd;
 338:         wakeup_writer t))
 339: 
 340: let handle_headers_block
 341:     t
 342:     ?(is_trailers = false)
 343:     stream
 344:     active_stream
 345:     partial_headers
 346:     flags
 347:     headers_block
 348:   =
 349:   let open AB in
 350:   let (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ }) = stream in
 351:   let end_headers = Flags.test_end_header flags in
 352:   (* From RFC7540§6.10:
 353:    *   An endpoint receiving HEADERS, PUSH_PROMISE, or CONTINUATION
 354:    *   frames needs to reassemble header blocks and perform decompression
 355:    *   even if the frames are to be discarded *)
 356:   let parse_state' =
 357:     AB.feed partial_headers.Stream.parse_state (`Bigstring headers_block)
 358:   in
 359:   if end_headers
 360:   then (
 361:     t.receiving_headers_for_stream <- None;
 362:     let parse_state' = AB.feed parse_state' `Eof in
 363:     match parse_state' with
 364:     | Done (_, Ok headers) ->
 365:       if not is_trailers
 366:       then (
 367:         (* Note:
 368:          *   the highest stream identifier that the server has seen is set here
 369:          *   (as opposed to when the stream was first opened - when handling
 370:          *   the first HEADERS frame) because it refers to the highest stream
 371:          *   identifier that the server will process.
 372:          *
 373:          * From RFC7540§6.8:
 374:          *   The last stream identifier in the GOAWAY frame contains the
 375:          *   highest-numbered stream identifier for which the sender of the
 376:          *   GOAWAY frame might have taken some action on or might yet take
 377:          *   action on. All streams up to and including the identified stream
 378:          *   might have been processed in some way. *)
 379:         t.max_client_stream_id <- reqd.Stream.id;
 380:         (* `handle_headers` will take care of transitioning the stream state *)
 381:         handle_headers
 382:           t
 383:           ~end_stream:partial_headers.end_stream
 384:           stream
 385:           active_stream
 386:           headers)
 387:       else if Headers.trailers_valid headers
 388:       then (
 389:         Reqd.deliver_trailer_headers reqd headers;
 390:         let request_body = Reqd.request_body reqd in
 391:         Body.Reader.close request_body)
 392:       else
 393:         (* From RFC7540§8.1.2.1:
 394:          *   Pseudo-header fields MUST NOT appear in trailers. Endpoints MUST
 395:          *   treat a request or response that contains undefined or invalid
 396:          *   pseudo-header fields as malformed (Section 8.1.2.6). *)
 397:         set_error_and_handle t reqd `Bad_request ProtocolError
 398:     (* From RFC7540§4.3:
 399:      *   A decoding error in a header block MUST be treated as a connection
 400:      *   error (Section 5.4.1) of type COMPRESSION_ERROR. *)
 401:     | Done (_, Error _) | Partial _ ->
 402:       report_connection_error t Error_code.CompressionError
 403:     | Fail (_, _, message) ->
 404:       report_connection_error t ~reason:message Error_code.CompressionError)
 405:   else partial_headers.parse_state <- parse_state'
 406: 
 407: let handle_trailer_headers = handle_headers_block ~is_trailers:true
 408: 
 409: let open_stream t ~priority stream_id =
 410:   if not Stream_identifier.(stream_id > t.max_client_stream_id)
 411:   then (
 412:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 413:      *   [...] The identifier of a newly established stream MUST be numerically
 414:      *   greater than all streams that the initiating endpoint has opened or
 415:      *   reserved. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected stream
 416:      *   identifier MUST respond with a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
 417:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 418:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError;
 419:     None)
 420:   else
 421:     (* From RFC7540§6.2:
 422:      *   The HEADERS frame (type=0x1) is used to open a stream (Section 5.1),
 423:      *   and additionally carries a header block fragment. HEADERS frames can
 424:      *   be sent on a stream in the "idle", "reserved (local)", "open", or
 425:      *   "half-closed (remote)" state. *)
 426:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
 427:     | None ->
 428:       let reqd =
 429:         Stream.create
 430:           stream_id
 431:           ~max_frame_size:t.settings.max_frame_size
 432:           t.writer
 433:           t.error_handler
 434:           (on_close_stream t stream_id)
 435:       in
 436:       let stream : Scheduler.nonroot Scheduler.node =
 437:         Scheduler.add
 438:           t.streams
 439:           ~priority
 440:           ~initial_send_window_size:t.settings.initial_window_size
 441:           ~initial_recv_window_size:t.config.initial_window_size
 442:           reqd
 443:       in
 444:       Some stream
 445:     | Some (Scheduler.Stream node as stream) ->
 446:       (* From RFC7540§6.9.2:
 447:        *   Both endpoints can adjust the initial window size for new streams
 448:        *   by including a value for SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE in the
 449:        *   SETTINGS frame.
 450:        *
 451:        * Note: we already have the stream in the priority tree, and the
 452:        * default initial window size for new streams could have changed
 453:        * between adding the (idle) stream and opening it.
 454:        *
 455:        * Note: `inflow` doesn't change, that's set by us statically via config.
 456:        *)
 457:       node.flow <- t.settings.initial_window_size;
 458:       Some stream
 459: 
 460: let process_first_headers_block t frame_header stream headers_block =
 461:   let (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ }) = stream in
 462:   let { Frame.stream_id; flags; _ } = frame_header in
 463:   let end_headers = Flags.test_end_header flags in
 464:   let headers_block_length = Bigstringaf.length headers_block in
 465:   let initial_buffer_size =
 466:     if end_headers
 467:     then headers_block_length
 468:     else
 469:       (* Conservative estimate that there's only going to be one CONTINUATION
 470:        * frame. *)
 471:       2 * headers_block_length
 472:   in
 473:   let partial_headers =
 474:     { Stream.parse_state =
 475:         AB.parse
 476:           ~initial_buffer_size
 477:           (Hpack.Decoder.decode_headers t.hpack_decoder)
 478:     ; end_stream = Flags.test_end_stream flags
 479:     }
 480:   in
 481:   let active_stream =
 482:     Reqd.create_active_stream
 483:       t.hpack_encoder
 484:       t.config.response_body_buffer_size
 485:       (create_push_stream t)
 486:   in
 487:   reqd.Stream.state <-
 488:     Active (Open (PartialHeaders partial_headers), active_stream);
 489:   if not end_headers then t.receiving_headers_for_stream <- Some stream_id;
 490:   handle_headers_block
 491:     t
 492:     stream
 493:     active_stream
 494:     partial_headers
 495:     flags
 496:     headers_block
 497: 
 498: let process_trailer_headers t stream active_stream frame_header headers_block =
 499:   let (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ }) = stream in
 500:   let { Frame.stream_id; flags; _ } = frame_header in
 501:   let end_stream = Flags.test_end_stream flags in
 502:   if not end_stream
 503:   then
 504:     (* From RFC7540§8.1:
 505:      *   A HEADERS frame (and associated CONTINUATION frames) can only appear
 506:      *   at the start or end of a stream. An endpoint that receives a HEADERS
 507:      *   frame without the END_STREAM flag set after receiving a final
 508:      *   (non-informational) status code MUST treat the corresponding request
 509:      *   or response as malformed (Section 8.1.2.6). *)
 510:     set_error_and_handle t reqd `Bad_request ProtocolError
 511:   else
 512:     let partial_headers =
 513:       { Stream.parse_state =
 514:           AB.parse (Hpack.Decoder.decode_headers t.hpack_decoder)
 515:           (* obviously true at this point. *)
 516:       ; end_stream
 517:       }
 518:     in
 519:     active_stream.Reqd.trailers_parser <- Some partial_headers;
 520:     if not Flags.(test_end_header flags)
 521:     then t.receiving_headers_for_stream <- Some stream_id;
 522:     (* trailer headers: RFC7230§4.4 *)
 523:     handle_trailer_headers
 524:       t
 525:       stream
 526:       active_stream
 527:       partial_headers
 528:       flags
 529:       headers_block
 530: 
 531: let process_headers_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } ~priority headers_block =
 532:   let { Frame.stream_id; _ } = frame_header in
 533:   let { Priority.stream_dependency; _ } = priority in
 534:   if not Stream_identifier.(is_request stream_id)
 535:   then
 536:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 537:      *   Streams initiated by a client MUST use odd-numbered stream
 538:      *   identifiers. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected
 539:      *   stream identifier MUST respond with a connection error
 540:      *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 541:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 542:   else if Stream_identifier.(stream_dependency === stream_id)
 543:   then
 544:     (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
 545:      *   A stream cannot depend on itself. An endpoint MUST treat this as a
 546:      *   stream error (Section 5.4.2) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 547:     report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.ProtocolError
 548:   else
 549:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
 550:     | None ->
 551:       (match open_stream t ~priority stream_id with
 552:       | Some reqd ->
 553:         process_first_headers_block t frame_header reqd headers_block
 554:       | None -> ())
 555:     | Some (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ } as stream) ->
 556:       (match reqd.state with
 557:       | Idle ->
 558:         (* From RFC7540§6.2:
 559:          *   HEADERS frames can be sent on a stream in the "idle", "reserved
 560:          *   (local)", "open", or "half-closed (remote)" state. *)
 561:         (match open_stream t ~priority stream_id with
 562:         | Some reqd ->
 563:           process_first_headers_block t frame_header reqd headers_block
 564:         | None -> ())
 565:       | Active (Open (WaitingForPeer | PartialHeaders _), _) ->
 566:         (* This case is unreachable because we check that partial HEADERS
 567:          * states must be followed by CONTINUATION frames elsewhere. *)
 568:         assert false
 569:       (* if we're getting a HEADERS frame at this point, they must be
 570:        * trailers, and the END_STREAM flag needs to be set. *)
 571:       | Active (Open (FullHeaders | ActiveMessage _), active_stream) ->
 572:         process_trailer_headers
 573:           t
 574:           stream
 575:           active_stream
 576:           frame_header
 577:           headers_block;
 578:         Stream.finish_stream reqd Finished
 579:       | Active (HalfClosed _, _)
 580:       (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 581:        *   half-closed (remote): [...] If an endpoint receives additional
 582:        *   frames, other than WINDOW_UPDATE, PRIORITY, or RST_STREAM, for a
 583:        *   stream that is in this state, it MUST respond with a stream
 584:        *   error (Section 5.4.2) of type STREAM_CLOSED. *)
 585:       | Closed { reason = ResetByThem _; _ } ->
 586:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 587:          *   closed: [...] An endpoint that receives any frame other than
 588:          *   PRIORITY after receiving a RST_STREAM MUST treat that as a
 589:          *   stream error (Section 5.4.2) of type STREAM_CLOSED. *)
 590:         report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.StreamClosed
 591:       (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 592:        *   reserved (local): [...] Receiving any type of frame other than
 593:        *   RST_STREAM, PRIORITY, or WINDOW_UPDATE on a stream in this state
 594:        *   MUST be treated as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type
 595:        *   PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 596:       | Reserved _ | Closed _ ->
 597:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 598:          *   Similarly, an endpoint that receives any frames after receiving
 599:          *   a frame with the END_STREAM flag set MUST treat that as a
 600:          *   connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type STREAM_CLOSED [...]. *)
 601:         report_connection_error t Error_code.StreamClosed)
 602: 
 603: let process_data_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } bstr =
 604:   let open Scheduler in
 605:   let { Frame.flags; stream_id; payload_length; _ } = frame_header in
 606:   if not (Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id)
 607:   then
 608:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 609:      *   Streams initiated by a client MUST use odd-numbered stream
 610:      *   identifiers. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected stream
 611:      *   identifier MUST respond with a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
 612:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 613:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 614:   else
 615:     (* From RFC7540§6.9:
 616:      *   A receiver that receives a flow-controlled frame MUST always account
 617:      *   for its contribution against the connection flow-control window,
 618:      *   unless the receiver treats this as a connection error (Section 5.4.1).
 619:      *   This is necessary even if the frame is in error. *)
 620:     let payload_len32 = Int32.of_int payload_length in
 621:     Scheduler.deduct_inflow t.streams payload_len32;
 622:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
 623:     | Some (Stream { descriptor; _ } as stream) ->
 624:       (match descriptor.state with
 625:       | Active (Open (ActiveMessage request_info), active_stream) ->
 626:         let request_body = Reqd.request_body descriptor in
 627:         request_info.request_body_bytes <-
 628:           Int64.(
 629:             add
 630:               request_info.request_body_bytes
 631:               (of_int (Bigstringaf.length bstr)));
 632:         let request = request_info.request in
 633:         if not Scheduler.(allowed_to_receive t.streams stream payload_len32)
 634:         then (
 635:           (* From RFC7540§6.9:
 636:            *  A receiver MAY respond with a stream error (Section 5.4.2) or
 637:            *  connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR if it
 638:            *  is unable to accept a frame. *)
 639:           send_window_update t t.streams payload_len32;
 640:           report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.FlowControlError)
 641:         else (
 642:           Scheduler.deduct_inflow stream payload_len32;
 643:           match Message.body_length request.headers with
 644:           | `Fixed len
 645:           (* Getting more than the client declared *)
 646:             when Int64.compare request_info.request_body_bytes len > 0 ->
 647:             (* Give back connection-level flow-controlled bytes (we use payload
 648:              * length to include any padding bytes that the frame might have
 649:              * included - which were ignored at parse time). *)
 650:             send_window_update t t.streams payload_len32;
 651:             (* From RFC7540§8.1.2.6:
 652:              *   A request or response is also malformed if the value of a
 653:              *   content-length header field does not equal the sum of the
 654:              *   DATA frame payload lengths that form the body. *)
 655:             set_error_and_handle t descriptor `Bad_request ProtocolError
 656:           | _ ->
 657:             let end_stream = Flags.test_end_stream flags in
 658:             if end_stream
 659:             then
 660:               (* From RFC7540§6.1:
 661:                * When set, bit 0 indicates that this frame is the last that
 662:                * the endpoint will send for the identified stream. Setting
 663:                * this flag causes the stream to enter one of the
 664:                * "half-closed" states or the "closed" state
 665:                * (Section 5.1). *)
 666:               if Reqd.requires_output descriptor
 667:               then
 668:                 (* There's a potential race condition here if the request
 669:                  * handler completes the response right after. *)
 670:                 descriptor.state <-
 671:                   Active (HalfClosed request_info, active_stream)
 672:               else Stream.finish_stream descriptor Finished;
 673:             (* From RFC7540§6.9.1:
 674:              *   The receiver of a frame sends a WINDOW_UPDATE frame as it
 675:              *   consumes data and frees up space in flow-control windows.
 676:              *   Separate WINDOW_UPDATE frames are sent for the stream- and
 677:              *   connection-level flow-control windows.
 678:              *
 679:              * Note: we send these WINDOW_UPDATE frames once the body bytes
 680:              * have been surfaced to the application. This is done in the
 681:              * record field `done_reading` of `Body.t`. *)
 682:             let faraday = Body.Reader.unsafe_faraday request_body in
 683:             if not (Faraday.is_closed faraday)
 684:             then (
 685:               Faraday.schedule_bigstring faraday bstr;
 686:               if end_stream then Body.Reader.close request_body);
 687:             Reqd.flush_request_body descriptor)
 688:       | Idle ->
 689:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 690:          *   idle: [...] Receiving any frame other than HEADERS or PRIORITY on
 691:          *   a stream in this state MUST be treated as a connection error
 692:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 693:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 694:       (* This is technically in the half-closed (local) state *)
 695:       | Closed { reason = ResetByUs NoError; _ } ->
 696:         (* From RFC7540§6.9:
 697:          *   A receiver that receives a flow-controlled frame MUST always
 698:          *   account for its contribution against the connection flow-control
 699:          *   window, unless the receiver treats this as a connection error
 700:          *   (Section 5.4.1). This is necessary even if the frame is in
 701:          *   error. *)
 702:         send_window_update t t.streams payload_len32
 703:       (* From RFC7540§6.4:
 704:        *   [...] after sending the RST_STREAM, the sending endpoint MUST be
 705:        *   prepared to receive and process additional frames sent on the
 706:        *   stream that might have been sent by the peer prior to the arrival
 707:        *   of the RST_STREAM.
 708:        *
 709:        * Note: after some writer yields / wake ups, we will have stopped
 710:        * keeping state information for the stream. This functions effectively
 711:        * as a way of only accepting frames after an RST_STREAM from us up to
 712:        * a time limit. *)
 713:       | _ ->
 714:         send_window_update t t.streams payload_len32;
 715:         (* From RFC7540§6.1:
 716:          *   If a DATA frame is received whose stream is not in "open" or
 717:          *   "half-closed (local)" state, the recipient MUST respond with a
 718:          *   stream error (Section 5.4.2) of type STREAM_CLOSED. *)
 719:         report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.StreamClosed)
 720:     | None ->
 721:       if not (was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id)
 722:       then
 723:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
 724:          *   idle: [...] Receiving any frame other than HEADERS or PRIORITY on
 725:          *   a stream in this state MUST be treated as a connection error
 726:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 727:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 728: 
 729: let process_priority_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } priority =
 730:   let { Frame.stream_id; _ } = frame_header in
 731:   let { Priority.stream_dependency; _ } = priority in
 732:   if not (Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id)
 733:   then
 734:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 735:      *   Streams initiated by a client MUST use odd-numbered stream
 736:      *   identifiers. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected stream
 737:      *   identifier MUST respond with a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
 738:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 739:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 740:   else if Stream_identifier.(stream_id === stream_dependency)
 741:   then
 742:     (* From RFC7540§5.3.1:
 743:      *   A stream cannot depend on itself. An endpoint MUST treat this as a
 744:      *   stream error (Section 5.4.2) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 745:     report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.ProtocolError
 746:   else
 747:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
 748:     | Some stream -> Scheduler.reprioritize_stream t.streams ~priority stream
 749:     | None ->
 750:       (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 751:        *   Endpoints SHOULD process PRIORITY frames, though they can be ignored
 752:        *   if the stream has been removed from the dependency tree (see Section
 753:        *   5.3.4).
 754:        *
 755:        * Note:
 756:        *   if we're receiving a PRIORITY frame for a stream that we already
 757:        *   removed from the tree (i.e. can't be found in the hash table, and
 758:        *   for which the stream ID is smaller then or equal to the max stream
 759:        *   id that the client has opened), don't bother processing it. *)
 760:       if not (was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id)
 761:       then
 762:         let reqd =
 763:           Stream.create
 764:             stream_id
 765:             ~max_frame_size:t.settings.max_frame_size
 766:             t.writer
 767:             t.error_handler
 768:             (on_close_stream t stream_id)
 769:         in
 770:         let _stream : Scheduler.nonroot Scheduler.node =
 771:           Scheduler.add
 772:             t.streams
 773:             ~priority
 774:             ~initial_send_window_size:t.settings.initial_window_size
 775:             ~initial_recv_window_size:t.config.initial_window_size
 776:             reqd
 777:         in
 778:         ()
 779: 
 780: let process_rst_stream_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } error_code =
 781:   let { Frame.stream_id; _ } = frame_header in
 782:   if not (Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id)
 783:   then
 784:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
 785:      *   Streams initiated by a client MUST use odd-numbered stream
 786:      *   identifiers. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected stream
 787:      *   identifier MUST respond with a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
 788:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 789:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 790:   else
 791:     match Scheduler.find t.streams stream_id with
 792:     | Some reqd ->
 793:       (match reqd.state with
 794:       | Idle ->
 795:         (* From RFC7540§6.4:
 796:          *   RST_STREAM frames MUST NOT be sent for a stream in the "idle"
 797:          *   state. If a RST_STREAM frame identifying an idle stream is
 798:          *   received, the recipient MUST treat this as a connection error
 799:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 800:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 801:       | _ ->
 802:         (* From RFC7540§6.4:
 803:          *   The RST_STREAM frame fully terminates the referenced stream and
 804:          *   causes it to enter the "closed" state. After receiving a
 805:          *   RST_STREAM on a stream, the receiver MUST NOT send additional
 806:          *   frames for that stream, with the exception of PRIORITY.
 807:          *
 808:          * Note:
 809:          *   This match branch also accepts streams in the `Closed` state. We
 810:          *   do that to comply with the following:
 811:          *
 812:          * From RFC7540§6.4:
 813:          *   [...] after sending the RST_STREAM, the sending endpoint MUST be
 814:          *   prepared to receive and process additional frames sent on the
 815:          *   stream that might have been sent by the peer prior to the arrival
 816:          *   of the RST_STREAM. *)
 817:         Stream.finish_stream reqd (ResetByThem error_code))
 818:     | None ->
 819:       (* We might have removed the stream from the hash table. If its stream
 820:        * id is smaller than or equal to the max client stream id we've seen,
 821:        * then it must have been closed. *)
 822:       if not (was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id)
 823:       then
 824:         (* From RFC7540§6.4:
 825:          *   RST_STREAM frames MUST NOT be sent for a stream in the "idle"
 826:          *   state. If a RST_STREAM frame identifying an idle stream is
 827:          *   received, the recipient MUST treat this as a connection error
 828:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR.
 829:          *
 830:          * Note:
 831:          *   If we didn't find the stream in the hash table it must be
 832:          *   "idle". *)
 833:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
 834: 
 835: let apply_settings_list t settings =
 836:   let open Scheduler in
 837:   (* From RFC7540§6.5:
 838:    *   Each parameter in a SETTINGS frame replaces any existing value for
 839:    *   that parameter. Parameters are processed in the order in which they
 840:    *   appear, and a receiver of a SETTINGS frame does not need to maintain
 841:    *   any state other than the current value of its parameters. *)
 842:   let new_settings =
 843:     List.fold_left
 844:       (fun (acc : Settings.t) item ->
 845:          match item with
 846:          | Settings.HeaderTableSize x ->
 847:            (* From RFC7540§6.5.2:
 848:             *   Allows the sender to inform the remote endpoint of the maximum
 849:             *   size of the header compression table used to decode header
 850:             *   blocks, in octets. *)
 851:            Hpack.Encoder.set_capacity t.hpack_encoder x;
 852:            { acc with header_table_size = x }
 853:          | EnablePush x ->
 854:            (* We've already verified that this setting is either 0 or 1 in the
 855:             * call to `Settings.check_settings_list` above. *)
 856:            { acc with enable_push = x = 1 }
 857:          | MaxConcurrentStreams x -> { acc with max_concurrent_streams = x }
 858:          | InitialWindowSize new_val ->
 859:            (* From RFC7540§6.9.2:
 860:             *   [...] a SETTINGS frame can alter the initial flow-control
 861:             *   window size for streams with active flow-control windows (that
 862:             *   is, streams in the "open" or "half-closed (remote)" state).
 863:             *   When the value of SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE changes, a
 864:             *   receiver MUST adjust the size of all stream flow-control
 865:             *   windows that it maintains by the difference between the new
 866:             *   value and the old value. *)
 867:            let old_val = acc.initial_window_size in
 868:            let growth = Int32.sub new_val old_val in
 869:            let exception Local in
 870:            (match
 871:               Scheduler.iter
 872:                 ~f:(fun stream ->
 873:                   (* From RFC7540§6.9.2:
 874:                    *   An endpoint MUST treat a change to
 875:                    *   SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE that causes any
 876:                    *   flow-control window to exceed the maximum size as a
 877:                    *   connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type
 878:                    *   FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR. *)
 879:                   if not (Scheduler.add_flow stream growth) then raise Local)
 880:                 t.streams
 881:             with
 882:            | () -> ()
 883:            | exception Local ->
 884:              report_connection_error
 885:                t
 886:                ~reason:
 887:                  (Format.sprintf
 888:                     "Window size for stream would exceed %ld"
 889:                     Settings.WindowSize.max_window_size)
 890:                Error_code.FlowControlError);
 891:            { acc with initial_window_size = new_val }
 892:          | MaxFrameSize x ->
 893:            (* XXX: We're probably not abiding entirely by this. If we get a
 894:             * MAX_FRAME_SIZE setting we'd need to reallocate the read buffer?
 895:             * This will need support from the I/O runtimes. *)
 896:            Scheduler.iter
 897:              ~f:(fun (Stream { descriptor; _ }) ->
 898:                if Reqd.requires_output descriptor
 899:                then descriptor.max_frame_size <- x)
 900:              t.streams;
 901:            { acc with max_frame_size = x }
 902:          | MaxHeaderListSize x -> { acc with max_header_list_size = Some x })
 903:       t.settings
 904:       settings
 905:   in
 906:   t.settings <- new_settings
 907: 
 908: let write_settings_frame t ~ack settings =
 909:   let flags =
 910:     if ack then Flags.(set_ack default_flags) else Flags.default_flags
 911:   in
 912:   let frame_info = Writer.make_frame_info ~flags Stream_identifier.connection in
 913:   Writer.write_settings t.writer frame_info settings;
 914:   if not ack
 915:   then
 916:     (* Don't expected our acknowledgements to be acknowledged... *)
 917:     t.unacked_settings <- t.unacked_settings + 1
 918: 
 919: let process_settings_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } settings =
 920:   let { Frame.flags; _ } = frame_header in
 921:   (* We already checked that an acked SETTINGS is empty. Don't need to do
 922:    * anything else in that case *)
 923:   if Flags.(test_ack flags)
 924:   then (
 925:     t.unacked_settings <- t.unacked_settings - 1;
 926:     if t.unacked_settings < 0
 927:     then
 928:       report_connection_error
 929:         t
 930:         ~reason:"Received unexpected SETTINGS frame with acknowledgement"
 931:         Error_code.ProtocolError)
 932:   else
 933:     match Settings.check_settings_list settings with
 934:     | Ok () ->
 935:       apply_settings_list t settings;
 936:       (* From RFC7540§6.5.2:
 937:        *   Once all values have been processed, the recipient MUST immediately
 938:        *   emit a SETTINGS frame with the ACK flag set.
 939:        *
 940:        * From RFC7540§6.5:
 941:        *   ACK (0x1): [...] When this bit is set, the payload of the SETTINGS
 942:        *   frame MUST be empty. *)
 943:       write_settings_frame t ~ack:true [];
 944:       wakeup_writer t
 945:     | Error error -> report_error t error
 946: 
 947: let process_ping_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } payload =
 948:   let { Frame.flags; _ } = frame_header in
 949:   (* From RFC7540§6.7:
 950:    *   ACK (0x1): When set, bit 0 indicates that this PING frame is a PING
 951:    *   response. [...] An endpoint MUST NOT respond to PING frames containing
 952:    *   this flag. *)
 953:   if not (Flags.test_ack flags)
 954:   then (
 955:     (* From RFC7540§6.7:
 956:      *   Receivers of a PING frame that does not include an ACK flag MUST send
 957:      *   a PING frame with the ACK flag set in response, with an identical
 958:      *   payload. PING responses SHOULD be given higher priority than any other
 959:      *   frame. *)
 960:     let frame_info =
 961:       Writer.make_frame_info
 962:       (* From RFC7540§6.7:
 963:        *   ACK (0x1): When set, bit 0 indicates that this PING frame is a
 964:        *   PING response. An endpoint MUST set this flag in PING
 965:        *   responses. *)
 966:         ~flags:Flags.(set_ack default_flags)
 967:         Stream_identifier.connection
 968:     in
 969:     (* From RFC7540§6.7:
 970:      *   Receivers of a PING frame that does not include an ACK flag MUST send
 971:      *   a PING frame with the ACK flag set in response, with an identical
 972:      *   payload. *)
 973:     Writer.write_ping t.writer frame_info payload;
 974:     wakeup_writer t)
 975: 
 976: let process_goaway_frame t _frame ~last_stream_id:_ ~data error =
 977:   let reason =
 978:     match Bigstringaf.length data with
 979:     | 0 -> None
 980:     | len -> Some (Bigstringaf.substring data ~off:0 ~len)
 981:   in
 982:   (* TODO(anmonteiro): I think we need to allow lower numbered streams to
 983:    * complete. *)
 984:   report_connection_error t ?reason error
 985: 
 986: let add_window_increment :
 987:     type a. t -> a Scheduler.PriorityTreeNode.node -> int32 -> unit
 988:   =
 989:  fun t stream increment ->
 990:   let open Scheduler in
 991:   let did_add = Scheduler.add_flow stream increment in
 992:   let stream_id = Scheduler.stream_id stream in
 993:   let new_flow =
 994:     match stream with
 995:     | Connection { flow; _ } -> flow
 996:     | Stream { flow; _ } -> flow
 997:   in
 998:   if did_add
 999:   then (
1000:     if Int32.compare new_flow 0l > 0
1001:     then
1002:       (* Don't bother waking up the writer if the new flow doesn't allow
1003:        * the stream to write. *)
1004:       wakeup_writer t)
1005:   else if Stream_identifier.is_connection stream_id
1006:   then
1007:     report_connection_error
1008:       t
1009:       ~reason:
1010:         (Printf.sprintf
1011:            "Window size for stream would exceed %ld"
1012:            Settings.WindowSize.max_window_size)
1013:       Error_code.FlowControlError
1014:   else report_stream_error t stream_id Error_code.FlowControlError
1015: 
1016: let process_window_update_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } window_increment =
1017:   let open Scheduler in
1018:   let { Frame.stream_id; _ } = frame_header in
1019:   (* From RFC7540§6.9:
1020:    *   The WINDOW_UPDATE frame can be specific to a stream or to the entire
1021:    *   connection. In the former case, the frame's stream identifier indicates
1022:    *   the affected stream; in the latter, the value "0" indicates that the
1023:    *   entire connection is the subject of the frame. *)
1024:   if Stream_identifier.is_connection stream_id
1025:   then add_window_increment t t.streams window_increment
1026:   else
1027:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
1028:     | Some (Stream { descriptor; _ } as stream_node) ->
1029:       (match descriptor.state with
1030:       | Idle ->
1031:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
1032:          *   idle: [...] Receiving any frame other than HEADERS or PRIORITY on
1033:          *   a stream in this state MUST be treated as a connection error
1034:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1035:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
1036:       | Active _
1037:       (* From RFC7540§5.1:
1038:        *   reserved (local): [...] A PRIORITY or WINDOW_UPDATE frame MAY be
1039:        *   received in this state. *)
1040:       | Reserved _ ->
1041:         add_window_increment t stream_node window_increment
1042:       | Closed _ ->
1043:         (* From RFC7540§6.9:
1044:          *   [...] a receiver could receive a WINDOW_UPDATE frame on a
1045:          *   "half-closed (remote)" or "closed" stream. A receiver MUST NOT
1046:          *   treat this as an error (see Section 5.1). *)
1047:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
1048:          *   Endpoints MUST ignore WINDOW_UPDATE or RST_STREAM frames received
1049:          *   in this state, though endpoints MAY choose to treat frames that
1050:          *   arrive a significant time after sending END_STREAM as a connection
1051:          *   error (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1052:         ())
1053:     | None ->
1054:       if not (was_closed_or_implicitly_closed t stream_id)
1055:       then
1056:         (* From RFC7540§5.1:
1057:          *   idle: [...] Receiving any frame other than HEADERS or PRIORITY on
1058:          *   a stream in this state MUST be treated as a connection error
1059:          *   (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1060:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
1061: 
1062: let process_continuation_frame t { Frame.frame_header; _ } headers_block =
1063:   let { Frame.stream_id; flags; _ } = frame_header in
1064:   if not (Stream_identifier.is_request stream_id)
1065:   then
1066:     (* From RFC7540§5.1.1:
1067:      *   Streams initiated by a client MUST use odd-numbered stream
1068:      *   identifiers. [...] An endpoint that receives an unexpected stream
1069:      *   identifier MUST respond with a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
1070:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1071:     report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
1072:   else
1073:     match Scheduler.get_node t.streams stream_id with
1074:     | Some (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor; _ } as stream) ->
1075:       (match descriptor.state with
1076:       | Active (Open (PartialHeaders partial_headers), active_stream) ->
1077:         handle_headers_block
1078:           t
1079:           stream
1080:           active_stream
1081:           partial_headers
1082:           flags
1083:           headers_block
1084:       | Active
1085:           ( Open (ActiveMessage _)
1086:           , ({ trailers_parser = Some partial_headers; _ } as active_stream) )
1087:         ->
1088:         handle_trailer_headers
1089:           t
1090:           stream
1091:           active_stream
1092:           partial_headers
1093:           flags
1094:           headers_block
1095:       | _ ->
1096:         (* TODO: maybe need to handle the case where the stream has been closed
1097:          * due to a stream error. *)
1098:         (* From RFC7540§6.10:
1099:          *   A RST_STREAM is the last frame that an endpoint can send on a
1100:          *   stream. The peer that sends the RST_STREAM frame MUST be prepared
1101:          *   to receive any frames that were sent or enqueued for sending by
1102:          *   the remote peer. These frames can be ignored, except where they
1103:          *   modify connection state (such as the state maintained for header
1104:          *   compression (Section 4.3) or flow control). *)
1105:         report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError)
1106:     | None ->
1107:       (* From RFC7540§6.10:
1108:        *   A CONTINUATION frame MUST be preceded by a HEADERS, PUSH_PROMISE or
1109:        *   CONTINUATION frame without the END_HEADERS flag set. A recipient
1110:        *   that observes violation of this rule MUST respond with a connection
1111:        *   error (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1112:       report_connection_error t Error_code.ProtocolError
1113: 
1114: let default_error_handler ?request:_ error handle =
1115:   let message =
1116:     match error with
1117:     | `Exn exn -> Printexc.to_string exn
1118:     | (#Status.client_error | #Status.server_error) as error ->
1119:       Status.to_string error
1120:   in
1121:   let body = handle Headers.empty in
1122:   Body.Writer.write_string body message;
1123:   Body.Writer.close body
1124: 
1125: let write_connection_preface t =
1126:   (* Check if the settings for the connection are different than the default
1127:    * HTTP/2 settings. In the event that they are, we need to send a non-empty
1128:    * SETTINGS frame advertising our configuration. *)
1129:   let settings = Settings.settings_for_the_connection t.settings in
1130:   (* XXX(anmonteiro): same as in the client. Revert
1131:    * [t.settings.initial_window_size] to the spec-default value until we
1132:    * receive a setting for it. *)
1133:   t.settings <-
1134:     { t.settings with
1135:       initial_window_size = Settings.default.initial_window_size
1136:     };
1137:   (* Now send the connection preface, including our settings for the
1138:    * connection.
1139:    *
1140:    * From RFC7540§3.5:
1141:    *   The server connection preface consists of a potentially empty
1142:    *   SETTINGS frame (Section 6.5) that MUST be the first frame the
1143:    *   server sends in the HTTP/2 connection. *)
1144:   write_settings_frame ~ack:false t settings;
1145:   (* If a higher value for initial window size is configured, add more
1146:    * tokens to the connection (we have no streams at this point). *)
1147:   if t.config.initial_window_size > Settings.default.initial_window_size
1148:   then
1149:     let diff =
1150:       Int32.sub
1151:         t.config.initial_window_size
1152:         Settings.default.initial_window_size
1153:     in
1154:     send_window_update t t.streams diff
1155: 
1156: let create_generic ~h2c ~config ~error_handler request_handler =
1157:   let settings = Config.to_settings config in
1158:   let writer = Writer.create settings.max_frame_size in
1159:   let rec connection_preface_handler recv_frame settings_list =
1160:     let t = Lazy.force t in
1161:     (* If this connection is `h2c` (HTTP/2 over TCP), we have already written
1162:      * the server connection preface. This is only necessary if we're
1163:      * responding to a client-initiated connection, but in `h2c` the server
1164:      * writes the preface first (handled below in `create_h2c`) *)
1165:     if not h2c then write_connection_preface t;
1166:     (* Now process the client's SETTINGS frame. `process_settings_frame` will
1167:      * take care of calling `wakeup_writer`. *)
1168:     process_settings_frame t recv_frame settings_list
1169:   and frame_handler r =
1170:     let t = Lazy.force t in
1171:     match r with
1172:     | Error e -> report_error t e
1173:     | Ok ({ Frame.frame_payload; frame_header } as frame) ->
1174:       (match t.receiving_headers_for_stream with
1175:       | Some stream_id
1176:         when (not Stream_identifier.(stream_id === frame_header.stream_id))
1177:              || frame_header.frame_type <> Continuation ->
1178:         (* From RFC7540§6.2:
1179:          *   A HEADERS frame without the END_HEADERS flag set MUST be followed
1180:          *   by a CONTINUATION frame for the same stream. A receiver MUST treat
1181:          *   the receipt of any other type of frame or a frame on a different
1182:          *   stream as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type
1183:          *   PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1184:         report_connection_error
1185:           t
1186:           ~reason:
1187:             "HEADERS or PUSH_PROMISE without the END_HEADERS flag set must be \
1188:              followed by a CONTINUATION frame for the same stream"
1189:           Error_code.ProtocolError
1190:       | _ ->
1191:         (match frame_payload with
1192:         | Headers (priority, headers_block) ->
1193:           process_headers_frame t frame ~priority headers_block
1194:         | Data bs -> process_data_frame t frame bs
1195:         | Priority priority -> process_priority_frame t frame priority
1196:         | RSTStream error_code -> process_rst_stream_frame t frame error_code
1197:         | Settings settings -> process_settings_frame t frame settings
1198:         | PushPromise _ ->
1199:           (* From RFC7540§8.2:
1200:            *   A client cannot push. Thus, servers MUST treat the receipt of a
1201:            *   PUSH_PROMISE frame as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type
1202:            *   PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
1203:           report_connection_error
1204:             t
1205:             ~reason:"Client cannot push"
1206:             Error_code.ProtocolError
1207:         | Ping data -> process_ping_frame t frame data
1208:         | GoAway (last_stream_id, error, data) ->
1209:           process_goaway_frame t frame ~last_stream_id ~data error
1210:         | WindowUpdate window_size ->
1211:           process_window_update_frame t frame window_size
1212:         | Continuation headers_block ->
1213:           process_continuation_frame t frame headers_block
1214:         | Unknown _ ->
1215:           (* TODO: in the future we can expose a hook for handling unknown
1216:            * frames, e.g. the ALTSVC frame defined in RFC7838§4
1217:            * (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7838#section-4) *)
1218:           (* From RFC7540§5.1:
1219:            *   Frames of unknown types are ignored. *)
1220:           ()))
1221:   and t =
1222:     lazy
1223:       { settings
1224:       ; reader =
1225:           Reader.server_frames
1226:             ~max_frame_size:settings.max_frame_size
1227:             connection_preface_handler
1228:             frame_handler
1229:       ; writer
1230:       ; config
1231:       ; request_handler
1232:       ; error_handler
1233:       ; streams = Scheduler.make_root ()
1234:       ; current_client_streams = 0
1235:       ; max_client_stream_id = 0l
1236:       ; max_pushed_stream_id = 0l
1237:       ; receiving_headers_for_stream = None
1238:       ; did_send_go_away = false
1239:       ; unacked_settings = 0
1240:       ; hpack_encoder = Hpack.Encoder.(create settings.header_table_size)
1241:       ; hpack_decoder = Hpack.Decoder.(create settings.header_table_size)
1242:       }
1243:   in
1244:   Lazy.force t
1245: 
1246: let create
1247:     ?(config = Config.default)
1248:     ?(error_handler = default_error_handler)
1249:     request_handler
1250:   =
1251:   (* `h2c` false = direct *)
1252:   create_generic ~h2c:false ~config ~error_handler request_handler
1253: 
1254: let handle_h2c_request t headers request_body_iovecs =
1255:   (* From RFC7540§3.2:
1256:    *   The HTTP/1.1 request that is sent prior to upgrade is assigned a stream
1257:    *   identifier of 1 (see Section 5.1.1) with default priority values
1258:    *   (Section 5.3.5). *)
1259:   match open_stream ~priority:Priority.default_priority t 1l with
1260:   | Some (Scheduler.Stream { descriptor = reqd; _ } as stream) ->
1261:     let active_stream =
1262:       Reqd.create_active_stream
1263:         t.hpack_encoder
1264:         t.config.response_body_buffer_size
1265:         (create_push_stream t)
1266:     in
1267:     t.max_client_stream_id <- reqd.Stream.id;
1268:     let lengthv = Httpun_types.IOVec.lengthv request_body_iovecs in
1269:     let end_stream = lengthv = 0 in
1270:     handle_headers t ~end_stream stream active_stream headers;
1271:     let request = Reqd.request reqd in
1272:     let request_body = Reqd.request_body reqd in
1273:     let request_info = Reqd.create_active_request request request_body in
1274:     request_info.request_body_bytes <-
1275:       Int64.(add request_info.request_body_bytes (of_int lengthv));
1276:     (* From RFC7540§3.2:
1277:      *   Stream 1 is implicitly "half-closed" from the client toward the server
1278:      *   (see Section 5.1), since the request is completed as an HTTP/1.1
1279:      *   request. After commencing the HTTP/2 connection, stream 1 is used for
1280:      *   the response. *)
1281:     reqd.state <- Active (HalfClosed request_info, active_stream);
1282:     if not end_stream
1283:     then
1284:       let faraday = Body.Reader.unsafe_faraday request_body in
1285:       if not (Faraday.is_closed faraday)
1286:       then (
1287:         List.iter
1288:           (fun { Httpun_types.IOVec.buffer; off; len } ->
1289:              Faraday.schedule_bigstring faraday ~off ~len buffer)
1290:           request_body_iovecs;
1291:         (* Close the request body, we're not expecting more input. *)
1292:         Body.Reader.close request_body)
1293:   | None -> ()
1294: 
1295: (* This function is meant to be called inside an HTTP/1.1 upgrade handler.
1296:  *
1297:  * It's useful to have the request body on the server because a single request
1298:  * handler may process both upgraded (h2c) and direct connections. Given the
1299:  * following, application code that calls this function needs to buffer the
1300:  * entire request body in memory if it wants, so we can just get a
1301:  * `Bigstringaf.t IOVec.t list`
1302:  *
1303:  *
1304:  * From RFC7540§3.2:
1305:  *   Requests that contain a payload body MUST be sent in their
1306:  *   entirety before the client can send HTTP/2 frames. This means
1307:  *   that a large request can block the use of the connection until
1308:  *   it is completely sent. *)
1309: let create_h2c
1310:     ?(config = Config.default)
1311:     ?(error_handler = default_error_handler)
1312:     ~headers
1313:     ~target
1314:     ~meth
1315:     ?(request_body = [])
1316:     request_handler
1317:   =
1318:   (* From RFC7540§3.2.1:
1319:    *   A request that upgrades from HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 MUST include exactly one
1320:    *   HTTP2-Settings header field.
1321:    *
1322:    *   [...] A server MUST NOT upgrade the connection to HTTP/2 if this header
1323:    *   field is not present or if more than one is present. A server MUST NOT
1324:    *   send this header field. *)
1325:   match
1326:     Httpun_types.Headers.(
1327:       get_multi headers "http2-settings", get_multi headers "connection")
1328:   with
1329:   | [ settings ], [ connection ] when Headers.is_valid_h2c_connection connection
1330:     ->
1331:     (* From RFC7540§3.2.1:
1332:      *   The content of the HTTP2-Settings header field is the payload of a
1333:      *   SETTINGS frame (Section 6.5), encoded as a base64url string (that is,
1334:      *   the URL- and filename-safe Base64 encoding described in Section 5 of
1335:      *   [RFC4648], with any trailing '=' characters omitted).
1336:      *
1337:      *   [...] A server decodes and interprets these values as it would any
1338:      *   other SETTINGS frame. Explicit acknowledgement of these settings
1339:      *   (Section 6.5.3) is not necessary, since a 101 response serves as
1340:      *   implicit acknowledgement. *)
1341:     (match Headers.of_http1 ~headers ~target ~meth with
1342:     | Ok h2_headers ->
1343:       (match Settings.of_base64 settings with
1344:       | Ok upgrade_settings ->
1345:         let settings_list =
1346:           Settings.settings_for_the_connection upgrade_settings
1347:         in
1348:         (match Settings.check_settings_list settings_list with
1349:         | Ok () ->
1350:           let t =
1351:             create_generic ~h2c:true ~config ~error_handler request_handler
1352:           in
1353:           apply_settings_list t settings_list;
1354:           (* From RFC7540§3.5:
1355:            *   The first HTTP/2 frame sent by the server MUST be a server
1356:            *   connection preface (Section 3.5) consisting of a SETTINGS
1357:            *   frame (Section 6.5).
1358:            *
1359:            *   Note: as opposed to a connection started by a client, in h2c
1360:            *   we're upgrading from HTTP/1.1 so the server is reponsible for
1361:            *   writing the HTTP/2 connection preface to the wire first. We
1362:            *   also configure the connection with `~h2c:true` above to not
1363:            *   send it a second time. *)
1364:           write_connection_preface t;
1365:           (* From RFC7540§3.2:
1366:            *   A server that supports HTTP/2 accepts the upgrade with a 101
1367:            *   (Switching Protocols) response. After the empty line that
1368:            *   terminates the 101 response, the server can begin sending
1369:            *   HTTP/2 frames. These frames MUST include a response to the
1370:            *   request that initiated the upgrade *)
1371:           handle_h2c_request t h2_headers request_body;
1372:           Ok t
1373:         | Error error -> Error (Error.message error))
1374:       | Error msg -> Error msg)
1375:     | Error msg -> Error msg)
1376:   | _ ->
1377:     Error
1378:       "A request that upgrades from HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 MUST include exactly \
1379:        one HTTP2-Settings header field and HTTP2-Settings as a connection \
1380:        option in the Connection header field."
1381: 
1382: let next_read_operation t =
1383:   if Reader.is_closed t.reader then shutdown_reader t;
1384:   match Reader.next t.reader with
1385:   | (`Read | `Close) as operation -> operation
1386:   | `Error e ->
1387:     report_error t e;
1388:     (match e with
1389:     | ConnectionError _ ->
1390:       (* From RFC7540§5.4.1:
1391:        *   A connection error is any error that prevents further processing
1392:        *   of the frame layer or corrupts any connection state. *)
1393:       `Close
1394:     | StreamError _ ->
1395:       (* From RFC7540§5.4.2:
1396:        *   A stream error is an error related to a specific stream that does
1397:        *   not affect processing of other streams. *)
1398:       `Read)
1399: 
1400: let read t bs ~off ~len = Reader.read_with_more t.reader bs ~off ~len Incomplete
1401: 
1402: let read_eof t bs ~off ~len =
1403:   Reader.read_with_more t.reader bs ~off ~len Complete
1404: 
1405: (* XXX(anmonteiro): this function is here to please the Gluten `RUNTIME`
1406:  * interface.
1407:  *
1408:  * We don't expect this function to ever be called. H2 never issues `Yield`
1409:  * commands because the connection is multiplexed, and it's therefore always
1410:  * looking to read frames from the peer. *)
1411: let yield_reader _t k = k ()
1412: 
1413: let next_write_operation t =
1414:   Scheduler.flush t.streams (t.max_client_stream_id, t.max_pushed_stream_id);
1415:   Writer.next t.writer
1416: 
1417: let report_write_result t result = Writer.report_result t.writer result
1418: let yield_writer t k = Writer.on_wakeup_writer t.writer k
````

## File: ../../../../.cache/opam/5.2.0+ox/lib/h2/settings.ml
````ocaml
  1: (*----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  2:  *  Copyright (c) 2019-2020 António Nuno Monteiro
  3:  *
  4:  *  All rights reserved.
  5:  *
  6:  *  Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
  7:  *  modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met:
  8:  *
  9:  *  1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice,
 10:  *  this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
 11:  *
 12:  *  2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright
 13:  *  notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
 14:  *  documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.
 15:  *
 16:  *  3. Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its
 17:  *  contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this
 18:  *  software without specific prior written permission.
 19:  *
 20:  *  THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS "AS IS"
 21:  *  AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE
 22:  *  IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE
 23:  *  ARE DISCLAIMED. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT HOLDER OR CONTRIBUTORS BE
 24:  *  LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR
 25:  *  CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF
 26:  *  SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS
 27:  *  INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN
 28:  *  CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE)
 29:  *  ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE
 30:  *  POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
 31:  *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*)
 32: 
 33: module WindowSize = struct
 34:   type t = int32
 35: 
 36:   (* From RFC7540§6.9.2:
 37:    *   When an HTTP/2 connection is first established, new streams are created
 38:    *   with an initial flow-control window size of 65,535 octets. *)
 39:   let default_initial_window_size = 65535l
 40: 
 41:   (* From RFC7540§6.9:
 42:    *   The legal range for the increment to the flow-control window is 1 to
 43:    *   2^31-1 (2,147,483,647) octets. *)
 44:   let max_window_size = Int32.max_int
 45: 
 46:   (* Ideally `n` here would be an unsigned 32-bit integer, but OCaml doesn't
 47:    * support them. We avoid introducing a new dependency on an unsigned integer
 48:    * library by letting it overflow at parse time and checking if bit 31 is set
 49:    * here, since * `Window.max_window_size` is never allowed to be above
 50:    * 2^31-1 (see `max_window_size` above).
 51:    * See http://caml.inria.fr/pub/ml-archives/caml-list/2004/07/f1c483068cc62075c916f7ad7d640ce0.fr.html
 52:    * for more info. *)
 53:   let is_window_overflow n = Util.test_bit_int32 n 31
 54: end
 55: 
 56: type setting =
 57:   | HeaderTableSize of int
 58:   | EnablePush of int
 59:   | MaxConcurrentStreams of int32
 60:   | InitialWindowSize of int32
 61:   | MaxFrameSize (* this means payload size *) of int
 62:   | MaxHeaderListSize of int
 63: 
 64: type settings_list = setting list
 65: 
 66: (* From RFC7540§6.5.1:
 67:  *   The payload of a SETTINGS frame consists of zero or more parameters,
 68:  *   each consisting of an unsigned 16-bit setting identifier and an
 69:  *   unsigned 32-bit value. *)
 70: let octets_per_setting = 6
 71: 
 72: let serialize_key = function
 73:   | HeaderTableSize _ -> 0x1
 74:   | EnablePush _ -> 0x2
 75:   | MaxConcurrentStreams _ -> 0x3
 76:   | InitialWindowSize _ -> 0x4
 77:   | MaxFrameSize _ -> 0x5
 78:   | MaxHeaderListSize _ -> 0x6
 79: 
 80: let check_value ~is_client = function
 81:   | EnablePush v ->
 82:     if v <> 0 && v <> 1
 83:     then
 84:       (* From RFC7540§6.5.2
 85:        *   The initial value is 1, which indicates that server push is
 86:        *   permitted. Any value other than 0 or 1 MUST be treated as a
 87:        *   connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 88:       Error
 89:         Error.(
 90:           ConnectionError (ProtocolError, "SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH must be 0 or 1"))
 91:     else if is_client && v = 1
 92:     then
 93:       (* From RFC7540§8.2:
 94:        *   Clients MUST reject any attempt to change the
 95:        *   SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH setting to a value other than 0 by
 96:        *   treating the message as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
 97:        *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
 98:       Error
 99:         Error.(
100:           ConnectionError
101:             (ProtocolError, "Server must not try to enable SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH"))
102:     else Ok ()
103:   | InitialWindowSize v when WindowSize.is_window_overflow v ->
104:     (* From RFC7540§6.5.2
105:      *   Values above the maximum flow-control window size of 2^31-1 MUST be
106:      *   treated as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of type
107:      *   FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR. *)
108:     Error
109:       Error.(
110:         ConnectionError
111:           ( FlowControlError
112:           , Format.sprintf
113:               "Window size must be less than or equal to %ld"
114:               WindowSize.max_window_size ))
115:   | MaxFrameSize v when v < 16384 || v > 16777215 ->
116:     (* From RFC7540§6.5.2
117:      *   The initial value is 214 (16,384) octets. The value advertised by an
118:      *   endpoint MUST be between this initial value and the maximum allowed
119:      *   frame size (224-1 or 16,777,215 octets), inclusive. Values outside
120:      *   this range MUST be treated as a connection error (Section 5.4.1) of
121:      *   type PROTOCOL_ERROR. *)
122:     Error
123:       Error.(
124:         ConnectionError
125:           (ProtocolError, "Max frame size must be in the 16384 - 16777215 range"))
126:   | _ -> Ok ()
127: 
128: (* Check incoming settings and report an error if any. *)
129: let check_settings_list ?(is_client = false) settings =
130:   let rec loop = function
131:     | [] -> Ok ()
132:     | x :: xs ->
133:       (match check_value ~is_client x with
134:       | Ok () -> loop xs
135:       | Error _ as err -> err)
136:   in
137:   loop settings
138: 
139: type t =
140:   { header_table_size : int
141:   ; enable_push : bool
142:   ; max_concurrent_streams : int32
143:   ; (* Indicates the amount tokens the peer allows an H2 endpoint to send. *)
144:     initial_window_size : WindowSize.t
145:   ; max_frame_size : int
146:   ; max_header_list_size : int option
147:   }
148: 
149: (* From RFC7540§11.3 *)
150: let default =
151:   { header_table_size = 0x1000
152:   ; enable_push =
153:       true
154:       (* From RFC7540§6.5.2:
155:        *   SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS (0x3): [...] Initially, there is no
156:        *   limit to this value. *)
157:   ; max_concurrent_streams = Int32.max_int
158:   ; initial_window_size = WindowSize.default_initial_window_size
159:   ; max_frame_size = 0x4000
160:   ; max_header_list_size = None
161:   }
162: 
163: let settings_for_the_connection settings =
164:   let settings_list =
165:     if settings.max_frame_size <> default.max_frame_size
166:     then [ MaxFrameSize settings.max_frame_size ]
167:     else []
168:   in
169:   let settings_list =
170:     if settings.max_concurrent_streams <> default.max_concurrent_streams
171:     then MaxConcurrentStreams settings.max_concurrent_streams :: settings_list
172:     else settings_list
173:   in
174:   let settings_list =
175:     if settings.initial_window_size <> default.initial_window_size
176:     then
177:       (* FIXME: don't convert *)
178:       InitialWindowSize settings.initial_window_size :: settings_list
179:     else settings_list
180:   in
181:   let settings_list =
182:     if settings.enable_push <> default.enable_push
183:     then EnablePush (if settings.enable_push then 1 else 0) :: settings_list
184:     else settings_list
185:   in
186:   settings_list
187: 
188: let parse_settings_payload num_settings =
189:   let open Angstrom in
190:   let rec parse_inner acc remaining =
191:     (* From RFC7540§6.5.3:
192:      *   The values in the SETTINGS frame MUST be processed in the order
193:      *   they appear, with no other frame processing between values. *)
194:     if remaining <= 0
195:     then return (List.rev acc)
196:     else
197:       lift2
198:         (fun k (v : int32) ->
199:            match k with
200:            | 0x1 -> HeaderTableSize (Int32.to_int v) :: acc
201:            | 0x2 -> EnablePush (Int32.to_int v) :: acc
202:            | 0x3 -> MaxConcurrentStreams v :: acc
203:            | 0x4 -> InitialWindowSize v :: acc
204:            | 0x5 -> MaxFrameSize (Int32.to_int v) :: acc
205:            | 0x6 -> MaxHeaderListSize (Int32.to_int v) :: acc
206:            | _ ->
207:              (* Note: This ignores unknown settings.
208:               *
209:               * From RFC7540§6.5.3:
210:               *   Unsupported parameters MUST be ignored.
211:               *)
212:              acc)
213:         BE.any_uint16
214:         BE.any_int32
215:       >>= fun acc' -> parse_inner acc' (remaining - 1)
216:   in
217:   parse_inner [] num_settings
218: 
219: let write_settings_payload t settings_list =
220:   let open Faraday in
221:   List.iter
222:     (fun setting ->
223:        (* From RFC7540§6.5.1:
224:         *   The payload of a SETTINGS frame consists of zero or more parameters,
225:         *   each consisting of an unsigned 16-bit setting identifier and an
226:         *   unsigned 32-bit value. *)
227:        BE.write_uint16 t (serialize_key setting);
228:        match setting with
229:        | MaxConcurrentStreams value | InitialWindowSize value ->
230:          BE.write_uint32 t value
231:        | HeaderTableSize value
232:        | EnablePush value
233:        | MaxFrameSize value
234:        | MaxHeaderListSize value ->
235:          BE.write_uint32 t (Int32.of_int value))
236:     settings_list
237: 
238: let of_settings_list settings =
239:   List.fold_left
240:     (fun (acc : t) item ->
241:        match item with
242:        | HeaderTableSize x -> { acc with header_table_size = x }
243:        | EnablePush x -> { acc with enable_push = x = 1 }
244:        | MaxConcurrentStreams x -> { acc with max_concurrent_streams = x }
245:        | InitialWindowSize new_val -> { acc with initial_window_size = new_val }
246:        | MaxFrameSize x -> { acc with max_frame_size = x }
247:        | MaxHeaderListSize x -> { acc with max_header_list_size = Some x })
248:     default
249:     settings
250: 
251: let of_base64 encoded =
252:   match Base64.decode ~alphabet:Base64.uri_safe_alphabet encoded with
253:   | Ok settings_payload ->
254:     let settings_payload_length =
255:       String.length settings_payload / octets_per_setting
256:     in
257:     (match
258:        Angstrom.parse_string
259:          ~consume:All
260:          (parse_settings_payload settings_payload_length)
261:          settings_payload
262:      with
263:     | Ok settings -> Ok (of_settings_list settings)
264:     | Error _ as e -> e)
265:   | Error (`Msg msg) -> Error msg
266: 
267: let to_base64 t =
268:   let settings = settings_for_the_connection t in
269:   let faraday = Faraday.create (List.length settings * 6) in
270:   write_settings_payload faraday settings;
271:   let settings_hex = Faraday.serialize_to_string faraday in
272:   match Base64.encode ~alphabet:Base64.uri_safe_alphabet settings_hex with
273:   | Ok r -> Ok r
274:   | Error (`Msg msg) -> Error msg
275: 
276: let pp_hum formatter t =
277:   let pp_elem formatter setting =
278:     let key, value =
279:       match setting with
280:       | HeaderTableSize v -> "HEADER_TABLE_SIZE", Int64.of_int v
281:       | EnablePush v -> "ENABLE_PUSH", Int64.of_int v
282:       | MaxConcurrentStreams v -> "MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS", Int64.of_int32 v
283:       | InitialWindowSize v -> "INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE", Int64.of_int32 v
284:       | MaxFrameSize v -> "MAX_FRAME_SIZE", Int64.of_int v
285:       | MaxHeaderListSize v -> "MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE", Int64.of_int v
286:     in
287:     Format.fprintf formatter "@[(%S %Ld)@]" key value
288:   in
289:   Format.fprintf formatter "@[(";
290:   Format.pp_print_list pp_elem formatter (settings_for_the_connection t);
291:   Format.fprintf formatter ")@]"
````

## File: ../../../../.cache/opam/5.2.0+ox/lib/h2/stream.ml
````ocaml
  1: (*----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  2:  *  Copyright (c) 2019 António Nuno Monteiro
  3:  *
  4:  *  All rights reserved.
  5:  *
  6:  *  Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
  7:  *  modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met:
  8:  *
  9:  *  1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice,
 10:  *  this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
 11:  *
 12:  *  2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright
 13:  *  notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
 14:  *  documentation and/or other materials provided with the distribution.
 15:  *
 16:  *  3. Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its
 17:  *  contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this
 18:  *  software without specific prior written permission.
 19:  *
 20:  *  THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS "AS IS"
 21:  *  AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE
 22:  *  IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE
 23:  *  ARE DISCLAIMED. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT HOLDER OR CONTRIBUTORS BE
 24:  *  LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR
 25:  *  CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF
 26:  *  SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS
 27:  *  INTERRUPTION) HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN
 28:  *  CONTRACT, STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE)
 29:  *  ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE
 30:  *  POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
 31:  *---------------------------------------------------------------------------*)
 32: 
 33: module AB = Angstrom.Buffered
 34: module Writer = Serialize.Writer
 35: 
 36: type partial_headers =
 37:   { mutable parse_state : (Headers.t, Hpack.error) result AB.state
 38:   ; end_stream : bool
 39:   }
 40: 
 41: type 'active_peer remote_state =
 42:   (* A stream is in this state when it's waiting for the peer to initiate a
 43:    * response. In practice, it only matters for the client implementation, when
 44:    * a client has opened a stream but is still waiting on the server to send
 45:    * the first bytes of the response. *)
 46:   | WaitingForPeer
 47:   (* A PartialHeaders state is entered when the endpoint sees the first HEADERS
 48:    * frame from the peer for a given stream. Its payload is an
 49:    * Angstrom.Buffered parse state. *)
 50:   | PartialHeaders of partial_headers
 51:   (* A stream transitions from the PartialHeaders state to the FullHeaders
 52:    * state when the endpoint has finished parsing all the bytes in a group of
 53:    * HEADER / CONTINUATION frames that the peer has sent.
 54:    * This state doesn't carry any payload because the stream will immediately
 55:    * transition to the ActiveMessage state once the message has been validated
 56:    * according to RFC7540§8.1.2. *)
 57:   | FullHeaders
 58:   (* The ActiveMessage state carries information about the current remote
 59:    * message being processed by the endpoint. *)
 60:   | ActiveMessage of 'active_peer
 61: 
 62: type closed_reason =
 63:   | Finished
 64:   (* TODO: we could abide by the following by either 1) having I/O runtime
 65:    * support for timers or 2) by simply counting the number of frames received
 66:    * after we've sent an RST_STREAM?
 67:    *
 68:    * From RFC7540§5.4.2:
 69:    *   Normally, an endpoint SHOULD NOT send more than one RST_STREAM frame for
 70:    *   any stream. However, an endpoint MAY send additional RST_STREAM frames
 71:    *   if it receives frames on a closed stream after more than a round-trip
 72:    *   time. This behavior is permitted to deal with misbehaving
 73:    *   implementations. *)
 74:   | ResetByUs of Error_code.t
 75:   (* Received an RST_STREAM frame from the peer. *)
 76:   | ResetByThem of Error_code.t
 77: 
 78: type closed =
 79:   { reason : closed_reason
 80:         (* When a stream is closed, we may want to keep it around in the hash
 81:          * table for a while (e.g. to know whether this stream was reset by the
 82:          * peer - some error handling code depends on that). We start with a
 83:          * default value, and on every writer yield we decrement it. If it
 84:          * reaches 0, the stream is finally removed from the hash table. *)
 85:   ; mutable ttl : int
 86:   }
 87: 
 88: type ('opn, 'half_closed) active_state =
 89:   | Open of 'opn remote_state
 90:   | HalfClosed of 'half_closed
 91: 
 92: type ('active_state, 'active, 'reserved) state =
 93:   | Idle
 94:   | Reserved of 'reserved
 95:   | Active of 'active_state * 'active
 96:   | Closed of closed
 97:   constraint 'active_state = (_, _) active_state
 98: 
 99: type 'a error_status =
100:   | No_error
101:   | Exn of exn
102:   | Other of
103:       { error : 'a
104:       ; code : Error_code.t
105:       }
106: 
107: type ('state, 'error, 'error_handler) t =
108:   { id : Stream_identifier.t
109:   ; writer : Serialize.Writer.t
110:   ; error_handler : 'error_handler
111:   ; mutable error_code : 'error error_status
112:   ; mutable state : 'state
113:         (* The largest frame payload we're allowed to write. *)
114:   ; mutable max_frame_size : int
115:   ; on_close : active:bool -> closed -> unit
116:   }
117:   constraint 'state = (_, _, _) state
118: 
119: let initial_ttl = 10
120: 
121: let create id ~max_frame_size writer error_handler on_close =
122:   { id
123:   ; writer
124:   ; error_handler
125:     (* From RFC7540§5.1:
126:      *   idle: All streams start in the "idle" state. *)
127:   ; state = Idle
128:   ; error_code = No_error
129:   ; max_frame_size
130:   ; on_close
131:   }
132: 
133: let id { id; _ } = id
134: let is_idle t = match t.state with Idle -> true | _ -> false
135: let is_open t = match t.state with Active (Open _, _) -> true | _ -> false
136: 
137: let finish_stream t reason =
138:   let active = match t.state with Active _ -> true | _ -> false in
139:   let closed = { reason; ttl = initial_ttl } in
140:   t.on_close ~active closed;
141:   t.state <- Closed closed
142: 
143: let error_code t =
144:   match t.error_code with
145:   | Exn exn -> Some (`Exn exn)
146:   | Other { error; _ } -> Some error
147:   | No_error -> None
148: 
149: let error_to_code error error_code =
150:   match error with
151:   | `Exn exn -> Exn exn
152:   | other -> Other { error = other; code = error_code }
153: 
154: let reset_stream t error_code =
155:   let frame_info = Writer.make_frame_info t.id in
156:   Writer.write_rst_stream t.writer frame_info error_code;
157:   finish_stream t (ResetByUs error_code)
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/certs.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** Local CA and server certificate generation using openssl. *)
  2: 
  3: open Eio.Std
  4: 
  5: let ( let* ) = Result.bind
  6: let quote = Filename.quote
  7: 
  8: let run_cmd cmd =
  9:   match Sys.command (cmd ^ " >/dev/null 2>&1") with
 10:   | 0 -> Ok ()
 11:   | code -> Error (Printf.sprintf "openssl command failed code=%d" code)
 12: 
 13: let write_file path contents =
 14:   let out = open_out path in
 15:   Fun.protect
 16:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr out)
 17:     (fun () -> output_string out contents)
 18: 
 19: let mkdir_p path =
 20:   ignore (Sys.command ("mkdir -p " ^ quote path))
 21: 
 22: let san_config ~cn ~dns ~ips =
 23:   let dns_lines =
 24:     List.mapi (fun i name -> Printf.sprintf "DNS.%d = %s" (i + 1) name) dns
 25:   in
 26:   let ip_lines =
 27:     List.mapi (fun i ip -> Printf.sprintf "IP.%d = %s" (i + 1) ip) ips
 28:   in
 29:   String.concat "\n"
 30:     ([ "[req]";
 31:        "distinguished_name = dn";
 32:        "prompt = no";
 33:        "req_extensions = req_ext";
 34:        "";
 35:        "[dn]";
 36:        "CN = " ^ cn;
 37:        "";
 38:        "[req_ext]";
 39:        "basicConstraints = critical,CA:FALSE";
 40:        "keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment";
 41:        "extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth";
 42:        "subjectAltName = @alt_names";
 43:        "";
 44:        "[alt_names]";
 45:      ]
 46:     @ dns_lines @ ip_lines @ [ "" ])
 47: 
 48: let create_ca root =
 49:   run_cmd
 50:     (String.concat " "
 51:        [
 52:          "openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes";
 53:          "-keyout " ^ quote (Filename.concat root "ca.key");
 54:          "-out " ^ quote (Filename.concat root "ca.pem");
 55:          "-days 30 -sha256";
 56:          "-subj " ^ quote "/CN=Eta HTTP Testsuite CA";
 57:          "-addext " ^ quote "basicConstraints=critical,CA:TRUE,pathlen:0";
 58:          "-addext " ^ quote "keyUsage=critical,keyCertSign,cRLSign";
 59:        ])
 60: 
 61: let create_leaf root ~name ~cn ~dns ~ips =
 62:   let cnf = Filename.concat root (name ^ ".cnf") in
 63:   let key = Filename.concat root (name ^ ".key") in
 64:   let csr = Filename.concat root (name ^ ".csr") in
 65:   let pem = Filename.concat root (name ^ ".pem") in
 66:   write_file cnf (san_config ~cn ~dns ~ips);
 67:   let* () =
 68:     run_cmd
 69:       (String.concat " "
 70:          [
 71:            "openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes";
 72:            "-keyout " ^ quote key;
 73:            "-out " ^ quote csr;
 74:            "-subj " ^ quote ("/CN=" ^ cn);
 75:            "-config " ^ quote cnf;
 76:          ])
 77:   in
 78:   run_cmd
 79:     (String.concat " "
 80:        [
 81:          "openssl x509 -req";
 82:          "-in " ^ quote csr;
 83:          "-CA " ^ quote (Filename.concat root "ca.pem");
 84:          "-CAkey " ^ quote (Filename.concat root "ca.key");
 85:          "-CAcreateserial";
 86:          "-out " ^ quote pem;
 87:          "-days 30 -sha256";
 88:          "-extfile " ^ quote cnf;
 89:          "-extensions req_ext";
 90:        ])
 91: 
 92: let prepare ~temp_dir =
````
