 93:   let cert_dir = Filename.concat temp_dir "certs" in
 94:   mkdir_p cert_dir;
 95:   let* () = create_ca cert_dir in
 96:   let* () =
 97:     create_leaf cert_dir ~name:"server" ~cn:"localhost"
 98:       ~dns:[ "localhost"; "*.local.test" ]
 99:       ~ips:[ "127.0.0.1" ]
100:   in
101:   Ok cert_dir
102: 
103: let ca_path cert_dir = Filename.concat cert_dir "ca.pem"
104: let cert_path cert_dir = Filename.concat cert_dir "server.pem"
105: let key_path cert_dir = Filename.concat cert_dir "server.key"
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/fixtures.ml
````ocaml
 1: (** Static fixture generation for server tests. *)
 2: 
 3: let write_file path contents =
 4:   let out = open_out_bin path in
 5:   Fun.protect
 6:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr out)
 7:     (fun () -> output_string out contents)
 8: 
 9: let generate ~dir =
10:   Util.mkdir_p dir;
11:   write_file (Filename.concat dir "empty.txt") "";
12:   write_file (Filename.concat dir "1k.bin") (String.make 1024 'x');
13:   write_file (Filename.concat dir "1m.bin") (String.make (1024 * 1024) 'x');
14:   let path_100m = Filename.concat dir "100m.bin" in
15:   if not (Sys.file_exists path_100m) then
16:     ignore
17:       (Sys.command
18:          (Printf.sprintf "dd if=/dev/zero of=%s bs=1M count=100 status=none"
19:             (Filename.quote path_100m)));
20:   dir
21: 
22: let path dir name = Filename.concat dir name
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/malicious_h2.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** Raw HTTP/2 frame construction and malicious server helpers.
  2:     Used by adversarial fixtures to craft pathological server behaviour. *)
  3: 
  4: open Eio.Std
  5: 
  6: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  7:    HPACK mini-encoder — enough for the status-line and a few literal headers
  8:    we need in adversarial fixtures.
  9:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 10: 
 11: let encode_int7 n =
 12:   if n < 128 then [ n ]
 13:   else
 14:     let rec loop n acc =
 15:       if n < 128 then n :: acc
 16:       else loop (n lsr 7) ((128 + (n land 127)) :: acc)
 17:     in
 18:     loop (n - 127) [ 127 ]
 19: 
 20: let hpack_literal ~name ~value =
 21:   let name_bytes = Bytes.of_string name in
 22:   let value_bytes = Bytes.of_string value in
 23:   let name_len = encode_int7 (Bytes.length name_bytes) in
 24:   let value_len = encode_int7 (Bytes.length value_bytes) in
 25:   let total_len = 1 + List.length name_len + Bytes.length name_bytes + List.length value_len + Bytes.length value_bytes in
 26:   let buf = Bytes.create total_len in
 27:   Bytes.set buf 0 '\x00'; (* literal, new name, no indexing *)
 28:   let offset = ref 1 in
 29:   List.iter (fun b -> Bytes.set buf !offset (Char.chr b); incr offset) name_len;
 30:   Bytes.blit name_bytes 0 buf !offset (Bytes.length name_bytes);
 31:   offset := !offset + Bytes.length name_bytes;
 32:   List.iter (fun b -> Bytes.set buf !offset (Char.chr b); incr offset) value_len;
 33:   Bytes.blit value_bytes 0 buf !offset (Bytes.length value_bytes);
 34:   Bytes.to_string buf
 35: 
 36: let hpack_indexed_status_200 = "\x88"
 37: 
 38: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 39:    Raw HTTP/2 frame construction
 40:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 41: 
 42: let frame ~ty ~flags ~stream_id payload =
 43:   let len = String.length payload in
 44:   let buf = Bytes.create (9 + len) in
 45:   Bytes.set buf 0 (Char.chr (len lsr 16));
 46:   Bytes.set buf 1 (Char.chr ((len lsr 8) land 0xFF));
 47:   Bytes.set buf 2 (Char.chr (len land 0xFF));
 48:   Bytes.set buf 3 (Char.chr ty);
 49:   Bytes.set buf 4 (Char.chr flags);
 50:   let sid = Int32.of_int stream_id in
 51:   Bytes.set_int32_be buf 5 sid;
 52:   Bytes.blit_string payload 0 buf 9 len;
 53:   Bytes.to_string buf
 54: 
 55: let settings_frame ?(ack=false) pairs =
 56:   let flags = if ack then 0x01 else 0x00 in
 57:   let payload = Buffer.create (6 * List.length pairs) in
 58:   List.iter (fun (id, value) ->
 59:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr (id lsr 8));
 60:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr (id land 0xFF));
 61:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr ((value lsr 24) land 0xFF));
 62:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr ((value lsr 16) land 0xFF));
 63:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr ((value lsr 8) land 0xFF));
 64:       Buffer.add_char payload (Char.chr (value land 0xFF));
 65:     ) pairs;
 66:   frame ~ty:0x04 ~flags ~stream_id:0 (Buffer.contents payload)
 67: 
 68: let headers_frame ~end_headers ~stream_id block =
 69:   let flags = if end_headers then 0x04 else 0x00 in
 70:   frame ~ty:0x01 ~flags ~stream_id block
 71: 
 72: let continuation_frame ~end_headers ~stream_id block =
 73:   let flags = if end_headers then 0x04 else 0x00 in
 74:   frame ~ty:0x09 ~flags ~stream_id block
 75: 
 76: let rst_stream_frame ~stream_id error_code =
 77:   let buf = Bytes.create 4 in
 78:   Bytes.set_int32_be buf 0 (Int32.of_int error_code);
 79:   frame ~ty:0x03 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id (Bytes.to_string buf)
 80: 
 81: let goaway_frame ~last_stream_id ~error_code ?(debug="") () =
 82:   let len = 8 + String.length debug in
 83:   let buf = Bytes.create len in
 84:   Bytes.set_int32_be buf 0 (Int32.logand (Int32.of_int last_stream_id) 0x7FFFFFFFl);
 85:   Bytes.set_int32_be buf 4 (Int32.of_int error_code);
 86:   Bytes.blit_string debug 0 buf 8 (String.length debug);
 87:   frame ~ty:0x07 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:0 (Bytes.to_string buf)
 88: 
 89: let ping_frame ~ack payload =
 90:   let flags = if ack then 0x01 else 0x00 in
 91:   let data = String.sub payload 0 (min 8 (String.length payload)) in
 92:   let padded = data ^ String.make (8 - String.length data) '\x00' in
 93:   frame ~ty:0x06 ~flags ~stream_id:0 padded
 94: 
 95: let window_update_frame ~stream_id increment =
 96:   let buf = Bytes.create 4 in
 97:   Bytes.set_int32_be buf 0 (Int32.logand (Int32.of_int increment) 0x7FFFFFFFl);
 98:   frame ~ty:0x08 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id (Bytes.to_string buf)
 99: 
100: let data_frame ~end_stream ~stream_id payload =
101:   let flags = if end_stream then 0x01 else 0x00 in
102:   frame ~ty:0x00 ~flags ~stream_id payload
103: 
104: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105:    Generic h2 malicious server lifecycle
106:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
107: 
108: let write_string flow s =
109:   let cs = Cstruct.of_string s in
110:   Eio.Flow.write flow [ cs ]
111: 
112: let read_preface flow =
113:   let buf = Cstruct.create 24 in
114:   let n = Eio.Flow.single_read flow buf in
115:   if n < 24 then failwith "short client preface"
116: 
117: let read_frame_header flow =
118:   let buf = Cstruct.create 9 in
119:   let n = Eio.Flow.single_read flow buf in
120:   if n < 9 then failwith "short frame header";
121:   let len = (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 0 lsl 16) lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 1 lsl 8) lor Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 2 in
122:   let ty = Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 3 in
123:   let flags = Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 4 in
124:   let stream_id =
125:     ((Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 5 land 0x7F) lsl 24)
126:     lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 6 lsl 16)
127:     lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 7 lsl 8)
128:     lor Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 8
129:   in
130:   (len, ty, flags, stream_id)
131: 
132: let skip_frame_payload flow len =
133:   if len > 0 then (
134:     let buf = Cstruct.create len in
135:     let n = Eio.Flow.single_read flow buf in
136:     if n < len then failwith "short frame payload")
137: 
138: let send_server_preface flow =
139:   (* Server connection preface is just a SETTINGS frame. *)
140:   write_string flow (settings_frame [])
141: 
142: let ack_settings flow =
143:   write_string flow (settings_frame ~ack:true [])
144: 
145: let with_client_stream ~env ~on_request f =
146:   let port = Util.random_port () in
147:   let server_done, resolve = Eio.Promise.create () in
148:   Eio.Switch.run (fun sw ->
149:       let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
150:       let socket = Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
151:           (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port)) in
152:       Eio.Fiber.fork ~sw (fun () ->
153:           Eio.Switch.run (fun conn_sw ->
154:               let flow, _addr = Eio.Net.accept ~sw:conn_sw socket in
155:               Fun.protect
156:                 ~finally:(fun () -> ignore (Eio.Promise.try_resolve resolve ()))
157:                 (fun () ->
158:                    send_server_preface flow;
159:                    read_preface flow;
160:                    (* client sends SETTINGS first; ack it *)
161:                    let len, ty, _flags, _sid = read_frame_header flow in
162:                    if ty = 0x04 then ack_settings flow
163:                    else skip_frame_payload flow len;
164:                    skip_frame_payload flow len;
165:                    on_request flow)));
166:       let result = f port in
167:       ignore (Eio.Promise.await server_done);
168:       result)
169: 
170: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
171:    Attack-specific server behaviours
172:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
173: 
174: let serve_rapid_reset ~env ~count ~delay_sec flow =
175:   (* Wait for first client HEADERS, then HEADERS+RST on every stream. *)
176:   let stream_count = ref 0 in
177:   let rec loop () =
178:     if !stream_count >= count then ()
179:     else
180:       let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
181:       skip_frame_payload flow len;
182:       if ty = 0x01 then (
183:         incr stream_count;
184:         write_string flow (headers_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:sid hpack_indexed_status_200);
185:         write_string flow (rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:sid 8);
186:         if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) delay_sec;
187:         loop ())
188:       else loop ()
189:   in
190:   loop ()
191: 
192: let serve_continuation_flood ~env ~frames ~delay_sec flow =
193:   let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
194:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
195:   if ty = 0x01 then (
196:     (* send HEADERS without END_HEADERS *)
197:     write_string flow (headers_frame ~end_headers:false ~stream_id:sid hpack_indexed_status_200);
198:     (* send many CONTINUATION frames without END_HEADERS *)
199:     for _i = 1 to frames do
200:       let chunk = hpack_literal ~name:"x-continuation" ~value:"a" in
201:       write_string flow (continuation_frame ~end_headers:false ~stream_id:sid chunk);
202:       if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) delay_sec
203:     done;
204:     (* final CONTINUATION with END_HEADERS, then RST_STREAM *)
205:     let final = hpack_literal ~name:"x-end" ~value:"done" in
206:     write_string flow (continuation_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:sid final);
207:     write_string flow (rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:sid 8))
208: 
209: let serve_hpack_bomb ~env ~decoded_size flow =
210:   let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
211:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
212:   if ty = 0x01 then (
213:     (* Send a HEADERS block that decodes to a very large header list.
214:        We do this by sending many literal header fields with large values
215:        in a single HEADERS frame. *)
216:     let big_value = String.make decoded_size 'x' in
217:     let block = hpack_literal ~name:"x-bomb" ~value:big_value in
218:     write_string flow (headers_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:sid block);
219:     write_string flow (data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:sid ""))
220: 
221: let serve_ping_flood ~env ~count ~delay_sec flow =
222:   let len, ty, _flags, _sid = read_frame_header flow in
223:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
224:   for _i = 1 to count do
225:     write_string flow (ping_frame ~ack:false "pingflood");
226:     if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) delay_sec
227:   done
228: 
229: let serve_settings_flood ~env ~count ~delay_sec flow =
230:   let len, ty, _flags, _sid = read_frame_header flow in
231:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
232:   for _i = 1 to count do
233:     write_string flow (settings_frame [ (0x3, 100 ); (0x4, 65535) ]);
234:     if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) delay_sec
235:   done
236: 
237: let serve_empty_frames_flood ~env ~count ~delay_sec flow =
238:   let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
239:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
240:   if ty = 0x01 then (
241:     write_string flow (headers_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:sid hpack_indexed_status_200);
242:     for _i = 1 to count do
243:       write_string flow (data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:sid "");
244:       if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) delay_sec
245:     done;
246:     write_string flow (data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:sid ""))
247: 
248: let serve_window_overflow ~env flow =
249:   let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
250:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
251:   if ty = 0x01 then (
252:     write_string flow (headers_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:sid hpack_indexed_status_200);
253:     write_string flow (window_update_frame ~stream_id:sid 0x7FFFFFFF);
254:     write_string flow (window_update_frame ~stream_id:0 0x7FFFFFFF))
255: 
256: let serve_goaway_churn ~env ~count flow =
257:   let len, ty, _flags, sid = read_frame_header flow in
258:   skip_frame_payload flow len;
259:   for _i = 1 to count do
260:     write_string flow (goaway_frame ~last_stream_id:sid ~error_code:0 ());
261:     Eio.Time.sleep (Eio.Stdenv.clock env) 0.1
262:   done
263: 
264: let generate_gzip_bomb ~path ~expanded_bytes =
265:   let zeros = String.make (min expanded_bytes (1024 * 1024)) '\x00' in
266:   let zeros_path = path ^ ".zeros" in
267:   let oc = open_out_bin zeros_path in
268:   Fun.protect
269:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr oc)
270:     (fun () ->
271:        let written = ref 0 in
272:        while !written < expanded_bytes do
273:          let chunk_size = min (expanded_bytes - !written) (String.length zeros) in
274:          output_substring oc zeros 0 chunk_size;
275:          written := !written + chunk_size
276:        done);
277:   ignore (Sys.command (Printf.sprintf "gzip -f %s && mv %s.gz %s" (Filename.quote zeros_path) (Filename.quote zeros_path) (Filename.quote path)))
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/h2spec/dune
````
 1: (executable
 2:  (name run)
 3:  (libraries eta_http_testsuite eta_http_eio eio_main yojson unix))
 4: 
 5: (rule
 6:  (alias h2spec)
 7:  (deps
 8:   (source_tree ../../lib)
 9:   run.exe)
10:  (action
11:   (run %{dep:run.exe})))
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/h2spec/run.ml
````ocaml
  1: open Eta_http_testsuite
  2: 
  3: type case = {
  4:   name : string;
  5:   transport : Types.transport;
  6:   port : int;
  7:   command : string list;
  8:   stdout_path : string;
  9:   stderr_path : string;
 10:   junit_path : string;
 11:   server_stdout_path : string;
 12:   server_stderr_path : string;
 13:   exit_code : int;
 14:   success : bool;
 15:   duration_ms : float;
 16: }
 17: 
 18: let fail msg =
 19:   prerr_endline msg;
 20:   exit 1
 21: 
 22: let expect_ok = function
 23:   | Ok value -> value
 24:   | Error error -> fail error
 25: 
 26: let transport_slug = function
 27:   | Types.Plain -> "h2c"
 28:   | TLS -> "h2-tls"
 29: 
 30: let transport_of_slug = function
 31:   | "h2c" -> Types.Plain
 32:   | "h2-tls" -> TLS
 33:   | other -> fail ("unknown h2spec server transport: " ^ other)
 34: 
 35: let conformance_config =
 36:   let body_limit = 16 * 1024 * 1024 in
 37:   let server =
 38:     {
 39:       Eta_http.Server.Config.default with
 40:       unread_body_policy = Drain_up_to body_limit;
 41:       limits =
 42:         {
 43:           Eta_http.Server.Config.default.limits with
 44:           max_request_body_bytes = Some body_limit;
 45:         };
 46:     }
 47:   in
 48:   { Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default with server }
 49: 
 50: let handler request =
 51:   if
 52:     Option.is_some
 53:       (Eta_http.Core.Header.get "trailer"
 54:          request.Eta_http.Server.Request.headers)
 55:   then
 56:     Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_server.text "eta-h2spec\n")
 57:   else
 58:     Eta_http.Server.Body.read_all request.Eta_http.Server.Request.body
 59:     |> Eta.Effect.map (fun _body -> Eta_server.text "eta-h2spec\n")
 60:     |> Eta.Effect.catch (fun _error -> Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_server.empty 500))
 61: 
 62: let tls_config cert_dir =
 63:   Eta_http.Tls.Config.default_server
 64:     ~certificate_chain_file:(Certs.cert_path cert_dir)
 65:     ~private_key_file:(Certs.key_path cert_dir)
 66:     ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ] ()
 67: 
 68: let serve transport port cert_dir =
 69:   Eio_main.run @@ fun env ->
 70:   Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
 71:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
 72:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
 73:   let addr = `Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port) in
 74:   match transport with
 75:   | Types.Plain ->
 76:       Eta_http_eio.Server.run_h2c ~sw ~net ~clock
 77:         ~config:conformance_config ~addr handler
 78:   | TLS ->
 79:       Eta_http_eio.Server.run_https ~sw ~net ~clock
 80:         ~config:conformance_config ~tls_config:(tls_config cert_dir) ~addr
 81:         handler
 82: 
 83: let close_noerr fd =
 84:   try Unix.close fd with
 85:   | Unix.Unix_error (Unix.EBADF, _, _) -> ()
 86:   | _ -> ()
 87: 
 88: let open_log path =
 89:   Unix.openfile path
 90:     [ Unix.O_WRONLY; Unix.O_CREAT; Unix.O_TRUNC; Unix.O_CLOEXEC ]
 91:     0o644
 92: 
 93: let spawn_server ~results_dir ~transport ~port ~cert_dir =
 94:   let name = transport_slug transport in
 95:   let stdout_path = Filename.concat results_dir (name ^ ".server.stdout.txt") in
 96:   let stderr_path = Filename.concat results_dir (name ^ ".server.stderr.txt") in
 97:   let stdout_fd = open_log stdout_path in
 98:   let stderr_fd = open_log stderr_path in
 99:   let argv =
100:     [|
101:       Sys.executable_name;
102:       "serve";
103:       name;
104:       string_of_int port;
105:       cert_dir;
106:     |]
107:   in
108:   let pid =
109:     Fun.protect
110:       ~finally:(fun () ->
111:         close_noerr stdout_fd;
112:         close_noerr stderr_fd)
113:       (fun () ->
114:         Unix.create_process Sys.executable_name argv Unix.stdin stdout_fd
115:           stderr_fd)
116:   in
117:   (pid, stdout_path, stderr_path)
118: 
119: let rec waitpid_nonblocking pid attempts =
120:   match Unix.waitpid [ Unix.WNOHANG ] pid with
121:   | 0, _ when attempts > 0 ->
122:       Unix.sleepf 0.05;
123:       waitpid_nonblocking pid (attempts - 1)
124:   | 0, _ ->
125:       (try Unix.kill pid Sys.sigkill with _ -> ());
126:       ignore (Unix.waitpid [] pid)
127:   | _, _ -> ()
128:   | exception Unix.Unix_error (Unix.ECHILD, _, _) -> ()
129: 
130: let stop_server pid =
131:   (try Unix.kill pid Sys.sigterm with _ -> ());
132:   waitpid_nonblocking pid 100
133: 
134: let try_connect port =
135:   let fd = Unix.socket Unix.PF_INET Unix.SOCK_STREAM 0 in
136:   Fun.protect
137:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_noerr fd)
138:     (fun () ->
139:       Unix.connect fd
140:         (Unix.ADDR_INET (Unix.inet_addr_loopback, port)))
141: 
142: let rec wait_for_tcp ~port attempts =
143:   if attempts = 0 then fail (Printf.sprintf "server did not listen on %d" port);
144:   match try_connect port with
145:   | () -> ()
146:   | exception _ ->
147:       Unix.sleepf 0.05;
148:       wait_for_tcp ~port (attempts - 1)
149: 
150: let shell_command ~stdout_path ~stderr_path command =
151:   String.concat " " (List.map Filename.quote command)
152:   ^ " > " ^ Filename.quote stdout_path
153:   ^ " 2> " ^ Filename.quote stderr_path
154: 
155: let h2spec_command ~port ~transport ~junit_path =
156:   let base =
157:     [
158:       "timeout";
159:       "900s";
160:       "h2spec";
161:       "--strict";
162:       "--timeout";
163:       "5";
164:       "--host";
165:       "127.0.0.1";
166:       "--port";
167:       string_of_int port;
168:       "--path";
169:       "/";
170:       "--junit-report";
171:       junit_path;
172:     ]
173:   in
174:   match transport with
175:   | Types.Plain -> base
176:   | TLS -> base @ [ "--tls"; "--insecure" ]
177: 
178: let run_process ~results_dir ~name command =
179:   let stdout_path = Filename.concat results_dir (name ^ ".stdout.txt") in
180:   let stderr_path = Filename.concat results_dir (name ^ ".stderr.txt") in
181:   let start = Util.now_ms () in
182:   let exit_code = Sys.command (shell_command ~stdout_path ~stderr_path command) in
183:   (stdout_path, stderr_path, exit_code, Util.now_ms () -. start)
184: 
185: let run_case ~results_dir ~cert_dir transport =
186:   let port = Util.random_port () in
187:   let name = transport_slug transport in
188:   let pid, server_stdout_path, server_stderr_path =
189:     spawn_server ~results_dir ~transport ~port ~cert_dir
190:   in
191:   Fun.protect
192:     ~finally:(fun () -> stop_server pid)
193:     (fun () ->
194:       wait_for_tcp ~port 100;
195:       let junit_path = Filename.concat results_dir (name ^ ".junit.xml") in
196:       let command = h2spec_command ~port ~transport ~junit_path in
197:       Printf.printf "h2spec %s port=%d\n%!" name port;
198:       let stdout_path, stderr_path, exit_code, duration_ms =
199:         run_process ~results_dir ~name command
200:       in
201:       {
202:         name;
203:         transport;
204:         port;
205:         command;
206:         stdout_path;
207:         stderr_path;
208:         junit_path;
209:         server_stdout_path;
210:         server_stderr_path;
211:         exit_code;
212:         success = exit_code = 0;
213:         duration_ms;
214:       })
215: 
216: let json_of_case case =
217:   `Assoc
218:     [
219:       ("name", `String case.name);
220:       ("transport", Json.yojson_of_transport case.transport);
221:       ("port", `Int case.port);
222:       ("command", `List (List.map (fun value -> `String value) case.command));
223:       ("stdout_path", `String case.stdout_path);
224:       ("stderr_path", `String case.stderr_path);
225:       ("junit_path", `String case.junit_path);
226:       ("server_stdout_path", `String case.server_stdout_path);
227:       ("server_stderr_path", `String case.server_stderr_path);
228:       ("exit_code", `Int case.exit_code);
229:       ("success", `Bool case.success);
230:       ("duration_ms", `Float case.duration_ms);
231:     ]
232: 
233: let render_command command =
234:   String.concat " " (List.map Filename.quote command)
235: 
236: let first_lines path limit =
237:   if not (Sys.file_exists path) then []
238:   else
239:     let ic = open_in path in
240:     Fun.protect
241:       ~finally:(fun () -> close_in_noerr ic)
242:       (fun () ->
243:         let rec loop acc remaining =
244:           if remaining = 0 then List.rev acc
245:           else
246:             match input_line ic with
247:             | line -> loop (line :: acc) (remaining - 1)
248:             | exception End_of_file -> List.rev acc
249:         in
250:         loop [] limit)
251: 
252: let render_report ~path cases =
253:   let oc = open_out path in
254:   Fun.protect
255:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr oc)
256:     (fun () ->
257:       Printf.fprintf oc "# Eta HTTP/2 h2spec Conformance\n\n";
258:       Printf.fprintf oc "- Tool: h2spec %s\n"
259:         (Util.version_of_cmd "h2spec --version");
260:       Printf.fprintf oc "- Strict mode: enabled\n";
261:       Printf.fprintf oc "- Generated: %s\n\n" (Util.utc_timestamp ());
262:       List.iter
263:         (fun case ->
264:           Printf.fprintf oc "## %s\n\n" case.name;
265:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Transport: %s\n" case.name;
266:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Port: %d\n" case.port;
267:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Exit code: %d\n" case.exit_code;
268:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Success: %b\n" case.success;
269:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Duration: %.0f ms\n" case.duration_ms;
270:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Command: `%s`\n" (render_command case.command);
271:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Stdout: `%s`\n" case.stdout_path;
272:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Stderr: `%s`\n" case.stderr_path;
273:           Printf.fprintf oc "- JUnit: `%s`\n" case.junit_path;
274:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Server stdout: `%s`\n" case.server_stdout_path;
275:           Printf.fprintf oc "- Server stderr: `%s`\n\n"
276:             case.server_stderr_path;
277:           let preview = first_lines case.stdout_path 40 in
278:           if preview <> [] then (
279:             Printf.fprintf oc "```text\n";
280:             List.iter (Printf.fprintf oc "%s\n") preview;
281:             Printf.fprintf oc "```\n\n"))
282:         cases)
283: 
284: let repo_root () =
285:   match Util.run_cmd_out "git rev-parse --show-toplevel" with
286:   | Ok (root :: _) -> String.trim root
287:   | Ok [] | Error _ -> Sys.getcwd ()
288: 
289: let run_parent () =
290:   let results_root =
291:     if Array.length Sys.argv > 1 then Sys.argv.(1)
292:     else Filename.concat (repo_root ()) "http-testsuite/results"
293:   in
294:   let run_id =
295:     Printf.sprintf "%s-%s" (Util.utc_timestamp ()) (Util.git_sha ())
296:   in
297:   let results_dir = Filename.concat results_root run_id in
298:   Util.mkdir_p results_dir;
299:   let temp_dir = Filename.concat results_dir "h2spec-fixtures" in
300:   Util.mkdir_p temp_dir;
301:   ignore (Fixtures.generate ~dir:temp_dir);
302:   let cert_dir = Certs.prepare ~temp_dir |> expect_ok in
303:   Printf.printf "h2spec_runner run_id=%s results_dir=%s\n%!" run_id
304:     results_dir;
305:   let h2c = run_case ~results_dir ~cert_dir Types.Plain in
306:   let h2_tls = run_case ~results_dir ~cert_dir TLS in
307:   let cases = [ h2c; h2_tls ] in
308:   Json.write_json
309:     ~path:(Filename.concat results_dir "h2spec.json")
310:     (`List (List.map json_of_case cases));
311:   render_report ~path:(Filename.concat results_dir "h2spec.md") cases;
312:   Printf.printf "h2spec_runner done results_dir=%s\n%!" results_dir;
313:   if List.exists (fun case -> not case.success) cases then exit 1
314: 
315: let () =
316:   match Array.to_list Sys.argv with
317:   | _ :: "serve" :: transport :: port :: cert_dir :: _ ->
318:       serve (transport_of_slug transport) (int_of_string port) cert_dir
319:   | _ -> run_parent ()
````

````ocaml
  1: type interrupt_id = int
  2: 
  3: type die = {
  4:   exn : exn;
  5:   backtrace : Printexc.raw_backtrace option;
  6:   span_name : string option;
  7:   annotations : (string * string) list;
  8: }
  9: 
 10: let equal_die left right =
 11:   left.exn == right.exn
 12:   && Option.equal String.equal left.span_name right.span_name
 13:   && List.equal
 14:        (fun (ak, av) (bk, bv) -> String.equal ak bk && String.equal av bv)
 15:        left.annotations right.annotations
 16: 
 17: let backtrace_string die = Option.map Printexc.raw_backtrace_to_string die.backtrace
 18: 
 19: let diagnostic_equal_die left right =
 20:   String.equal (Printexc.exn_slot_name left.exn)
 21:     (Printexc.exn_slot_name right.exn)
 22:   && String.equal (Printexc.to_string left.exn) (Printexc.to_string right.exn)
 23:   && Option.equal String.equal (backtrace_string left) (backtrace_string right)
 24:   && Option.equal String.equal left.span_name right.span_name
 25:   && List.equal
 26:        (fun (ak, av) (bk, bv) -> String.equal ak bk && String.equal av bv)
 27:        left.annotations right.annotations
 28: 
 29: let pp_annotations fmt annotations =
 30:   Format.fprintf fmt "[%a]"
 31:     (Format.pp_print_list
 32:        ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
 33:        (fun fmt (key, value) -> Format.fprintf fmt "%s=%S" key value))
 34:     annotations
 35: 
 36: let pp_backtrace fmt = function
 37:   | None -> ()
 38:   | Some bt ->
 39:       Format.fprintf fmt "; backtrace=%S" (Printexc.raw_backtrace_to_string bt)
 40: 
 41: let pp_die fmt die =
 42:   Format.fprintf fmt "Die{exn=%S" (Printexc.to_string die.exn);
 43:   (match die.span_name with
 44:   | None -> ()
 45:   | Some name -> Format.fprintf fmt "; span_name=%S" name);
 46:   (match die.annotations with
 47:   | [] -> ()
 48:   | annotations ->
 49:       Format.fprintf fmt "; annotations=%a" pp_annotations annotations);
 50:   pp_backtrace fmt die.backtrace;
 51:   Format.pp_print_string fmt "}"
 52: 
 53: module Finalizer = struct
 54:   type t =
 55:     | Fail of string
 56:     | Die of die
 57:     | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
 58:     | Sequential of t list
 59:     | Concurrent of t list
 60:     | Finalizer of t
 61:     | Suppressed of { primary : t; finalizer : t }
 62: 
 63:   let rec equal left right =
 64:     match (left, right) with
 65:     | Fail a, Fail b -> String.equal a b
 66:     | Die a, Die b -> equal_die a b
 67:     | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
 68:     | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
 69:         List.equal equal a b
 70:     | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> equal a b
 71:     | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
 72:         equal a.primary b.primary && equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
 73:     | _ -> false
 74: 
 75:   let rec diagnostic_equal left right =
 76:     match (left, right) with
 77:     | Fail a, Fail b -> String.equal a b
 78:     | Die a, Die b -> diagnostic_equal_die a b
 79:     | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
 80:     | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
 81:         List.equal diagnostic_equal a b
 82:     | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> diagnostic_equal a b
 83:     | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
 84:         diagnostic_equal a.primary b.primary
 85:         && diagnostic_equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
 86:     | _ -> false
 87: 
 88:   let rec pp fmt = function
 89:     | Fail err -> Format.fprintf fmt "Fail(%S)" err
 90:     | Die die -> pp_die fmt die
 91:     | Interrupt None -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "Interrupt"
 92:     | Interrupt (Some id) -> Format.fprintf fmt "Interrupt(%d)" id
 93:     | Sequential causes ->
 94:         Format.fprintf fmt "Sequential[%a]"
 95:           (Format.pp_print_list
 96:              ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
 97:              pp)
 98:           causes
 99:     | Concurrent causes ->
100:         Format.fprintf fmt "Concurrent[%a]"
101:           (Format.pp_print_list
102:              ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
103:              pp)
104:           causes
105:     | Finalizer cause -> Format.fprintf fmt "Finalizer(%a)" pp cause
106:     | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
107:         Format.fprintf fmt "Suppressed{primary=%a; finalizer=%a}" pp primary pp
108:           finalizer
109: 
110:   let rec is_interrupt_only = function
111:     | Interrupt _ -> true
112:     | Sequential causes | Concurrent causes -> List.for_all is_interrupt_only causes
113:     | Finalizer cause -> is_interrupt_only cause
114:     | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
115:         is_interrupt_only primary && is_interrupt_only finalizer
116:     | Fail _ | Die _ -> false
117: end
118: 
119: module Finalizer_cause = Finalizer
120: 
121: type 'err t =
122:   | Fail of 'err
123:   | Die of die
124:   | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
125:   | Sequential of 'err t list
126:   | Concurrent of 'err t list
127:   | Finalizer of Finalizer.t
128:   | Suppressed of { primary : 'err t; finalizer : Finalizer.t }
129: 
130: type 'err same_domain_t = 'err t
131: 
132: module Portable = struct
133:   type die = {
134:     kind : string;
135:     message : string;
136:     backtrace : string option;
137:     span_name : string option;
138:     annotations : (string * string) list;
139:   }
140: 
141:   module Finalizer = struct
142:     type t =
143:       | Fail of string
144:       | Die of die
145:       | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
146:       | Sequential of t list
147:       | Concurrent of t list
148:       | Finalizer of t
149:       | Suppressed of { primary : t; finalizer : t }
150: 
151:     let die_of_cause die =
152:       {
153:         kind = Printexc.exn_slot_name die.exn;
154:         message = Printexc.to_string die.exn;
155:         backtrace = Option.map Printexc.raw_backtrace_to_string die.backtrace;
156:         span_name = die.span_name;
157:         annotations = die.annotations;
158:       }
159: 
160:     let rec of_finalizer : Finalizer_cause.t -> t = function
161:       | Finalizer_cause.Fail err -> Fail err
162:       | Finalizer_cause.Die die -> Die (die_of_cause die)
163:       | Finalizer_cause.Interrupt id -> Interrupt id
164:       | Finalizer_cause.Sequential causes -> Sequential (List.map of_finalizer causes)
165:       | Finalizer_cause.Concurrent causes -> Concurrent (List.map of_finalizer causes)
166:       | Finalizer_cause.Finalizer cause -> Finalizer (of_finalizer cause)
167:       | Finalizer_cause.Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
168:           Suppressed
169:             {
170:               primary = of_finalizer primary;
171:               finalizer = of_finalizer finalizer;
172:             }
173: 
174:     let equal_die left right =
175:       String.equal left.kind right.kind
176:       && String.equal left.message right.message
177:       && Option.equal String.equal left.backtrace right.backtrace
178:       && Option.equal String.equal left.span_name right.span_name
179:       && List.equal
180:            (fun (ak, av) (bk, bv) -> String.equal ak bk && String.equal av bv)
181:            left.annotations right.annotations
182: 
183:     let rec equal left right =
184:       match (left, right) with
185:       | Fail a, Fail b -> String.equal a b
186:       | Die a, Die b -> equal_die a b
187:       | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
188:       | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
189:           List.equal equal a b
190:       | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> equal a b
191:       | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
192:           equal a.primary b.primary && equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
193:       | _ -> false
194: 
195:     let pp_backtrace fmt = function
196:       | None -> ()
197:       | Some bt -> Format.fprintf fmt "; backtrace=%S" bt
198: 
199:     let pp_die fmt die =
200:       Format.fprintf fmt "Die{kind=%S; message=%S" die.kind die.message;
201:       (match die.span_name with
202:       | None -> ()
203:       | Some name -> Format.fprintf fmt "; span_name=%S" name);
204:       (match die.annotations with
205:       | [] -> ()
206:       | annotations ->
207:           Format.fprintf fmt "; annotations=%a" pp_annotations annotations);
208:       pp_backtrace fmt die.backtrace;
209:       Format.pp_print_string fmt "}"
210: 
211:     let rec pp fmt = function
212:       | Fail err -> Format.fprintf fmt "Fail(%S)" err
213:       | Die die -> pp_die fmt die
214:       | Interrupt None -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "Interrupt"
215:       | Interrupt (Some id) -> Format.fprintf fmt "Interrupt(%d)" id
216:       | Sequential causes ->
217:           Format.fprintf fmt "Sequential[%a]"
218:             (Format.pp_print_list
219:                ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
220:                pp)
221:             causes
222:       | Concurrent causes ->
223:           Format.fprintf fmt "Concurrent[%a]"
224:             (Format.pp_print_list
225:                ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
226:                pp)
227:             causes
228:       | Finalizer cause -> Format.fprintf fmt "Finalizer(%a)" pp cause
229:       | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
230:           Format.fprintf fmt "Suppressed{primary=%a; finalizer=%a}" pp primary pp
231:             finalizer
232:   end
233: 
234:   type ('err) t =
235:     | Fail of 'err
236:     | Die of die
237:     | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
238:     | Sequential of 'err t list
239:     | Concurrent of 'err t list
240:     | Finalizer of Finalizer.t
241:     | Suppressed of { primary : 'err t; finalizer : Finalizer.t }
242: 
243:   let die_of_cause die =
244:     {
245:       kind = Printexc.exn_slot_name die.exn;
246:       message = Printexc.to_string die.exn;
247:       backtrace = Option.map Printexc.raw_backtrace_to_string die.backtrace;
248:       span_name = die.span_name;
249:       annotations = die.annotations;
250:     }
251: 
252:   let rec of_cause :
253:       type err portable_err.
254:       (err -> portable_err) -> err same_domain_t -> portable_err t =
255:    fun convert -> function
256:     | Fail err -> Fail (convert err)
257:     | Die die -> Die (die_of_cause die)
258:     | Interrupt id -> Interrupt id
259:     | Sequential causes -> Sequential (List.map (of_cause convert) causes)
260:     | Concurrent causes -> Concurrent (List.map (of_cause convert) causes)
261:     | Finalizer cause -> Finalizer (Finalizer.of_finalizer cause)
262:     | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
263:         Suppressed
264:           {
265:             primary = of_cause convert primary;
266:             finalizer = Finalizer.of_finalizer finalizer;
267:           }
268: 
269:   let equal_die left right =
270:     String.equal left.kind right.kind
271:     && String.equal left.message right.message
272:     && Option.equal String.equal left.backtrace right.backtrace
273:     && Option.equal String.equal left.span_name right.span_name
274:     && List.equal
275:          (fun (ak, av) (bk, bv) -> String.equal ak bk && String.equal av bv)
276:          left.annotations right.annotations
277: 
278:   let rec equal (equal_err) left right =
279:     match (left, right) with
280:     | Fail a, Fail b -> equal_err a b
281:     | Die a, Die b -> equal_die a b
282:     | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
283:     | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
284:         List.equal (equal equal_err) a b
285:     | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> Finalizer.equal a b
286:     | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
287:         equal equal_err a.primary b.primary
288:         && Finalizer.equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
289:     | _ -> false
290: 
291:   let pp_backtrace fmt = function
292:     | None -> ()
293:     | Some bt -> Format.fprintf fmt "; backtrace=%S" bt
294: 
295:   let pp_die fmt die =
296:     Format.fprintf fmt "Die{kind=%S; message=%S" die.kind die.message;
297:     (match die.span_name with
298:     | None -> ()
299:     | Some name -> Format.fprintf fmt "; span_name=%S" name);
300:     (match die.annotations with
301:     | [] -> ()
302:     | annotations ->
303:         Format.fprintf fmt "; annotations=%a" pp_annotations annotations);
304:     pp_backtrace fmt die.backtrace;
305:     Format.pp_print_string fmt "}"
306: 
307:   let rec pp (pp_err) fmt = function
308:     | Fail err -> Format.fprintf fmt "Fail(%a)" pp_err err
309:     | Die die -> pp_die fmt die
310:     | Interrupt None -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "Interrupt"
311:     | Interrupt (Some id) -> Format.fprintf fmt "Interrupt(%d)" id
312:     | Sequential causes ->
313:         Format.fprintf fmt "Sequential[%a]"
314:           (Format.pp_print_list
315:              ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
316:              (pp pp_err))
317:           causes
318:     | Concurrent causes ->
319:         Format.fprintf fmt "Concurrent[%a]"
320:           (Format.pp_print_list
321:              ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
322:              (pp pp_err))
323:           causes
324:     | Finalizer cause ->
325:         Format.fprintf fmt "Finalizer(%a)" Finalizer.pp cause
326:     | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
327:         Format.fprintf fmt "Suppressed{primary=%a; finalizer=%a}" (pp pp_err)
328:           primary Finalizer.pp finalizer
329: end
330: 
331: let fail err = Fail err
332: let die_with_diagnostics ?backtrace ?span_name ?(annotations = []) exn =
333:   Die { exn; backtrace; span_name; annotations }
334: 
335: let die exn = die_with_diagnostics exn
336: let die_with_backtrace exn bt = die_with_diagnostics ~backtrace:bt exn
337: let interrupt_id_counter = Atomic.make 0
338: let fresh_interrupt_id () = Atomic.fetch_and_add interrupt_id_counter 1 + 1
339: let equal_interrupt_id a b = Int.equal a b
340: let interrupt = Interrupt None
341: let interrupt_with_id id = Interrupt (Some id)
342: 
343: let sequential = function
344:   | [] -> invalid_arg "Cause.sequential: empty"
345:   | [ one ] -> one
346:   | causes -> Sequential causes
347: 
348: let concurrent = function
349:   | [] -> invalid_arg "Cause.concurrent: empty"
350:   | [ one ] -> one
351:   | causes -> Concurrent causes
352: 
353: let finalizer = function
354:   | Finalizer.Finalizer inner -> Finalizer inner
355:   | cause -> Finalizer cause
356: 
357: let suppressed ~primary ~finalizer = Suppressed { primary; finalizer }
358: let to_portable = Portable.of_cause
359: 
360: let rec finalizer_of_cause :
361:     type err. (err -> string) -> err t -> Finalizer.t =
362:  fun (render) -> function
363:   | Fail err -> Finalizer.Fail (render err)
364:   | Die die -> Finalizer.Die die
365:   | Interrupt id -> Finalizer.Interrupt id
366:   | Sequential causes -> Finalizer.Sequential (List.map (finalizer_of_cause render) causes)
367:   | Concurrent causes -> Finalizer.Concurrent (List.map (finalizer_of_cause render) causes)
368:   | Finalizer cause -> Finalizer.Finalizer cause
369:   | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
370:       Finalizer.Suppressed
371:         { primary = finalizer_of_cause render primary; finalizer }
372: 
373: let rec map : type err mapped. (err -> mapped) -> err t -> mapped t =
374:  fun (f) -> function
375:   | Fail err -> Fail (f err)
376:   | Die die -> Die die
377:   | Interrupt id -> Interrupt id
378:   | Sequential causes -> Sequential (List.map (map f) causes)
379:   | Concurrent causes -> Concurrent (List.map (map f) causes)
380:   | Finalizer cause -> Finalizer cause
381:   | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
382:       Suppressed { primary = map f primary; finalizer }
383: 
384: let rec is_interrupt_only : type err. err t -> bool = function
385:   | Interrupt _ -> true
386:   | Sequential causes | Concurrent causes -> List.for_all is_interrupt_only causes
387:   | Finalizer cause -> Finalizer.is_interrupt_only cause
388:   | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
389:       is_interrupt_only primary && Finalizer.is_interrupt_only finalizer
390:   | Fail _ | Die _ -> false
391: 
392: let rec equal : type err. (err -> err -> bool) -> err t -> err t -> bool =
393:  fun (equal_err) left right ->
394:   match (left, right) with
395:   | Fail a, Fail b -> equal_err a b
396:   | Die a, Die b -> equal_die a b
397:   | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
398:   | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
399:       List.equal (equal equal_err) a b
400:   | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> Finalizer.equal a b
401:   | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
402:       equal equal_err a.primary b.primary
403:       && Finalizer.equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
404:   | _ -> false
405: 
406: let rec diagnostic_equal :
407:     type err. (err -> err -> bool) -> err t -> err t -> bool =
408:  fun (equal_err) left right ->
409:   match (left, right) with
410:   | Fail a, Fail b -> equal_err a b
411:   | Die a, Die b -> diagnostic_equal_die a b
412:   | Interrupt a, Interrupt b -> Option.equal Int.equal a b
413:   | Sequential a, Sequential b | Concurrent a, Concurrent b ->
414:       List.equal (diagnostic_equal equal_err) a b
415:   | Finalizer a, Finalizer b -> Finalizer.diagnostic_equal a b
416:   | Suppressed a, Suppressed b ->
417:       diagnostic_equal equal_err a.primary b.primary
418:       && Finalizer.diagnostic_equal a.finalizer b.finalizer
419:   | _ -> false
420: 
421: let rec pp :
422:     type err.
423:     (Format.formatter -> err -> unit) -> Format.formatter -> err t -> unit
424:     =
425:  fun (pp_err) fmt -> function
426:   | Fail err -> Format.fprintf fmt "Fail(%a)" pp_err err
427:   | Die die -> pp_die fmt die
428:   | Interrupt None -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "Interrupt"
429:   | Interrupt (Some id) -> Format.fprintf fmt "Interrupt(%d)" id
430:   | Sequential causes ->
431:       Format.fprintf fmt "Sequential[%a]"
432:         (Format.pp_print_list
433:            ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
434:            (pp pp_err))
435:         causes
436:   | Concurrent causes ->
437:       Format.fprintf fmt "Concurrent[%a]"
438:         (Format.pp_print_list
439:            ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "; ")
440:            (pp pp_err))
441:         causes
442:   | Finalizer cause ->
443:       Format.fprintf fmt "Finalizer(%a)" Finalizer.pp cause
444:   | Suppressed { primary; finalizer } ->
445:       Format.fprintf fmt "Suppressed{primary=%a; finalizer=%a}" (pp pp_err)
446:         primary Finalizer.pp finalizer
````

## File: lib/eta/cause.mli
````ocaml
  1: (** Failure causes that cannot fit in OCaml's [('a, 'e) result].
  2: 
  3:     [Fail] is the typed error channel. [Die] is an unchecked exception
  4:     from user code or the host runtime, with diagnostic context captured by
  5:     the runtime. [Interrupt] is cancellation, with an optional runtime-owned
  6:     interruption identity.
  7: 
  8:     [Sequential] preserves ordered failures from sequential composition.
  9:     [Concurrent] preserves failures observed from parallel composition.
 10:     [Finalizer] marks diagnostic failures produced while cleaning up after a
 11:     successful primary eff; ordinary [Effect.catch] leaves failures under
 12:     this node untouched. Finalizer failures are rendered to strings when they
 13:     leave the cleanup eff, so they are no longer part of the typed error
 14:     channel.
 15:     [Suppressed] preserves a primary failure together with a finalizer failure
 16:     that occurred while cleaning up the primary failure. *)
 17: 
 18: type interrupt_id
 19: 
 20: (** Diagnostic payload for unchecked defects.
 21: 
 22:     [span_name] and [annotations] are copied from the active Eta
 23:     [named]/[annotate] context when the defect is captured. [backtrace] is
 24:     controlled by [Eta_eio.Runtime.create ?capture_backtrace]. *)
 25: type die = {
 26:   exn : exn;
 27:   backtrace : Printexc.raw_backtrace option;
 28:   span_name : string option;
 29:   annotations : (string * string) list;
 30: }
 31: 
 32: module Finalizer : sig
 33:   type t =
 34:     | Fail of string
 35:     | Die of die
 36:     | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
 37:     | Sequential of t list
 38:     | Concurrent of t list
 39:     | Finalizer of t
 40:     | Suppressed of { primary : t; finalizer : t }
 41: 
 42:   val equal : t -> t -> bool
 43:   val diagnostic_equal : t -> t -> bool
 44:   val pp : Format.formatter -> t -> unit
 45:   val is_interrupt_only : t -> bool
 46: end
 47: 
 48: type 'err t =
 49:   | Fail of 'err
 50:   | Die of die
 51:   | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
 52:   | Sequential of 'err t list
 53:   | Concurrent of 'err t list
 54:   | Finalizer of Finalizer.t
 55:   | Suppressed of { primary : 'err t; finalizer : Finalizer.t }
 56: 
 57: type 'err same_domain_t = 'err t
 58: 
 59: (** Portable mirror for failures that cross domain boundaries.
 60: 
 61:     Same-domain {!t} keeps raw [exn] and [Printexc.raw_backtrace] so local
 62:     diagnostics preserve OCaml exception identity. [Portable.t] materializes
 63:     those raw fields into strings before moving causes across domains. *)
 64: module Portable : sig
 65:   type die = {
 66:     kind : string;
 67:     message : string;
 68:     backtrace : string option;
 69:     span_name : string option;
 70:     annotations : (string * string) list;
 71:   }
 72: 
 73:   module Finalizer : sig
 74:     type t =
 75:       | Fail of string
 76:       | Die of die
 77:       | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
 78:       | Sequential of t list
 79:       | Concurrent of t list
 80:       | Finalizer of t
 81:       | Suppressed of { primary : t; finalizer : t }
 82: 
 83:     val equal : t -> t -> bool
 84:     val pp : Format.formatter -> t -> unit
 85:   end
 86: 
 87:   type ('err) t =
 88:     | Fail of 'err
 89:     | Die of die
 90:     | Interrupt of interrupt_id option
 91:     | Sequential of 'err t list
 92:     | Concurrent of 'err t list
 93:     | Finalizer of Finalizer.t
 94:     | Suppressed of { primary : 'err t; finalizer : Finalizer.t }
 95: 
 96:   val of_cause :
 97:     ('err -> 'portable_err) -> 'err same_domain_t -> 'portable_err t
 98:   val equal : ('err -> 'err -> bool) -> 'err t -> 'err t -> bool
 99:   val pp :
100:     (Format.formatter -> 'err -> unit) -> Format.formatter -> 'err t -> unit
101: end
102: 
103: val fail : 'err -> 'err t
104: val die : exn -> 'err t
105: val die_with_backtrace : exn -> Printexc.raw_backtrace -> 'err t
106: val die_with_diagnostics :
107:   ?backtrace:Printexc.raw_backtrace ->
108:   ?span_name:string ->
109:   ?annotations:(string * string) list ->
110:   exn ->
111:   'err t
112: (** Build a [Die] cause with explicit diagnostic context. Runtime defect
113:     capture uses this constructor internally; most application code should not
114:     need it. *)
115: val fresh_interrupt_id : unit -> interrupt_id
116: val equal_interrupt_id : interrupt_id -> interrupt_id -> bool
117: val interrupt : 'err t
118: val interrupt_with_id : interrupt_id -> 'err t
119: val sequential : 'err t list -> 'err t
120: (** @raise Invalid_argument if the list is empty. *)
121: val concurrent : 'err t list -> 'err t
122: (** @raise Invalid_argument if the list is empty. *)
123: val finalizer : Finalizer.t -> 'err t
124: val suppressed : primary:'err t -> finalizer:Finalizer.t -> 'err t
125: 
126: val is_interrupt_only : 'err t -> bool
127: val map : ('err1 -> 'err2) -> 'err1 t -> 'err2 t
128: val finalizer_of_cause : ('err -> string) -> 'err t -> Finalizer.t
129: 
130: val equal : ('err -> 'err -> bool) -> 'err t -> 'err t -> bool
131: (** Structural equality for causes. [Die] causes compare by physical exception
132:     identity, plus diagnostic span and annotation metadata. This preserves
133:     same-domain exception identity; use {!diagnostic_equal} when test code wants
134:     to compare materialized exception diagnostics instead. *)
135: 
136: val diagnostic_equal : ('err -> 'err -> bool) -> 'err t -> 'err t -> bool
137: (** Diagnostic equality for causes. [Die] causes compare exception slot,
138:     rendered exception message, rendered backtrace, span name, and annotations. *)
139: 
140: val pp :
141:   (Format.formatter -> 'err -> unit) -> Format.formatter -> 'err t -> unit
142: val to_portable : ('err -> 'portable_err) -> 'err t -> 'portable_err Portable.t
````

## File: lib/eta/channel.ml
````ocaml
  1: type 'err send_result =
  2:   [ `Sent | `Full | `Closed | `Closed_with_error of 'err ]
  3: 
  4: type ('a, 'err) recv_result =
  5:   [ `Item of 'a | `Empty | `Closed | `Closed_with_error of 'err ]
  6: 
  7: type 'err close_reason = Clean | Error of 'err
  8: 
  9: type ('a, 'err) sender = {
 10:   value : 'a;
 11:   contract : Runtime_contract.t;
 12:   resolver : 'err send_result Runtime_contract.resolver;
 13:   mutable active : bool;
 14: }
 15: 
 16: type ('a, 'err) receiver = {
 17:   contract : Runtime_contract.t;
 18:   resolver : ('a, 'err) recv_result Runtime_contract.resolver;
 19:   mutable state : 'a receiver_state;
 20: }
 21: 
 22: and 'a receiver_state =
 23:   | Waiting
 24:   | Delivered of 'a
 25:   | Claimed
 26:   | Cancelled
 27: 
 28: type ('a, 'err) t = {
 29:   mutex : Sync_lock.t;
 30:   buffer : 'a option array;
 31:   senders : ('a, 'err) sender Stdlib.Queue.t;
 32:   receivers : ('a, 'err) receiver Stdlib.Queue.t;
 33:   capacity : int;
 34:   mutable head : int;
 35:   mutable tail : int;
 36:   mutable depth : int;
 37:   mutable pending_receivers : int;
 38:   mutable sent : int;
 39:   mutable received : int;
 40:   mutable closed : 'err close_reason option;
 41:   mutable waiting_senders : int;
 42:   mutable waiting_receivers : int;
 43:   mutable cancelled_senders : int;
 44: }
 45: 
 46: type stats = {
 47:   depth : int;
 48:   sent : int;
 49:   received : int;
 50:   closed : bool;
 51:   waiting_senders : int;
 52:   waiting_receivers : int;
 53:   cancelled_senders : int;
 54: }
 55: 
 56: let create ~capacity () =
 57:   if capacity <= 0 then invalid_arg "Eta.Channel.create: capacity must be > 0";
 58:   {
 59:     mutex = Sync_lock.create ();
 60:     buffer = Array.make capacity None;
 61:     senders = Stdlib.Queue.create ();
 62:     receivers = Stdlib.Queue.create ();
 63:     capacity;
 64:     head = 0;
 65:     tail = 0;
 66:     depth = 0;
 67:     pending_receivers = 0;
 68:     sent = 0;
 69:     received = 0;
 70:     closed = None;
 71:     waiting_senders = 0;
 72:     waiting_receivers = 0;
 73:     cancelled_senders = 0;
 74:   }
 75: 
 76: let close_result = function
 77:   | Clean -> `Closed
 78:   | Error error -> `Closed_with_error error
 79: 
 80: let resolve_sender (sender : ('a, 'err) sender) (result : 'err send_result) =
 81:   sender.contract.Runtime_contract.resolve_promise sender.resolver result
 82: 
 83: let resolve_receiver
 84:     (receiver : ('a, 'err) receiver)
 85:     (result : ('a, 'err) recv_result) =
 86:   receiver.contract.Runtime_contract.resolve_promise receiver.resolver result
 87: 
 88: let capacity_used (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
 89:   t.depth + t.pending_receivers
 90: 
 91: let ensure_capacity (t : ('a, 'err) t) operation =
 92:   if capacity_used t >= t.capacity then
 93:     invalid_arg ("Eta.Channel." ^ operation ^ ": capacity exceeded")
 94: 
 95: let push (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
 96:   ensure_capacity t "push";
 97:   t.buffer.(t.tail) <- Some value;
 98:   t.tail <- (t.tail + 1) mod t.capacity;
 99:   t.depth <- t.depth + 1
100: 
101: let push_counted (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
102:   push t value;
103:   t.sent <- t.sent + 1
104: 
105: let push_front (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
106:   ensure_capacity t "push_front";
107:   t.head <- (t.head + t.capacity - 1) mod t.capacity;
108:   t.buffer.(t.head) <- Some value;
109:   t.depth <- t.depth + 1
110: 
111: let pop_raw (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
112:   match t.buffer.(t.head) with
113:   | None -> invalid_arg "Eta.Channel.pop: empty slot"
114:   | Some value ->
115:       t.buffer.(t.head) <- None;
116:       t.head <- (t.head + 1) mod t.capacity;
117:       t.depth <- t.depth - 1;
118:       value
119: 
120: let pop (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
121:   let value = pop_raw t in
122:   t.received <- t.received + 1;
123:   value
124: 
125: let rec take_active_sender
126:     (q : ('a, 'err) sender Stdlib.Queue.t) :
127:     ('a, 'err) sender option =
128:   if Stdlib.Queue.is_empty q then None
129:   else
130:     let waiter = Stdlib.Queue.take q in
131:     if waiter.active then Some waiter else take_active_sender q
132: 
133: let rec take_active_receiver
134:     (q : ('a, 'err) receiver Stdlib.Queue.t) :
135:     ('a, 'err) receiver option =
136:   if Stdlib.Queue.is_empty q then None
137:   else
138:     let waiter = Stdlib.Queue.take q in
139:     match waiter.state with
140:     | Waiting -> Some waiter
141:     | Delivered _ | Claimed | Cancelled -> take_active_receiver q
142: 
143: let compact_cancelled_senders_locked (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
144:   if t.cancelled_senders > 0 then (
145:     let live = Stdlib.Queue.create () in
146:     Stdlib.Queue.iter
147:       (fun (sender : ('a, 'err) sender) ->
148:         if sender.active then Stdlib.Queue.push sender live)
149:       t.senders;
150:     Stdlib.Queue.clear t.senders;
151:     Stdlib.Queue.iter (fun sender -> Stdlib.Queue.push sender t.senders) live)
152: 
153: let compact_cancelled_receivers_locked (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
154:   let live = Stdlib.Queue.create () in
155:   Stdlib.Queue.iter
156:     (fun (receiver : ('a, 'err) receiver) ->
157:       match receiver.state with
158:       | Waiting -> Stdlib.Queue.push receiver live
159:       | Delivered _ | Claimed | Cancelled -> ())
160:     t.receivers;
161:   Stdlib.Queue.clear t.receivers;
162:   Stdlib.Queue.iter (fun receiver -> Stdlib.Queue.push receiver t.receivers) live
163: 
164: let take_sender (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
165:   match take_active_sender t.senders with
166:   | None -> None
167:   | Some sender ->
168:       sender.active <- false;
169:       t.waiting_senders <- t.waiting_senders - 1;
170:       Some sender
171: 
172: let take_receiver (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
173:   match take_active_receiver t.receivers with
174:   | None -> None
175:   | Some receiver ->
176:       t.waiting_receivers <- t.waiting_receivers - 1;
177:       Some receiver
178: 
179: let reserve_receiver_delivery (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
180:   ensure_capacity t "deliver_receiver";
181:   t.pending_receivers <- t.pending_receivers + 1
182: 
183: let release_receiver_delivery (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
184:   if t.pending_receivers <= 0 then
185:     invalid_arg "Eta.Channel.deliver_receiver: pending receiver underflow";
186:   t.pending_receivers <- t.pending_receivers - 1
187: 
188: let deliver_receiver (t : ('a, 'err) t) (receiver : ('a, 'err) receiver) value =
189:   reserve_receiver_delivery t;
190:   receiver.state <- Delivered value;
191:   resolve_receiver receiver (`Item value)
192: 
193: let claim_receiver (t : ('a, 'err) t) receiver =
194:   match receiver.state with
195:   | Delivered _ ->
196:       receiver.state <- Claimed;
197:       release_receiver_delivery t;
198:       t.received <- t.received + 1
199:   | Waiting | Claimed | Cancelled -> ()
200: 
201: let return_unclaimed_value (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
202:   match take_receiver t with
203:   | Some receiver -> deliver_receiver t receiver value
204:   | None -> push_front t value
205: 
206: let rec drain_buffer_to_receivers (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
207:   if t.depth > 0 then
208:     match take_receiver t with
209:     | None -> ()
210:     | Some receiver ->
211:         let value = pop_raw t in
212:         deliver_receiver t receiver value;
213:         drain_buffer_to_receivers t
214: 
215: let rec admit_waiting_senders (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
216:   if Option.is_none t.closed && capacity_used t < t.capacity then
217:     match take_sender t with
218:     | None -> ()
219:     | Some sender -> (
220:         if t.depth = 0 then
221:           match take_receiver t with
222:           | Some receiver ->
223:               t.sent <- t.sent + 1;
224:               deliver_receiver t receiver sender.value;
225:               resolve_sender sender `Sent;
226:               admit_waiting_senders t
227:           | None ->
228:               push_counted t sender.value;
229:               resolve_sender sender `Sent;
230:               admit_waiting_senders t
231:         else (
232:           push_counted t sender.value;
233:           resolve_sender sender `Sent;
234:           admit_waiting_senders t))
235: 
236: let pump (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
237:   drain_buffer_to_receivers t;
238:   admit_waiting_senders t
239: 
240: let with_lock (t : ('a, 'err) t) f =
241:   Sync_lock.use t.mutex f
242: 
243: let with_lock_during_cancel contract t f =
244:   contract.Runtime_contract.protect (fun () -> with_lock t f)
245: 
246: let enqueue_sender contract (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
247:   let promise, resolver = contract.Runtime_contract.create_promise () in
248:   let sender = { value; contract; resolver; active = true } in
249:   Stdlib.Queue.push sender t.senders;
250:   t.waiting_senders <- t.waiting_senders + 1;
251:   (promise, sender)
252: 
253: let enqueue_receiver contract (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
254:   let promise, resolver = contract.Runtime_contract.create_promise () in
255:   let receiver = { contract; resolver; state = Waiting } in
256:   Stdlib.Queue.push receiver t.receivers;
257:   t.waiting_receivers <- t.waiting_receivers + 1;
258:   (promise, receiver)
259: 
260: let cancel_sender (t : ('a, 'err) t) (sender : ('a, 'err) sender) =
261:   if sender.active then (
262:     sender.active <- false;
263:     t.waiting_senders <- t.waiting_senders - 1;
264:     t.cancelled_senders <- t.cancelled_senders + 1;
265:     compact_cancelled_senders_locked t;
266:     pump t)
267: 
268: let cancel_receiver (t : ('a, 'err) t) (receiver : ('a, 'err) receiver) =
269:   match receiver.state with
270:   | Waiting ->
271:       receiver.state <- Cancelled;
272:       t.waiting_receivers <- t.waiting_receivers - 1;
273:       compact_cancelled_receivers_locked t;
274:       pump t
275:   | Delivered value ->
276:       receiver.state <- Cancelled;
277:       release_receiver_delivery t;
278:       return_unclaimed_value t value;
279:       pump t
280:   | Claimed | Cancelled -> ()
281: 
282: let close_locked (t : ('a, 'err) t) reason =
283:   if Option.is_none t.closed then (
284:     t.closed <- Some reason;
285:     let rec close_senders () =
286:       match take_sender t with
287:       | None -> ()
288:       | Some sender ->
289:           resolve_sender sender (close_result reason);
290:           close_senders ()
291:     in
292:     let rec close_receivers () =
293:       match take_receiver t with
294:       | None -> ()
295:       | Some receiver ->
296:           receiver.state <- Cancelled;
297:           resolve_receiver receiver (close_result reason);
298:           close_receivers ()
299:     in
300:     close_senders ();
301:     close_receivers ())
302: 
303: let send_sync contract (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
304:   match
305:     with_lock t @@ fun () ->
306:     match t.closed with
307:     | Some reason -> `Ready (close_result reason)
308:     | None when capacity_used t < t.capacity && t.depth = 0 -> (
309:         match take_receiver t with
310:         | Some receiver ->
311:             t.sent <- t.sent + 1;
312:             deliver_receiver t receiver value;
313:             `Ready `Sent
314:         | None ->
315:             push_counted t value;
316:             `Ready `Sent)
317:     | None when capacity_used t < t.capacity ->
318:         push_counted t value;
319:         `Ready `Sent
320:     | None ->
321:         let promise, sender = enqueue_sender contract t value in
322:         `Wait (promise, sender)
323:   with
324:   | `Ready result -> result
325:   | `Wait (promise, sender) -> (
326:       try contract.Runtime_contract.await_promise promise
327:       with exn when Option.is_some (contract.Runtime_contract.cancellation_reason exn) ->
328:         with_lock_during_cancel contract t (fun () -> cancel_sender t sender);
329:         raise exn)
330: 
331: let recv_sync contract (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
332:   match
333:     with_lock t @@ fun () ->
334:     if t.depth > 0 then (
335:       let value = pop t in
336:       pump t;
337:       `Ready (`Item value))
338:     else
339:       match take_sender t with
340:       | Some sender ->
341:           t.sent <- t.sent + 1;
342:           t.received <- t.received + 1;
343:           resolve_sender sender `Sent;
344:           `Ready (`Item sender.value)
345:       | None -> (
346:           match t.closed with
347:           | Some reason -> `Ready (close_result reason)
348:           | None ->
349:               let promise, receiver = enqueue_receiver contract t in
350:               `Wait (promise, receiver))
351:   with
352:   | `Ready result -> result
353:   | `Wait (promise, receiver) -> (
354:       try
355:         match contract.Runtime_contract.await_promise promise with
356:         | `Item _ as result ->
357:             with_lock t (fun () ->
358:                 claim_receiver t receiver;
359:                 pump t);
360:             result
361:         | (`Empty | `Closed | `Closed_with_error _) as result -> result
362:       with exn when Option.is_some (contract.Runtime_contract.cancellation_reason exn) ->
363:         with_lock_during_cancel contract t (fun () -> cancel_receiver t receiver);
364:         raise exn)
365: 
366: let send t value =
367:   Effect_erasure.effect_to_public
368:     (Effect_core.sync_frame (fun frame ->
369:          send_sync frame.Effect_core.runtime.Runtime_core.contract t value))
370:   |> Effect.bind (function
371:        | `Sent -> Effect.unit
372:        | `Full -> assert false
373:        | `Closed -> Effect.fail `Closed
374:        | `Closed_with_error error -> Effect.fail (`Closed_with_error error))
375: 
376: let recv t =
377:   Effect_erasure.effect_to_public
378:     (Effect_core.sync_frame (fun frame ->
379:          recv_sync frame.Effect_core.runtime.Runtime_core.contract t))
380:   |> Effect.bind (function
381:        | `Item value -> Effect.pure value
382:        | `Empty -> assert false
383:        | `Closed -> Effect.fail `Closed
384:        | `Closed_with_error error -> Effect.fail (`Closed_with_error error))
385: 
386: let try_send (t : ('a, 'err) t) value =
387:   Effect.sync @@ fun () ->
388:   with_lock t @@ fun () ->
389:   match t.closed with
390:   | Some reason -> close_result reason
391:   | None when capacity_used t < t.capacity && t.depth = 0 -> (
392:       match take_receiver t with
393:       | Some receiver ->
394:           t.sent <- t.sent + 1;
395:           deliver_receiver t receiver value;
396:           `Sent
397:       | None ->
398:           push_counted t value;
399:           `Sent)
400:   | None when capacity_used t = t.capacity -> `Full
401:   | None ->
402:       push_counted t value;
403:       `Sent
404: 
405: let try_recv (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
406:   Effect.sync @@ fun () ->
407:   with_lock t @@ fun () ->
408:   if t.depth > 0 then (
409:     let value = pop t in
410:     pump t;
411:     `Item value)
412:   else
413:     match take_sender t with
414:     | Some sender ->
415:         t.sent <- t.sent + 1;
416:         t.received <- t.received + 1;
417:         resolve_sender sender `Sent;
418:         `Item sender.value
419:     | None -> (
420:         match t.closed with
421:         | Some reason -> close_result reason
422:         | None -> `Empty)
423: 
424: let close (t : ('a, 'err) t) = with_lock t @@ fun () -> close_locked t Clean
425: 
426: let close_with_error (t : ('a, 'err) t) error =
427:   with_lock t @@ fun () -> close_locked t (Error error)
428: 
429: let stats (t : ('a, 'err) t) =
430:   Sync_lock.use t.mutex @@ fun () ->
431:   {
432:     depth = t.depth;
433:     sent = t.sent;
434:     received = t.received;
435:     closed = Option.is_some t.closed;
436:     waiting_senders = t.waiting_senders;
437:     waiting_receivers = t.waiting_receivers;
438:     cancelled_senders = t.cancelled_senders;
439:   }
````

## File: lib/eta/duration.ml
````ocaml
 1: type t = { ms : int } [@@unboxed]
 2: 
 3: let zero = { ms = 0 }
 4: let clamp_nonnegative n = if n < 0 then 0 else n
 5: let checked_mul name n factor =
 6:   if n <= 0 then 0
 7:   else if n > max_int / factor then invalid_arg name
 8:   else n * factor
 9: 
10: let checked_add name a b =
11:   if a > max_int - b then invalid_arg name else a + b
12: 
13: let ms n = { ms = clamp_nonnegative n }
14: let seconds n = { ms = checked_mul "Duration.seconds" n 1_000 }
15: let minutes n = { ms = checked_mul "Duration.minutes" n 60_000 }
16: let hours n = { ms = checked_mul "Duration.hours" n 3_600_000 }
17: let days n = { ms = checked_mul "Duration.days" n 86_400_000 }
18: let weeks n = { ms = checked_mul "Duration.weeks" n 604_800_000 }
19: let to_ms t = t.ms
20: let to_seconds_float t = float_of_int t.ms /. 1000.0
21: let is_zero t = t.ms = 0
22: let add a b = { ms = checked_add "Duration.add" a.ms b.ms }
23: let ( + ) a b = add a b
24: let subtract a b = ms (a.ms - b.ms)
25: let times t n =
26:   if n <= 0 then zero
27:   else if t.ms > max_int / n then invalid_arg "Duration.times"
28:   else { ms = t.ms * n }
29: 
30: let divide t by = if by = 0 then None else Some (ms (t.ms / by))
31: let min a b = if a.ms <= b.ms then a else b
32: let max a b = if a.ms >= b.ms then a else b
33: let clamp ~min:min_ ~max:max_ t = t |> max min_ |> min max_
34: let between ~min:min_ ~max:max_ t = min_.ms <= t.ms && t.ms <= max_.ms
35: let compare a b = Int.compare a.ms b.ms
36: let scale t f =
37:   let f = if f < 0.0 then 0.0 else f in
38:   if Float.equal f 1.0 then t
39:   else
40:     let scaled = float_of_int t.ms *. f in
41:     if Float.is_nan scaled || scaled >= float_of_int max_int then
42:       invalid_arg "Duration.scale"
43:     else { ms = clamp_nonnegative (int_of_float scaled) }
44: let pp ppf t = Format.fprintf ppf "%dms" t.ms
45: let equal a b = a.ms = b.ms
````

## File: lib/eta/duration.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Millisecond-precision durations. Same shape as v1 \u2014 pure and small. *)
 2: 
 3: type t
 4: 
 5: val zero : t
 6: val ms : int -> t
 7: val seconds : int -> t
 8: val minutes : int -> t
 9: val hours : int -> t
10: val days : int -> t
11: val weeks : int -> t
12: val to_ms : t -> int
13: val to_seconds_float : t -> float
14: val is_zero : t -> bool
15: val add : t -> t -> t
16: val ( + ) : t -> t -> t
17: val subtract : t -> t -> t
18: val times : t -> int -> t
19: val divide : t -> int -> t option
20: val min : t -> t -> t
21: val max : t -> t -> t
22: val clamp : min:t -> max:t -> t -> t
23: val between : min:t -> max:t -> t -> bool
24: val compare : t -> t -> int
25: val scale : t -> float -> t
26: val pp : Format.formatter -> t -> unit
27: val equal : t -> t -> bool
````

## File: lib/eta/effect_core.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** Core Effect machinery: frame infrastructure, the [('a, 'err) t] type, and
  2:     basic combinators (pure/fail/bind/map/catch/timeout/retry). Internal: see
  3:     Effect for the public surface. *)
  4: 
  5: open Runtime_core
  6: 
  7: module RObs = Runtime_observability
  8: module Sch = Schedule
  9: module P_atomic = Atomic
 10: 
 11: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 12: (* Frame infrastructure                                              *)
 13: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 14: 
 15: type frame = {
 16:   runtime : Obj.t Runtime_core.t;
 17:   error_renderer : (Obj.t -> string);
 18:   fail_key : Runtime_core.Typed_fail.key;
 19:   sw : Runtime_contract.scope;
 20:   finalizers : (unit -> unit) list ref;
 21: }
 22: 
 23: let switch_run frame (f) =
 24:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.run_scope f
 25: 
 26: let switch_fail frame sw exn =
 27:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.fail_scope sw exn
 28: 
 29: let fiber_fork frame ~sw (f) =
 30:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.fork sw f
 31: 
 32: let fiber_fork_daemon frame ~sw f =
 33:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.fork_daemon sw f
 34: 
 35: let fiber_await_cancel frame =
 36:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.await_cancel ()
 37: 
 38: let fiber_yield frame =
 39:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.yield ()
 40: 
 41: let cancel_sub frame (f) =
 42:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.cancel_sub f
 43: 
 44: let cancel_cancel frame cancel_context exn =
 45:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.cancel cancel_context exn
 46: 
 47: let render_error frame err =
 48:   RObs.render_typed_failure ~error_renderer:frame.error_renderer (Obj.repr err)
 49: 
 50: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 51: (* Effect type and basic constructors                                *)
 52: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 53: 
 54: let ok value = Exit.Ok value
 55: let[@cold] [@zero_alloc assume error] error cause = Exit.Error cause
 56: let default_renderer _ = "<typed failure>"
 57: 
 58: type ('a, +'err) t =
 59:   | Pure : 'a -> ('a, 'err) t
 60:   | Fail : 'err -> ('a, 'err) t
 61:   | Custom :
 62:       {
 63:         eval : frame -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t;
 64:         leaf_name : string option;
 65:         names : string list;
 66:       }
 67:       -> ('a, 'err) t
 68:   | Map :
 69:       {
 70:         inner : ('a, 'err) t;
 71:         f : 'a -> 'b;
 72:       }
 73:       -> ('b, 'err) t
 74:   | Bind :
 75:       {
 76:         inner : ('a, 'err) t;
 77:         k : 'a -> ('b, 'err) t;
 78:       }
 79:       -> ('b, 'err) t
 80: 
 81: let rec names : type a err. (a, err) t -> string list = function
 82:   | Pure _ | Fail _ -> []
 83:   | Custom { names; _ } -> names
 84:   | Map { inner; _ } -> names inner
 85:   | Bind { inner; _ } -> names inner
 86: 
 87: let leaf_name : type a err. (a, err) t -> string option = function
 88:   | Custom { leaf_name; _ } -> leaf_name
 89:   | Pure _ | Fail _ | Map _ | Bind _ -> None
 90: 
 91: let make ?leaf_name ?(names = []) eval = Custom { eval; leaf_name; names }
 92: let preserve eff (eval) = make ~names:(names eff) eval
 93: let concat_names effects = List.concat_map names effects
 94: 
 95: let[@inline always] [@zero_alloc opt] exit_to_value frame = function
 96:   | Exit.Ok value -> value
 97:   | Exit.Error cause -> Runtime_core.raise_cause frame.fail_key cause
 98: 
 99: let[@cold] [@zero_alloc assume error] exit_of_exn frame exn =
100:   Exit.Error (Runtime_core.cause_of_exn_runtime frame.runtime frame.fail_key exn)
101: 
102: let rec eval : type a err. frame -> (a, err) t -> (a, err) Exit.t =
103:  fun frame -> function
104:   | Pure value -> ok value
105:   | Fail err -> error (Cause.Fail err)
106:   | Custom { eval; _ } -> eval frame
107:   | Map { inner; f; _ } -> (
108:       match eval frame inner with
109:       | Exit.Ok value -> ok (f value)
110:       | Exit.Error _ as err -> err)
111:   | Bind { inner; k; _ } -> (
112:       match eval frame inner with
113:       | Exit.Ok value -> eval frame (k value)
114:       | Exit.Error _ as err -> err)
115: 
116: let with_names names eff =
117:   match eff with
118:   | Custom custom -> Custom { custom with names }
119:   | Pure _ | Fail _ | Map _ | Bind _ -> make ~names (fun frame -> eval frame eff)
120: 
121: let run_to_exit frame eff =
122:   try eval frame eff with
123:   | exn when Runtime_core.is_cancellation frame.runtime.contract exn -> raise exn
124:   | exn -> exit_of_exn frame exn
125: 
126: let run_to_value frame eff = exit_to_value frame (run_to_exit frame eff)
127: 
128: type internal_cancel = {
129:   interrupt_id : Cause.interrupt_id;
130:   matches_cancel : exn -> bool;
131: }
132: 
133: let interrupt_of_cancel = function
134:   | Some { interrupt_id; matches_cancel } ->
135:       fun reason ->
136:         if matches_cancel reason then Cause.interrupt_with_id interrupt_id
137:         else Cause.interrupt
138:   | None -> fun _ -> Cause.interrupt
139: 
140: let run_scope_body ?sw ?internal_cancel frame (body) =
141:   let finalizers = ref [] in
142:   let sw = Option.value sw ~default:frame.sw in
143:   let child_frame = { frame with sw; finalizers } in
144:   let interrupt_of_cancel = interrupt_of_cancel internal_cancel in
145:   try
146:     ok
147:       (Runtime_core.with_finalizers ~runtime:frame.runtime
148:          ~interrupt_of_cancel
149:          ~fail_key:frame.fail_key
150:          ~error_renderer:child_frame.error_renderer finalizers (fun () ->
151:            body child_frame))
152:   (* Child scopes report cancellation as an Exit so concurrent combinators,
153:      retry/repeat, and supervisors can compose interruption with finalizers
154:      uniformly. Root Runtime.run remains the boundary that re-raises plain
155:      runtime cancellation to callers. *)
156:   with
157:   | exn -> (
158:       match Runtime_core.cancellation_reason frame.runtime.contract exn with
159:       | Some reason -> error (interrupt_of_cancel reason)
160:       | None -> exit_of_exn child_frame exn)
161: 
162: let run_scope ?sw ?internal_cancel frame eff =
163:   run_scope_body ?sw ?internal_cancel frame (fun child_frame ->
164:       run_to_value child_frame eff)
165: 
166: let run_scope_value ?sw frame eff = exit_to_value frame (run_scope ?sw frame eff)
167: 
168: let run_scope_body_value ?sw frame body =
169:   exit_to_value frame (run_scope_body ?sw frame body)
170: 
171: let pure value = Pure value
172: let fail err = Fail err
173: let unit = pure ()
174: let from_result = function Stdlib.Ok value -> pure value | Stdlib.Error err -> fail err
175: 
176: let sync_frame f =
177:   make (fun frame ->
178:       try ok (f frame) with
179:       | exn when Runtime_core.is_cancellation frame.runtime.contract exn ->
180:           raise exn
181:       | exn -> exit_of_exn frame exn)
182: 
183: let sync f = sync_frame (fun _frame -> f ())
184: 
185: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
186: (* Combinators                                                       *)
187: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------- *)
188: 
189: let map (f) eff =
190:   Map { inner = eff; f }
191: 
192: let bind (k) eff =
193:   Bind { inner = eff; k }
194: 
195: let ( >>= ) eff (k) = bind k eff
196: let tap (k) eff = bind (fun value -> map (fun () -> value) (k value)) eff
197: let seq next self = bind (fun () -> next) self
198: 
199: let concat effects =
200:   with_names (concat_names effects)
201:     (List.fold_left (fun acc eff -> seq eff acc) unit effects)
202: 
203: let combine_stripped combine causes =
204:   match List.filter_map Fun.id causes with
205:   | [] -> None
206:   | causes -> Some (combine causes)
207: 
208: let rec stripped_uncatchable : type err mapped. err Cause.t -> mapped Cause.t option =
209:   (* ZIO [catchAll]/[foldZIO] and eff-ts [catch]/[findError] select one
210:      recoverable [Fail]; they do not traverse a composite cause running one
211:      recovery eff per leaf. Eta keeps the additional local invariant that
212:      defects, interruption, and finalizer diagnostics are not caught. If any of
213:      those uncatchable leaves remain, return them without invoking the handler:
214:      handler side effects must not run when the operation is still going to
215:      fail, and old typed failures cannot be preserved across [catch]'s new
216:      error type without running the handler. *)
217:   function
218:   | Cause.Fail _ -> None
219:   | Cause.Die die -> Some (Cause.Die die)
220:   | Cause.Interrupt id -> Some (Cause.Interrupt id)
221:   | Cause.Finalizer cause -> Some (Cause.Finalizer cause)
222:   | Cause.Sequential causes ->
223:       combine_stripped Cause.sequential (List.map stripped_uncatchable causes)
224:   | Cause.Concurrent causes ->
225:       combine_stripped Cause.concurrent (List.map stripped_uncatchable causes)
226:   | Cause.Suppressed { primary; finalizer } -> (
227:       match stripped_uncatchable primary with
228:       | None -> Some (Cause.finalizer finalizer)
229:       | Some primary -> Some (Cause.suppressed ~primary ~finalizer))
230: 
231: let rec first_typed_failure : type err. err Cause.t -> err option = function
232:   | Cause.Fail err -> Some err
233:   | Cause.Sequential causes | Cause.Concurrent causes ->
234:       List.find_map first_typed_failure causes
235:   | Cause.Suppressed { primary; _ } -> first_typed_failure primary
236:   | Cause.Die _ | Cause.Interrupt _ | Cause.Finalizer _ -> None
237: 
238: let catch :
239:     type a err1 err2. (err1 -> (a, err2) t) -> (a, err1) t -> (a, err2) t =
240:  fun (handler) eff ->
241:  match eff with
242:   | Pure value -> Pure value
243:   | _ ->
244:       preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
245:       match eval frame eff with
246:       | Exit.Ok value -> ok value
247:       | Exit.Error cause -> (
248:           match stripped_uncatchable cause with
249:           | Some cause -> error cause
250:           | None -> (
251:               match first_typed_failure cause with
252:               | Some err -> eval frame (handler err)
