````ocaml
253:               | None -> invalid_arg "Effect.catch: empty composite cause"))
254: 
255: let map_cause_error = Cause.map
256: 
257: let render_cause_error frame cause =
258:   Cause.finalizer_of_cause (render_error frame) cause
259: 
260: let finalizer_cause frame cause = Cause.finalizer (render_cause_error frame cause)
261: 
262: let map_error (f) eff =
263:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
264:   match eval frame eff with
265:   | Exit.Ok _ as ok -> ok
266:   | Exit.Error cause -> error (map_cause_error f cause)
267: 
268: let tap_error (observe) eff =
269:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
270:   match eval frame eff with
271:   | Exit.Ok _ as ok -> ok
272:   | Exit.Error (Cause.Fail err) as original -> (
273:       try
274:         observe err;
275:         original
276:       with exn ->
277:         let finalizer =
278:           Runtime_core.cause_of_exn_runtime frame.runtime frame.fail_key exn
279:         in
280:         error
281:           (Cause.suppressed ~primary:(Cause.Fail err)
282:              ~finalizer:(render_cause_error frame finalizer)))
283:   | Exit.Error _ as err -> err
284: 
285: let delay duration eff =
286:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
287:   frame.runtime.sleep duration;
288:   eval frame eff
289: 
290: let timeout_as duration ~on_timeout eff =
291:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
292:   let body_result = ref None in
293:   let timeout_fired = ref false in
294:   let winner = ref None in
295:   let exception Timeout_selected in
296:   let select sw selected =
297:     match !winner with
298:     | Some _ -> ()
299:     | None ->
300:         winner := Some selected;
301:         switch_fail frame sw Timeout_selected;
302:         fiber_await_cancel frame
303:   in
304:   (try
305:      switch_run frame @@ fun timeout_sw ->
306:      fiber_fork frame ~sw:timeout_sw (fun () ->
307:          frame.runtime.sleep duration;
308:          timeout_fired := true;
309:          select timeout_sw `Timeout);
310:      fiber_fork frame ~sw:timeout_sw (fun () ->
311:          let result =
312:            frame.runtime.tracer#with_task_context frame.runtime.contract
313:            @@ fun () ->
314:            run_scope ~sw:timeout_sw frame eff
315:          in
316:          body_result := Some result;
317:          select timeout_sw `Body);
318:      fiber_await_cancel frame
319:    with Timeout_selected -> ());
320:   match (!winner, !timeout_fired, !body_result) with
321:   | Some `Body, _, Some result -> result
322:   | Some `Timeout, true, Some (Exit.Ok _ as result) ->
323:       (* Timeout cancellation waits for body cleanup. If the body reports a
324:          successful result during that required cleanup, it had already
325:          committed a value before the timeout could safely discard it. *)
326:       result
327:   | Some `Timeout, true, Some (Exit.Error cause)
328:     when not (Cause.is_interrupt_only cause) ->
329:       error (Cause.concurrent [ Cause.Fail on_timeout; cause ])
330:   | Some `Timeout, _, _ -> error (Cause.Fail on_timeout)
331:   | None, true, _ -> error (Cause.Fail on_timeout)
332:   | None, false, Some result -> result
333:   | None, false, None -> error Cause.interrupt
334:   | Some `Body, _, None -> error Cause.interrupt
335: 
336: let timeout duration eff = timeout_as duration ~on_timeout:`Timeout eff
337: 
338: let uninterruptible eff =
339:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
340:   frame.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.protect (fun () -> eval frame eff)
341: 
342: let repeat schedule eff =
343:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
344:   let run_iteration () =
345:     run_scope_value frame eff
346:   in
347:   try
348:     run_iteration ();
349:     let driver = ref (Sch.start ~random:frame.runtime.random schedule) in
350:     let continue = ref true in
351:     while !continue do
352:       match Sch.next !driver with
353:       | None -> continue := false
354:       | Some (duration, next_driver) ->
355:           driver := next_driver;
356:           frame.runtime.sleep duration;
357:           run_iteration ()
358:     done;
359:     ok ()
360:   with exn -> exit_of_exn frame exn
361: 
362: let retry schedule (predicate) eff =
363:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
364:   let driver = ref (Sch.start ~random:frame.runtime.random schedule) in
365:   let run_attempt () = run_scope frame eff in
366:   let rec loop () =
367:     match run_attempt () with
368:     | Exit.Ok _ as ok -> ok
369:     | Exit.Error (Cause.Fail err) when predicate err -> (
370:         match Sch.next !driver with
371:         | Some (duration, next_driver) ->
372:             driver := next_driver;
373:             frame.runtime.sleep duration;
374:             loop ()
375:         | None -> error (Cause.Fail err))
376:     | Exit.Error _ as err -> err
377:   in
378:   loop ()
379: 
380: let name eff = leaf_name eff
381: let collect_names eff = names eff
````

## File: lib/eta/effect_resource.ml
````ocaml
 1: (** Effect lifecycle: finalizers, [acquire_release], [scoped]. Internal: see
 2:     Effect for the public surface. *)
 3: 
 4: open Effect_core
 5: 
 6: let finally cleanup eff =
 7:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
 8:   try
 9:     let finalizers = ref [ (fun () -> run_scope_value frame cleanup) ] in
10:     ok
11:       (Runtime_core.with_finalizers ~runtime:frame.runtime
12:          ~fail_key:frame.fail_key ~error_renderer:frame.error_renderer finalizers
13:          (fun () -> run_to_value frame eff))
14:   with
15:   | exn when Runtime_core.is_cancellation frame.runtime.contract exn -> raise exn
16:   | exn -> exit_of_exn frame exn
17: 
18: let acquire_release ~acquire ~(release) =
19:   preserve acquire @@ fun frame ->
20:   match eval frame acquire with
21:   | Exit.Error _ as err -> err
22:   | Exit.Ok value ->
23:       frame.finalizers :=
24:         (fun () ->
25:           run_scope_value frame (release value))
26:         :: !(frame.finalizers);
27:       ok value
28: 
29: let scoped eff =
30:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
31:   try
32:     ok
33:       (let run_scoped sw =
34:          run_scope_value ~sw frame eff
35:        in
36:     switch_run frame run_scoped)
37:   with exn -> exit_of_exn frame exn
38: 
39: let acquire_use_release ~acquire ~(release) (body) =
40:   scoped (acquire_release ~acquire ~release |> bind body)
````

## File: lib/eta/effect.ml
````ocaml
 1: (** Public Effect implementation. *)
 2: 
 3: open Effect_core
 4: 
 5: include Effect_core
 6: include Effect_resource
 7: include Effect_concurrent
 8: include Effect_observability
 9: include Effect_supervisor_scope
10: 
11: let daemon_internal eff =
12:   preserve eff @@ fun frame ->
13:   Runtime_core.incr_active frame.runtime;
14:   fiber_fork_daemon frame ~sw:frame.runtime.outer_scope (fun () ->
15:       frame.runtime.tracer#with_task_context frame.runtime.contract @@ fun () ->
16:       Fun.protect
17:         ~finally:(fun () -> Runtime_core.decr_active frame.runtime)
18:         (fun () ->
19:           (try
20:              switch_run frame @@ fun sw ->
21:              let finalizers = ref [] in
22:              (* Daemons report failures after their caller has returned, so they
23:                 use the runtime's daemon fail key and opaque typed-failure
24:                 renderer instead of inheriting a caller-specific renderer whose
25:                 typed error scope may no longer be meaningful. *)
26:              let child_frame =
27:                { frame with sw; finalizers; error_renderer = default_renderer }
28:              in
29:              Runtime_core.with_finalizers ~runtime:frame.runtime
30:                ~fail_key:frame.runtime.default_fail_key
31:                ~error_renderer:child_frame.error_renderer finalizers (fun () ->
32:                  run_to_value child_frame eff)
33:            with exn ->
34:              Runtime_core.cause_of_exn_runtime frame.runtime
35:                frame.runtime.default_fail_key exn
36:              |> Runtime_core.emit_daemon_failure frame.runtime);
37:           `Stop_daemon));
38:   ok ()
39: 
40: let daemon = daemon_internal
41: 
42: module Expert = struct
43:   type context = Effect_core.frame
44: 
45:   let make ?leaf_name ?names f = Effect_core.make ?leaf_name ?names f
46:   let contract context = context.runtime.Runtime_core.contract
47:   let current_scope context = context.sw
48:   let outer_scope context = context.runtime.Runtime_core.outer_scope
49:   let runtime_service context key = Runtime_core.service context.runtime key
50:   let auto_instrument context = context.runtime.Runtime_core.auto_instrument
51: 
52:   let instrument_leaf context ~name f =
53:     Runtime_instrument.instrument_leaf ~runtime:context.runtime
54:       ~error_renderer:context.error_renderer ~fail_key:context.fail_key ~name f
55: 
56:   let emit_trace_event context ~name ~attrs =
57:     let runtime = context.runtime in
58:     if runtime.Runtime_core.tracing_enabled then
59:       match
60:         runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.local_get
61:           Runtime_observability.active_span_key
62:       with
63:       | None -> ()
64:       | Some span_id ->
65:           runtime.tracer#add_event runtime.contract ~span_id ~name
66:             ~ts_ms:(runtime.now_ms ()) ~attrs
67: 
68:   let record_metric context ~name ~description ~unit_ ~kind ~attrs ~value =
69:     let runtime = context.runtime in
70:     if runtime.Runtime_core.metrics_enabled then
71:       runtime.meter#record ~name ~description ~unit_ ~kind ~attrs ~value
72:         ~ts_ms:(runtime.now_ms ())
73: 
74:   let fork_daemon context f =
75:     Runtime_core.incr_active context.runtime;
76:     context.runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.fork_daemon
77:       context.runtime.outer_scope (fun () ->
78:           context.runtime.tracer#with_task_context context.runtime.contract
79:           @@ fun () ->
80:           Fun.protect
81:             ~finally:(fun () -> Runtime_core.decr_active context.runtime)
82:             f)
83: 
84:   let eval context eff = Effect_core.eval context eff
85:   let eval_in_scope context sw eff = Effect_core.run_scope ~sw context eff
86:   let exit_of_exn context exn = Effect_core.exit_of_exn context exn
87: end
````

## File: lib/eta/effect.mli
````ocaml
  1: (** Lazy, runtime-interpreted effects.
  2: 
  3:     {v
  4:       ('a, 'err) Effect.t
  5:        ^^   ^^^
  6:        ok   error
  7:     v}
  8: 
  9:     - ['a] is the success value.
 10:     - ['err] is the typed failure channel. Polymorphic variants work well:
 11:       [[> `Http_404 | `Db_unavailable ]].
 12: 
 13:     Dependencies are ordinary OCaml values: pass records, modules, closures, or
 14:     concrete handles into functions that construct effects. Eta does not own a
 15:     ZIO-style environment or layer graph.
 16: 
 17:     This signature is intentionally a facade over Eta's eff algebra,
 18:     structured concurrency, and observability hooks. Keep
 19:     implementation-only representation details out of this file:
 20:     if a helper is needed only by Runtime or private modules, put it behind a
 21:     private module such as Runtime_erasure instead of widening Effect. *)
 22: 
 23: type ('a, 'err) t
 24: 
 25: type ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope
 26: 
 27: type ('a, 'err) supervisor_body = {
 28:   run : 's. ('s, 'err) supervisor -> ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope;
 29: }
 30: 
 31: and ('s, !'err) supervisor
 32: and ('s, !'err, !'a) supervisor_child
 33: 
 34: val pure : 'a -> ('a, 'err) t
 35: val fail : 'err -> ('a, 'err) t
 36: val unit : (unit, 'err) t
 37: val from_result : ('a, 'err) result -> ('a, 'err) t
 38: 
 39: val sync : (unit -> 'a) -> ('a, 'err) t
 40: (** [sync f] lifts an OCaml function into an eff. Use {!Effect.named} to
 41:     attach a span name for tracing.
 42: 
 43:     Ordinary OCaml exceptions raised by [f] are unchecked defects and surface
 44:     as {!Cause.Die}. They are not converted into the typed error channel and
 45:     are not caught by {!catch}. Return an explicit [result] and use
 46:     {!from_result} when a leaf operation has an expected typed failure.
 47:     Runtime cancellation exceptions remain interruption. *)
 48: 
 49: val map : ('a -> 'b) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('b, 'err) t
 50: val bind : ('a -> ('b, 'err) t) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('b, 'err) t
 51: val ( >>= ) : ('a, 'err) t -> ('a -> ('b, 'err) t) -> ('b, 'err) t
 52: 
 53: val tap : ('a -> (unit, 'err) t) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
 54: 
 55: val seq : (unit, 'err) t -> (unit, 'err) t -> (unit, 'err) t
 56: val concat : (unit, 'err) t list -> (unit, 'err) t
 57: 
 58: val race : ('a, 'err) t list -> ('a, 'err) t
 59: (** First child to produce a value wins; the rest are cancelled.
 60: 
 61:     Losers' values are discarded by design. Resource lifetime is owned by
 62:     scopes, not by race: a loser that holds its resource under
 63:     {!acquire_release} / {!Semaphore.with_permits} has it released when it is
 64:     cancelled, even if it ran to completion before losing. An acquisition whose
 65:     ownership is carried through a value can be discarded by race; use
 66:     {!Semaphore.with_permits_or_abort} when racing permit acquisition against an
 67:     abort signal. *)
 68: 
 69: val par : ('a, 'err) t -> ('b, 'err) t -> ('a * 'b, 'err) t
 70: (** Run two effects concurrently; collect both successes as a pair.
 71:     Fail-fast: the first child failure cancels the sibling and the
 72:     cause propagates upward.
 73: 
 74:     This is eff concurrency on the current runtime substrate, not CPU
 75:     parallelism. Use the optional [eta_par] package for worker-domain offload
 76:     or explicit fork-join parallel algorithms. *)
 77: 
 78: val all : ('a, 'err) t list -> ('a list, 'err) t
 79: (** Run effects concurrently, collecting results in input order.
 80:     Fail-fast: the first child failure cancels the others; the cause
 81:     of the first observed failure propagates. *)
 82: 
 83: val all_settled :
 84:   ('a, 'err) t list -> (('a, 'err Cause.t) result list, 'outer_err) t
 85: (** Run effects concurrently and collect every child outcome in input order.
 86:     Child failures are returned as [Error cause] values instead of failing the
 87:     outer eff. *)
 88: 
 89: val for_each_par : 'x list -> ('x -> ('a, 'err) t) -> ('a list, 'err) t
 90: (** Map over [xs] concurrently with [f]; collect results in input order.
 91:     Fail-fast like {!all}.
 92: 
 93:     This runs child effects as concurrent fibers on the current runtime
 94:     substrate. It does not move arbitrary effects to worker domains; use the
 95:     optional [eta_par] package for CPU-bound batch work. *)
 96: 
 97: val for_each_par_bounded :
 98:   max:int -> 'x list -> ('x -> ('a, 'err) t) -> ('a list, 'err) t
 99: (** Map over [xs] with at most [max] child effects running at once. Results
100:     are returned in input order and failures are fail-fast like {!for_each_par}.
101:     The bound limits concurrent fibers, not domain workers.
102:     @raise Invalid_argument if [max <= 0]. *)
103: 
104: val uninterruptible : ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
105: (** Defer parent cancellation while running the wrapped eff.
106: 
107:     This maps to backend cancellation protection. It does not turn
108:     interruption into a typed failure, and it does not catch defects. *)
109: 
110: val catch :
111:   ('err1 -> ('a, 'err2) t) -> ('a, 'err1) t -> ('a, 'err2) t
112: (** Handle typed failures in an eff's cause tree.
113: 
114:     [catch handler eff] does not catch unchecked defects, interruption, or
115:     cleanup/finalizer failures. This matches the ordinary typed-error recovery
116:     shape in ZIO [catchAll]/[foldZIO] and eff-ts [catch]/[findError]: one
117:     recovery decision is made from the cause, rather than traversing every
118:     [Fail] leaf and running one handler per branch.
119: 
120:     If any uncatchable defect, interruption, or finalizer diagnostic remains in
121:     the cause tree, the handler is not invoked and the eff stays failed with
122:     those uncatchable diagnostics. This avoids running recovery side effects for
123:     an operation that still fails, and avoids preserving old typed [Fail]
124:     payloads after [catch] changes the error type.
125: 
126:     If only typed failures remain, [catch] invokes the handler once with the
127:     first typed failure in cause order. Other concurrent/sequential typed
128:     failures are not recoverable values; use [all_settled] or explicit result
129:     values when every branch outcome matters. Recover or ignore cleanup failure
130:     inside the cleanup eff itself. *)
131: 
132: val map_error : ('err1 -> 'err2) -> ('a, 'err1) t -> ('a, 'err2) t
133: (** Transform typed failures while preserving unchecked defects, interruption,
134:     and the surrounding cause structure. [Cause.Fail] values in the primary
135:     cause tree are mapped, including failures nested under [Sequential] and
136:     [Concurrent]. Cleanup/finalizer failures are already rendered into
137:     {!Cause.Finalizer} nodes and are preserved unchanged, including
138:     [Cause.Suppressed.finalizer] branches. *)
139: 
140: val tap_error : ('err -> unit) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
141: (** Run an observer when the eff fails with a typed error, then rethrow the
142:     original typed failure. If the observer raises, the runtime reports a
143:     suppressed cause with the original [Cause.Fail] as [primary] and the
144:     observer exception as [finalizer], so diagnostics retain both failures. *)
145: 
146: val retry : Schedule.t -> ('err -> bool) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
147: 
148: val delay : Duration.t -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
149: val timeout : Duration.t -> ('a, [> `Timeout ] as 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
150: val timeout_as :
151:   Duration.t -> on_timeout:'err -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
152: (** Like {!timeout}, but fails with [on_timeout] instead of widening the error
153:     row with raw Timeout. *)
154: val repeat : Schedule.t -> (unit, 'err) t -> (unit, 'err) t
155: 
156: val finally : (unit, 'cleanup_err) t -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
157: (** [finally cleanup eff] runs [cleanup] after [eff] settles, on success,
158:     typed failure, unchecked defect, or cancellation.
159: 
160:     [cleanup] runs in a cancellation-protected cleanup frame. If [eff]
161:     succeeds but [cleanup] fails, the cleanup failure is reported as
162:     [Cause.Finalizer]. If both fail, the cleanup failure is reported as a
163:     suppressed finalizer failure under the primary cause, matching
164:     {!acquire_release} finalizer reporting.
165: 
166:     This is for one-shot cleanup around an eff. Use {!acquire_release} and
167:     {!scoped} for resource lifetimes. *)
168: 
169: val acquire_release :
170:   acquire:('a, 'err) t ->
171:   release:('a -> (unit, 'release_err) t) ->
172:   ('a, 'err) t
173: (** Acquire a resource and register [release] to run when the current runtime
174:     boundary, scope, supervisor scope, or daemon body exits. The release eff
175:     runs on success and on typed failure; release failures are reported as
176:     [Cause.Finalizer] after a successful body or suppressed onto the primary
177:     failure after a failed body. *)
178: 
179: val acquire_use_release :
180:   acquire:('a, 'err) t ->
181:   release:('a -> (unit, 'release_err) t) ->
182:   ('a -> ('b, 'err) t) ->
183:   ('b, 'err) t
184: (** Acquire a resource, run [body], and release it when [body] finishes.
185: 
186:     This is a lexical bracket. Unlike {!acquire_release}, it opens a local scope
187:     around [body], so repeated [acquire_use_release] calls do not retain
188:     resources until the surrounding runtime boundary exits. Release ordering,
189:     cancellation protection, and suppressed finalizer failure reporting match
190:     scoped {!acquire_release}. *)
191: 
192: val scoped : ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
193: 
194: val with_background :
195:   ?name:string -> (unit, 'err) t -> (unit -> ('a, 'err) t) -> ('a, 'err) t
196: (** Run [background] while [use] executes, then cancel the background child when
197:     [use] returns or fails. This is the structured replacement for
198:     daemon-shaped application work that does not need to expose a child handle. *)
199: 
200: val daemon : (unit, 'err) t -> (unit, 'err) t
201: (** Start runtime-owned finite background work on the runtime's outer switch.
202: 
203:     Daemons are for Eta modules that own a lifecycle beyond the caller's local
204:     scope, such as pool eviction loops and protocol readers. Application code
205:     should prefer {!with_background} when the work belongs to one request,
206:     server, stream, or resource scope.
207: 
208:     Failures bypass the typed result and are reported as runtime daemon
209:     diagnostics. Use {!Runtime.drain} to wait for currently running finite
210:     daemon work before process shutdown or tests that assert daemon effects. *)
211: 
212: val supervisor_scoped :
213:   ?max_failures:int -> ('a, 'err) supervisor_body -> ('a, 'err) t
214: (** Low-level abstract supervisor-scope runner used by {!Supervisor}. Prefer
215:     {!Supervisor.scoped} and {!Supervisor.Scope} in user code. *)
216: 
217: val with_error_renderer : ('err -> string) -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
218: (** Render typed failures in observability span status and exception events for
219:     the wrapped eff. The renderer is scoped to this eff's error channel. *)
220: 
221: val suppress_observability : ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
222: (** Run the wrapped eff without emitting tracer, logger, or meter events
223:     from inside the subtree.
224: 
225:     This is intended for observability exporters and other observer backends
226:     that must call Eta-based libraries without recursively observing their own
227:     export path. It does not change typed errors, resource finalization, or
228:     defect diagnostics. *)
229: 
230: val supervisor_pure : 'a -> ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope
231: val supervisor_lift : ('a, 'err) t -> ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope
232: val supervisor_fail : 'err -> ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope
233: 
234: val supervisor_bind :
235:   ('a -> ('s, 'b, 'err) supervisor_scope) ->
236:   ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope ->
237:   ('s, 'b, 'err) supervisor_scope
238: 
239: val supervisor_start :
240:   ('s, 'err) supervisor ->
241:   ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope ->
242:   ('s, ('s, 'err, 'a) supervisor_child, 'outer_err) supervisor_scope
243: 
244: val supervisor_await :
245:   ('s, 'err, 'a) supervisor_child -> ('s, 'a, 'err) supervisor_scope
246: 
247: val supervisor_cancel :
248:   ('s, 'err, 'a) supervisor_child -> ('s, unit, 'err) supervisor_scope
249: 
250: val supervisor_failures :
251:   ('s, 'err) supervisor -> ('s, 'err Cause.t list, 'outer_err) supervisor_scope
252: 
253: val supervisor_check :
254:   ('s, [> `Supervisor_failed of int ] as 'err) supervisor ->
255:   ('s, unit, 'err) supervisor_scope
256: 
257: val supervisor_yield : ('s, unit, 'err) supervisor_scope
258: (** Low-level abstract supervisor-scope builders used by {!Supervisor.Scope}.
259:     They intentionally do not expose the interpreter AST constructors. *)
260: 
261: val named :
262:   ?error_renderer:('err -> string) ->
263:   string ->
264:   ('a, 'err) t ->
265:   ('a, 'err) t
266: (** [named name body] attaches a span name for tracing around [body]. *)
267: 
268: module Expert : sig
269:   type context
270: 
271:   val make :
272:     ?leaf_name:string ->
273:     ?names:string list ->
274:     (context -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t) ->
275:     ('a, 'err) t
276:   (** Build a runtime-backed effect without exposing Eta's internal effect
277:       representation. Runtime-specific packages use this to attach operations
278:       to the current {!Runtime_contract.t}; ordinary user code should prefer
279:       the typed combinators in this module. *)
280: 
281:   val contract : context -> Runtime_contract.t
282:   (** Runtime contract selected by the current interpreter. *)
283: 
284:   val current_scope : context -> Runtime_contract.scope
285:   (** Current lexical runtime scope. *)
286: 
287:   val outer_scope : context -> Runtime_contract.scope
288:   (** Runtime boundary scope used for runtime-owned background work. *)
289: 
290:   val runtime_service : context -> 'a Runtime_contract.service_key -> 'a option
291:   (** Runtime-package service attached when the interpreter was created. *)
292: 
293:   val auto_instrument : context -> bool
294:   (** Whether runtime leaf auto-instrumentation is enabled. *)
295: 
296:   val instrument_leaf : context -> name:string -> (unit -> 'a) -> 'a
297:   (** Run a leaf body under Eta's standard runtime instrumentation. *)
298: 
299:   val emit_trace_event :
300:     context -> name:string -> attrs:(string * string) list -> unit
301:   (** Emit an event on the active span, if tracing is enabled and sampled. *)
302: 
303:   val record_metric :
304:     context ->
305:     name:string ->
306:     description:string ->
307:     unit_:string ->
308:     kind:Capabilities.metric_kind ->
309:     attrs:(string * string) list ->
310:     value:Capabilities.metric_value ->
311:     unit
312:   (** Record a metric point when runtime metrics are enabled. *)
313: 
314:   val fork_daemon : context -> (unit -> [ `Stop_daemon ]) -> unit
315:   (** Fork runtime-owned finite background work and include it in
316:       {!Runtime.drain} accounting. *)
317: 
318:   val eval : context -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t
319:   (** Evaluate a child effect in the current runtime context. *)
320: 
321:   val eval_in_scope :
322:     context -> Runtime_contract.scope -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t
323:   (** Evaluate a child effect in an explicit runtime scope. *)
324: 
325:   val exit_of_exn : context -> exn -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t
326:   (** Convert an unchecked exception raised by a custom operation into Eta's
327:       diagnostic cause using the current runtime settings. *)
328: end
329: (** Narrow extension point for runtime packages. This module is intentionally
330:     small: it lets optional packages implement backend-specific leaves while
331:     keeping the root [Effect.t] representation private. *)
332: 
333: val named_kind :
334:   ?error_renderer:('err -> string) ->
335:   kind:Capabilities.span_kind ->
336:   string ->
337:   ('a, 'err) t ->
338:   ('a, 'err) t
339: val annotate : key:string -> value:string -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
340: (** Attach a string attribute to the active span. If no span is active, the
341:     attribute is buffered and attached to the next span opened by the same
342:     fiber. The same annotation is also included in defect diagnostics produced
343:     by the wrapped eff. *)
344: 
345: val annotate_all : (string * string) list -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
346: (** Attach several span attributes with the same semantics as {!annotate}. The
347:     list order is preserved. *)
348: 
349: val event : ?attrs:(string * string) list -> string -> (unit, 'err) t
350: (** Add an event to the currently active span. If tracing is disabled or no span
351:     is active, this is a no-op. Use this for structured progress markers inside
352:     a span; use {!log} for log records and {!metric_update} for metrics. *)
353: 
354: val with_result_attrs :
355:   ok_attrs:('a -> (string * string) list) ->
356:   err_attrs:('err -> (string * string) list) ->
357:   ('a, 'err) t ->
358:   ('a, 'err) t
359: (** Attach attributes derived from the eff outcome to the active span and
360:     preserve the original result.
361: 
362:     [ok_attrs] is evaluated after success. [err_attrs] is evaluated for every
363:     typed [Cause.Fail] in the failure cause. Defects and interruption are not
364:     passed to [err_attrs].
365: 
366:     The attributes are recorded only when a span is active at the point the
367:     wrapped eff settles. Put this combinator inside {!named} or {!fn} when
368:     the attributes should land on that span:
369: 
370:     {[
371:       Effect.named "load.rows"
372:         (Effect.with_result_attrs
373:            ~ok_attrs:(fun rows -> [ ("row_count", string_of_int (List.length rows)) ])
374:            ~err_attrs:(fun `Db_error -> [ ("result", "db_error") ])
375:            load_rows)
376:     ]} *)
377: 
378: val link_span :
379:   ?attrs:(string * string) list ->
380:   trace_id:string ->
381:   span_id:string ->
382:   ('a, 'err) t ->
383:   ('a, 'err) t
384: (** Attach a {!Capabilities.span_link} to the span opened by [body]. If [body]
385:     has no enclosing {!named} span, the link buffers and attaches to the next
386:     one (mirrors the buffered-attribute semantics). *)
387: 
388: val with_external_parent :
389:   trace_id:string -> span_id:string -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
390: (** Compatibility wrapper for {!with_context} when only a trace ID and parent
391:     span ID are available. New boundary code should prefer {!Trace_context.extract}
392:     plus {!with_context} so trace flags, tracestate, and baggage are preserved. *)
393: 
394: val with_context :
395:   Capabilities.trace_context -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
396: (** Run [body] with an inbound or otherwise external trace context. The next
397:     opened {!named} span uses this context as parent, parent-based sampling sees
398:     its sampled flag, and baggage/tracestate remain visible through
399:     {!current_context}. *)
400: 
401: val current_span : (Capabilities.span_info option, 'err) t
402: (** Yield the {!Capabilities.span_info} of the currently active span on this
403:     fiber, or [None] if none is open. *)
404: 
405: val current_context : (Capabilities.trace_context option, 'err) t
406: (** Yield the current propagation context. When a span is active this is that
407:     span's context; otherwise it is the ambient context installed by
408:     {!with_context}, if any. *)
409: 
410: val log :
411:   ?level:Capabilities.log_level ->
412:   ?attrs:(string * string) list ->
413:   string ->
414:   (unit, 'err) t
415: (** Emit a structured log record to the runtime's logger. The runtime
416:     automatically populates the record's [trace_id]/[span_id] from the
417:     active span and [ts_ms] from the runtime's clock. *)
418: 
419: val metric_update :
420:   ?description:string ->
421:   ?unit_:string ->
422:   ?attrs:(string * string) list ->
423:   name:string ->
424:   kind:Capabilities.metric_kind ->
425:   Capabilities.metric_value ->
426:   (unit, 'err) t
427: (** Records a runtime observation, not part of the eff's success value or
428:     typed error channel. Runtimes without a meter may ignore it. *)
429: 
430: val here_attr : string * int * int * int -> ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) t
431: (** Intended for wrappers that pass [__POS__] through unchanged; synthesized
432:     locations make traces harder to correlate with source. *)
433: 
434: val fn :
435:   ?kind:Capabilities.span_kind ->
436:   ?error_renderer:('err -> string) ->
437:   ?attrs:(string * string) list ->
438:   string * int * int * int ->
439:   string ->
440:   ('a, 'err) t ->
441:   ('a, 'err) t
442: (** [fn __POS__ __FUNCTION__ body] names [body] after the current binding and
443:     records the source location as a [loc] span attribute. [?attrs] attaches
444:     additional attributes to the same span. *)
445: 
446: val name : ('a, 'err) t -> string option
447: val collect_names : ('a, 'err) t -> string list
448: (** [collect_names eff] returns names that are statically present in
449:     [eff]'s current description.
450: 
451:     This is a preflight/documentation helper, not a complete runtime inventory.
452:     Continuation-producing nodes such as [bind], [catch], [for_each_par],
453:     [for_each_par_bounded], and [supervisor_scoped] are not forced or traversed,
454:     so names created by those continuations are intentionally absent. *)
````

## File: lib/eta/exit.ml
````ocaml
 1: type ('a, 'err) t =
 2:   | Ok of 'a
 3:   | Error of 'err Cause.t
 4: 
 5: let ok value = Ok value
 6: let error cause = Error cause
 7: 
 8: let to_result = function
 9:   | Ok value -> Some (Stdlib.Ok value)
10:   | Error (Cause.Fail err) -> Some (Stdlib.Error err)
11:   | Error
12:       ( Cause.Die _ | Interrupt _ | Sequential _ | Concurrent _ | Finalizer _
13:       | Suppressed _ )
14:     ->
15:       None
16: 
17: let equal equal_a equal_err left right =
18:   match (left, right) with
19:   | Ok a, Ok b -> equal_a a b
20:   | Error a, Error b -> Cause.equal equal_err a b
21:   | _ -> false
22: 
23: let pp pp_a pp_err fmt = function
24:   | Ok value -> Format.fprintf fmt "Ok(%a)" pp_a value
25:   | Error cause -> Format.fprintf fmt "Error(%a)" (Cause.pp pp_err) cause
````

## File: lib/eta/exit.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Runtime result of an eff. *)
 2: 
 3: type ('a, 'err) t =
 4:   | Ok of 'a
 5:   | Error of 'err Cause.t
 6: 
 7: val ok : 'a -> ('a, 'err) t
 8: val error : 'err Cause.t -> ('a, 'err) t
 9: 
10: val to_result : ('a, 'err) t -> ('a, 'err) result option
11: (** Convert to OCaml [result] only when the exit is [Ok _] or a single
12:     typed [Fail _]. [Die], [Interrupt], [Sequential], [Concurrent], and
13:     [Suppressed] have no faithful [result] representation. *)
14: 
15: val equal :
16:   ('a -> 'a -> bool) ->
17:   ('err -> 'err -> bool) ->
18:   ('a, 'err) t ->
19:   ('a, 'err) t ->
20:   bool
21: 
22: val pp :
23:   (Format.formatter -> 'a -> unit) ->
24:   (Format.formatter -> 'err -> unit) ->
25:   Format.formatter ->
26:   ('a, 'err) t ->
27:   unit
````

## File: lib/eta/resource.ml
````ocaml
  1: type ('a, 'err) t = {
  2:   load : ('a, 'err) Effect.t;
  3:   mutex : Sync_lock.t;
  4:   mutable value : 'a option;
  5:   mutable next_version : int;
  6:   mutable published_version : int;
  7:   mutable failures : 'err Cause.t list;
  8: }
  9: 
 10: let with_lock resource (f) =
 11:   Sync_lock.use resource.mutex f
 12: 
 13: let loaded load value =
 14:   {
 15:     load;
 16:     mutex = Sync_lock.create ();
 17:     value = Some value;
 18:     next_version = 0;
 19:     published_version = 0;
 20:     failures = [];
 21:   }
 22: 
 23: let begin_load resource =
 24:   Effect.sync (fun () ->
 25:       with_lock resource @@ fun () ->
 26:       resource.next_version <- resource.next_version + 1;
 27:       resource.next_version)
 28: 
 29: let publish resource version value =
 30:   Effect.sync (fun () ->
 31:       with_lock resource @@ fun () ->
 32:       if version >= resource.published_version then (
 33:         resource.value <- Some value;
 34:         resource.published_version <- version))
 35: 
 36: let refresh resource =
 37:   begin_load resource
 38:   |> Effect.bind (fun version ->
 39:          resource.load
 40:          |> Effect.bind (fun value -> publish resource version value))
 41: 
 42: let get resource =
 43:   Effect.sync (fun () -> with_lock resource @@ fun () -> resource.value)
 44:   |> Effect.bind (function
 45:        | Some value -> Effect.pure value
 46:        | None ->
 47:            begin_load resource
 48:            |> Effect.bind (fun version ->
 49:                   resource.load
 50:                   |> Effect.bind (fun value ->
 51:                          publish resource version value
 52:                          |> Effect.map (fun () -> value))))
 53: 
 54: let manual load =
 55:   load |> Effect.map (loaded load)
 56: 
 57: let failures resource =
 58:   Effect.named "resource.failures"
 59:     (Effect.sync (fun () ->
 60:          with_lock resource @@ fun () -> List.rev resource.failures))
 61: 
 62: let auto ?(on_error) ~load ?random ~schedule () =
 63:   let add_failure resource cause =
 64:     Effect.sync (fun () ->
 65:         with_lock resource @@ fun () ->
 66:         resource.failures <- cause :: resource.failures)
 67:   in
 68:   let record_failure resource cause =
 69:     Effect.named "resource.auto.refresh_failed"
 70:       (add_failure resource cause
 71:       |> Effect.bind (fun () ->
 72:              match (cause, on_error) with
 73:              | Cause.Fail err, Some f ->
 74:                  Effect.sync (fun () ->
 75:                      try f err; None
 76:                      with exn ->
 77:                        Some
 78:                          (Cause.die_with_backtrace exn
 79:                             (Printexc.get_raw_backtrace ())))
 80:                  |> Effect.bind (function
 81:                       | None -> Effect.unit
 82:                       | Some defect -> add_failure resource defect)
 83:              | _ -> Effect.unit))
 84:   in
 85:   let rec refresh_loop resource driver =
 86:     match Schedule.next driver with
 87:     | None -> Effect.unit
 88:     | Some (delay, driver') ->
 89:         let refresh_once =
 90:           Effect.all_settled [ refresh resource ]
 91:           |> Effect.bind (function
 92:                | [ Ok () ] -> Effect.unit
 93:                | [ Error cause ] -> record_failure resource cause
 94:                | results ->
 95:                    Effect.sync (fun () ->
 96:                        invalid_arg
 97:                          ("Eta.Resource.auto: expected one refresh result, got "
 98:                          ^ string_of_int (List.length results))))
 99:         in
100:         refresh_once
101:         |> Effect.delay delay
102:         |> Effect.bind (fun () -> refresh_loop resource driver')
103:   in
104:   load
105:   |> Effect.map (loaded load)
106:   |> Effect.bind (fun resource ->
107:          let driver = Schedule.start ?random schedule in
108:          Effect.daemon (refresh_loop resource driver)
109:          |> Effect.map (fun () -> resource))
````

## File: lib/eta/schedule.ml
````ocaml
  1: type t =
  2:   | Recurs of int
  3:   | Forever
  4:   | Spaced of Duration.t
  5:   | Fixed of Duration.t
  6:   | Exponential of Duration.t * float
  7:   | Linear of { initial : Duration.t; step : Duration.t }
  8:   | Both of t * t
  9:   | Either of t * t
 10:   | And_then of t * t
 11:   | Jittered of t * float * float
 12:   | Named of t * string
 13: 
 14: let recurs n = Recurs (max 0 n)
 15: let forever = Forever
 16: let spaced d = Spaced d
 17: let fixed d = Fixed d
 18: let exponential ?(factor = 2.0) initial = Exponential (initial, factor)
 19: let linear ~initial ~step = Linear { initial; step }
 20: let both a b = Both (a, b)
 21: let either a b = Either (a, b)
 22: let and_then a b = And_then (a, b)
 23: let jittered ?(min = 0.8) ?(max = 1.2) self =
 24:   let lo = if min < 0.0 then 0.0 else min in
 25:   let hi = if max < lo then lo else max in
 26:   Jittered (self, lo, hi)
 27: let named name s = Named (s, name)
 28: 
 29: let rec pp ppf = function
 30:   | Recurs n -> Format.fprintf ppf "Recurs(%d)" n
 31:   | Forever -> Format.fprintf ppf "Forever"
 32:   | Spaced d -> Format.fprintf ppf "Spaced(%a)" Duration.pp d
 33:   | Fixed d -> Format.fprintf ppf "Fixed(%a)" Duration.pp d
 34:   | Exponential (d, f) -> Format.fprintf ppf "Exponential(%a, %g)" Duration.pp d f
 35:   | Linear { initial; step } ->
 36:       Format.fprintf ppf "Linear(%a, %a)" Duration.pp initial Duration.pp step
 37:   | Both (a, b) -> Format.fprintf ppf "Both(%a,%a)" pp a pp b
 38:   | Either (a, b) -> Format.fprintf ppf "Either(%a,%a)" pp a pp b
 39:   | And_then (a, b) -> Format.fprintf ppf "AndThen(%a,%a)" pp a pp b
 40:   | Jittered (s, lo, hi) -> Format.fprintf ppf "Jittered(%a,%g,%g)" pp s lo hi
 41:   | Named (s, n) -> Format.fprintf ppf "Named(%a, %S)" pp s n
 42: 
 43: let pow_factor f step = f ** float_of_int step
 44: 
 45: let scale_capped d factor =
 46:   let factor = if factor < 0.0 then 0.0 else factor in
 47:   let scaled = float_of_int (Duration.to_ms d) *. factor in
 48:   match classify_float scaled with
 49:   | FP_nan -> invalid_arg "Duration.scale"
 50:   | FP_infinite -> Duration.ms max_int
 51:   | FP_normal | FP_subnormal | FP_zero ->
 52:       if scaled >= float_of_int max_int then Duration.ms max_int
 53:       else Duration.scale d factor
 54: 
 55: let add_capped a b =
 56:   let a_ms = Duration.to_ms a in
 57:   let b_ms = Duration.to_ms b in
 58:   if a_ms > max_int - b_ms then Duration.ms max_int
 59:   else Duration.ms (a_ms + b_ms)
 60: 
 61: let default_random = lazy (Capabilities.random_default ())
 62: 
 63: type state =
 64:   | Driver_recurs of int
 65:   | Driver_forever
 66:   | Driver_spaced of Duration.t
 67:   | Driver_fixed of Duration.t
 68:   | Driver_exponential of Duration.t * float * int
 69:   | Driver_linear of {
 70:       initial : Duration.t;
 71:       step : Duration.t;
 72:       index : int;
 73:     }
 74:   | Driver_both of state * state
 75:   | Driver_either of state * state
 76:   | Driver_and_then of state * t
 77:   | Driver_jittered of state * float * float
 78:   | Driver_named of state
 79: 
 80: type driver = {
 81:   random : Capabilities.random;
 82:   state : state;
 83: }
 84: 
 85: let rec state_of_schedule = function
 86:   | Recurs n -> Driver_recurs n
 87:   | Forever -> Driver_forever
 88:   | Spaced d -> Driver_spaced d
 89:   | Fixed d -> Driver_fixed d
 90:   | Exponential (d, factor) -> Driver_exponential (d, factor, 0)
 91:   | Linear { initial; step } -> Driver_linear { initial; step; index = 0 }
 92:   | Both (a, b) -> Driver_both (state_of_schedule a, state_of_schedule b)
 93:   | Either (a, b) -> Driver_either (state_of_schedule a, state_of_schedule b)
 94:   | And_then (a, b) -> Driver_and_then (state_of_schedule a, b)
 95:   | Jittered (inner, lo, hi) ->
 96:       Driver_jittered (state_of_schedule inner, lo, hi)
 97:   | Named (inner, _) -> Driver_named (state_of_schedule inner)
 98: 
 99: let start ?(random = Lazy.force default_random) schedule =
100:   { random; state = state_of_schedule schedule }
101: 
102: let rec next_state random = function
103:   | Driver_recurs remaining ->
104:       if remaining > 0 then Some (Duration.zero, Driver_recurs (remaining - 1))
105:       else None
106:   | Driver_forever -> Some (Duration.zero, Driver_forever)
107:   | Driver_spaced d -> Some (d, Driver_spaced d)
108:   | Driver_fixed d -> Some (d, Driver_fixed d)
109:   | Driver_exponential (d, factor, step) ->
110:       Some
111:         ( scale_capped d (pow_factor factor step),
112:           Driver_exponential (d, factor, step + 1) )
113:   | Driver_linear { initial; step; index } ->
114:       let delta = scale_capped step (float_of_int index) in
115:       Some
116:         ( add_capped initial delta,
117:           Driver_linear { initial; step; index = index + 1 } )
118:   | Driver_both (a, b) -> (
119:       match next_state random a, next_state random b with
120:       | Some (da, a'), Some (db, b') ->
121:           Some (Duration.max da db, Driver_both (a', b'))
122:       | _ -> None)
123:   | Driver_either (a, b) -> (
124:       match next_state random a, next_state random b with
125:       | Some (da, a'), Some (db, b') ->
126:           Some (Duration.min da db, Driver_either (a', b'))
127:       | Some (d, a'), None -> Some (d, a')
128:       | None, Some (d, b') -> Some (d, b')
129:       | None, None -> None)
130:   | Driver_and_then (a, b) -> (
131:       match next_state random a with
132:       | Some (d, a') -> Some (d, Driver_and_then (a', b))
133:       | None -> next_state random (state_of_schedule b))
134:   | Driver_jittered (inner, lo, hi) -> (
135:       match next_state random inner with
136:       | None -> None
137:       | Some (d, inner') ->
138:           let factor =
139:             lo +. ((hi -. lo) *. Capabilities.random_float random 1.0)
140:           in
141:           Some (scale_capped d factor, Driver_jittered (inner', lo, hi)))
142:   | Driver_named inner -> (
143:       match next_state random inner with
144:       | None -> None
145:       | Some (d, inner') -> Some (d, Driver_named inner'))
146: 
147: let next driver =
148:   match next_state driver.random driver.state with
149:   | None -> None
150:   | Some (delay, state) -> Some (delay, { driver with state })
151: 
152: let next_delay ?random sch ~step =
153:   let rec advance driver remaining =
154:     match next driver with
155:     | None -> None
156:     | Some (delay, driver') ->
157:         if remaining <= 0 then Some delay else advance driver' (remaining - 1)
158:   in
159:   advance (start ?random sch) step
````

````ocaml
  1: let[@zero_alloc] is_trim_space = function

  3:   | _ -> false
  4: 
  5: let[@zero_alloc] lowercase_ascii_char c =

  7:   | 'A' .. 'Z' -> Char.unsafe_chr (Char.code c + 32)
  8:   | _ -> c
  9: 
 10: let[@zero_alloc] ascii_equal_ci left right =
 11:   Char.equal (lowercase_ascii_char left) (lowercase_ascii_char right)
 12: 
 13: let[@zero_alloc] lower_hex_digit value =
 14:   if value < 10 then Char.unsafe_chr (Char.code '0' + value)
 15:   else Char.unsafe_chr (Char.code 'a' + value - 10)
 16: 
 17: let[@zero_alloc] upper_hex_digit value =
 18:   if value < 10 then Char.unsafe_chr (Char.code '0' + value)
 19:   else Char.unsafe_chr (Char.code 'A' + value - 10)
 20: 
 21: let[@zero_alloc] trim_left value start finish =
 22:   let index = ref start in
 23:   while !index < finish && is_trim_space (String.unsafe_get value !index) do
 24:     incr index
 25:   done;
 26:   !index
 27: 
 28: let[@zero_alloc] trim_right value start finish =
 29:   let index = ref finish in
 30:   while !index > start && is_trim_space (String.unsafe_get value (!index - 1)) do
 31:     decr index
 32:   done;
 33:   !index
 34: 
 35: let trim_bounds value =
 36:   let len = String.length value in
 37:   let start = trim_left value 0 len in
 38:   (start, trim_right value start len)
 39: 
 40: let[@zero_alloc] is_blank value =
 41:   let len = String.length value in
 42:   trim_left value 0 len = len
 43: 
 44: let trim value =
 45:   let start, stop = trim_bounds value in
 46:   if start = 0 && stop = String.length value then value
 47:   else String.sub value start (stop - start)
 48: 
 49: let[@zero_alloc] has_uppercase_ascii value start stop =
 50:   let index = ref start in
 51:   let found = ref false in
 52:   while (not !found) && !index < stop do
 53:     let c = String.unsafe_get value !index in
 54:     found := c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z';
 55:     incr index
 56:   done;
 57:   !found
 58: 
 59: let lowercase_ascii_trim value =
 60:   let value_len = String.length value in
 61:   let start = trim_left value 0 value_len in
 62:   let stop = trim_right value start value_len in
 63:   if start = 0 && stop = value_len && not (has_uppercase_ascii value start stop)
 64:   then value
 65:   else
 66:     let len = stop - start in
 67:     let bytes = Bytes.create len in
 68:     for index = 0 to len - 1 do
 69:       Bytes.unsafe_set bytes index
 70:         (lowercase_ascii_char (String.unsafe_get value (start + index)))
 71:     done;
 72:     Bytes.unsafe_to_string bytes
 73: 
 74: let lowercase_ascii value =
 75:   let len = String.length value in
 76:   if not (has_uppercase_ascii value 0 len) then value
 77:   else
 78:     let bytes = Bytes.create len in
 79:     for index = 0 to len - 1 do
 80:       Bytes.unsafe_set bytes index
 81:         (lowercase_ascii_char (String.unsafe_get value index))
 82:     done;
 83:     Bytes.unsafe_to_string bytes
 84: 
 85: let[@zero_alloc] contains_ascii_ci haystack needle =
 86:   let haystack_len = String.length haystack in
 87:   let needle_len = String.length needle in
 88:   if needle_len = 0 then true
 89:   else if needle_len > haystack_len then false
 90:   else (
 91:     let pos = ref 0 in
 92:     let found = ref false in
 93:     while (not !found) && !pos + needle_len <= haystack_len do
 94:       let index = ref 0 in
 95:       while
 96:         !index < needle_len
 97:         && ascii_equal_ci
 98:              (String.unsafe_get haystack (!pos + !index))
 99:              (String.unsafe_get needle !index)
100:       do
101:         incr index
102:       done;
103:       found := !index = needle_len;
104:       incr pos
105:     done;
106:     !found)
107: 
108: let[@zero_alloc] is_http_token_space = function
109:   | ' ' | '\t' -> true
110:   | _ -> false
111: 
112: let[@zero_alloc] trim_equal_ascii_ci_bounds value start stop token =
113:   let start = ref start in
114:   let stop = ref stop in
115:   while !start < !stop && is_http_token_space (String.unsafe_get value !start) do
116:     incr start
117:   done;
118:   while !stop > !start && is_http_token_space (String.unsafe_get value (!stop - 1)) do
119:     decr stop
120:   done;
121:   let len = !stop - !start in
122:   let token_len = String.length token in
123:   if len <> token_len then false
124:   else (
125:     let index = ref 0 in
126:     while
127:       !index < token_len
128:       && ascii_equal_ci
129:            (String.unsafe_get value (!start + !index))
130:            (String.unsafe_get token !index)
131:     do
132:       incr index
133:     done;
134:     !index = token_len)
135: 
136: let[@zero_alloc] contains_token_ascii_ci value token =

138:   let start = ref 0 in
139:   let found = ref false in
140:   while (not !found) && !start <= len do
141:     let stop =
142:       match String.index_from_opt value !start ',' with
143:       | None -> len
144:       | Some index -> index
145:     in
146:     found := trim_equal_ascii_ci_bounds value !start stop token;
147:     start := stop + 1
148:   done;
149:   !found
150: 
151: let[@zero_alloc] starts_with_at value ~offset prefix =
152:   let value_len = String.length value in

154:   if offset < 0 || value_len - offset < prefix_len then false

156:     let index = ref 0 in

158:       !index < prefix_len

160:            (String.unsafe_get value (offset + !index))

162:     do
163:       incr index
164:     done;
165:     !index = prefix_len)
166: 
167: let[@zero_alloc] starts_with value ~prefix = starts_with_at value ~offset:0 prefix
168: 
169: let[@zero_alloc] ends_with value ~suffix =

177:       !index < suffix_len
178:       && Char.equal
179:            (String.unsafe_get value (offset + !index))
180:            (String.unsafe_get suffix !index)
181:     do
182:       incr index
183:     done;

185:
````

## File: lib/http/body/stream.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: open Eta
  4: 
  5: module Error = Error
  6: 
  7: type read_result = Chunk of bytes | Last of bytes | End
  8: 
  9: type t = {
 10:   read_next : (unit -> (read_result, Error.t) Effect.t);
 11:   release : (unit -> (unit, Error.t) Effect.t);
 12:   mutable released : bool;
 13:   active : bool Atomic.t;
 14: }
 15: 
 16: let concurrent_use () =
 17:   Error.make ~method_:"*" ~uri:"*"
 18:     (Decode_error
 19:        {
 20:          codec = "body-stream";
 21:          message = "concurrent body stream operation";
 22:        })
 23: 
 24: let with_operation t eff =
 25:   if not (Atomic.compare_and_set t.active false true) then
 26:     Effect.fail (concurrent_use ())
 27:   else (
 28:     eff
 29:     |> Effect.finally (Effect.sync (fun () -> Atomic.set t.active false)))
 30: 
 31: let release_once t =
 32:   if t.released then Effect.unit
 33:   else (
 34:     t.released <- true;
 35:     t.release ())
 36: 
 37: let empty () =
 38:   {
 39:     read_next = (fun () -> Effect.pure End);
 40:     release = (fun () -> Effect.unit);
 41:     released = false;
 42:     active = Atomic.make false;
 43:   }
 44: 
 45: let of_reader ?(release = fun () -> Effect.unit) (read_next) =
 46:   { read_next; release; released = false; active = Atomic.make false }
 47: 
 48: let of_bytes ?(release = fun () -> Effect.unit) chunks =
 49:   let chunks = Array.of_list chunks in
 50:   let next = ref 0 in
 51:   let read_next () =
 52:     if !next >= Array.length chunks then Effect.pure End
 53:     else
 54:       let chunk = Bytes.copy chunks.(!next) in
 55:       incr next;
 56:       if !next >= Array.length chunks then Effect.pure (Last chunk)
 57:       else Effect.pure (Chunk chunk)
 58:   in
 59:   of_reader ~release read_next
 60: 
 61: let default_max_bytes = 1_048_576
 62: 
 63: let read_unlocked t =
 64:   if t.released then Effect.pure None
 65:   else
 66:     Effect.sync (fun () -> ref true)
 67:     |> Effect.bind (fun release_needed ->
 68:            let release_if_needed =
 69:              Effect.sync (fun () -> !release_needed)
 70:              |> Effect.bind (fun needed ->
 71:                     if needed then release_once t else Effect.unit)
 72:            in
 73:            t.read_next ()
 74:            |> Effect.bind (function
 75:                 | End -> Effect.pure None
 76:                 | Chunk chunk ->
 77:                     release_needed := false;
 78:                     Effect.pure (Some chunk)
 79:                 | Last chunk -> Effect.pure (Some chunk))
 80:            |> Effect.finally release_if_needed)
 81: 
 82: let read t = with_operation t (read_unlocked t)
 83: 
 84: let body_too_large ~limit ~length =
 85:   Error.make ~method_:"*" ~uri:"*" (Body_too_large { limit; length })
 86: 
 87: let read_all ?(max_bytes = default_max_bytes) t =
 88:   if max_bytes < 0 then
 89:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.Body.Stream.read_all: max_bytes must be >= 0";
 90:   let rec loop acc total =
 91:     read_unlocked t
 92:     |> Effect.bind (function
 93:          | None ->
 94:              let out = Bytes.create total in
 95:              let _ =
 96:                List.fold_left
 97:                  (fun off chunk ->
 98:                    let len = Bytes.length chunk in
 99:                    Bytes.blit chunk 0 out off len;
100:                    off + len)
101:                  0 (List.rev acc)
102:              in
103:              Effect.pure out
104:          | Some chunk ->
105:              let length = total + Bytes.length chunk in
106:              if length < total || length > max_bytes then
107:                Effect.fail (body_too_large ~limit:max_bytes ~length)
108:              else loop (chunk :: acc) length)
109:   in
110:   with_operation t
111:     (Effect.scoped
112:        (Effect.acquire_release ~acquire:Effect.unit
113:           ~release:(fun () -> release_once t)
114:        |> Effect.bind (fun () -> loop [] 0)))
115: 
116: let discard t = with_operation t (release_once t)
````

## File: lib/http/body/stream.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Pull-based byte body stream for eta-http responses. *)
 2: 
 3: type t
 4: (** One-shot body stream.
 5: 
 6:     A stream permits one active operation at a time. Concurrent [read],
 7:     [read_all], or [discard] calls fail with a typed [Decode_error] instead of
 8:     racing the mutable stream state. *)
 9: 
10: type read_result = Chunk of bytes | Last of bytes | End
11: 
12: val empty : unit -> t
13: val of_bytes :
14:   ?release:(unit -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
15:   bytes list ->
16:   t
17: val of_reader :
18:   ?release:(unit -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
19:   (unit -> (read_result, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
20:   t
21: 
22: val default_max_bytes : int
23: (** Default maximum bytes accumulated by [read_all]. *)
24: 
25: val read : t -> (bytes option, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
26: val read_all : ?max_bytes:int -> t -> (bytes, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
27: (** Read the full stream into memory, failing with [Body_too_large] if more
28:     than [max_bytes] would be accumulated. *)
29: val discard : t -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
````

## File: lib/http/core/header.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type t = (string * string) list
  4: type name = string
  5: type value = string
  6: 
  7: let empty = []
  8: let header_invalid reason = Error.Header_invalid { reason }
  9: 
 10: let[@zero_alloc] is_tchar = function
 11:   | '!' | '#' | '$' | '%' | '&' | '\'' | '*' | '+' | '-' | '.' | '^' | '_'
 12:   | '`' | '|' | '~'
 13:   | '0' .. '9' | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' ->
 14:       true
 15:   | _ -> false
 16: 
 17: let[@zero_alloc] rec valid_name_loop name len index =
 18:   index = len
 19:   || (is_tchar (String.unsafe_get name index)
 20:       && valid_name_loop name len (index + 1))
 21: 
 22: let[@zero_alloc] valid_name name =
 23:   let len = String.length name in
 24:   len > 0 && valid_name_loop name len 0
 25: 
 26: let[@zero_alloc] invalid_value_char c =
 27:   let code = Char.code c in
 28:   (code < 32 && code <> 9) || code = 127
 29: 
 30: let[@zero_alloc] rec valid_value_loop value len index =
 31:   index = len
 32:   || ((not (invalid_value_char (String.unsafe_get value index)))
 33:       && valid_value_loop value len (index + 1))
 34: 
 35: let[@zero_alloc] valid_value value =
 36:   valid_value_loop value (String.length value) 0
 37: 
 38: let[@zero_alloc] valid_header (name, value) = valid_name name && valid_value value
 39: 
 40: let[@zero_alloc] rec valid = function
 41:   | [] -> true
 42:   | header :: rest -> valid_header header && valid rest
 43: 
 44: let validate_name name =
 45:   if valid_name name then None
 46:   else if String.equal name "" then Some (header_invalid "empty header name")
 47:   else Some (header_invalid "invalid header name")
 48: 
 49: let validate_value value =
 50:   if valid_value value then None
 51:   else Some (header_invalid "invalid header value")
 52: 
 53: let validate_header (name, value) =
 54:   match validate_name name with
 55:   | Some error -> Some error
 56:   | None -> validate_value value
 57: 
 58: let rec validate = function
 59:   | [] -> None
 60:   | header :: rest -> (
 61:       match validate_header header with
 62:       | Some error -> Some error
 63:       | None -> validate rest)
 64: 
 65: let name value =
 66:   match validate_name value with
 67:   | None -> Ok value
 68:   | Some error -> Error error
 69: 
 70: let value value =
 71:   match validate_value value with
 72:   | None -> Ok value
 73:   | Some error -> Error error
 74: 
 75: let pair name value =
 76:   match validate_header (name, value) with
 77:   | None -> Ok (name, value)
 78:   | Some error -> Error error
 79: 
 80: let add name value headers =
 81:   match pair name value with
 82:   | Ok header -> Ok (header :: headers)
 83:   | Error error -> Error error
 84: 
 85: let of_list headers =
 86:   match validate headers with
 87:   | None -> Ok headers
 88:   | Some error -> Error error
 89: 
 90: let unsafe_add name value headers = (name, value) :: headers
 91: let unsafe_of_list headers = headers
 92: let to_list headers = headers
 93: let normalize_name = Eta.String_helpers.lowercase_ascii_trim
 94: 
 95: let equal_normalized_name = Eta.String_helpers.trim_equal_ascii_ci
 96: 
 97: let get_all name headers =
 98:   let normalized = normalize_name name in
 99:   let rec loop acc = function
100:     | [] -> List.rev acc
101:     | (candidate, value) :: rest ->
102:         if equal_normalized_name normalized candidate then loop (value :: acc) rest
103:         else loop acc rest
104:   in
105:   loop [] headers
106: 
107: let get name headers =
108:   let normalized = normalize_name name in
109:   let rec loop = function
110:     | [] -> None
111:     | (candidate, value) :: rest ->
112:         if equal_normalized_name normalized candidate then Some value else loop rest
113:   in
114:   loop headers
115: 
116: let remove name headers =
117:   let normalized = normalize_name name in
118:   let rec loop acc = function
119:     | [] -> List.rev acc
120:     | (candidate, _) as header :: rest ->
121:         if equal_normalized_name normalized candidate then loop acc rest
122:         else loop (header :: acc) rest
123:   in
124:   loop [] headers
````

## File: lib/http/core/header.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP header list helpers.
 2: 
 3:     Header names accepted by validated constructors are RFC token strings.
 4:     Header values permit HTAB and reject CR, LF, NUL, and other control bytes
 5:     so callers cannot inject additional wire header lines through serialized
 6:     requests. *)
 7: 
 8: type t = (string * string) list
 9: type name = private string
10: type value = private string
11: 
12: val empty : t
13: val name : string -> (name, Error.kind) result
14: val value : string -> (value, Error.kind) result
15: val pair : string -> string -> (name * value, Error.kind) result
16: val add : string -> string -> t -> (t, Error.kind) result
17: val of_list : (string * string) list -> (t, Error.kind) result
18: val unsafe_add : string -> string -> t -> t
19: val unsafe_of_list : (string * string) list -> t
20: val to_list : t -> (string * string) list
21: val normalize_name : string -> string
22: val validate_name : string -> Error.kind option
23: val validate_value : string -> Error.kind option
24: val validate_header : string * string -> Error.kind option
25: val validate : t -> Error.kind option
26: val[@zero_alloc] valid : t -> bool
27: val get : string -> t -> string option
28: val get_all : string -> t -> string list
29: val remove : string -> t -> t
````

````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP request methods. *)

 3: type t =
 4:   [ `GET
 5:   | `HEAD
 6:   | `POST
 7:   | `PUT
 8:   | `DELETE
 9:   | `CONNECT
10:   | `OPTIONS
11:   | `TRACE
12:   | `PATCH
13:   | `Other of string ]
````

## File: lib/http/core/url.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** RFC 3986 client-subset URLs.
 2: 
 3:     The parser accepts absolute [http] and [https] URLs with authority, optional
 4:     port, path, query, and fragment. It rejects userinfo and unsupported
 5:     schemes because eta-http v1 does not own authentication policy. *)
 6: 
 7: type t
 8: 
 9: type scheme = Http | Https
10: 
11: type parse_error =
12:   | Empty
13:   | Missing_scheme
14:   | Unsupported_scheme of string
15:   | Missing_authority
16:   | Missing_host
17:   | Userinfo_not_supported
18:   | Invalid_port of string
19:   | Invalid_character of {
20:       component : string;
21:       index : int;
22:       char : char;
23:     }
24: 
25: val parse : string -> (t, parse_error) result
26: val of_string : string -> t
27: (** Parse a URL or raise [Invalid_argument]. Prefer {!parse} at API boundaries. *)
28: 
29: val pp_parse_error : Format.formatter -> parse_error -> unit
30: val parse_error_to_string : parse_error -> string
31: 
32: val to_string : t -> string
33: val scheme : t -> scheme
34: val scheme_to_string : scheme -> string
35: val host : t -> string
36: (** Host without URI brackets. IPv6 literals such as [https://[::1]/] return
37:     ["::1"] so transport peer-identity checks can parse the IP literal. *)
38: val port : t -> int option
39: val default_port : scheme -> int
40: val effective_port : t -> int
41: val path : t -> string
42: val query : t -> string option
43: val fragment : t -> string option
44: val authority : t -> string
45: (** Host plus optional port for HTTP authority and Host headers. IPv6 literals
46:     include URI brackets, for example ["[::1]:8443"]. *)
47: val origin_form : t -> string
48: val blit_authority : bytes -> pos:int -> t -> int
49: (** Write the URL authority into [bytes] at [pos]. Returns the next offset, or
50:     a negative value if [bytes] is too small. *)
51: 
52: val[@zero_alloc] blit_authority_raw : bytes -> int -> t -> int
53: 
54: val blit_origin_form : bytes -> pos:int -> t -> int
55: (** Write the origin-form request target into [bytes] at [pos]. Returns the
56:     next offset, or a negative value if [bytes] is too small. *)
57: 
58: val[@zero_alloc] blit_origin_form_raw : bytes -> int -> t -> int
59: 
60: val redacted : t -> string
````

## File: lib/http/h2/admission.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type state = Active | Cancelled | Complete | Released
  4: 
  5: type permit = {
  6:   id : int;
  7:   mutable state : state;
  8: }
  9: 
 10: type stats = {
 11:   active : int;
 12:   cancelled : int;
 13:   inflight : int;
 14:   opened : int;
 15:   completed : int;
 16:   local_resets : int;
 17:   remote_resets : int;
 18:   admission_rejected : int;
 19:   max_inflight : int;
 20:   max_concurrent : int;
 21: }
 22: 
 23: type release = Queue_rst | No_rst
 24: 
 25: type t = {
 26:   max_concurrent : int;
 27:   mutable next_id : int;
 28:   mutable active : int;
 29:   mutable cancelled : int;
 30:   mutable opened : int;
 31:   mutable completed : int;
 32:   mutable local_resets : int;
 33:   mutable remote_resets : int;
 34:   mutable admission_rejected : int;
 35:   mutable max_inflight : int;
 36:   mutable closed : bool;
 37: }
 38: 
 39: let create ~max_concurrent =
 40:   if max_concurrent <= 0 then
 41:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.H2.Admission.create: max_concurrent must be > 0";
 42:   {
 43:     max_concurrent;
 44:     next_id = 1;
 45:     active = 0;
 46:     cancelled = 0;
 47:     opened = 0;
 48:     completed = 0;
 49:     local_resets = 0;
 50:     remote_resets = 0;
 51:     admission_rejected = 0;
 52:     max_inflight = 0;
 53:     closed = false;
 54:   }
 55: 
 56: let inflight t = t.active + t.cancelled
 57: 
 58: let update_max_inflight t =
 59:   t.max_inflight <- max t.max_inflight (inflight t)
 60: 
 61: let try_acquire t =
 62:   if t.closed || inflight t >= t.max_concurrent then (
 63:     t.admission_rejected <- t.admission_rejected + 1;
 64:     Error ())
 65:   else
 66:     let permit = { id = t.next_id; state = Active } in
 67:     t.next_id <- t.next_id + 2;
 68:     t.active <- t.active + 1;
 69:     t.opened <- t.opened + 1;
 70:     update_max_inflight t;
 71:     Ok permit
 72: 
 73: let stream_id permit = permit.id
 74: 
 75: let mark_remote_reset t permit =
 76:   match permit.state with
 77:   | Active ->
 78:       permit.state <- Cancelled;
 79:       t.active <- max 0 (t.active - 1);
 80:       t.cancelled <- t.cancelled + 1;
 81:       t.remote_resets <- t.remote_resets + 1;
 82:       update_max_inflight t
 83:   | Cancelled | Complete | Released -> ()
 84: 
 85: let mark_complete _t permit =
 86:   match permit.state with
 87:   | Active -> permit.state <- Complete
 88:   | Cancelled | Complete | Released -> ()
 89: 
 90: let release t permit =
 91:   match permit.state with
 92:   | Active ->
 93:       permit.state <- Released;
 94:       t.active <- max 0 (t.active - 1);
 95:       t.local_resets <- t.local_resets + 1;
 96:       t.completed <- t.completed + 1;
 97:       Queue_rst
 98:   | Cancelled ->
 99:       permit.state <- Released;
100:       t.cancelled <- max 0 (t.cancelled - 1);
101:       t.completed <- t.completed + 1;
102:       No_rst
103:   | Complete ->
104:       permit.state <- Released;
105:       t.active <- max 0 (t.active - 1);
106:       t.completed <- t.completed + 1;
107:       No_rst
108:   | Released -> No_rst
109: 
110: let close t =
111:   t.closed <- true;
112:   t.active <- 0;
113:   t.cancelled <- 0
114: 
115: let stats t =
116:   {
117:     active = t.active;
118:     cancelled = t.cancelled;
119:     inflight = inflight t;
120:     opened = t.opened;
121:     completed = t.completed;
122:     local_resets = t.local_resets;
123:     remote_resets = t.remote_resets;
124:     admission_rejected = t.admission_rejected;
125:     max_inflight = t.max_inflight;
126:     max_concurrent = t.max_concurrent;
127:   }
````

## File: lib/http/h2/admission.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP/2 stream admission counter.
 2: 
 3:     Remotely reset streams remain admitted until local response-body release
 4:     observes and releases their permit. This preserves the H-D1
 5:     ACTIVE+CANCELLED invariant used to cap churn attacks. *)
 6: 
 7: type t
 8: 
 9: type permit
10: 
11: type stats = {
12:   active : int;
13:   cancelled : int;
14:   inflight : int;
15:   opened : int;
16:   completed : int;
17:   local_resets : int;
18:   remote_resets : int;
19:   admission_rejected : int;
20:   max_inflight : int;
21:   max_concurrent : int;
22: }
23: 
24: type release = Queue_rst | No_rst
25: 
26: val create : max_concurrent:int -> t
27: val try_acquire : t -> (permit, unit) result
28: val stream_id : permit -> int
29: val mark_remote_reset : t -> permit -> unit
30: val mark_complete : t -> permit -> unit
31: val release : t -> permit -> release
32: val close : t -> unit
33: val stats : t -> stats
````

## File: lib/http/h2/frame.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type frame_type =
  4:   | Data
  5:   | Headers
  6:   | Priority
  7:   | Rst_stream
  8:   | Settings
  9:   | Push_promise
 10:   | Ping
 11:   | Goaway
 12:   | Window_update
 13:   | Continuation
 14:   | Other of int
 15: 
 16: type envelope = {
 17:   length : int;
 18:   frame_type : int;
 19:   flags : int;
 20:   stream_id : int;
 21: }
 22: 
 23: let header_size = 9
 24: 
 25: let frame_type_code = function
 26:   | Data -> 0x0
 27:   | Headers -> 0x1
 28:   | Priority -> 0x2
 29:   | Rst_stream -> 0x3
 30:   | Settings -> 0x4
 31:   | Push_promise -> 0x5
 32:   | Ping -> 0x6
 33:   | Goaway -> 0x7
 34:   | Window_update -> 0x8
 35:   | Continuation -> 0x9
 36:   | Other code ->
 37:       if code < 0 || code > 0xff then
 38:         invalid_arg "Eta_http.H2.Frame.frame_type_code: code outside uint8";
 39:       code
 40: 
 41: let byte n = Char.chr (n land 0xff)
 42: 
 43: let validate_range label ~max value =
 44:   if value < 0 || value > max then
 45:     invalid_arg
 46:       (Printf.sprintf "Eta_http.H2.Frame.%s outside 0..%d" label max)
 47: 
 48: let validate_header_bounds label len off =
 49:   if off < 0 || off > len - header_size then
 50:     invalid_arg
 51:       (Printf.sprintf "Eta_http.H2.Frame.%s: need %d bytes at offset %d" label
 52:          header_size off)
 53: 
 54: let parse_header_with ~label ~len ~get ~off =
 55:   validate_header_bounds label len off;
 56:   let byte index = Char.code (get (off + index)) in
 57:   {
 58:     length = (byte 0 lsl 16) lor (byte 1 lsl 8) lor byte 2;
 59:     frame_type = byte 3;
 60:     flags = byte 4;
 61:     stream_id =
 62:       ((byte 5 land 0x7f) lsl 24)
 63:       lor (byte 6 lsl 16) lor (byte 7 lsl 8) lor byte 8;
 64:   }
 65: 
 66: let parse_header_string data ~off =
 67:   parse_header_with ~label:"parse_header_string" ~len:(String.length data)
 68:     ~get:(String.unsafe_get data) ~off
 69: 
 70: let parse_header_bytes data ~off =
 71:   parse_header_with ~label:"parse_header_bytes" ~len:(Bytes.length data)
 72:     ~get:(Bytes.unsafe_get data) ~off
 73: 
 74: let parse_header_buffer data ~off =
 75:   parse_header_with ~label:"parse_header_buffer" ~len:(Buffer.length data)
 76:     ~get:(Buffer.nth data) ~off
 77: 
 78: let header ~length ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id =
 79:   validate_range "length" ~max:0xffffff length;
 80:   validate_range "flags" ~max:0xff flags;
 81:   validate_range "stream_id" ~max:0x7fffffff stream_id;
 82:   let frame_type = frame_type_code frame_type in
 83:   String.init header_size @@ function
 84:   | 0 -> byte (length lsr 16)
 85:   | 1 -> byte (length lsr 8)
 86:   | 2 -> byte length
 87:   | 3 -> byte frame_type
 88:   | 4 -> byte flags
 89:   | 5 -> byte (stream_id lsr 24)
 90:   | 6 -> byte (stream_id lsr 16)
 91:   | 7 -> byte (stream_id lsr 8)
 92:   | 8 -> byte stream_id
 93:   | _ -> assert false
 94: 
 95: let uint32 n =
 96:   if n < 0 || n > 0xffff_ffff then
 97:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.H2.Frame.uint32: value outside uint32";
 98:   String.init 4 @@ function
 99:   | 0 -> byte (n lsr 24)
100:   | 1 -> byte (n lsr 16)
101:   | 2 -> byte (n lsr 8)
102:   | 3 -> byte n
103:   | _ -> assert false
104: 
105: let settings = header ~length:0 ~frame_type:Settings ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0
106: 
107: let goaway_no_error ~last_stream_id =
108:   validate_range "last_stream_id" ~max:0x7fffffff last_stream_id;
109:   header ~length:8 ~frame_type:Goaway ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0
110:   ^ uint32 last_stream_id
111:   ^ uint32 0
112: 
113: let payload len =
114:   if len < 0 then invalid_arg "Eta_http.H2.Frame.payload: negative length";
115:   String.make len '\000'
````

## File: lib/http/h2/frame.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Small raw HTTP/2 frame helpers for tests and defensive probes. *)
 2: 
 3: type frame_type =
 4:   | Data
 5:   | Headers
 6:   | Priority
 7:   | Rst_stream
 8:   | Settings
 9:   | Push_promise
10:   | Ping
11:   | Goaway
12:   | Window_update
13:   | Continuation
14:   | Other of int
15: 
16: type envelope = {
17:   length : int;
18:   frame_type : int;
19:   flags : int;
20:   stream_id : int;
21: }
22: (** Parsed HTTP/2 frame envelope. [stream_id] masks out the reserved high bit,
23:     matching RFC 9113 section 4.1. *)
24: 
25: val header_size : int
26: val frame_type_code : frame_type -> int
27: val parse_header_string : string -> off:int -> envelope
28: val parse_header_bytes : bytes -> off:int -> envelope
29: val parse_header_buffer : Buffer.t -> off:int -> envelope
30: val header : length:int -> frame_type:frame_type -> flags:int -> stream_id:int -> string
31: val uint32 : int -> string
32: val settings : string
33: val goaway_no_error : last_stream_id:int -> string
34: val payload : int -> string
````

## File: lib/http/h2/informational_filter.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Input filter for HTTP/2 informational response headers.
 2: 
 3:     ocaml-h2 0.13 treats the first response HEADERS as the active response,
 4:     including 1xx interim responses. This filter sits before ocaml-h2's client
 5:     parser, consumes server HEADERS/CONTINUATION blocks, drops interim 1xx
 6:     blocks, and re-encodes final response/trailer blocks so ocaml-h2's HPACK
 7:     decoder sees a coherent stream. *)
 8: 
 9: type t
10: 
11: val create : unit -> t
12: 
13: val feed :
14:   t ->
15:   string ->
16:   off:int ->
17:   len:int ->
18:   (unit, Error.kind) result
19: 
20: val take : t -> string
21: 
22: val forget_stream : t -> int -> unit
23: (** Forget state associated with a stream that was locally released or reset.
24:     The filter observes remote END_STREAM/RST frames itself; local teardown must
25:     call this explicitly because no inbound frame will arrive to clear the
26:     final-response marker. *)
27: 
28: val buffered_bytes : t -> int
29: 
30: (** [is_passthrough t] is always false. A safe raw passthrough mode would need
31:     to be stream-specific; callers should continue feeding server-to-client
32:     bytes through the filter. *)
33: val is_passthrough : t -> bool
````

## File: lib/http/h2/stream_state.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: module Admission = Admission
  4: module P_atomic = Atomic
  5: 
  6: type status = Active | Remote_reset | Complete | Released
  7: 
  8: type stream = {
  9:   id : int;
 10:   tag : int;
 11:   permit : Admission.permit;
 12:   status : status P_atomic.t;
 13: }
 14: 
 15: type stats = {
 16:   active : int;
 17:   cancelled : int;
 18:   inflight : int;
 19:   live : int;
 20:   opened : int;
 21:   completed : int;
 22:   local_resets : int;
 23:   remote_resets : int;
 24:   admission_rejected : int;
 25:   max_inflight : int;
 26:   max_concurrent : int;
 27: }
 28: 
 29: type release = Queue_rst | No_rst
 30: 
 31: type t = {
 32:   admission : Admission.t;
 33:   streams : (int, stream) Hashtbl.t;
 34:   mutable closed : bool;
 35: }
 36: 
 37: let create ~max_concurrent =
 38:   {
 39:     admission = Admission.create ~max_concurrent;
 40:     streams = Hashtbl.create max_concurrent;
 41:     closed = false;
 42:   }
 43: 
 44: let id stream = stream.id
 45: let tag stream = stream.tag
 46: let status stream = P_atomic.get stream.status
 47: let is_client_stream_id id = id > 0 && (id land 1) = 1
 48: 
 49: let cas_status stream seen replace_with =
 50:   P_atomic.compare_and_set stream.status seen replace_with
 51: 
 52: let open_stream t ~tag =
 53:   if t.closed then Error ()
 54:   else
 55:     match Admission.try_acquire t.admission with
 56:     | Error () -> Error ()
 57:     | Ok permit ->
 58:         let id = Admission.stream_id permit in
 59:         if not (is_client_stream_id id) then
 60:           invalid_arg
 61:             "Eta_http.H2.Stream_state.open_stream: client stream id must be positive odd";
 62:         let stream = { id; tag; permit; status = P_atomic.make Active } in
 63:         Hashtbl.replace t.streams id stream;
 64:         Ok stream
 65: 
 66: let find t stream_id =
 67:   if t.closed then None else Hashtbl.find_opt t.streams stream_id
 68: 
 69: let rec mark_remote_reset t stream_id =
 70:   match find t stream_id with
 71:   | None -> ()
 72:   | Some stream -> (
 73:       match status stream with
 74:       | Active ->
 75:           if cas_status stream Active Remote_reset then
 76:             Admission.mark_remote_reset t.admission stream.permit
 77:           else mark_remote_reset t stream_id
 78:       | Remote_reset | Complete | Released -> ())
 79: 
 80: let rec mark_complete t stream =
 81:   match status stream with
 82:   | Active ->
 83:       if cas_status stream Active Complete then
 84:         Admission.mark_complete t.admission stream.permit
 85:       else mark_complete t stream
 86:   | Remote_reset | Complete | Released -> ()
 87: 
 88: let release_result = function
 89:   | Admission.Queue_rst -> Queue_rst
 90:   | Admission.No_rst -> No_rst
 91: 
 92: let rec release t stream =
 93:   match status stream with
 94:   | Released -> No_rst
 95:   | Active ->
 96:       if cas_status stream Active Released then (
 97:         Hashtbl.remove t.streams stream.id;
 98:         release_result (Admission.release t.admission stream.permit))
 99:       else release t stream
100:   | Remote_reset ->
101:       if cas_status stream Remote_reset Released then (
102:         Hashtbl.remove t.streams stream.id;
103:         release_result (Admission.release t.admission stream.permit))
104:       else release t stream
105:   | Complete ->
106:       if cas_status stream Complete Released then (
107:         Hashtbl.remove t.streams stream.id;
108:         release_result (Admission.release t.admission stream.permit))
109:       else release t stream
110: 
111: let close t =
112:   if not t.closed then (
113:     t.closed <- true;
114:     Hashtbl.iter
115:       (fun _ stream -> P_atomic.set stream.status Released)
116:       t.streams;
117:     Hashtbl.clear t.streams;
118:     Admission.close t.admission)
119: 
120: let stats t =
121:   let admission = Admission.stats t.admission in
122:   {
123:     active = admission.active;
124:     cancelled = admission.cancelled;
125:     inflight = admission.inflight;
126:     live = Hashtbl.length t.streams;
127:     opened = admission.opened;
128:     completed = admission.completed;
129:     local_resets = admission.local_resets;
130:     remote_resets = admission.remote_resets;
131:     admission_rejected = admission.admission_rejected;
132:     max_inflight = admission.max_inflight;
133:     max_concurrent = admission.max_concurrent;
134:   }
````

## File: lib/http/h2/stream_state.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP/2 per-stream lifecycle state.
 2: 
 3:     This layer owns eta-http's public stream lifetime around the lower-level
 4:     ocaml-h2 state machine. Remotely reset streams continue to occupy
 5:     admission until the caller releases the response body. *)
 6: 
 7: type status = Active | Remote_reset | Complete | Released
 8: 
 9: type stream
10: 
11: type stats = {
12:   active : int;
13:   cancelled : int;
14:   inflight : int;
15:   live : int;
16:   opened : int;
17:   completed : int;
18:   local_resets : int;
19:   remote_resets : int;
20:   admission_rejected : int;
21:   max_inflight : int;
22:   max_concurrent : int;
23: }
24: 
25: type release = Queue_rst | No_rst
26: 
27: type t
28: 
29: val create : max_concurrent:int -> t
30: val open_stream : t -> tag:int -> (stream, unit) result
31: val id : stream -> int
32: val tag : stream -> int
33: val status : stream -> status
34: val is_client_stream_id : int -> bool
35: val find : t -> int -> stream option
36: val mark_remote_reset : t -> int -> unit
37: val mark_complete : t -> stream -> unit
````
