````ocaml
38: val release : t -> stream -> release
39: val close : t -> unit
40: val stats : t -> stats
````

## File: lib/http/transport/alpn.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type protocol = H1 | H2
  4: 
  5: type pending = { id : int } [@@unboxed]
  6: 
  7: type state =
  8:   | Empty
  9:   | Pending of pending
 10:   | Ready of protocol
 11: 
 12: type begin_result =
 13:   | Leader of pending
 14:   | Wait of pending
 15:   | Ready of protocol
 16: 
 17: type resolve_result =
 18:   | Installed of protocol
 19:   | Already_ready of protocol
 20:   | Ignored
 21: 
 22: type stats = {
 23:   leaders : int;
 24:   waiters : int;
 25:   redundant_cancelled : int;
 26:   h1_resolved : int;
 27:   h2_resolved : int;
 28: }
 29: 
 30: type t = {
 31:   mutable next_pending_id : int;
 32:   mutable state : state;
 33:   mutable leaders : int;
 34:   mutable waiters : int;
 35:   mutable redundant_cancelled : int;
 36:   mutable h1_resolved : int;
 37:   mutable h2_resolved : int;
 38: }
 39: 
 40: let create () =
 41:   {
 42:     next_pending_id = 1;
 43:     state = Empty;
 44:     leaders = 0;
 45:     waiters = 0;
 46:     redundant_cancelled = 0;
 47:     h1_resolved = 0;
 48:     h2_resolved = 0;
 49:   }
 50: 
 51: let pending_id pending = pending.id
 52: 
 53: let begin_request t =
 54:   match t.state with
 55:   | Ready protocol -> Ready protocol
 56:   | Pending pending ->
 57:       t.waiters <- t.waiters + 1;
 58:       t.redundant_cancelled <- t.redundant_cancelled + 1;
 59:       Wait pending
 60:   | Empty ->
 61:       let pending = { id = t.next_pending_id } in
 62:       t.next_pending_id <- t.next_pending_id + 1;
 63:       t.state <- Pending pending;
 64:       t.leaders <- t.leaders + 1;
 65:       Leader pending
 66: 
 67: let record_resolution t = function
 68:   | H1 -> t.h1_resolved <- t.h1_resolved + 1
 69:   | H2 -> t.h2_resolved <- t.h2_resolved + 1
 70: 
 71: let same_pending left right = left.id = right.id
 72: 
 73: let resolve t pending protocol =
 74:   match t.state with
 75:   | Empty -> Ignored
 76:   | Ready ready -> Already_ready ready
 77:   | Pending current when same_pending pending current ->
 78:       t.state <- Ready protocol;
 79:       record_resolution t protocol;
 80:       Installed protocol
 81:   | Pending _ -> Ignored
 82: 
 83: let cancel t pending =
 84:   match t.state with
 85:   | Pending current when same_pending pending current -> t.state <- Empty
 86:   | Empty | Ready _ | Pending _ -> ()
 87: 
 88: let protocol_of_alpn = function
 89:   | Some "h2" -> Ok H2
 90:   | Some "http/1.1" | None -> Ok H1
 91:   | Some protocol -> Error protocol
 92: 
 93: let stats t =
 94:   {
 95:     leaders = t.leaders;
 96:     waiters = t.waiters;
 97:     redundant_cancelled = t.redundant_cancelled;
 98:     h1_resolved = t.h1_resolved;
 99:     h2_resolved = t.h2_resolved;
100:   }
````

## File: lib/http/transport/alpn.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** ALPN dispatch state machine.
 2: 
 3:     This module is deliberately pure. It records the H-D5 first-arrival
 4:     collapse rules without owning sockets, fibers, or h1/h2 connection
 5:     resources. The transport dispatcher supplies those resources around the
 6:     decisions returned here. *)
 7: 
 8: type protocol = H1 | H2
 9: 
10: type pending
11: 
12: type t
13: 
14: type begin_result =
15:   | Leader of pending
16:   | Wait of pending
17:   | Ready of protocol
18: 
19: type resolve_result =
20:   | Installed of protocol
21:   | Already_ready of protocol
22:   | Ignored
23: 
24: type stats = {
25:   leaders : int;
26:   waiters : int;
27:   redundant_cancelled : int;
28:   h1_resolved : int;
29:   h2_resolved : int;
30: }
31: 
32: val create : unit -> t
33: val pending_id : pending -> int
34: val begin_request : t -> begin_result
35: val resolve : t -> pending -> protocol -> resolve_result
36: val cancel : t -> pending -> unit
37: val protocol_of_alpn : string option -> (protocol, string) result
38: val stats : t -> stats
````

## File: lib/http/transport/dispatch.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: (** Protocol dispatch policy for negotiated HTTP transports. *)
 4: 
 5: type protocol = H1 | H2
 6: type decision = Use_h1 | Use_h2
 7: 
 8: let protocol_to_string = function H1 -> "h1" | H2 -> "h2"
 9: let decision_protocol = function Use_h1 -> H1 | Use_h2 -> H2
10: 
11: let decide_alpn alpn =
12:   match Alpn.protocol_of_alpn alpn with
13:   | Ok Alpn.H1 -> Ok Use_h1
14:   | Ok Alpn.H2 -> Ok Use_h2
15:   | Error protocol -> Error protocol
16: 
17: let unsupported_alpn request protocol =
18:   Error.make ~protocol:Error.Unknown ~method_:request.Request.method_
19:     ~uri:request.uri
20:     (Tls_handshake_error
21:        {
22:          stage = Alpn_negotiation;
23:          message = "unsupported ALPN protocol " ^ protocol;
24:        })
25: 
26: let dispatch_alpn ~close ~use_h1 ~use_h2 request alpn =
27:   match decide_alpn alpn with
28:   | Error protocol ->
29:       close ()
30:       |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun () ->
31:              Eta.Effect.fail (unsupported_alpn request protocol))
32:   | Ok Use_h1 -> use_h1 ()
33:   | Ok Use_h2 -> use_h2 ()
````

## File: lib/http/transport/dispatch.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Protocol dispatch policy for negotiated HTTP transports. *)
 2: 
 3: type protocol = H1 | H2
 4: type decision = Use_h1 | Use_h2
 5: 
 6: val protocol_to_string : protocol -> string
 7: val decision_protocol : decision -> protocol
 8: 
 9: val decide_alpn : string option -> (decision, string) result
10: (** Convert a negotiated ALPN value into an eta-http protocol route.
11: 
12:     [None] is HTTP/1.1 for servers that complete TLS without ALPN. Unknown
13:     protocol strings are returned to the caller so they can be reported with
14:     request context. *)
15: 
16: val dispatch_alpn :
17:   close:(unit -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
18:   use_h1:(unit -> ('a, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
19:   use_h2:(unit -> ('a, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
20:   Request.t ->
21:   string option ->
22:   ('a, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
23: (** Run the selected protocol branch for a negotiated ALPN value. Unsupported
24:     ALPN values close the underlying transport before failing with an
25:     [Alpn_negotiation] TLS error. *)
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/red_probes/h2_frames/run.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* HTTP/2 frame / state-machine red probes.
  2:    Each probe starts an Eta H2C server and drives it with pathological frames.
  3:    The runner exits 0 even when probes find bugs. *)
  4: 
  5: open Eta_http_testsuite
  6: 
  7: let h2_client_preface = Adversarial.h2_client_preface
  8: let h2_request_headers = Adversarial.h2_request_headers
  9: let h2_adversarial_config = Adversarial.h2_adversarial_config
 10: let h2_basic_handler = Adversarial.h2_basic_handler
 11: let tcp_port = Adversarial.tcp_port
 12: 
 13: type status = PASS | FAIL | HANG | CRASH | POLICY_GAP
 14: 
 15: let string_of_status = function
 16:   | PASS -> "PASS"
 17:   | FAIL -> "FAIL"
 18:   | HANG -> "HANG"
 19:   | CRASH -> "CRASH"
 20:   | POLICY_GAP -> "POLICY_GAP"
 21: 
 22: let print_probe name status detail =
 23:   Printf.printf "probe %s %s%s\n%!" name (string_of_status status)
 24:     (match detail with None -> "" | Some d -> " " ^ d)
 25: 
 26: type outcome =
 27:   | Closed of string
 28:   | Read_timeout of string
 29:   | Probe_timeout
 30:   | Errored of string
 31: 
 32: (** Parse a raw byte sequence into a list of (type, flags, stream_id). *)
 33: let parse_frames s =
 34:   let rec loop off acc =
 35:     if off + 9 > String.length s then List.rev acc
 36:     else
 37:       let len =
 38:         (Char.code s.[off] lsl 16)
 39:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 1] lsl 8)
 40:         lor Char.code s.[off + 2]
 41:       in
 42:       let ty = Char.code s.[off + 3] in
 43:       let flags = Char.code s.[off + 4] in
 44:       let stream_id =
 45:         ((Char.code s.[off + 5] land 0x7F) lsl 24)
 46:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 6] lsl 16)
 47:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 7] lsl 8)
 48:         lor Char.code s.[off + 8]
 49:       in
 50:       if off + 9 + len > String.length s then List.rev acc
 51:       else loop (off + 9 + len) ((ty, flags, stream_id) :: acc)
 52:   in
 53:   loop 0 []
 54: 
 55: let has_frame_type ty frames = List.exists (fun (t, _, _) -> t = ty) frames
 56: let has_headers = has_frame_type 0x01
 57: let has_data = has_frame_type 0x00
 58: let has_rst_stream = has_frame_type 0x03
 59: let has_settings = has_frame_type 0x04
 60: let has_ping = has_frame_type 0x06
 61: let has_goaway = has_frame_type 0x07
 62: 
 63: (** Read from [flow] until it closes, but cap total bytes. *)
 64: let read_until_close ?(max_bytes = 64 * 1024) flow =
 65:   let buffer = Buffer.create 256 in
 66:   let scratch = Cstruct.create 1024 in
 67:   let rec loop total =
 68:     if total >= max_bytes then Buffer.contents buffer
 69:     else
 70:       let len = min (Cstruct.length scratch) (max_bytes - total) in
 71:       match Eio.Flow.single_read flow (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 len) with
 72:       | 0 -> Buffer.contents buffer
 73:       | n ->
 74:           Buffer.add_string buffer
 75:             (Cstruct.to_string (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 n));
 76:           loop (total + n)
 77:       | exception End_of_file -> Buffer.contents buffer
 78:   in
 79:   loop 0
 80: 
 81: (** Drain whatever is currently available with a short timeout. *)
 82: let drain_available ~clock ~seconds flow =
 83:   let buffer = Buffer.create 256 in
 84:   let scratch = Cstruct.create 1024 in
 85:   try
 86:     Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock seconds (fun () ->
 87:         let rec loop () =
 88:           match Eio.Flow.single_read flow scratch with
 89:           | 0 -> ()
 90:           | n ->
 91:               Buffer.add_string buffer
 92:                 (Cstruct.to_string (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 n));
 93:               loop ()
 94:           | exception End_of_file -> ()
 95:         in
 96:         loop ();
 97:         `Drained (Buffer.contents buffer))
 98:   with
 99:   | Eio.Time.Timeout -> `Timeout (Buffer.contents buffer)
100:   | exn -> `Error (Printexc.to_string exn, Buffer.contents buffer)
101: 
102: (** Start an Eta H2C server on a random port, connect, run [client_fn], and
103:     record what happens. *)
104: let run_h2c_probe ~env ~name ~deadline_sec ~read_deadline_sec client_fn =
105:   let outcome =
106:     try
107:       Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
108:       let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
109:       let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
110:       let socket =
111:         Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
112:           (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
113:       in
114:       let port = tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
115:       let config = h2_adversarial_config () in
116:       Eio.Fiber.fork_daemon ~sw (fun () ->
117:           Eio.Switch.run @@ fun conn_sw ->
118:           let flow, peer = Eio.Net.accept ~sw:conn_sw socket in
119:           let runtime_factory ~sw ~connection:_ () =
120:             Eta_eio.Runtime.create ~sw ~clock ()
121:           in
122:           Eta_http_eio.H2.Server_connection.run_h2c ~sw:conn_sw ~clock
123:             ~flow:(flow :> Eta_http_eio.H2.Server_connection.flow)
124:             ~peer ~config ~runtime_factory h2_basic_handler;
125:           `Stop_daemon);
126:       let flow =
127:         Eio.Net.connect ~sw net
128:           (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
129:       in
130:       Fun.protect
131:         ~finally:(fun () ->
132:           try Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `All with _ -> ())
133:         (fun () ->
134:           try
135:             Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock deadline_sec (fun () ->
136:                 client_fn ~clock flow;
137:                 match drain_available ~clock ~seconds:read_deadline_sec flow with
138:                 | `Drained bytes -> Closed bytes
139:                 | `Timeout bytes -> Read_timeout bytes
140:                 | `Error (msg, bytes) -> Errored (msg ^ "; drained=" ^ string_of_int (String.length bytes)))
141:           with
142:           | Eio.Time.Timeout -> Probe_timeout
143:           | exn -> Errored (Printexc.to_string exn))
144:     with exn -> Errored (Printexc.to_string exn)
145:   in
146:   name, outcome
147: 
148: (** Assess an outcome for probes that expect the server to reject the input
149:     and close the connection. *)
150: let assess_expect_close name outcome =
151:   match outcome with
152:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
153:   | Errored msg ->
154:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
155:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
156:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
157:   | Read_timeout bytes ->
158:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
159:       if has_goaway frames || has_rst_stream frames then
160:         print_probe name PASS (Some "server closed with error frame")
161:       else
162:         print_probe name POLICY_GAP
163:           (Some
164:              (Printf.sprintf "server kept connection open (drained %d bytes)"
165:                 (String.length bytes)))
166:   | Closed bytes ->
167:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
168:       if has_goaway frames || has_rst_stream frames then
169:         print_probe name PASS (Some "connection closed after error frame")
170:       else
171:         print_probe name PASS
172:           (Some
173:              (Printf.sprintf "connection closed (%d bytes, no error frame)"
174:                 (String.length bytes)))
175: 
176: (** Assess an outcome for probes that expect the server to tolerate the input
177:     and respond to a subsequent valid request. *)
178: let assess_expect_response name outcome =
179:   match outcome with
180:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
181:   | Errored msg ->
182:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
183:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
184:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
185:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
186:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
187:       if has_headers frames then
188:         print_probe name PASS
189:           (Some
190:              (Printf.sprintf "server responded (%d bytes)" (String.length bytes)))
191:       else if has_goaway frames then
192:         print_probe name POLICY_GAP
193:           (Some "server sent GOAWAY instead of tolerating frame")
194:       else
195:         print_probe name FAIL
196:           (Some
197:              (Printf.sprintf "server closed without response (%d bytes)"
198:                 (String.length bytes)))
199: 
200: (* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
201: (* Probe implementations                                                      *)
202: (* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
203: 
204: let probe_data_before_headers ~env () =
205:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"data_before_headers" ~deadline_sec:3.0
206:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
207:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
208:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
209:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:1 "x")
210:         flow;
211:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
212:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
213: 
214: let probe_headers_after_end_stream ~env () =
215:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"headers_after_end_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
216:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
217:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
218:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
219:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ())
220:         flow;
221:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
222:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ())
223:         flow;
224:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
225:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
226: 
227: let probe_continuation_without_headers ~env () =
228:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"continuation_without_headers" ~deadline_sec:3.0
229:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
230:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
231:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
232:         (Malicious_h2.continuation_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:1
233:            (Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:":status" ~value:"200"))
234:         flow;
235:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
236:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
237: 
238: let probe_headers_on_stream_zero ~env () =
239:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"headers_on_stream_zero" ~deadline_sec:3.0
240:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
241:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
242:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:0 ()) flow;
243:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
244:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
245: 
246: let probe_headers_on_even_stream ~env () =
247:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"headers_on_even_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
248:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
249:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
250:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:2 ()) flow;
251:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
252:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
253: 
254: let probe_client_push_promise ~env () =
255:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"client_push_promise" ~deadline_sec:3.0
256:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
257:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
258:       let promised_stream_id = 2 in
259:       let hpack_block =
260:         String.concat ""
261:           [
262:             Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:":method" ~value:"GET";
263:             Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:":path" ~value:"/push";
264:             Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:":scheme" ~value:"http";
265:             Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:":authority" ~value:"example.test";
266:           ]
267:       in
268:       let payload =
269:         let buf = Bytes.create 4 in
270:         Bytes.set_int32_be buf 0
271:           (Int32.logand (Int32.of_int promised_stream_id) 0x7FFFFFFFl);
272:         Bytes.to_string buf ^ hpack_block
273:       in
274:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
275:         (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x05 ~flags:0x04 ~stream_id:1 payload)
276:         flow;
277:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
278:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
279: 
280: let probe_priority_ignored ~env () =
281:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"priority_ignored" ~deadline_sec:3.0
282:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
283:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
284:       (* PRIORITY frame: 5-byte payload exclusive + dependency + weight *)
285:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
286:         (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x02 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:1
287:            "\x00\x00\x00\x00\x10")
288:         flow;
289:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()) flow;
290:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
291:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
292: 
293: let probe_unknown_frame_ignored ~env () =
294:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"unknown_frame_ignored" ~deadline_sec:3.0
295:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
296:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
297:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
298:         (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0xFF ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:0 "hello")
299:         flow;
300:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()) flow;
301:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
302:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
303: 
304: let probe_rst_stream_mid_response ~env () =
305:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"rst_stream_mid_response" ~deadline_sec:3.0
306:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
307:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
308:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
309:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:false ())
310:         flow;
311:       (* Give the server a moment to start the response, then cancel. *)
312:       Eio.Time.sleep clock 0.1;
313:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
314:         (Malicious_h2.rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:1 8)
315:         flow;
316:       (* A new request on a fresh stream should still succeed. *)
317:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:3 ()) flow;
318:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
319:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
320: 
321: let probe_goaway_mid_stream ~env () =
322:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"goaway_mid_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
323:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
324:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
325:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
326:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:false ())
327:         flow;
328:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
329:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:3 ~end_stream:false ())
330:         flow;
331:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
332:         (Malicious_h2.goaway_frame ~last_stream_id:3 ~error_code:0 ())
333:         flow;
334:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
335:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
336:     (* After GOAWAY the server may close. We mainly care that it does not hang. *)
337:     match outcome with
338:     | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
339:     | Errored msg ->
340:         if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
341:           print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
342:         else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
343:     | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
344:         let frames = parse_frames bytes in
345:         if has_goaway frames then
346:           print_probe name PASS (Some "server sent GOAWAY")
347:         else if has_headers frames then
348:           print_probe name PASS (Some "active streams completed")
349:         else
350:           print_probe name POLICY_GAP
351:             (Some
352:                (Printf.sprintf "server closed without GOAWAY (%d bytes)"
353:                   (String.length bytes)))
354: 
355: let probe_settings_mid_stream ~env () =
356:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"settings_mid_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
357:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
358:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
359:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
360:         (h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:false ())
361:         flow;
362:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
363:         (Malicious_h2.settings_frame [ (0x3, 100) ])
364:         flow;
365:       (* Wait for SETTINGS ACK before finishing the request body. *)
366:       let rec wait_ack () =
367:         let len, ty, flags, _sid = Malicious_h2.read_frame_header flow in
368:         if ty = 0x04 && flags land 0x01 <> 0 then ()
369:         else (
370:           Malicious_h2.skip_frame_payload flow len;
371:           wait_ack ())
372:       in
373:       wait_ack ();
374:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
375:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:1 "hello")
376:         flow;
377:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
378:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
379: 
380: let probe_ping_requires_ack ~env () =
381:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"ping_requires_ack" ~deadline_sec:3.0
382:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
383:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
384:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
385:         (Malicious_h2.ping_frame ~ack:false "ping!!!!")
386:         flow;
387:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()) flow;
388:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
389:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
390:     match outcome with
391:     | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
392:     | Errored msg ->
393:         if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
394:           print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
395:         else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
396:     | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
397:         let frames = parse_frames bytes in
398:         let ping_acked =
399:           List.exists
400:             (fun (t, f, _) -> t = 0x06 && f land 0x01 <> 0)
401:             frames
402:         in
403:         if ping_acked && has_headers frames then
404:           print_probe name PASS (Some "PING ACK and response received")
405:         else if has_headers frames then
406:           print_probe name PASS (Some "response received (PING ACK not checked)")
407:         else if ping_acked then
408:           print_probe name POLICY_GAP (Some "PING ACK but no response")
409:         else
410:           print_probe name FAIL (Some "no PING ACK or response")
411: 
412: let probe_window_update_before_headers ~env () =
413:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"window_update_before_headers" ~deadline_sec:3.0
414:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
415:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
416:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
417:         (Malicious_h2.window_update_frame ~stream_id:1 1000)
418:         flow;
419:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
420:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
421: 
422: let probe_data_after_rst_stream ~env () =
423:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"data_after_rst_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
424:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
425:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
426:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
427:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:false ())
428:         flow;
429:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
430:         (Malicious_h2.rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:1 8)
431:         flow;
432:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
433:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:1 "x")
434:         flow;
435:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:3 ()) flow;
436:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
437:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
438: 
439: let probe_continuation_fragmentation ~env () =
440:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"continuation_fragmentation" ~deadline_sec:3.0
441:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
442:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
443:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
444:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_headers:false ~end_stream:true ())
445:         flow;
446:       for _i = 1 to 5 do
447:         Eio.Flow.copy_string
448:           (Malicious_h2.continuation_frame ~end_headers:false ~stream_id:1
449:              (Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:"x-fragment" ~value:"a"))
450:           flow
451:       done;
452:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
453:         (Malicious_h2.continuation_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:1
454:            (Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:"x-end" ~value:"done"))
455:         flow;
456:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
457:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_response name outcome
458: 
459: let probe_rst_stream_on_idle ~env () =
460:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"rst_stream_on_idle" ~deadline_sec:3.0
461:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
462:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
463:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
464:         (Malicious_h2.rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:1 8)
465:         flow;
466:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
467:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
468: 
469: let probe_data_on_stream_zero ~env () =
470:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"data_on_stream_zero" ~deadline_sec:3.0
471:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
472:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
473:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
474:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:0 "x")
475:         flow;
476:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
477:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
478: 
479: let probe_settings_on_nonzero_stream ~env () =
480:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"settings_on_nonzero_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
481:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
482:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
483:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
484:         (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x04 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:1 "")
485:         flow;
486:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
487:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
488: 
489: let probe_ping_on_nonzero_stream ~env () =
490:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"ping_on_nonzero_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
491:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
492:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
493:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
494:         (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x06 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:1
495:            (String.make 8 '\x00'))
496:         flow;
497:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
498:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
499: 
500: let probe_continuation_wrong_stream ~env () =
501:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"continuation_wrong_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
502:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
503:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
504:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
505:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_headers:false ())
506:         flow;
507:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
508:         (Malicious_h2.continuation_frame ~end_headers:true ~stream_id:3
509:            (Malicious_h2.hpack_literal ~name:"x-end" ~value:"done"))
510:         flow;
511:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
512:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_expect_close name outcome
513: 
514: let probe_goaway_lower_last_stream ~env () =
515:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"goaway_lower_last_stream" ~deadline_sec:3.0
516:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
517:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
518:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()) flow;
519:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:3 ()) flow;
520:       (* GOAWAY says the last processed stream was 1, so stream 3 should be
521:          rejected/ignored. The server should still respond on stream 1. *)
522:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
523:         (Malicious_h2.goaway_frame ~last_stream_id:1 ~error_code:0 ())
524:         flow;
525:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
526:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
527:     match outcome with
528:     | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
529:     | Errored msg ->
530:         if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
531:           print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
532:         else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
533:     | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
534:         let frames = parse_frames bytes in
535:         if has_goaway frames && has_headers frames then
536:           print_probe name PASS (Some "GOAWAY and response for stream 1")
537:         else if has_headers frames then
538:           print_probe name PASS (Some "response for stream 1")
539:         else if has_goaway frames then
540:           print_probe name POLICY_GAP (Some "GOAWAY but no response")
541:         else
542:           print_probe name POLICY_GAP
543:             (Some
544:                (Printf.sprintf "server closed without response (%d bytes)"
545:                   (String.length bytes)))
546: 
547: let probe_headers_without_end_headers ~env () =
548:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"headers_without_end_headers" ~deadline_sec:3.0
549:     ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
550:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (h2_client_preface ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
551:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
552:         (h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_headers:false ())
553:         flow;
554:       (* Do not send CONTINUATION; the server must eventually timeout or error. *)
555:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
556:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
557:     (* The server may close, send GOAWAY, or hang waiting for CONTINUATION. *)
558:     match outcome with
559:     | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
560:     | Errored msg ->
561:         if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
562:           print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
563:         else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
564:     | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
565:         let frames = parse_frames bytes in
566:         if has_goaway frames || has_rst_stream frames then
567:           print_probe name PASS (Some "server rejected incomplete headers")
568:         else
569:           print_probe name POLICY_GAP
570:             (Some
571:                (Printf.sprintf "server closed without error frame (%d bytes)"
572:                   (String.length bytes)))
573: 
574: (* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
575: (* Main                                                                       *)
576: (* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
577: 
578: let () =
579:   Eio_main.run @@ fun env ->
580:   let probes =
581:     [
582:       probe_data_before_headers;
583:       probe_headers_after_end_stream;
584:       probe_continuation_without_headers;
585:       probe_headers_on_stream_zero;
586:       probe_headers_on_even_stream;
587:       probe_client_push_promise;
588:       probe_priority_ignored;
589:       probe_unknown_frame_ignored;
590:       probe_rst_stream_mid_response;
591:       probe_goaway_mid_stream;
592:       probe_settings_mid_stream;
593:       probe_ping_requires_ack;
594:       probe_window_update_before_headers;
595:       probe_data_after_rst_stream;
596:       probe_continuation_fragmentation;
597:       probe_rst_stream_on_idle;
598:       probe_data_on_stream_zero;
599:       probe_settings_on_nonzero_stream;
600:       probe_ping_on_nonzero_stream;
601:       probe_continuation_wrong_stream;
602:       probe_goaway_lower_last_stream;
603:       probe_headers_without_end_headers;
604:     ]
605:   in
606:   List.iter (fun f -> f ~env ()) probes;
607:   Printf.printf "h2_frames done\n%!"
````

## File: lib/eta/runtime_core.ml
````ocaml
  1: module P_atomic = Atomic
  2: module RObs = Runtime_observability
  3: module Sch = Schedule
  4: 
  5: let option_map f = function None -> None | Some value -> Some (f value)
  6: 
  7: (* Typed failures cross runtime fibers through OCaml exceptions. This is the only
  8:    place that may pack/unpack a typed Cause through [Obj.t]. The fresh
  9:    [Typed_fail] key is the dynamic proof that the unpacking interpreter is the
 10:    one that packed the value; mismatched keys are treated as ordinary defects by
 11:    [cause_of_exn]. Do not copy this erasure pattern into feature modules. *)
 12: exception Raised_cause of int * Obj.t
 13: 
 14: module Typed_fail : sig
 15:   type key
 16: 
 17:   val fresh : unit -> key
 18:   val int : key -> int
 19: end = struct
 20:   type key = int
 21:   let counter = Atomic.make 0
 22:   let fresh () = Atomic.fetch_and_add counter 1 + 1
 23:   let int key = key
 24: end
 25: 
 26: let[@cold][@zero_alloc assume error] raise_cause key cause =
 27:   raise (Raised_cause (Typed_fail.int key, Obj.repr cause))
 28: 
 29: let[@cold][@zero_alloc assume error] raise_fail key err = raise_cause key (Cause.Fail err)
 30: 
 31: let cancellation_reason contract exn =
 32:   contract.Runtime_contract.cancellation_reason exn
 33: 
 34: let is_cancellation contract exn =
 35:   Option.is_some (cancellation_reason contract exn)
 36: 
 37: let rec cause_of_exn ?backtrace ~contract ~capture_backtrace key exn =
 38:   match exn with
 39:   | Raised_cause (k, cause) when k = Typed_fail.int key -> Obj.obj cause
 40:   (* Bare [Stdlib.Exit] is user code raising a normal OCaml exception. Eta
 41:      cancellation must be recognized by the runtime contract, including
 42:      internal cancellations whose reason value happens to be [Exit]. *)
 43:   | Fun.Finally_raised exn ->
 44:       cause_of_exn ~contract ~capture_backtrace key exn
 45:   | exn -> (
 46:       match cancellation_reason contract exn with
 47:       | Some _ -> Cause.interrupt
 48:       | None -> (
 49:           match contract.Runtime_contract.multiple_exceptions exn with
 50:           | Some causes ->
 51:               let causes =
 52:                 List.map
 53:                   (fun (exn, bt) ->
 54:                     cause_of_exn ~contract ~backtrace:bt ~capture_backtrace key
 55:                       exn)
 56:                   causes
 57:               in
 58:               (match causes with
 59:               | [] -> failwith "Runtime_contract.multiple_exceptions: empty"
 60:               | causes when List.for_all Cause.is_interrupt_only causes ->
 61:                   Cause.interrupt
 62:               | [ cause ] -> cause
 63:               | causes -> Cause.concurrent causes)
 64:           | None -> RObs.die_of_exn contract ?backtrace ~capture_backtrace exn))
 65: 
 66: type drain_waiter = {
 67:   drain_resolver : unit Runtime_contract.resolver;
 68:   mutable drain_active : bool;
 69: }
 70: 
 71: type 'err t = {
 72:   sleep : Duration.t -> unit;
 73:   now_ms : unit -> int;
 74:   tracer : Capabilities.tracer;
 75:   tracing_enabled : bool;
 76:   sampler : Sampler.t;
 77:   auto_instrument : bool;
 78:   logger : Capabilities.logger;
 79:   logging_enabled : bool;
 80:   meter : Capabilities.meter;
 81:   metrics_enabled : bool;
 82:   random : Capabilities.random;
 83:   services : (int, Runtime_contract.service) Hashtbl.t;
 84:   services_lock : Sync_lock.t;
 85:   contract : Runtime_contract.t;
 86:   capture_backtrace : bool;
 87:   outer_scope : Runtime_contract.scope;
 88:   active : int P_atomic.t;
 89:   active_lock : Sync_lock.t;
 90:   mutable active_waiters : drain_waiter list;
 91:   default_fail_key : Typed_fail.key;
 92: }
 93: 
 94: let create_with_contract ~contract ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer
 95:     ?(sampler = Sampler.always_on) ?(auto_instrument = false) ?logger ?meter
 96:     ?random ?(services = []) ?(capture_backtrace = true) () =
 97:   let tracing_enabled = Option.is_some tracer in
 98:   let logging_enabled = Option.is_some logger in
 99:   let metrics_enabled = Option.is_some meter in
100:   let tracer = Option.value tracer ~default:Tracer.noop in
101:   let logger = Option.value logger ~default:Logger.noop in
102:   let meter = Option.value meter ~default:Meter.noop in
103:   let sleep = Option.value sleep ~default:contract.Runtime_contract.sleep in
104:   let now_ms = Option.value now_ms ~default:contract.Runtime_contract.now_ms in
105:   let contract =
106:     { contract with Runtime_contract.sleep = sleep; now_ms = now_ms }
107:   in
108:   let random =
109:     match random with
110:     | Some random -> random
111:     | None ->
112:         let seed = (now_ms () * 1_000) land 0x3fffffff in
113:         Capabilities.random_of_seed seed
114:   in
115:   let services_table = Hashtbl.create (List.length services) in
116:   List.iter
117:     (fun (Runtime_contract.Service (key, value)) ->
118:       Hashtbl.replace services_table
119:         (Runtime_contract.Backend.service_key_id key)
120:         (Runtime_contract.Service (key, value)))
121:     services;
122:   {
123:     sleep;
124:     now_ms;
125:     tracer;
126:     tracing_enabled;
127:     sampler;
128:     auto_instrument;
129:     logger;
130:     logging_enabled;
131:     meter;
132:     metrics_enabled;
133:     random;
134:     services = services_table;
135:     services_lock = Sync_lock.create ();
136:     contract;
137:     capture_backtrace;
138:     outer_scope = contract.Runtime_contract.root_scope;
139:     active = P_atomic.make 0;
140:     active_lock = Sync_lock.create ();
141:     active_waiters = [];
142:     default_fail_key = Typed_fail.fresh ();
143:   }
144: 
145: let resolve_drain_waiters runtime waiters =
146:   List.iter
147:     (fun waiter ->
148:       if waiter.drain_active then (
149:         waiter.drain_active <- false;
150:         runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.resolve_promise waiter.drain_resolver
151:           ()))
152:     waiters
153: 
154: let incr_active runtime =
155:   Sync_lock.use runtime.active_lock @@ fun () -> P_atomic.incr runtime.active
156: 
157: let decr_active runtime =
158:   let waiters =
159:     Sync_lock.use runtime.active_lock @@ fun () ->
160:     P_atomic.decr runtime.active;
161:     if P_atomic.get runtime.active = 0 then (
162:       let waiters = runtime.active_waiters in
163:       runtime.active_waiters <- [];
164:       waiters)
165:     else []
166:   in
167:   resolve_drain_waiters runtime waiters
168: 
169: let wait_active_zero runtime =
170:   let rec loop () =
171:     match
172:       Sync_lock.use runtime.active_lock @@ fun () ->
173:       if P_atomic.get runtime.active = 0 then None
174:       else
175:         let promise, resolver =
176:           runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.create_promise ()
177:         in
178:         let waiter = { drain_resolver = resolver; drain_active = true } in
179:         runtime.active_waiters <- waiter :: runtime.active_waiters;
180:         Some (promise, waiter)
181:     with
182:     | None -> ()
183:     | Some (promise, waiter) -> (
184:         try
185:           runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.await_promise promise;
186:           loop ()
187:         with exn
188:           when Option.is_some
189:                  (runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.cancellation_reason exn) ->
190:           runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.protect (fun () ->
191:               Sync_lock.use runtime.active_lock @@ fun () ->
192:               waiter.drain_active <- false);
193:           raise exn)
194:   in
195:   loop ()
196: 
197: let emit_daemon_failure runtime cause =
198:   RObs.emit_daemon_failure ~contract:runtime.contract ~now_ms:runtime.now_ms
199:     ~logging_enabled:runtime.logging_enabled ~logger:runtime.logger
200:     ~tracing_enabled:runtime.tracing_enabled ~tracer:runtime.tracer cause
201: 
202: let cause_of_exn_runtime ?backtrace runtime key exn =
203:   cause_of_exn ?backtrace ~contract:runtime.contract
204:     ~capture_backtrace:runtime.capture_backtrace key exn
205: 
206: let die_of_exn_runtime runtime exn =
207:   RObs.die_of_exn runtime.contract
208:     ~capture_backtrace:runtime.capture_backtrace exn
209: 
210: let service runtime key =
211:   Sync_lock.use runtime.services_lock @@ fun () ->
212:   match
213:     Hashtbl.find_opt runtime.services
214:       (Runtime_contract.Backend.service_key_id key)
215:   with
216:   | None -> None
217:   | Some service -> Runtime_contract.Backend.service_value key service
218: 
219: let run_finalizers ~runtime ~fail_key finalizers =
220:   runtime.contract.Runtime_contract.protect @@ fun () ->
221:   match !finalizers with
222:   | [] -> None
223:   | fs ->
224:       finalizers := [];
225:       fs
226:       |> List.filter_map (fun f ->
227:              try
228:                f ();
229:                None
230:              with exn -> Some (cause_of_exn_runtime runtime fail_key exn))
231:       |> (function
232:            | [] -> None
233:            | [ cause ] -> Some cause
234:            | causes -> Some (Cause.sequential causes))
235: 
236: let render_finalizer_cause ~error_renderer cause =
237:   Cause.finalizer_of_cause
238:     (fun err ->
239:       RObs.render_typed_failure ~error_renderer (Obj.repr err))
240:     cause
241: 
242: let with_finalizers ?(interrupt_of_cancel = fun _ -> Cause.interrupt) ~runtime
243:     ~fail_key ~error_renderer finalizers body =
244:   match body () with
245:   | value -> (
246:       match run_finalizers ~runtime ~fail_key finalizers with
247:       | None -> value
248:       | Some finalizer ->
249:           raise_cause fail_key
250:             (Cause.finalizer (render_finalizer_cause ~error_renderer finalizer)))
251:   | exception exn when is_cancellation runtime.contract exn -> (
252:       let reason =
253:         match cancellation_reason runtime.contract exn with
254:         | Some reason -> reason
255:         | None -> assert false
256:       in
257:       match run_finalizers ~runtime ~fail_key finalizers with
258:       | None -> raise exn
259:       | Some finalizer ->
260:           raise_cause fail_key
261:             (Cause.suppressed ~primary:(interrupt_of_cancel reason)
262:                ~finalizer:(render_finalizer_cause ~error_renderer finalizer)))
263:   | exception exn ->
264:       let primary = cause_of_exn_runtime runtime fail_key exn in
265:       let cause =
266:         match run_finalizers ~runtime ~fail_key finalizers with
267:         | None -> primary
268:         | Some finalizer ->
269:             Cause.suppressed ~primary
270:               ~finalizer:(render_finalizer_cause ~error_renderer finalizer)
271:       in
272:       raise_cause fail_key cause
````

## File: lib/eta/runtime.ml
````ocaml
 1: type 'err t = 'err Runtime_core.t
 2: 
 3: let create_with_contract contract ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer ?sampler ?auto_instrument
 4:     ?logger ?meter ?random ?services ?capture_backtrace () =
 5:   Runtime_core.create_with_contract ~contract ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer ?sampler
 6:     ?auto_instrument ?logger ?meter ?random ?services ?capture_backtrace ()
 7: 
 8: let create_with_runtime backend ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer ?sampler ?auto_instrument
 9:     ?logger ?meter ?random ?services ?capture_backtrace () =
10:   create_with_contract (Runtime_contract.of_runtime backend) ?sleep ?now_ms
11:     ?tracer ?sampler ?auto_instrument ?logger ?meter ?random ?services
12:     ?capture_backtrace ()
13: 
14: let run_effect (runtime : 'err Runtime_core.t) (eff : ('a, 'err) Effect.t) :
15:     ('a, 'err) Exit.t =
16:   if runtime.Runtime_core.contract.Runtime_contract.in_worker_context () then
17:     invalid_arg
18:       "Eta.Runtime.run must not be called from inside a runtime worker callback";
19:   runtime.Runtime_core.tracer#with_task_context runtime.Runtime_core.contract
20:   @@ fun () ->
21:   let finalizers = ref [] in
22:   let frame =
23:     {
24:       Effect_core.runtime = Runtime_erasure.erase_runtime_error runtime;
25:       error_renderer = Effect_core.default_renderer;
26:       fail_key = runtime.Runtime_core.default_fail_key;
27:       sw = runtime.Runtime_core.outer_scope;
28:       finalizers;
29:     }
30:   in
31:   try
32:     Exit.Ok
33:       (Runtime_core.with_finalizers ~runtime
34:          ~fail_key:runtime.Runtime_core.default_fail_key
35:          ~error_renderer:frame.error_renderer finalizers (fun () ->
36:            Effect_core.run_to_value frame
37:              (Runtime_erasure.effect_of_public eff)))
38:   with
39:   | exn when Runtime_core.is_cancellation runtime.Runtime_core.contract exn ->
40:       raise exn
41:   | exn ->
42:       Exit.Error
43:         (Runtime_core.cause_of_exn_runtime runtime
44:            runtime.Runtime_core.default_fail_key exn)
45: 
46: let run runtime eff = run_effect runtime eff
47: 
48: let run_exn t eff =
49:   let pp_typed_failure fmt err =
50:     let raw = Obj.repr err in
51:     if Obj.is_int raw then Format.pp_print_int fmt (Obj.obj raw : int)
52:     else
53:       match Obj.tag raw with
54:       | tag when tag = Obj.string_tag ->
55:           Format.fprintf fmt "%S" (Obj.obj raw : string)
56:       | tag when tag = Obj.double_tag ->
57:           Format.pp_print_float fmt (Obj.obj raw : float)
58:       | _ -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "<typed failure>"
59:   in
60:   match run t eff with
61:   | Exit.Ok value -> value
62:   | Exit.Error (Cause.Die { exn; backtrace = Some backtrace; _ }) ->
63:       Printexc.raise_with_backtrace exn backtrace
64:   | Exit.Error (Cause.Die { exn; backtrace = None; _ }) -> raise exn
65:   | Exit.Error cause ->
66:       failwith
67:         (Format.asprintf "Eta.Runtime.run_exn: %a"
68:            (Cause.pp pp_typed_failure)
69:            cause)
70: 
71: let drain t = Runtime_core.wait_active_zero t
72: 
73: module Make (R : Runtime_contract.RUNTIME) = struct
74:   let backend = (module R : Runtime_contract.RUNTIME)
75: 
76:   let create ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer ?sampler ?auto_instrument ?logger ?meter ?random
77:       ?services ?capture_backtrace () =
78:     create_with_runtime backend ?sleep ?now_ms ?tracer ?sampler
79:       ?auto_instrument ?logger ?meter ?random ?services ?capture_backtrace ()
80: 
81:   let run = run
82:   let run_exn = run_exn
83:   let drain = drain
84: end
````

## File: lib/eta/runtime.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Runtime-backed interpreter for Eta effects. *)
 2: 
 3: type 'err t
 4: 
 5: val create_with_runtime :
 6:   (module Runtime_contract.RUNTIME) ->
 7:   ?sleep:(Duration.t -> unit) ->
 8:   ?now_ms:(unit -> int) ->
 9:   ?tracer:Capabilities.tracer ->
10:   ?sampler:Sampler.t ->
11:   ?auto_instrument:bool ->
12:   ?logger:Capabilities.logger ->
13:   ?meter:Capabilities.meter ->
14:   ?random:Capabilities.random ->
15:   ?services:Runtime_contract.service list ->
16:   ?capture_backtrace:bool ->
17:   unit ->
18:   'err t
19: (** Create an interpreter from a module-shaped backend runtime.
20: 
21:     Runtime packages should implement {!Runtime_contract.RUNTIME} and pass the
22:     module here. Eta currently erases that module into the interpreter's
23:     internal representation; the module boundary is the public contract for
24:     future functorized runtimes.
25: 
26:     [services] attaches optional runtime-package services. Root [eta] does not
27:     inspect their types; packages such as [eta_blocking] own their keys and
28:     retrieve them through [Effect.Expert].
29: 
30:     [capture_backtrace] controls whether unchecked exceptions captured as
31:     [Cause.Die] carry [Printexc.raw_backtrace]. It defaults to [true]. Disable
32:     it only for runtimes where defect-path allocation cost matters more than
33:     diagnostics. *)
34: 
35: module Make (_ : Runtime_contract.RUNTIME) : sig
36:   val create :
37:     ?sleep:(Duration.t -> unit) ->
38:     ?now_ms:(unit -> int) ->
39:     ?tracer:Capabilities.tracer ->
40:     ?sampler:Sampler.t ->
41:     ?auto_instrument:bool ->
42:     ?logger:Capabilities.logger ->
43:     ?meter:Capabilities.meter ->
44:     ?random:Capabilities.random ->
45:     ?services:Runtime_contract.service list ->
46:     ?capture_backtrace:bool ->
47:     unit ->
48:     'err t
49:   (** Create an interpreter using the runtime module applied to this functor. *)
50: 
51:   val run : 'err t -> ('a, 'err) Effect.t -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t
52: 
53:   val run_exn : 'err t -> ('a, 'err) Effect.t -> 'a
54:   val drain : 'err t -> unit
55: end
56: (** Functor-shaped runtime constructor for backends that want a statically
57:     applied module instead of passing a first-class module through each create
58:     call. *)
59: 
60: val run : 'err t -> ('a, 'err) Effect.t -> ('a, 'err) Exit.t
61: (** Run an eff to completion. *)
62: 
63: val run_exn : 'err t -> ('a, 'err) Effect.t -> 'a
64: (** Run an eff and raise on non-success. Prefer {!run} when
65:     inspecting failures. *)
66: 
67: val drain : 'err t -> unit
68: (** Wait until currently runtime-owned finite background fibers complete. *)
````

## File: lib/eta/semaphore.ml
````ocaml
  1: type waiter_state = Waiting | Resolved_unclaimed | Claimed | Cancelled
  2: 
  3: type waiter = {
  4:   permits : int;
  5:   contract : Runtime_contract.t;
  6:   resolver : unit Runtime_contract.resolver;
  7:   mutable state : waiter_state;
  8: }
  9: 
 10: type t = {
 11:   max_permits : int;
 12:   mutex : Sync_lock.t;
 13:   mutable available : int;
 14:   waiters : waiter Stdlib.Queue.t;
 15:   mutable cancelled_waiters : int;
 16: }
 17: 
 18: let make ~permits =
 19:   if permits <= 0 then invalid_arg "Eta.Semaphore.make: permits must be > 0";
 20:   {
 21:     max_permits = permits;
 22:     mutex = Sync_lock.create ();
 23:     available = permits;
 24:     waiters = Stdlib.Queue.create ();
 25:     cancelled_waiters = 0;
 26:   }
 27: 
 28: let available t = Sync_lock.use t.mutex @@ fun () -> t.available
 29: let waiting t =
 30:   Sync_lock.use t.mutex @@ fun () ->
 31:   let count = ref 0 in
 32:   Stdlib.Queue.iter
 33:     (fun waiter -> match waiter.state with Waiting -> incr count | _ -> ())
 34:     t.waiters;
 35:   !count
 36: 
 37: let cancelled_waiters t =
 38:   Sync_lock.use t.mutex @@ fun () -> t.cancelled_waiters
 39: 
 40: let with_lock t f =
 41:   Sync_lock.use t.mutex f
 42: 
 43: let with_lock_during_cancel contract t f =
 44:   contract.Runtime_contract.protect (fun () -> with_lock t f)
 45: 
 46: let validate_request name t n =
 47:   if n <= 0 || n > t.max_permits then
 48:     invalid_arg
 49:       ("Eta.Semaphore." ^ name ^ ": n must be between 1 and max_permits")
 50: 
 51: let rec take_ready_waiter t =
 52:   if Stdlib.Queue.is_empty t.waiters then None
 53:   else
 54:     let waiter = Stdlib.Queue.peek t.waiters in
 55:     match waiter.state with
 56:     | Waiting when t.available >= waiter.permits ->
 57:         ignore (Stdlib.Queue.take t.waiters : waiter);
 58:         Some waiter
 59:     | Waiting -> None
 60:     | Resolved_unclaimed | Claimed | Cancelled ->
 61:         ignore (Stdlib.Queue.take t.waiters : waiter);
 62:         take_ready_waiter t
 63: 
 64: let compact_cancelled_waiters_locked t =
 65:   if t.cancelled_waiters > 0 then (
 66:     let live = Stdlib.Queue.create () in
 67:     Stdlib.Queue.iter
 68:       (fun waiter ->
 69:         match waiter.state with
 70:         | Cancelled -> ()
 71:         | Waiting | Resolved_unclaimed | Claimed -> Stdlib.Queue.push waiter live)
 72:       t.waiters;
 73:     Stdlib.Queue.clear t.waiters;
 74:     Stdlib.Queue.iter (fun waiter -> Stdlib.Queue.push waiter t.waiters) live)
 75: 
 76: let rec wake_waiters_locked t =
 77:   match take_ready_waiter t with
 78:   | None -> ()
 79:   | Some waiter ->
 80:       t.available <- t.available - waiter.permits;
 81:       waiter.state <- Resolved_unclaimed;
 82:       waiter.contract.Runtime_contract.resolve_promise waiter.resolver ();
 83:       wake_waiters_locked t
 84: 
 85: let try_acquire t n =
 86:   validate_request "try_acquire" t n;
 87:   with_lock t @@ fun () ->
 88:   compact_cancelled_waiters_locked t;
 89:   wake_waiters_locked t;
 90:   if not (Stdlib.Queue.is_empty t.waiters) then false
 91:   else if t.available >= n then (
 92:     t.available <- t.available - n;
 93:     true)
 94:   else false
 95: 
 96: let release t n =
 97:   if n <= 0 then invalid_arg "Eta.Semaphore.release: n must be > 0";
 98:   with_lock t @@ fun () ->
 99:   if t.available + n > t.max_permits then
100:     invalid_arg "Eta.Semaphore.release: release would exceed semaphore capacity";
101:   t.available <- t.available + n;
102:   wake_waiters_locked t
103: 
104: let cleanup_waiter contract t waiter =
105:   with_lock_during_cancel contract t @@ fun () ->
106:   (* Invariant (validated): on cancellation a permit is returned only while the
107:      waiter has not yet taken ownership. [Waiting] was never granted a permit;
108:      [Resolved_unclaimed] was granted one that the fiber never claimed, so it
109:      is given back. Once [Claimed] (or already [Cancelled]) ownership has
110:      transferred to the caller and cleanup must NOT release — the caller
111:      (e.g. [with_permits], [with_permits_or_abort], or Pool) is responsible. *)
112:   match waiter.state with
113:   | Waiting ->
114:       waiter.state <- Cancelled;
115:       t.cancelled_waiters <- t.cancelled_waiters + 1;
116:       compact_cancelled_waiters_locked t;
117:       wake_waiters_locked t
118:   | Resolved_unclaimed ->
119:       waiter.state <- Cancelled;
120:       t.cancelled_waiters <- t.cancelled_waiters + 1;
121:       t.available <- min t.max_permits (t.available + waiter.permits);
122:       compact_cancelled_waiters_locked t;
123:       wake_waiters_locked t
124:   | Claimed | Cancelled -> ()
125: 
126: let acquire t n =
127:   validate_request "acquire" t n;
128:   Effect_erasure.effect_to_public
129:     (Effect_core.sync_frame (fun frame ->
130:          let contract = frame.Effect_core.runtime.Runtime_core.contract in
131:          let promise, resolver = contract.Runtime_contract.create_promise () in
132:          let waiter = { permits = n; contract; resolver; state = Waiting } in
133:          let acquisition =
134:            with_lock t @@ fun () ->
135:            compact_cancelled_waiters_locked t;
136:            wake_waiters_locked t;
137:            if Stdlib.Queue.is_empty t.waiters && t.available >= n then (
138:              t.available <- t.available - n;
139:              `Acquired)
140:            else (
141:              Stdlib.Queue.push waiter t.waiters;
142:              `Waiting (contract, promise, waiter))
143:          in
144:          match acquisition with
145:          | `Acquired -> ()
146:          | `Waiting (contract, promise, waiter) -> (
147:              try
148:                contract.Runtime_contract.await_promise promise;
149:                with_lock t @@ fun () ->
150:                match waiter.state with
151:                | Resolved_unclaimed -> waiter.state <- Claimed
152:                | Waiting | Claimed | Cancelled -> ()
153:              with exn ->
154:                (match contract.Runtime_contract.cancellation_reason exn with
155:                | Some _ -> cleanup_waiter contract t waiter
156:                | None -> ());
157:                raise exn)))
158: 
159: let with_permits_or_abort t n ~abort (f) =
160:   (* [claimed] means this combinator owns a granted permit. The finalizer is the
161:      only release path, so permits cannot escape through a result that a race or
162:      parent cancellation later discards. *)
163:   let claimed = Atomic.make false in
164:   let release_claimed =
165:     Effect.sync (fun () ->
166:         if Atomic.compare_and_set claimed true false then release t n)
167:   in
168:   let body =
169:     Effect.race
170:       [ acquire t n |> Effect.map (fun () -> Atomic.set claimed true; true);
171:         abort |> Effect.map (fun _ -> false) ]
172:     |> Effect.bind (fun acquired ->
173:            if acquired then f () |> Effect.map Option.some else Effect.pure None)
174:   in
175:   Effect.finally release_claimed body
176: 
177: let with_permits t n (f) =
178:   Effect.scoped
179:     (Effect.acquire_release
180:        ~acquire:(acquire t n)
181:        ~release:(fun () -> Effect.sync (fun () -> release t n))
182:     |> Effect.bind (fun () -> f ()))
````

## File: lib/http/core/method.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type t =
 4:   [ `GET
 5:   | `HEAD
 6:   | `POST
 7:   | `PUT
 8:   | `DELETE
 9:   | `CONNECT
10:   | `OPTIONS
11:   | `TRACE
12:   | `PATCH
13:   | `Other of string ]
14: 
15: let of_string method_ =
16:   match method_ with
17:   | "GET" -> `GET
18:   | "HEAD" -> `HEAD
19:   | "POST" -> `POST
20:   | "PUT" -> `PUT
21:   | "DELETE" -> `DELETE
22:   | "CONNECT" -> `CONNECT
23:   | "OPTIONS" -> `OPTIONS
24:   | "TRACE" -> `TRACE
25:   | "PATCH" -> `PATCH
26:   | other -> `Other other
27: 
28: let to_string = function
29:   | `GET -> "GET"
30:   | `HEAD -> "HEAD"
31:   | `POST -> "POST"
32:   | `PUT -> "PUT"
33:   | `DELETE -> "DELETE"
34:   | `CONNECT -> "CONNECT"
35:   | `OPTIONS -> "OPTIONS"
36:   | `TRACE -> "TRACE"
37:   | `PATCH -> "PATCH"
38:   | `Other method_ -> method_
39: 
40: let pp fmt t = Format.pp_print_string fmt (to_string t)
41: 
42: let is_idempotent = function
43:   | `GET | `HEAD | `PUT | `DELETE | `OPTIONS | `TRACE -> true
44:   | `POST | `PATCH | `CONNECT | `Other _ -> false
````

## File: lib/http/core/status.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type t = int
 4: 
 5: let of_int status =
 6:   if status >= 100 && status <= 599 then Some status else None
 7: 
 8: let unsafe_of_int status =
 9:   match of_int status with
10:   | Some status -> status
11:   | None -> invalid_arg "Http.Status.unsafe_of_int"
12: 
13: let to_int status = status
14: let class_ status = string_of_int (status / 100) ^ "xx"
15: let is_informational status = status >= 100 && status <= 199
16: let forbids_response_body status =
17:   is_informational status || status = 204 || status = 205 || status = 304
18: 
19: let is_success status = status >= 200 && status <= 299
20: let is_redirection status = status >= 300 && status <= 399
21: let is_client_error status = status >= 400 && status <= 499
22: let is_server_error status = status >= 500 && status <= 599
````

## File: lib/http/core/status.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP response status helpers. *)
 2: 
 3: type t = int
 4: 
 5: val of_int : int -> t option
 6: val unsafe_of_int : int -> t
 7: val to_int : t -> int
 8: val class_ : t -> string
 9: val is_informational : t -> bool
10: val forbids_response_body : t -> bool
11: val is_success : t -> bool
12: val is_redirection : t -> bool
13: val is_client_error : t -> bool
14: val is_server_error : t -> bool
````

## File: lib/http/core/url.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type scheme = Http | Https
  4: 
  5: type parse_error =
  6:   | Empty
  7:   | Missing_scheme
  8:   | Unsupported_scheme of string
  9:   | Missing_authority
 10:   | Missing_host
 11:   | Userinfo_not_supported
 12:   | Invalid_port of string
 13:   | Invalid_character of {
 14:       component : string;
 15:       index : int;
 16:       char : char;
 17:     }
 18: 
 19: type span = {
 20:   off : int;
 21:   len : int;
 22: }
 23: 
 24: type host_kind = Reg_name | Ip_literal
 25: 
 26: type t = {
 27:   raw : string;
 28:   scheme : scheme;
 29:   host : span;
 30:   host_kind : host_kind;
 31:   port : int option;
 32:   path : span option;
 33:   query : span option;
 34:   fragment : span option;
 35: }
 36: 
 37: let span ~off ~len = { off; len }
 38: let slice raw span = String.sub raw span.off span.len
 39: 
 40: let lowercase_ascii_slice raw off len =
 41:   let bytes = Bytes.create len in
 42:   for index = 0 to len - 1 do
 43:     Bytes.unsafe_set bytes index
 44:       (Eta.String_helpers.lowercase_ascii_char (String.unsafe_get raw (off + index)))
 45:   done;
 46:   Bytes.unsafe_to_string bytes
 47: 
 48: let is_hexdig = function
 49:   | '0' .. '9' | 'A' .. 'F' | 'a' .. 'f' -> true
 50:   | _ -> false
 51: 
 52: let is_unreserved = function
 53:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | '-' | '.' | '_' | '~' -> true
 54:   | _ -> false
 55: 
 56: let is_sub_delim = function
 57:   | '!' | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' ->
 58:       true
 59:   | _ -> false
 60: 
 61: let is_pchar = function
 62:   | ':' | '@' -> true
 63:   | char -> is_unreserved char || is_sub_delim char
 64: 
 65: let is_reg_name_char = function
 66:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | '-' | '.' | '_' | '~' | '!'
 67:   | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' ->
 68:       true
 69:   | _ -> false
 70: 
 71: let is_ipvfuture_tail_char = function
 72:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | ':' | '.' | '-' | '_' | '~'
 73:   | '!' | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' ->
 74:       true
 75:   | _ -> false
 76: 
 77: let invalid_character raw component index =
 78:   Error
 79:     (Invalid_character
 80:        { component; index; char = String.unsafe_get raw index })
 81: 
 82: let validate_percent_encoded_component raw component start finish is_allowed =
 83:   let rec loop index =
 84:     if index >= finish then Ok ()
 85:     else
 86:       match String.unsafe_get raw index with
 87:       | '%' ->
 88:           if index + 2 >= finish then invalid_character raw component index
 89:           else
 90:             let first = String.unsafe_get raw (index + 1) in
 91:             let second = String.unsafe_get raw (index + 2) in
 92:             if is_hexdig first && is_hexdig second then loop (index + 3)
 93:             else invalid_character raw component index
 94:       | char ->
 95:           if is_allowed char then loop (index + 1)
 96:           else invalid_character raw component index
 97:   in
 98:   loop start
 99: 
100: let validate_path raw start finish =
101:   validate_percent_encoded_component raw "path" start finish (function
102:     | '/' -> true
103:     | char -> is_pchar char)
104: 
105: let validate_query_like component raw start finish =
106:   validate_percent_encoded_component raw component start finish (function
107:     | '/' | '?' -> true
108:     | char -> is_pchar char)
109: 
110: let validate_query raw start finish = validate_query_like "query" raw start finish
111: let validate_fragment raw start finish =
112:   validate_query_like "fragment" raw start finish
113: 
114: let validate_reg_name raw component start finish =
115:   let rec loop index =
116:     if index >= finish then Ok ()
117:     else
118:       match String.unsafe_get raw index with
119:       | '%' ->
120:           if index + 2 >= finish then invalid_character raw component index
121:           else
122:             let first = String.unsafe_get raw (index + 1) in
123:             let second = String.unsafe_get raw (index + 2) in
124:             if is_hexdig first && is_hexdig second then loop (index + 3)
125:             else invalid_character raw component index
126:       | char ->
127:           if is_reg_name_char char then loop (index + 1)
128:           else invalid_character raw component index
129:   in
130:   loop start
131: 
132: let validate_ipvfuture raw component start finish =
133:   let rec version_loop index =
134:     if index >= finish then invalid_character raw component start
135:     else
136:       match String.unsafe_get raw index with
137:       | '.' when index = start + 1 -> invalid_character raw component index
138:       | '.' -> tail_loop (index + 1)
139:       | char ->
140:           if is_hexdig char then version_loop (index + 1)
141:           else invalid_character raw component index
142:   and tail_loop index =
143:     if index >= finish then invalid_character raw component (finish - 1)
144:     else tail_chars_loop index
145:   and tail_chars_loop index =
146:     if index >= finish then Ok ()
147:     else
148:       let char = String.unsafe_get raw index in
149:       if is_ipvfuture_tail_char char then tail_chars_loop (index + 1)
150:       else invalid_character raw component index
151:   in
152:   version_loop (start + 1)
153: 
154: let validate_ip_literal raw component start finish =
155:   match String.unsafe_get raw start with
156:   | 'v' | 'V' -> validate_ipvfuture raw component start finish
157:   | _ -> (
158:       match Ipaddr.V6.of_string (String.sub raw start (finish - start)) with
159:       | Ok _ -> Ok ()
160:       | Error _ -> invalid_character raw component start)
161: 
162: let validate_host raw host_kind start finish =
163:   match host_kind with
164:   | Reg_name -> validate_reg_name raw "host" start finish
165:   | Ip_literal -> validate_ip_literal raw "host" start finish
166: 
167: let find_from raw start stop char =
168:   let rec loop index =
169:     if index >= stop then None
170:     else if Char.equal raw.[index] char then Some index
171:     else loop (index + 1)
172:   in
173:   loop start
174: 
175: let find_first_path_mark raw start =
176:   let len = String.length raw in
177:   let rec loop index =
178:     if index >= len then len
179:     else
180:       match raw.[index] with
181:       | '/' | '?' | '#' -> index
182:       | _ -> loop (index + 1)
183:   in
184:   loop start
185: 
186: let[@zero_alloc] rec equal_ascii_case_insensitive_slice_loop raw token len index =
187:   index = len
188:   ||
189:   let c = Char.lowercase_ascii raw.[index] in
190:   Char.equal c token.[index]
191:   && equal_ascii_case_insensitive_slice_loop raw token len (index + 1)
192: 
193: let[@zero_alloc] equal_ascii_case_insensitive_prefix raw token len =
194:   len = String.length token
195:   && equal_ascii_case_insensitive_slice_loop raw token len 0
196: 
197: let parse_scheme raw =
198:   match find_from raw 0 (String.length raw) ':' with
199:   | None -> Error Missing_scheme
200:   | Some 0 -> Error Missing_scheme
201:   | Some colon ->
202:       if equal_ascii_case_insensitive_prefix raw "http" colon then Ok (Http, colon)
203:       else if equal_ascii_case_insensitive_prefix raw "https" colon then
204:         Ok (Https, colon)
205:       else Error (Unsupported_scheme (lowercase_ascii_slice raw 0 colon))
206: 
207: let parse_port raw start finish =
208:   if start = finish then Error (Invalid_port "")
209:   else
210:     let rec loop index acc =
211:       if index >= finish then
212:         if acc >= 1 && acc <= 65535 then Ok acc
213:         else Error (Invalid_port (String.sub raw start (finish - start)))
214:       else
215:         match raw.[index] with
216:         | '0' .. '9' as c ->
217:             let next = (acc * 10) + Char.code c - Char.code '0' in
218:             if next > 65535 then
219:               Error (Invalid_port (String.sub raw start (finish - start)))
220:             else loop (index + 1) next
221:         | _ -> Error (Invalid_port (String.sub raw start (finish - start)))
222:     in
223:     loop start 0
224: 
225: let parse_authority raw start finish =
226:   if start >= finish then Error Missing_authority
227:   else
228:     match find_from raw start finish '@' with
229:     | Some _ -> Error Userinfo_not_supported
230:     | None -> (
231:         match raw.[start] with
232:         | '[' -> (
233:             match find_from raw (start + 1) finish ']' with
234:             | None -> Error Missing_host
235:             | Some close when close = start + 1 -> Error Missing_host
236:             | Some close ->
237:                 let host = span ~off:(start + 1) ~len:(close - start - 1) in
238:                 if close + 1 = finish then Ok (host, Ip_literal, None)
239:                 else if Char.equal raw.[close + 1] ':' then
240:                   parse_port raw (close + 2) finish
241:                   |> Result.map (fun port -> (host, Ip_literal, Some port))
242:                 else Error (Invalid_port (String.sub raw (close + 1) (finish - close - 1))))
243:         | _ ->
244:             let colon = find_from raw start finish ':' in
245:             let host_finish = Option.value ~default:finish colon in
246:             if host_finish = start then Error Missing_host
247:             else
248:               let host = span ~off:start ~len:(host_finish - start) in
249:               match colon with
250:               | None -> Ok (host, Reg_name, None)
251:               | Some colon ->
252:                   parse_port raw (colon + 1) finish
253:                   |> Result.map (fun port -> (host, Reg_name, Some port)))
254: 
255: let parse_path_query_fragment raw start =
256:   let len = String.length raw in
257:   let path_start = if start < len && Char.equal raw.[start] '/' then start else len in
258:   let fragment_mark = find_from raw start len '#' in
259:   let query_end = Option.value ~default:len fragment_mark in
260:   let query_mark = find_from raw start query_end '?' in
261:   let path_end =
262:     match (query_mark, fragment_mark) with
263:     | Some query, Some fragment -> min query fragment
264:     | Some query, None -> query
265:     | None, Some fragment -> fragment
266:     | None, None -> len
267:   in
268:   let path =
269:     if path_start = len || path_end <= path_start then None
270:     else Some (span ~off:path_start ~len:(path_end - path_start))
271:   in
272:   let query =
273:     match query_mark with
274:     | None -> None
275:     | Some query_start ->
276:         Some (span ~off:(query_start + 1) ~len:(query_end - query_start - 1))
277:   in
278:   let fragment =
279:     match fragment_mark with
280:     | None -> None
281:     | Some fragment_start ->
282:         Some
283:           (span ~off:(fragment_start + 1)
284:              ~len:(len - fragment_start - 1))
285:   in
286:   (path, query, fragment)
287: 
288: let parse raw =
289:   if String.equal raw "" then Error Empty
290:   else
291:     match parse_scheme raw with
292:     | Error _ as error -> error
293:     | Ok (scheme, colon) ->
294:         let len = String.length raw in
295:         let authority_start = colon + 1 in
296:         if authority_start + 1 >= len
297:            || not
298:                 (Char.equal raw.[authority_start] '/'
299:                 && Char.equal raw.[authority_start + 1] '/')
300:         then Error Missing_authority
301:         else
302:           let authority_start = authority_start + 2 in
303:           let authority_end = find_first_path_mark raw authority_start in
304:           (match parse_authority raw authority_start authority_end with
305:           | Error _ as error -> error
306:           | Ok (host, host_kind, port) -> (
307:               match validate_host raw host_kind host.off (host.off + host.len) with
308:               | Error _ as error -> error
309:               | Ok () ->
310:                   let path, query, fragment =
311:                     parse_path_query_fragment raw authority_end
312:                   in
313:                   let validate_opt validate = function
314:                     | None -> Ok ()
315:                     | Some span ->
316:                         validate raw span.off (span.off + span.len)
317:                   in
318:                   Result.bind (validate_opt validate_path path) (fun () ->
319:                       Result.bind (validate_opt validate_query query) (fun () ->
320:                           Result.map
321:                             (fun () ->
322:                               {
323:                                 raw;
324:                                 scheme;
325:                                 host;
326:                                 host_kind;
327:                                 port;
328:                                 path;
329:                                 query;
330:                                 fragment;
331:                               })
332:                             (validate_opt validate_fragment fragment)))))
333: 
334: let pp_parse_error fmt = function
335:   | Empty -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "empty URL"
336:   | Missing_scheme -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "missing URL scheme"
337:   | Unsupported_scheme scheme ->
338:       Format.fprintf fmt "unsupported URL scheme %S" scheme
339:   | Missing_authority -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "missing URL authority"
340:   | Missing_host -> Format.pp_print_string fmt "missing URL host"
341:   | Userinfo_not_supported ->
342:       Format.pp_print_string fmt "userinfo is not supported"
343:   | Invalid_port port -> Format.fprintf fmt "invalid URL port %S" port
344:   | Invalid_character { component; index; char } ->
345:       Format.fprintf fmt "invalid character %C in URL %s at byte %d" char
346:         component index
347: 
348: let parse_error_to_string error = Format.asprintf "%a" pp_parse_error error
349: 
350: let of_string raw =
351:   match parse raw with
352:   | Ok t -> t
353:   | Error error -> invalid_arg ("Http.Url.of_string: " ^ parse_error_to_string error)
354: 
355: let to_string t = t.raw
356: let scheme t = t.scheme
357: let scheme_to_string = function Http -> "http" | Https -> "https"
358: let host t = lowercase_ascii_slice t.raw t.host.off t.host.len
359: let port t = t.port
360: let default_port = function Http -> 80 | Https -> 443
361: let effective_port t = Option.value ~default:(default_port t.scheme) t.port
362: let path t = match t.path with None -> "/" | Some span -> slice t.raw span
363: let query t = Option.map (slice t.raw) t.query
364: let fragment t = Option.map (slice t.raw) t.fragment
365: 
366: let authority t =
367:   let host = host t in
368:   let host =
369:     match t.host_kind with Reg_name -> host | Ip_literal -> "[" ^ host ^ "]"
370:   in
371:   match t.port with
372:   | None -> host
373:   | Some port -> host ^ ":" ^ string_of_int port
374: 
375: let origin_form t =
376:   match query t with
377:   | None -> path t
378:   | Some query -> path t ^ "?" ^ query
379: 
380: let[@zero_alloc] has_capacity dst pos len =
381:   pos >= 0 && len >= 0 && pos <= Bytes.length dst - len
382: 
383: let[@zero_alloc] blit_literal dst pos value =
384:   let len = String.length value in
385:   if has_capacity dst pos len then (
386:     Bytes.blit_string value 0 dst pos len;
387:     pos + len)
388:   else -1
389: 
390: let[@zero_alloc] blit_span dst pos raw span =
391:   if has_capacity dst pos span.len then (
392:     Bytes.blit_string raw span.off dst pos span.len;
393:     pos + span.len)
394:   else -1
395: 
396: let[@zero_alloc] lowercase_ascii c =
397:   match c with 'A' .. 'Z' -> Char.chr (Char.code c + 32) | _ -> c
398: 
399: let[@zero_alloc] blit_lowercase_span dst pos raw span =
400:   if has_capacity dst pos span.len then (
401:     for offset = 0 to span.len - 1 do
402:       Bytes.unsafe_set dst (pos + offset)
403:         (lowercase_ascii (String.unsafe_get raw (span.off + offset)))
404:     done;
405:     pos + span.len)
406:   else -1
407: 
408: let[@zero_alloc] decimal_digits value =
409:   let rec loop digits n =
410:     if n < 10 then digits else loop (digits + 1) (n / 10)
411:   in
412:   loop 1 value
413: 
414: let[@zero_alloc] blit_int dst pos value =
415:   if value < 0 then -1
416:   else
417:     let digits = decimal_digits value in
418:     if not (has_capacity dst pos digits) then -1
419:     else (
420:       let n = ref value in
421:       for index = digits - 1 downto 0 do
422:         let digit = !n mod 10 in
423:         Bytes.unsafe_set dst (pos + index) (Char.chr (Char.code '0' + digit));
424:         n := !n / 10
425:       done;
426:       pos + digits)
427: 
428: let[@zero_alloc] blit_authority_raw dst pos t =
429:   let pos =
430:     match t.host_kind with
431:     | Reg_name -> blit_lowercase_span dst pos t.raw t.host
432:     | Ip_literal ->
433:         let pos = blit_literal dst pos "[" in
434:         if pos < 0 then pos
435:         else
436:           let pos = blit_lowercase_span dst pos t.raw t.host in
437:           if pos < 0 then pos else blit_literal dst pos "]"
438:   in
439:   if pos < 0 then pos
440:   else
441:     match t.port with
442:     | None -> pos
443:     | Some port ->
444:         let pos = blit_literal dst pos ":" in
445:         if pos < 0 then pos else blit_int dst pos port
446: 
447: let blit_authority dst ~pos t = blit_authority_raw dst pos t
448: 
449: let[@zero_alloc] blit_origin_form_raw dst pos t =
450:   let pos =
451:     match t.path with
452:     | None -> blit_literal dst pos "/"
453:     | Some path -> blit_span dst pos t.raw path
454:   in
455:   if pos < 0 then pos
456:   else
457:     match t.query with
458:     | None -> pos
459:     | Some query ->
````
