````ocaml
460:         let pos = blit_literal dst pos "?" in
461:         if pos < 0 then pos else blit_span dst pos t.raw query
462: 
463: let blit_origin_form dst ~pos t = blit_origin_form_raw dst pos t
464: 
465: let redacted t = Redaction.uri t.raw
````

## File: lib/http/error/error.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type protocol = H1 | H2 | Unknown
  4: 
  5: type layer =
  6:   | Tcp
  7:   | Tls
  8:   | Alpn
  9:   | Pool
 10:   | Http_request
 11:   | Http_response
 12:   | Body_decode
 13:   | Cancellation
 14: 
 15: type retryability =
 16:   | Retryable
 17:   | Retryable_if_body_replayable
 18:   | Not_retryable
 19: 
 20: type tls_stage = Tls_handshake | Alpn_negotiation
 21: 
 22: type certificate_reason =
 23:   | Expired
 24:   | Name_mismatch
 25:   | Untrusted_chain
 26:   | Revoked
 27:   | Certificate_policy_error of string
 28: 
 29: type kind =
 30:   | Dns_error of { host : string; message : string }
 31:   | Connect_error of { message : string }
 32:   | Connect_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 33:   | Tls_handshake_error of { stage : tls_stage; message : string }
 34:   | Tls_certificate_error of { reason : certificate_reason; message : string }
 35:   | Connection_closed of { during : layer }
 36:   | Pool_shutdown
 37:   | Pool_acquire_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 38:   | Response_header_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 39:   | Response_body_idle_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 40:   | Total_request_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 41:   | HTTP_status of { status : int; headers : (string * string) list }
 42:   | Decode_error of { codec : string; message : string }
 43:   | Body_too_large of { limit : int; length : int }
 44:   | Connection_protocol_violation of { kind : string; message : string }
 45:   | Hpack_decode_overflow of { decoded_bytes : int; limit_bytes : int }
 46:   | Continuation_flood of {
 47:       accumulated_bytes : int;
 48:       limit_bytes : int;
 49:       frames : int;
 50:     }
 51:   | Stream_admission_rejected of { limit : int }
 52:   | Rst_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
 53:   | Ping_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
 54:   | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded of {
 55:       observed_count : int;
 56:       limit : int;
 57:     }
 58:   | Window_update_count_exceeded of {
 59:       observed_count : int;
 60:       limit : int;
 61:     }
 62:   | Settings_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
 63:   | Response_header_count_exceeded of {
 64:       observed_count : int;
 65:       limit : int;
 66:     }
 67:   | Header_invalid of { reason : string }
 68: 
 69: type context = {
 70:   method_ : string;
 71:   uri : string;
 72:   protocol : protocol;
 73: }
 74: 
 75: type t = {
 76:   context : context;
 77:   kind : kind;
 78: }
 79: 
 80: let make ?(protocol = Unknown) ~method_ ~uri kind =
 81:   { context = { method_; uri; protocol }; kind }
 82: 
 83: let protocol_to_string = function
 84:   | H1 -> "h1"
 85:   | H2 -> "h2"
 86:   | Unknown -> "unknown"
 87: 
 88: let layer_to_string = function
 89:   | Tcp -> "tcp"
 90:   | Tls -> "tls"
 91:   | Alpn -> "alpn"
 92:   | Pool -> "pool"
 93:   | Http_request -> "http_request"
 94:   | Http_response -> "http_response"
 95:   | Body_decode -> "body_decode"
 96:   | Cancellation -> "cancellation"
 97: 
 98: let retryability_to_string = function
 99:   | Retryable -> "retryable"
100:   | Retryable_if_body_replayable -> "retryable_if_body_replayable"
101:   | Not_retryable -> "not_retryable"
102: 
103: let kind_name = function
104:   | Dns_error _ -> "Dns_error"
105:   | Connect_error _ -> "Connect_error"
106:   | Connect_timeout _ -> "Connect_timeout"
107:   | Tls_handshake_error _ -> "Tls_handshake_error"
108:   | Tls_certificate_error _ -> "Tls_certificate_error"
109:   | Connection_closed _ -> "Connection_closed"
110:   | Pool_shutdown -> "Pool_shutdown"
111:   | Pool_acquire_timeout _ -> "Pool_acquire_timeout"
112:   | Response_header_timeout _ -> "Response_header_timeout"
113:   | Response_body_idle_timeout _ -> "Response_body_idle_timeout"
114:   | Total_request_timeout _ -> "Total_request_timeout"
115:   | HTTP_status _ -> "HTTP_status"
116:   | Decode_error _ -> "Decode_error"
117:   | Body_too_large _ -> "Body_too_large"
118:   | Connection_protocol_violation _ -> "Connection_protocol_violation"
119:   | Hpack_decode_overflow _ -> "Hpack_decode_overflow"
120:   | Continuation_flood _ -> "Continuation_flood"
121:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ -> "Stream_admission_rejected"
122:   | Rst_count_exceeded _ -> "Rst_count_exceeded"
123:   | Ping_count_exceeded _ -> "Ping_count_exceeded"
124:   | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded _ -> "Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded"
125:   | Window_update_count_exceeded _ -> "Window_update_count_exceeded"
126:   | Settings_count_exceeded _ -> "Settings_count_exceeded"
127:   | Response_header_count_exceeded _ -> "Response_header_count_exceeded"
128:   | Header_invalid _ -> "Header_invalid"
129: 
130: let layer t =
131:   match t.kind with
132:   | Dns_error _ | Connect_error _ | Connect_timeout _ -> Tcp
133:   | Tls_handshake_error { stage = Tls_handshake; _ } -> Tls
134:   | Tls_handshake_error { stage = Alpn_negotiation; _ } -> Alpn
135:   | Tls_certificate_error _ -> Tls
136:   | Connection_closed { during } -> during
137:   | Pool_shutdown | Pool_acquire_timeout _ -> Pool
138:   | Response_header_timeout _ | Total_request_timeout _ -> Http_request
139:   | Response_body_idle_timeout _ | HTTP_status _ -> Http_response
140:   | Decode_error _ | Body_too_large _ -> Body_decode
141:   | Connection_protocol_violation _ | Hpack_decode_overflow _
142:   | Continuation_flood _ | Stream_admission_rejected _ | Rst_count_exceeded _
143:   | Ping_count_exceeded _ | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded _
144:   | Window_update_count_exceeded _ | Settings_count_exceeded _
145:   | Response_header_count_exceeded _ | Header_invalid _ ->
146:       Http_response
147: 
148: let retryability t =
149:   match t.kind with
150:   | Tls_certificate_error _ -> Not_retryable
151:   | Tls_handshake_error { stage = Alpn_negotiation; _ } -> Not_retryable
152:   | Decode_error _ -> Retryable_if_body_replayable
153:   | Body_too_large _ -> Not_retryable
154:   | HTTP_status { status; _ } when status = 408 || status = 429 ->
155:       Retryable_if_body_replayable
156:   | HTTP_status { status; _ } when status >= 500 && status <= 599 ->
157:       Retryable_if_body_replayable
158:   | HTTP_status _ -> Not_retryable
159:   | Connection_protocol_violation _ | Hpack_decode_overflow _
160:   | Continuation_flood _ | Stream_admission_rejected _ | Rst_count_exceeded _
161:   | Ping_count_exceeded _ | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded _
162:   | Window_update_count_exceeded _ | Settings_count_exceeded _
163:   | Response_header_count_exceeded _ | Header_invalid _ ->
164:       Not_retryable
165:   | Pool_shutdown -> Not_retryable
166:   | Dns_error _ | Connect_error _ | Connect_timeout _ | Pool_acquire_timeout _
167:   | Response_header_timeout _ | Response_body_idle_timeout _
168:   | Total_request_timeout _ | Connection_closed _ | Tls_handshake_error _ ->
169:       Retryable_if_body_replayable
170: 
171: let status t = match t.kind with HTTP_status { status; _ } -> Some status | _ -> None
172: 
173: let status_class t =
174:   match status t with
175:   | None -> None
176:   | Some status when status >= 100 && status <= 599 ->
177:       Some (string_of_int (status / 100) ^ "xx")
178:   | Some _ -> Some "invalid"
179: 
180: let error_class t =
181:   match t.kind with
182:   | Dns_error _ -> "dns_error"
183:   | Connect_error _ -> "connect_error"
184:   | Connect_timeout _ -> "connect_timeout"
185:   | Tls_handshake_error { stage = Tls_handshake; _ } -> "tls_handshake_error"
186:   | Tls_handshake_error { stage = Alpn_negotiation; _ } ->
187:       "alpn_negotiation_error"
188:   | Tls_certificate_error _ -> "tls_certificate_error"
189:   | Connection_closed _ -> "connection_closed"
190:   | Pool_shutdown -> "pool_shutdown"
191:   | Pool_acquire_timeout _ -> "pool_acquire_timeout"
192:   | Response_header_timeout _ -> "response_header_timeout"
193:   | Response_body_idle_timeout _ -> "response_body_idle_timeout"
194:   | Total_request_timeout _ -> "total_request_timeout"
195:   | HTTP_status _ -> (
196:       match status_class t with
197:       | Some class_ -> "http_status_" ^ class_
198:       | None -> "http_status")
199:   | Decode_error _ -> "decode_error"
200:   | Body_too_large _ -> "body_too_large"
201:   | Connection_protocol_violation _ -> "connection_protocol_violation"
202:   | Hpack_decode_overflow _ -> "hpack_decode_overflow"
203:   | Continuation_flood _ -> "continuation_flood"
204:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ -> "stream_admission_rejected"
205:   | Rst_count_exceeded _ -> "rst_count_exceeded"
206:   | Ping_count_exceeded _ -> "ping_count_exceeded"
207:   | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded _ -> "empty_data_frame_count_exceeded"
208:   | Window_update_count_exceeded _ -> "window_update_count_exceeded"
209:   | Settings_count_exceeded _ -> "settings_count_exceeded"
210:   | Response_header_count_exceeded _ -> "response_header_count_exceeded"
211:   | Header_invalid _ -> "header_invalid"
212: 
213: let headers t =
214:   match t.kind with HTTP_status { headers; _ } -> headers | _ -> []
215: 
216: let pp fmt t =
217:   Format.fprintf fmt
218:     "eta-http error=%s method=%s uri=%s protocol=%s layer=%s retryability=%s \
219:      error_class=%s"
220:     (kind_name t.kind) t.context.method_ (Redaction.uri t.context.uri)
221:     (protocol_to_string t.context.protocol)
222:     (layer_to_string (layer t))
223:     (retryability_to_string (retryability t))
224:     (error_class t);
225:   (match status t with
226:   | None -> ()
227:   | Some status ->
228:       Format.fprintf fmt " status=%d status_class=%s" status
229:         (Option.value ~default:"none" (status_class t)));
230:   (match headers t with
231:   | [] -> ()
232:   | headers ->
233:       let pp_header fmt (name, value) =
234:         let value = if Redaction.is_sensitive name then "<redacted>" else value in
235:         Format.fprintf fmt "%s=%s" name value
236:       in
237:       Format.fprintf fmt " headers=[%a]"
238:         (Format.pp_print_list ~pp_sep:(fun fmt () -> Format.fprintf fmt "; ")
239:            pp_header)
240:         headers);
241:   Format.fprintf fmt " body=<omitted>"
242: 
243: let to_string t = Format.asprintf "%a" pp t
````

## File: lib/http/error/error.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Typed eta-http client errors. *)
 2: 
 3: type protocol = H1 | H2 | Unknown
 4: 
 5: type layer =
 6:   | Tcp
 7:   | Tls
 8:   | Alpn
 9:   | Pool
10:   | Http_request
11:   | Http_response
12:   | Body_decode
13:   | Cancellation
14: 
15: type retryability =
16:   | Retryable
17:   | Retryable_if_body_replayable
18:   | Not_retryable
19: 
20: type tls_stage = Tls_handshake | Alpn_negotiation
21: 
22: type certificate_reason =
23:   | Expired
24:   | Name_mismatch
25:   | Untrusted_chain
26:   | Revoked
27:   | Certificate_policy_error of string
28: 
29: type kind =
30:   | Dns_error of { host : string; message : string }
31:   | Connect_error of { message : string }
32:   | Connect_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
33:   | Tls_handshake_error of { stage : tls_stage; message : string }
34:   | Tls_certificate_error of { reason : certificate_reason; message : string }
35:   | Connection_closed of { during : layer }
36:   | Pool_shutdown
37:   | Pool_acquire_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
38:   | Response_header_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
39:   | Response_body_idle_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
40:   | Total_request_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
41:   | HTTP_status of { status : int; headers : (string * string) list }
42:   | Decode_error of { codec : string; message : string }
43:   | Body_too_large of { limit : int; length : int }
44:   | Connection_protocol_violation of { kind : string; message : string }
45:   | Hpack_decode_overflow of { decoded_bytes : int; limit_bytes : int }
46:   | Continuation_flood of {
47:       accumulated_bytes : int;
48:       limit_bytes : int;
49:       frames : int;
50:     }
51:   | Stream_admission_rejected of { limit : int }
52:   | Rst_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
53:   | Ping_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
54:   | Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded of {
55:       observed_count : int;
56:       limit : int;
57:     }
58:   | Window_update_count_exceeded of {
59:       observed_count : int;
60:       limit : int;
61:     }
62:   | Settings_count_exceeded of { observed_count : int; limit : int }
63:   | Response_header_count_exceeded of {
64:       observed_count : int;
65:       limit : int;
66:     }
67:   | Header_invalid of { reason : string }
68: 
69: type context = {
70:   method_ : string;
71:   uri : string;
72:   protocol : protocol;
73: }
74: 
75: type t = {
76:   context : context;
77:   kind : kind;
78: }
79: 
80: val make : ?protocol:protocol -> method_:string -> uri:string -> kind -> t
81: val protocol_to_string : protocol -> string
82: val layer_to_string : layer -> string
83: val retryability_to_string : retryability -> string
84: val kind_name : kind -> string
85: val layer : t -> layer
86: val retryability : t -> retryability
87: val status : t -> int option
88: val status_class : t -> string option
89: val error_class : t -> string
90: val headers : t -> (string * string) list
91: val pp : Format.formatter -> t -> unit
92: val to_string : t -> string
````

## File: lib/http/h2/informational_filter.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: (* ocaml-h2 currently delivers 1xx informational responses through the same
  4:    final-response path Eta exposes to callers. This ingress filter is the
  5:    smallest local boundary that preserves Eta's public client contract without
  6:    forking H2: it removes interim HEADERS frames before H2 updates stream
  7:    state, and forwards DATA/RST/final HEADERS unchanged except when HPACK has
  8:    to be re-emitted after decoding. If H2 grows native interim-response
  9:    handling, this module should disappear rather than become another protocol
 10:    layer. *)
 11: 
 12: type pending_headers = {
 13:   stream_id : int;
 14:   end_stream : bool;
 15:   block : Buffer.t;
 16: }
 17: 
 18: type open_header_block =
 19:   | Response_headers of pending_headers
 20:   | Passthrough_headers of { stream_id : int }
 21: 
 22: type t = {
 23:   decoder : Hpack.Decoder.t;
 24:   encoder : Hpack.Encoder.t;
 25:   output : Buffer.t;
 26:   final_seen : (int, unit) Hashtbl.t;
 27:   pending : Buffer.t;
 28:   mutable pending_off : int;
 29:   mutable headers : open_header_block option;
 30: }
 31: 
 32: let frame_data = 0x0
 33: let frame_headers = 0x1
 34: let frame_rst_stream = 0x3
 35: let frame_push_promise = 0x5
 36: let frame_continuation = 0x9
 37: 
 38: let flag_end_stream = 0x1
 39: let flag_end_headers = 0x4
 40: let flag_padded = 0x8
 41: let flag_priority = 0x20
 42: 
 43: let max_frame_payload = 16 * 1024
 44: 
 45: let create () =
 46:   {
 47:     decoder = Hpack.Decoder.create 4096;
 48:     encoder = Hpack.Encoder.create 4096;
 49:     output = Buffer.create 4096;
 50:     final_seen = Hashtbl.create 16;
 51:     pending = Buffer.create 4096;
 52:     pending_off = 0;
 53:     headers = None;
 54:   }
 55: 
 56: let error message =
 57:   Error
 58:     (Error.Connection_protocol_violation
 59:        { kind = "h2_informational_filter"; message })
 60: 
 61: let code s i = Char.code (String.unsafe_get s i)
 62: 
 63: let append_pending t data ~off ~len =
 64:   if len > 0 then Buffer.add_substring t.pending data off len
 65: 
 66: let compact_pending t =
 67:   if t.pending_off > 0 then
 68:     let total = Buffer.length t.pending in
 69:     if t.pending_off >= total then (
 70:       Buffer.clear t.pending;
 71:       t.pending_off <- 0)
 72:     else (
 73:       let remaining = Buffer.sub t.pending t.pending_off (total - t.pending_off) in
 74:       Buffer.clear t.pending;
 75:       Buffer.add_string t.pending remaining;
 76:       t.pending_off <- 0)
 77: 
 78: let emit_frame t ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id payload =
 79:   Buffer.add_string t.output
 80:     (Frame.header ~length:(String.length payload)
 81:        ~frame_type:(Other frame_type) ~flags ~stream_id);
 82:   Buffer.add_string t.output payload
 83: 
 84: let emit_header_block t ~stream_id ~end_stream block =
 85:   let total = String.length block in
 86:   let rec loop off first =
 87:     let remaining = total - off in
 88:     if remaining <= max_frame_payload then
 89:       let flags =
 90:         flag_end_headers
 91:         lor (if first && end_stream then flag_end_stream else 0)
 92:       in
 93:       let frame_type = if first then frame_headers else frame_continuation in
 94:       emit_frame t ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id
 95:         (String.sub block off remaining)
 96:     else
 97:       let flags = if first && end_stream then flag_end_stream else 0 in
 98:       let frame_type = if first then frame_headers else frame_continuation in
 99:       emit_frame t ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id
100:         (String.sub block off max_frame_payload);
101:       loop (off + max_frame_payload) false
102:   in
103:   loop 0 true
104: 
105: let header_block_fragment flags payload =
106:   let len = String.length payload in
107:   let pos = ref 0 in
108:   let pad_len =
109:     if flags land flag_padded = 0 then 0
110:     else if len = 0 then -1
111:     else (
112:       pos := 1;
113:       code payload 0)
114:   in
115:   if pad_len < 0 then error "PADDED HEADERS frame is missing Pad Length"
116:   else (
117:     if flags land flag_priority <> 0 then pos := !pos + 5;
118:     if !pos > len || !pos + pad_len > len then
119:       error "HEADERS padding/priority fields exceed frame payload"
120:     else Ok (String.sub payload !pos (len - !pos - pad_len)))
121: 
122: let decode_headers t block =
123:   match
124:     Angstrom.parse_string ~consume:Angstrom.Consume.All
125:       (Hpack.Decoder.decode_headers t.decoder)
126:       block
127:   with
128:   | Ok (Ok headers) -> Ok headers
129:   | Ok (Error Hpack.Decoding_error) -> error "HPACK decoding error"
130:   | Error message -> error ("HPACK parser error: " ^ message)
131: 
132: let encode_headers t headers =
133:   let faraday = Faraday.create 0x1000 in
134:   List.iter (Hpack.Encoder.encode_header t.encoder faraday) headers;
135:   Faraday.serialize_to_string faraday
136: 
137: let status_code headers =
138:   let rec loop = function
139:     | [] -> None
140:     | ({ Hpack.name = ":status"; value; _ } : Hpack.header) :: _ ->
141:         int_of_string_opt value
142:     | _ :: rest -> loop rest
143:   in
144:   loop headers
145: 
146: let has_status_header headers =
147:   List.exists
148:     (fun ({ Hpack.name; _ } : Hpack.header) -> String.equal name ":status")
149:     headers
150: 
151: let has_pseudo_header headers =
152:   List.exists
153:     (fun ({ Hpack.name; _ } : Hpack.header) ->
154:       String.length name > 0 && Char.equal name.[0] ':')
155:     headers
156: 
157: let is_informational = function
158:   | Some status -> status >= 100 && status < 200 && status <> 101
159:   | None -> false
160: 
161: let complete_headers t { stream_id; end_stream; block } =
162:   let already_final = Hashtbl.mem t.final_seen stream_id in
163:   match decode_headers t (Buffer.contents block) with
164:   | Error _ as error -> error
165:   | Ok headers ->
166:       let status = status_code headers in
167:       if already_final && has_pseudo_header headers then
168:         error "HTTP/2 trailers contained a pseudo-header"
169:       else if has_status_header headers && Option.is_none status then
170:         error "HTTP/2 response used invalid :status"
171:       else if Option.equal Int.equal status (Some 101) then
172:         error "HTTP/2 response used forbidden :status 101"
173:       else if
174:         match status with
175:         | Some status -> status < 100 || status > 599
176:         | None -> false
177:       then error "HTTP/2 response used invalid :status"
178:       else if (not already_final) && is_informational status then
179:         if end_stream then error "informational response carried END_STREAM"
180:         else Ok ()
181:       else (
182:         if not already_final then Hashtbl.replace t.final_seen stream_id ();
183:         emit_header_block t ~stream_id ~end_stream (encode_headers t headers);
184:         if end_stream then Hashtbl.remove t.final_seen stream_id;
185:         Ok ())
186: 
187: let pass_frame t frame_type flags stream_id ~off ~total =
188:   if frame_type = frame_rst_stream then Hashtbl.remove t.final_seen stream_id;
189:   if frame_type = frame_data && flags land flag_end_stream <> 0 then
190:     Hashtbl.remove t.final_seen stream_id;
191:   Buffer.add_string t.output (Buffer.sub t.pending off total);
192:   Ok ()
193: 
194: let handle_headers t ~flags ~stream_id payload =
195:   match t.headers with
196:   | Some _ -> error "HEADERS arrived while a header block is open"
197:   | None -> (
198:       match header_block_fragment flags payload with
199:       | Error _ as error -> error
200:       | Ok fragment ->
201:           let pending =
202:             {
203:               stream_id;
204:               end_stream = flags land flag_end_stream <> 0;
205:               block = Buffer.create (String.length fragment);
206:             }
207:           in
208:           Buffer.add_string pending.block fragment;
209:           if flags land flag_end_headers <> 0 then complete_headers t pending
210:           else (
211:             t.headers <- Some (Response_headers pending);
212:             Ok ()))
213: 
214: let handle_push_promise t ~flags ~stream_id ~off ~total =
215:   if flags land flag_end_headers = 0 then
216:     t.headers <- Some (Passthrough_headers { stream_id });
217:   pass_frame t frame_push_promise flags stream_id ~off ~total
218: 
219: let handle_continuation t ~flags ~stream_id payload =
220:   match t.headers with
221:   | None -> error "CONTINUATION arrived without an open header block"
222:   | Some (Passthrough_headers _) ->
223:       error "CONTINUATION arrived without a response header block"
224:   | Some (Response_headers pending) when pending.stream_id <> stream_id ->
225:       error "CONTINUATION stream does not match open header block"
226:   | Some (Response_headers pending) ->
227:       Buffer.add_string pending.block payload;
228:       if flags land flag_end_headers = 0 then Ok ()
229:       else (
230:         t.headers <- None;
231:         complete_headers t pending)
232: 
233: let handle_frame t ~off ~total =
234:   let open Frame in
235:   let { length; frame_type; flags; stream_id } =
236:     parse_header_buffer t.pending ~off
237:   in
238:   match (t.headers, frame_type) with
239:   | Some _, frame when frame <> frame_continuation ->
240:       error "non-CONTINUATION frame arrived while a header block is open"
241:   | _, frame when frame = frame_headers && stream_id > 0 ->
242:       let payload = Buffer.sub t.pending (off + header_size) length in
243:       handle_headers t ~flags ~stream_id payload
244:   | _, frame when frame = frame_push_promise ->
245:       handle_push_promise t ~flags ~stream_id ~off ~total
246:   | Some (Passthrough_headers { stream_id = open_stream }), frame
247:     when frame = frame_continuation && open_stream = stream_id ->
248:       if flags land flag_end_headers <> 0 then t.headers <- None;
249:       pass_frame t frame_continuation flags stream_id ~off ~total
250:   | Some (Passthrough_headers _), frame when frame = frame_continuation ->
251:       error "CONTINUATION stream does not match open header block"
252:   | _, frame when frame = frame_continuation -> (
253:       let payload = Buffer.sub t.pending (off + header_size) length in
254:       match handle_continuation t ~flags ~stream_id payload with
255:       | Error _ as error -> error
256:       | Ok () -> Ok ())
257:   | _ -> pass_frame t frame_type flags stream_id ~off ~total
258: 
259: let rec process t =
260:   let available = Buffer.length t.pending - t.pending_off in
261:   if available < Frame.header_size then (
262:     compact_pending t;
263:     Ok ())
264:   else
265:     let open Frame in
266:     let { length; _ } = parse_header_buffer t.pending ~off:t.pending_off in
267:     let total = header_size + length in
268:     if available < total then (
269:       compact_pending t;
270:       Ok ())
271:     else
272:       let frame_off = t.pending_off in
273:       t.pending_off <- t.pending_off + total;
274:       match handle_frame t ~off:frame_off ~total with
275:       | Error _ as error -> error
276:       | Ok () -> process t
277: 
278: let feed t data ~off ~len =
279:   append_pending t data ~off ~len;
280:   process t
281: 
282: let take t =
283:   let data = Buffer.contents t.output in
284:   Buffer.clear t.output;
285:   data
286: 
287: let forget_stream t stream_id =
288:   Hashtbl.remove t.final_seen stream_id
289: 
290: let buffered_bytes t =
291:   Buffer.length t.pending - t.pending_off
292:   +
293:   match t.headers with
294:   | None -> 0
295:   | Some (Response_headers headers) -> Buffer.length headers.block
296:   | Some (Passthrough_headers _) -> 0
297: 
298: let is_passthrough _ = false
````

## File: lib/http/server_body.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Pull-based eta-http server request body. *)
 2: 
 3: type t
 4: 
 5: val empty : unit -> t
 6: 
 7: val of_reader :
 8:   ?release:(unit -> (unit, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
 9:   ?discard:(drain:bool -> (unit, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
10:   (unit -> (bytes option, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
11:   t
12: (** Build a body from an adapter-owned reader.
13: 
14:     [read] arms one upstream read and returns [None] at EOF. [release] runs
15:     once when EOF is reached or [read_all] finishes. [discard] runs once when
16:     callers explicitly discard unread data. *)
17: 
18: val read : t -> (bytes option, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t
19: 
20: val read_all :
21:   ?max_bytes:int ->
22:   t ->
23:   (bytes, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t
24: 
25: val discard :
26:   ?drain:bool ->
27:   t ->
28:   (unit, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t
````

## File: lib/http/server_handler.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Handler helpers for eta-http servers. *)
 2: 
 3: type t = Server_request.t -> (Server_response.t, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t
 4: 
 5: val map_error :
 6:   ('err -> Server_error.t) ->
 7:   (Server_request.t -> (Server_response.t, 'err) Eta.Effect.t) ->
 8:   t
 9: 
10: val with_default_error_response :
11:   ?renderer:(Server_error.t -> Server_response.t) ->
12:   t ->
13:   t
14: 
15: val default_error_response : Server_error.t -> Server_response.t
16: val route_not_found : t
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/connection.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: module Error = Error
  4: module Multiplexer = Multiplexer
  5: module Writer = Writer
  6: 
  7: (* Connection owns socket lifetime, writes, and failure fan-out. Multiplexer
  8:    owns H2 stream admission and body-reader bookkeeping. The read loop crosses
  9:    that boundary because ocaml-h2 exposes a single mutable Client_connection
 10:    state machine; keep all direct flow I/O in this module and all per-stream
 11:    table updates in Multiplexer. *)
 12: 
 13: type flow = Connect.tcp_flow
 14: 
 15: type failure_waiter = {
 16:   mutable active : bool;
 17:   notify : (Error.kind -> unit);
 18: }
 19: 
 20: type t = {
 21:   sw : Eio.Switch.t;
 22:   mux : Multiplexer.t;
 23:   client : H2.Client_connection.t;
 24:   reader : Multiplexer.client_reader;
 25:   flow : flow;
 26:   mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 27:   mutable closed : bool;
 28:   mutable failure : Error.kind option;
 29:   mutable failure_waiters : failure_waiter list;
 30:   mutable wake_writer : (unit -> unit) option;
 31:   on_close : (unit -> unit);
 32: }
 33: 
 34: let close_kind = Error.Connection_closed { during = Error.Http_response }
 35: 
 36: let pp_client_error = function
 37:   | `Malformed_response message -> "malformed_response:" ^ message
 38:   | `Invalid_response_body_length _ -> "invalid_response_body_length"
 39:   | `Protocol_error (code, message) ->
 40:       Format.asprintf "protocol_error:%a:%s" H2.Error_code.pp_hum code message
 41:   | `Exn exn -> "exn:" ^ Printexc.to_string exn
 42: 
 43: let with_lock t (f) =
 44:   Eio.Mutex.lock t.mutex;
 45:   Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> Eio.Mutex.unlock t.mutex) f
 46: 
 47: let notify_close_once t =
 48:   let should_notify =
 49:     with_lock t (fun () ->
 50:         if t.closed then false
 51:         else (
 52:           t.closed <- true;
 53:           true))
 54:   in
 55:   if should_notify then t.on_close ()
 56: 
 57: let set_failure t kind =
 58:   let waiters =
 59:     with_lock t (fun () ->
 60:         let first = Option.is_none t.failure in
 61:         if first then t.failure <- Some kind;
 62:         let waiters = t.failure_waiters in
 63:         t.failure_waiters <- [];
 64:         if first then waiters else [])
 65:   in
 66:   List.iter
 67:     (fun waiter ->
 68:       if waiter.active then try waiter.notify kind with _ -> ())
 69:     waiters
 70: 
 71: let shutdown t =
 72:   notify_close_once t;
 73:   set_failure t close_kind;
 74:   (match with_lock t (fun () -> t.wake_writer) with
 75:   | Some wake -> wake ()
 76:   | None -> ());
 77:   Multiplexer.shutdown t.mux;
 78:   (try Eio.Flow.shutdown t.flow `All with _ -> ());
 79:   (try Eio.Flow.close t.flow with _ -> ())
 80: 
 81: let fail_connection t kind =
 82:   set_failure t kind;
 83:   shutdown t
 84: 
 85: let write_iovecs flow iovecs =
 86:   Writer.write_iovecs ~flow iovecs
 87: 
 88: let rec writer_loop t =
 89:   if not (is_closed t) then
 90:     match H2.Client_connection.next_write_operation t.client with
 91:     | `Write iovecs ->
 92:         let written = write_iovecs t.flow iovecs in
 93:         H2.Client_connection.report_write_result t.client (`Ok written);
 94:         writer_loop t
 95:     | `Yield ->
 96:         let promise, resolver = Eio.Promise.create () in
 97:         with_lock t (fun () ->
 98:             t.wake_writer <-
 99:               Some (fun () -> ignore (Eio.Promise.try_resolve resolver ())));
100:         H2.Client_connection.yield_writer t.client (fun () ->
101:             ignore (Eio.Promise.try_resolve resolver ()));
102:         Eio.Promise.await promise;
103:         with_lock t (fun () -> t.wake_writer <- None);
104:         writer_loop t
105:     | `Close _ ->
106:         H2.Client_connection.report_write_result t.client `Closed;
107:         shutdown t
108: 
109: and reader_loop ~security_error_handler t =
110:   if not (is_closed t) then
111:     match Multiplexer.read_client_once ~flow:t.flow t.reader with
112:     | Read _ -> reader_loop ~security_error_handler t
113:     | Security_error kind ->
114:         security_error_handler kind;
115:         fail_connection t kind
116:     | Eof _ | Close -> shutdown t
117: 
118: and is_closed t =
119:   with_lock t (fun () -> t.closed || H2.Client_connection.is_closed t.client)
120: 
121: let run_owner_loop ?(on_error = fun _ -> ()) loop t =
122:   try loop t
123:   with
124:   | End_of_file -> shutdown t
125:   | Eio.Cancel.Cancelled _ -> shutdown t
126:   | exn ->
127:       let kind =
128:         Error.Connection_protocol_violation
129:           { kind = "h2_owner_loop"; message = Printexc.to_string exn }
130:       in
131:       on_error kind;
132:       fail_connection t kind
133: 
134: let create ~sw ~flow ?max_concurrent ?config ?push_handler
135:     ?(error_handler = fun _ -> ()) ?(security_error_handler = fun _ -> ())
136:     ?(on_close = fun () -> ()) ?(reader_buffer_size = 64 * 1024) () =
137:   let holder = ref None in
138:   let security = Security.create () in
139:   let mux =
140:     Multiplexer.create ?max_concurrent ?config ?push_handler
141:       ~security ~error_handler:(fun error ->
142:         error_handler error;
143:         match !holder with
144:         | None -> ()
145:         | Some t ->
146:             fail_connection t
147:               (Error.Connection_protocol_violation
148:                  {
149:                    kind = "h2_connection";
150:                    message = pp_client_error error;
151:                  }))
152:       ()
153:   in
154:   let client = Multiplexer.client_connection mux in
155:   let t =
156:     {
157:       sw;
158:       mux;
159:       client;
160:       reader =
161:         Multiplexer.create_reader ~buffer_size:reader_buffer_size mux;
162:       flow;
163:       mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
164:       closed = false;
165:       failure = None;
166:       failure_waiters = [];
167:       wake_writer = None;
168:       on_close;
169:     }
170:   in
171:   holder := Some t;
172:   Eio.Fiber.fork_daemon ~sw (fun () ->
173:       run_owner_loop writer_loop t;
174:       `Stop_daemon);
175:   Eio.Fiber.fork_daemon ~sw (fun () ->
176:       run_owner_loop ~on_error:security_error_handler
177:         (reader_loop ~security_error_handler)
178:         t;
179:       `Stop_daemon);
180:   t
181: 
182: let request t ~tag ?trailers_handler request ~error_handler ~response_handler =
183:   match with_lock t (fun () -> (t.closed, t.failure)) with
184:   | true, _ | _, Some _ -> Error Multiplexer.Connection_closed
185:   | false, None ->
186:       Multiplexer.request t.mux ~tag ?trailers_handler request ~error_handler
187:         ~response_handler
188: 
189: let register_failure_handler t notify =
190:   let waiter = { active = true; notify } in
191:   let immediate =
192:     with_lock t (fun () ->
193:         match t.failure with
194:         | Some kind -> Some kind
195:         | None ->
196:             t.failure_waiters <- waiter :: t.failure_waiters;
197:             None)
198:   in
199:   (match immediate with Some kind -> notify kind | None -> ());
200:   fun () -> waiter.active <- false
201: 
202: let mux t = t.mux
203: let client t = t.client
204: let stats t = Multiplexer.stats t.mux
205: 
206: let fork_daemon t f =
207:   Eio.Fiber.fork_daemon ~sw:t.sw (fun () ->
208:       f ();
209:       `Stop_daemon)
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/connection.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Owned HTTP/2 client connection loop.
 2: 
 3:     A connection owns the Eio flow and runs one reader fiber plus one writer
 4:     fiber. Request callers open streams through the multiplexer; they never read
 5:     from or write to the socket directly. *)
 6: 
 7: type t
 8: 
 9: type flow = Connect.tcp_flow
10: 
11: val create :
12:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
13:   flow:flow ->
14:   ?max_concurrent:int ->
15:   ?config:H2.Config.t ->
16:   ?push_handler:
17:     (H2.Request.t -> (H2.Client_connection.response_handler, unit) result) ->
18:   ?error_handler:(H2.Client_connection.error -> unit) ->
19:   ?security_error_handler:(Error.kind -> unit) ->
20:   ?on_close:(unit -> unit) ->
21:   ?reader_buffer_size:int ->
22:   unit ->
23:   t
24: 
25: val request :
26:   t ->
27:   tag:int ->
28:   ?trailers_handler:(H2.Headers.t -> unit) ->
29:   H2.Request.t ->
30:   error_handler:(Multiplexer.stream -> H2.Client_connection.error -> unit) ->
31:   response_handler:(Multiplexer.stream -> H2.Response.t -> H2.Body.Reader.t -> unit) ->
32:   (Multiplexer.opened_request, Multiplexer.request_error) result
33: 
34: val register_failure_handler :
35:   t -> (Error.kind -> unit) -> (unit -> unit)
36: 
37: val mux : t -> Multiplexer.t
38: val client : t -> H2.Client_connection.t
39: val stats : t -> Stream_state.stats
40: val fork_daemon : t -> (unit -> unit) -> unit
41: val is_closed : t -> bool
42: val shutdown : t -> unit
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/multiplexer.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: module Stream_state = Stream_state
  4: module Security = Security
  5: 
  6: (* Multiplexer is intentionally socket-free. It adapts ocaml-h2's mutable
  7:    Client_connection callbacks into Eta stream state, while Connection owns the
  8:    actual flow reads/writes and shutdown. Do not add Eio.Flow operations here;
  9:    that keeps connection lifetime and per-stream lifetime auditable separately. *)
 10: 
 11: type stream = Stream_state.stream
 12: 
 13: type request_error =
 14:   | Admission_rejected of { limit : int }
 15:   | Connection_closed
 16:   | Request_failed of string
 17: 
 18: type opened_request = {
 19:   stream : stream;
 20:   request_body : H2.Body.Writer.t;
 21: }
 22: 
 23: type h2_request =
 24:   H2.Client_connection.t ->
 25:   ?trailers_handler:(H2.Headers.t -> unit) ->
 26:   H2.Request.t ->
 27:   error_handler:(H2.Client_connection.error -> unit) ->
 28:   response_handler:(H2.Response.t -> H2.Body.Reader.t -> unit) ->
 29:   H2.Body.Writer.t
 30: 
 31: type t = {
 32:   client : H2.Client_connection.t;
 33:   streams : Stream_state.t;
 34:   request_bodies : (int, H2.Body.Writer.t) Hashtbl.t;
 35:   response_bodies : (int, H2.Body.Reader.t) Hashtbl.t;
 36:   filter : Informational_filter.t;
 37:   security : Security.t option;
 38:   mutable closed : bool;
 39: }
 40: 
 41: let create ?(max_concurrent = 128) ?config ?push_handler
 42:     ?security ?(error_handler = fun _ -> ()) () =
 43:   let holder = ref None in
 44:   let client =
 45:     H2.Client_connection.create ?config ?push_handler
 46:       ~error_handler:(fun error ->
 47:         (match !holder with Some t -> t.closed <- true | None -> ());
 48:         error_handler error)
 49:       ()
 50:   in
 51:   let t =
 52:     {
 53:       client;
 54:       streams = Stream_state.create ~max_concurrent;
 55:       request_bodies = Hashtbl.create max_concurrent;
 56:       response_bodies = Hashtbl.create max_concurrent;
 57:       filter = Informational_filter.create ();
 58:       security;
 59:       closed = false;
 60:     }
 61:   in
 62:   holder := Some t;
 63:   t
 64: 
 65: let client_connection t = t.client
 66: let stats t = Stream_state.stats t.streams
 67: let complete_security_stream t stream_id =
 68:   Option.iter (fun security -> Security.complete_stream security stream_id) t.security
 69: 
 70: let mark_complete t stream =
 71:   Stream_state.mark_complete t.streams stream;
 72:   complete_security_stream t (Stream_state.id stream)
 73: 
 74: let mark_remote_reset t stream_id =
 75:   Stream_state.mark_remote_reset t.streams stream_id;
 76:   complete_security_stream t stream_id
 77: 
 78: let close_request_body body =
 79:   try
 80:     if not (H2.Body.Writer.is_closed body) then H2.Body.Writer.close body
 81:   with _ -> ()
 82: 
 83: let release t stream =
 84:   let stream_id = Stream_state.id stream in
 85:   let decision = Stream_state.release t.streams stream in
 86:   (match Hashtbl.find_opt t.request_bodies stream_id with
 87:   | Some body ->
 88:       close_request_body body;
 89:       Hashtbl.remove t.request_bodies stream_id
 90:   | None -> ());
 91:   (match Hashtbl.find_opt t.response_bodies stream_id with
 92:   | Some body ->
 93:       if not (H2.Body.Reader.is_closed body) then H2.Body.Reader.close body;
 94:       Hashtbl.remove t.response_bodies stream_id
 95:   | None -> ());
 96:   Informational_filter.forget_stream t.filter stream_id;
 97:   complete_security_stream t stream_id;
 98:   decision
 99: 
100: let shutdown t =
101:   if not t.closed then (
102:     t.closed <- true;
103:     H2.Client_connection.shutdown t.client;
104:     Stream_state.close t.streams;
105:     Hashtbl.iter (fun _ body -> close_request_body body) t.request_bodies;
106:     Hashtbl.clear t.request_bodies;
107:     Hashtbl.clear t.response_bodies)
108: 
109: let request_with_h2_request h2_request t ~tag ?trailers_handler request
110:     ~error_handler ~response_handler =
111:   if t.closed || H2.Client_connection.is_closed t.client then
112:     Error Connection_closed
113:   else
114:     match Stream_state.open_stream t.streams ~tag with
115:     | Error () ->
116:         let stats = Stream_state.stats t.streams in
117:         Error (Admission_rejected { limit = stats.max_concurrent })
118:     | Ok stream ->
119:         let stream_id = Stream_state.id stream in
120:         try
121:           let request_body =
122:             h2_request t.client ?trailers_handler request
123:               ~error_handler:(fun error ->
124:                 Stream_state.mark_remote_reset t.streams stream_id;
125:                 error_handler stream error)
126:               ~response_handler:(fun response body ->
127:                 Hashtbl.replace t.response_bodies stream_id body;
128:                 response_handler stream response body)
129:           in
130:           Hashtbl.replace t.request_bodies stream_id request_body;
131:           Ok { stream; request_body }
132:         with exn ->
133:           ignore (release t stream);
134:           Error (Request_failed (Printexc.to_string exn))
135: 
136: let request =
137:   request_with_h2_request (fun client ?trailers_handler request ~error_handler
138:                               ~response_handler ->
139:       H2.Client_connection.request client request ?trailers_handler ~error_handler
140:         ~response_handler)
141: 
142: type client_reader = {
143:   client : H2.Client_connection.t;
144:   security : Security.t;
145:   filter : Informational_filter.t;
146:   buffer : Bigstringaf.t;
147:   mutable filtered : string;
148:   mutable filtered_off : int;
149:   mutable off : int;
150:   mutable len : int;
151:   mutable eof : bool;
152: }
153: 
154: type read_result =
155:   | Read of int
156:   | Eof of int
157:   | Close
158:   | Security_error of Error.kind
159: 
160: type body_event =
161:   | Body_chunk of bytes
162:   | Body_eof
163: 
164: let create_client_reader_with_filter ?(buffer_size = 64 * 1024) ?security
165:     ?security_config ~filter client =
166:   if buffer_size <= 0 then
167:     invalid_arg "Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.create_client_reader: buffer_size must be > 0";
168:   let security =
169:     match (security, security_config) with
170:     | Some _, Some _ ->
171:         invalid_arg
172:           "Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.create_client_reader: pass either security or security_config, not both"
173:     | Some security, None -> security
174:     | None, _ -> Security.create ?config:security_config ()
175:   in
176:   let buffer = Bigstringaf.create buffer_size in
177:   {
178:     client;
179:     security;
180:     filter;
181:     buffer;
182:     filtered = "";
183:     filtered_off = 0;
184:     off = 0;
185:     len = 0;
186:     eof = false;
187:   }
188: 
189: let create_client_reader ?buffer_size ?security ?security_config client =
190:   create_client_reader_with_filter ?buffer_size ?security ?security_config
191:     ~filter:(Informational_filter.create ()) client
192: 
193: let create_reader ?buffer_size ?security_config (t : t) =
194:   let security = t.security in
195:   create_client_reader_with_filter ?buffer_size ?security ?security_config
196:     ~filter:t.filter t.client
197: 
198: let client reader = reader.client
199: let reader_is_passthrough reader = Informational_filter.is_passthrough reader.filter
200: let capacity reader = Bigstringaf.length reader.buffer
201: 
202: let compact reader =
203:   if reader.off > 0 && reader.len > 0 then (
204:     Bigstringaf.blit reader.buffer ~src_off:reader.off reader.buffer ~dst_off:0
205:       ~len:reader.len;
206:     reader.off <- 0)
207:   else if reader.len = 0 then reader.off <- 0
208: 
209: let feed_pending reader =
210:   let consumed =
211:     H2.Client_connection.read reader.client reader.buffer ~off:reader.off
212:       ~len:reader.len
213:   in
214:   reader.off <- reader.off + consumed;
215:   reader.len <- reader.len - consumed;
216:   consumed
217: 
218: let feed_eof reader =
219:   if reader.eof then Eof 0
220:   else (
221:     reader.eof <- true;
222:     let consumed =
223:       H2.Client_connection.read_eof reader.client reader.buffer ~off:reader.off
224:         ~len:reader.len
225:     in
226:     reader.off <- reader.off + consumed;
227:     reader.len <- reader.len - consumed;
228:     Eof consumed)
229: 
230: let copy_filtered reader =
231:   let available = String.length reader.filtered - reader.filtered_off in
232:   if available <= 0 then 0
233:   else
234:     let copied = min available (capacity reader - reader.len) in
235:     Bigstringaf.blit_from_string reader.filtered ~src_off:reader.filtered_off
236:       reader.buffer ~dst_off:reader.len ~len:copied;
237:     reader.filtered_off <- reader.filtered_off + copied;
238:     reader.len <- reader.len + copied;
239:     if reader.filtered_off >= String.length reader.filtered then (
240:       reader.filtered <- "";
241:       reader.filtered_off <- 0);
242:     copied
243: 
244: let read_more ~flow reader =
245:   compact reader;
246:   match copy_filtered reader with
247:   | copied when copied > 0 -> `Read_more copied
248:   | _ when reader.len >= capacity reader -> `Buffer_full
249:   | _ ->
250:       let free_space = capacity reader - reader.len in
251:       let view = Cstruct.of_bigarray reader.buffer ~off:reader.len ~len:free_space in
252:       try
253:         let read = Eio.Flow.single_read flow view in
254:         (match Security.observe reader.security reader.buffer ~off:reader.len ~len:read with
255:         | Some error -> `Security_error error
256:         | None ->
257:             if Informational_filter.is_passthrough reader.filter then (
258:               reader.len <- reader.len + read;
259:               `Read_more read)
260:             else (
261:               let raw = Bigstringaf.substring reader.buffer ~off:reader.len ~len:read in
262:               match Informational_filter.feed reader.filter raw ~off:0 ~len:read with
263:               | Error error -> `Security_error error
264:               | Ok () ->
265:                   reader.filtered <- Informational_filter.take reader.filter;
266:                   reader.filtered_off <- 0;
267:                   let copied = copy_filtered reader in
268:                   if copied = 0
269:                      && Informational_filter.buffered_bytes reader.filter
270:                         >= capacity reader
271:                   then `Buffer_full
272:                   else `Read_more copied))
273:       with
274:       | Eio.Cancel.Cancelled _ as exn -> raise exn
275:       | End_of_file -> `Eof
276:       (* The public read_client_once contract returns typed read errors rather
277:          than raising. Translate non-EOF flow failures (socket/TLS/mock) into a
278:          typed connection-closed result. *)
279:       | _ ->
280:           `Security_error
281:             (Error.Connection_closed { during = Error.Http_response })
282: 
283: let buffer_exhausted reader =
284:   Security_error
285:     (Error.Connection_protocol_violation
286:        {
287:          kind = "h2_read_buffer_exhausted";
288:          message =
289:            Printf.sprintf
290:              "h2 read buffer of %d bytes filled without parser progress"
291:              (capacity reader);
292:        })
293: 
294: let has_filtered reader =
295:   String.length reader.filtered > reader.filtered_off
296: 
297: let rec read_client_once ~flow reader =
298:   if reader.len > 0 then
299:     match feed_pending reader with
300:     | consumed when consumed > 0 -> Read consumed
301:     | _ -> read_client_next ~flow reader
302:   else if has_filtered reader then
303:     match copy_filtered reader with
304:     | copied when copied > 0 -> read_client_once ~flow reader
305:     | _ -> read_client_next ~flow reader
306:   else read_client_next ~flow reader
307: 
308: and read_client_next ~flow reader =
309:   match H2.Client_connection.next_read_operation reader.client with
310:   | `Close -> Close
311:   | `Read -> (
312:       if reader.eof then Eof 0
313:       else
314:         match read_more ~flow reader with
315:         | `Read_more _ -> read_client_once ~flow reader
316:         | `Security_error error -> Security_error error
317:         | `Eof -> feed_eof reader
318:         | `Buffer_full -> buffer_exhausted reader)
319: 
320: let body_stream ?(poll_error = fun () -> None) ?(on_eof = fun () -> ())
321:     ?(on_release = fun _ -> Eta.Effect.unit) ~closed_error ~pump t stream body =
322:   let events = Queue.create () in
323:   let scheduled = ref false in
324:   let eof = ref false in
325:   let push_event event = Queue.push event events in
326:   let pop_event () =
327:     if Queue.is_empty events then None else Some (Queue.take events)
328:   in
329:   let finish_eof () =
330:     if not !eof then (
331:       eof := true;
332:       on_eof ();
333:       mark_complete t stream)
334:   in
335:   let schedule_read () =
336:     if (not !scheduled) && not !eof then (
337:       scheduled := true;
338:       H2.Body.Reader.schedule_read body
339:         ~on_eof:(fun () ->
340:           scheduled := false;
341:           finish_eof ();
342:           push_event Body_eof)
343:         ~on_read:(fun bs ~off ~len ->
344:           scheduled := false;
345:           let chunk = Bytes.create len in
346:           Bigstringaf.blit_to_bytes bs ~src_off:off chunk ~dst_off:0 ~len;
347:           push_event (Body_chunk chunk)))
348:   in
349:   let release_body () =
350:     let decision = release t stream in
351:     on_release decision
352:   in
353:   let emit_event = function
354:     | Body_chunk chunk -> Eta.Effect.pure (Stream.Chunk chunk)
355:     | Body_eof -> Eta.Effect.pure Stream.End
356:   in
357:   let closed_or_error () =
358:     match poll_error () with
359:     | Some error -> Eta.Effect.fail error
360:     | None when !eof -> Eta.Effect.pure Stream.End
361:     | None -> Eta.Effect.fail closed_error
362:   in
363:   let rec read_next () =
364:     match pop_event () with
365:     | Some event -> emit_event event
366:     | None -> (
367:         match poll_error () with
368:         | Some error -> Eta.Effect.fail error
369:         | None when !eof -> Eta.Effect.pure Stream.End
370:         | None -> read_after_schedule ())
371:   and read_after_schedule () =
372:     schedule_read ();
373:     match pop_event () with
374:     | Some event -> emit_event event
375:     | None ->
376:         pump ()
377:         |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun result ->
378:                match pop_event () with
379:                | Some event -> emit_event event
380:                | None -> (
381:                    match result with
382:                    | Read _ -> read_next ()
383:                    | Security_error _ | Eof _ | Close -> closed_or_error ()))
384:   in
385:   schedule_read ();
386:   Stream.of_reader ~release:release_body read_next
387: 
388: type body_async_state = {
389:   mutable scheduled : bool;
390:   mutable eof : bool;
391: }
392: 
393: let body_stream_async ?(poll_error = fun () -> None) ?(on_eof = fun () -> ())
394:     ?(on_release = fun _ -> Eta.Effect.unit) ~closed_error t stream body =
395:   let mutex = Eio.Mutex.create () in
396:   let condition = Eio.Condition.create () in
397:   let events = Queue.create () in
398:   let state = { scheduled = false; eof = false } in
399:   let notify () = Eio.Condition.broadcast condition in
400:   let with_lock f =
401:     Eio.Mutex.lock mutex;
402:     Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> Eio.Mutex.unlock mutex) f
403:   in
404:   let finish_eof () =
405:     let first =
406:       with_lock (fun () ->
407:           if state.eof then false
408:           else (
409:             state.eof <- true;
410:             Queue.push Body_eof events;
411:             true))
412:     in
413:     if first then (
414:       on_eof ();
415:       mark_complete t stream;
416:       notify ())
417:   in
418:   let push_chunk bs ~off ~len =
419:     let chunk = Bytes.create len in
420:     Bigstringaf.blit_to_bytes bs ~src_off:off chunk ~dst_off:0 ~len;
421:     with_lock (fun () ->
422:         state.scheduled <- false;
423:         Queue.push (Body_chunk chunk) events);
424:     notify ()
425:   in
426:   let schedule_read () =
427:     (* Arm exactly one upstream read per call. ocaml-h2 may invoke [on_read]
428:        synchronously when a frame is already buffered; we must NOT re-arm in a
429:        loop on synchronous delivery, or a large pre-buffered body would be
430:        drained in full into the unbounded [events] queue, circumventing
431:        backpressure. Subsequent reads are driven by the consumer pulling the
432:        stream (await_event -> schedule_read), so at most one chunk is buffered
433:        ahead of demand.
434: 
435:        We deliberately do NOT gate on [is_closed body]: a closed h2 body can
436:        still hold buffered DATA, and [H2.Body.Reader.schedule_read] drains it
437:        (delivering [on_read] for buffered bytes, or [on_eof] only once truly
438:        empty). Gating on closed here would drop the tail of the body. *)
439:     let should_schedule =
440:       with_lock (fun () -> (not state.scheduled) && not state.eof)
441:     in
442:     if should_schedule then (
443:       with_lock (fun () -> state.scheduled <- true);
444:       H2.Body.Reader.schedule_read body
445:         ~on_eof:(fun () ->
446:           with_lock (fun () -> state.scheduled <- false);
447:           finish_eof ())
448:         ~on_read:(fun bs ~off ~len -> push_chunk bs ~off ~len))
449:   in
450:   let release_body () =
451:     let decision = release t stream in
452:     notify ();
453:     on_release decision
454:   in
455:   let emit_event = function
456:     | Body_chunk chunk -> Eta.Effect.pure (Stream.Chunk chunk)
457:     | Body_eof -> Eta.Effect.pure Stream.End
458:   in
459:   let next_locked () =
460:     match poll_error () with
461:     | Some error -> Some (`Error error)
462:     | None when not (Queue.is_empty events) -> Some (`Event (Queue.take events))
463:     | None when state.eof -> Some (`Event Body_eof)
464:     | None -> None
465:   in
466:   let await_event () =
467:     match with_lock next_locked with
468:     | None ->
469:         schedule_read ();
470:         with_lock (fun () ->
471:             let rec loop () =
472:               match next_locked () with
473:               | None ->
474:                   Eio.Condition.await condition mutex;
475:                   loop ()
476:               | Some result -> result
477:             in
478:             loop ())
479:     | Some result -> result
480:   in
481:   let read_next () =
482:     Eta.Effect.sync await_event
483:     |> Eta.Effect.bind (function
484:          | `Event event -> emit_event event
485:          | `Error error -> Eta.Effect.fail error)
486:   in
487:   schedule_read ();
488:   (Stream.of_reader ~release:release_body read_next, notify)
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/multiplexer.mli
````ocaml
  1: (** HTTP/2 multiplexer adapter pieces. *)
  2: 
  3: type t
  4: type stream = Stream_state.stream
  5: 
  6: type request_error =
  7:   | Admission_rejected of { limit : int }
  8:   | Connection_closed
  9:   | Request_failed of string
 10: 
 11: type opened_request = {
 12:   stream : stream;
 13:   request_body : H2.Body.Writer.t;
 14: }
 15: 
 16: type client_reader
 17: 
 18: type read_result =
 19:   | Read of int
 20:   | Eof of int
 21:   | Close
 22:   | Security_error of Error.kind
 23: 
 24: val create :
 25:   ?max_concurrent:int ->
 26:   ?config:H2.Config.t ->
 27:   ?push_handler:
 28:     (H2.Request.t -> (H2.Client_connection.response_handler, unit) result) ->
 29:   ?security:Security.t ->
 30:   ?error_handler:(H2.Client_connection.error -> unit) ->
 31:   unit ->
 32:   t
 33: 
 34: val client_connection : t -> H2.Client_connection.t
 35: val stats : t -> Stream_state.stats
 36: val mark_complete : t -> stream -> unit
 37: val mark_remote_reset : t -> int -> unit
 38: val release : t -> stream -> Stream_state.release
 39: val shutdown : t -> unit
 40: 
 41: val request :
 42:   t ->
 43:   tag:int ->
 44:   ?trailers_handler:(H2.Headers.t -> unit) ->
 45:   H2.Request.t ->
 46:   error_handler:(stream -> H2.Client_connection.error -> unit) ->
 47:   response_handler:(stream -> H2.Response.t -> H2.Body.Reader.t -> unit) ->
 48:   (opened_request, request_error) result
 49: 
 50: val create_client_reader :
 51:   ?buffer_size:int ->
 52:   ?security:Security.t ->
 53:   ?security_config:Security.config ->
 54:   H2.Client_connection.t ->
 55:   client_reader
 56: 
 57: val create_reader :
 58:   ?buffer_size:int ->
 59:   ?security_config:Security.config ->
 60:   t ->
 61:   client_reader
 62: (** Create a reader bound to [t]'s informational-response filter. Readers built
 63:     this way are notified when {!release} locally resets a stream, so per-stream
 64:     filter markers do not outlive cancelled streams. *)
 65: 
 66: val client : client_reader -> H2.Client_connection.t
 67: 
 68: val reader_is_passthrough : client_reader -> bool
 69: (** Always false while the informational filter must inspect response HEADERS. *)
 70: 
 71: val read_client_once :
 72:   flow:[> Eio.Flow.source_ty] Eio.Resource.t ->
 73:   client_reader ->
 74:   read_result
 75: (** Feed one HTTP/2 client-read step from [flow]. If buffered network bytes fill
 76:     the adapter buffer without parser progress, returns
 77:     [Security_error (Connection_protocol_violation { kind =
 78:     "h2_read_buffer_exhausted"; _ })] instead of raising. *)
 79: 
 80: val body_stream :
 81:   ?poll_error:(unit -> Error.t option) ->
 82:   ?on_eof:(unit -> unit) ->
 83:   ?on_release:
 84:     (Stream_state.release -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
 85:   closed_error:Error.t ->
 86:   pump:(unit -> (read_result, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
 87:   t ->
 88:   stream ->
 89:   H2.Body.Reader.t ->
 90:   Stream.t
 91: 
 92: val body_stream_async :
 93:   ?poll_error:(unit -> Error.t option) ->
 94:   ?on_eof:(unit -> unit) ->
 95:   ?on_release:
 96:     (Stream_state.release -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
 97:   closed_error:Error.t ->
 98:   t ->
 99:   stream ->
100:   H2.Body.Reader.t ->
101:   Stream.t * (unit -> unit)
102: (** Like {!body_stream}, but waits for callbacks delivered by a background
103:     owner reader instead of reading the socket itself. The second result wakes a
104:     blocked reader when external state such as [poll_error] changes.
105: 
106:     Pull-based with backpressure: each consumer demand arms at most one upstream
107:     [H2.Body.Reader.schedule_read], so the internal buffer never runs more than
108:     one chunk ahead of the consumer even when h2 delivers synchronously from a
109:     large pre-buffered frame. The full body is delivered without loss, including
110:     data still buffered after the h2 body is closed. *)
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/writer.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type drain_result =
 4:   | Yield of { written : int }
 5:   | Close of {
 6:       written : int;
 7:       code : int;
 8:     }
 9: 
10: let cstruct_of_iovec ({ H2.IOVec.buffer; off; len } : Bigstringaf.t H2.IOVec.t) =
11:   Cstruct.of_bigarray ~off ~len buffer
12: 
13: let cstructs_of_iovecs iovecs = List.map cstruct_of_iovec iovecs
14: 
15: let cstruct_of_iovecs iovecs =
16:   let len = H2.IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
17:   let bytes = Bytes.create len in
18:   let dst_off = ref 0 in
19:   List.iter
20:     (fun ({ H2.IOVec.buffer; off; len } : Bigstringaf.t H2.IOVec.t) ->
21:       Bigstringaf.blit_to_bytes buffer ~src_off:off bytes ~dst_off:!dst_off
22:         ~len;
23:       dst_off := !dst_off + len)
24:     iovecs;
25:   Cstruct.of_bytes bytes
26: 
27: let write_iovecs ~flow iovecs =
28:   let len = H2.IOVec.lengthv iovecs in
29:   if len > 0 then Eio.Flow.write flow [ cstruct_of_iovecs iovecs ];
30:   len
31: 
32: let rec drain_client_loop ~flow client written =
33:   match H2.Client_connection.next_write_operation client with
34:   | `Write iovecs ->
35:       let count = write_iovecs ~flow iovecs in
36:       H2.Client_connection.report_write_result client (`Ok count);
37:       drain_client_loop ~flow client (written + count)
38:   | `Yield -> Yield { written }
39:   | `Close code ->
40:       H2.Client_connection.report_write_result client `Closed;
41:       Close { written; code }
42: 
43: let drain_client ~flow client = drain_client_loop ~flow client 0
44: 
45: let wait_writer client =
46:   let wake = Eta.Channel.create ~capacity:1 () in
47:   H2.Client_connection.yield_writer client (fun () -> Eta.Channel.close wake);
48:   Eta.Channel.recv wake
49:   |> Eta.Effect.map (fun _ -> ())
50:   |> Eta.Effect.catch (function
51:        | `Closed | `Closed_with_error _ -> Eta.Effect.unit)
52: 
53: let rec run_client ~write client =
54:   match H2.Client_connection.next_write_operation client with
55:   | `Write iovecs ->
56:       write iovecs
57:       |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun count ->
58:              Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
59:                  H2.Client_connection.report_write_result client (`Ok count)))
60:       |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun () -> run_client ~write client)
61:   | `Yield -> wait_writer client |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun () -> run_client ~write client)
62:   | `Close _ ->
63:       Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
64:           H2.Client_connection.report_write_result client `Closed)
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2/writer.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP/2 client socket writer adapter. *)
 2: 
 3: type drain_result =
 4:   | Yield of { written : int }
 5:   | Close of {
 6:       written : int;
 7:       code : int;
 8:     }
 9: 
10: val cstructs_of_iovecs : Bigstringaf.t H2.IOVec.t list -> Cstruct.t list
11: val write_iovecs :
12:   flow:[> Eio.Flow.sink_ty] Eio.Resource.t ->
13:   Bigstringaf.t H2.IOVec.t list ->
14:   int
15: 
16: val drain_client :
17:   flow:[> Eio.Flow.sink_ty] Eio.Resource.t ->
18:   H2.Client_connection.t ->
19:   drain_result
20: 
21: val run_client :
22:   write:(Bigstringaf.t H2.IOVec.t list -> (int, 'err) Eta.Effect.t) ->
23:   H2.Client_connection.t ->
24:   (unit, 'err) Eta.Effect.t
````

## File: lib/http_eio/alpn_server.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type unsupported = { protocol : string }
 4: 
 5: let dispatch ~close ~use_h1 ~use_h2 alpn =
 6:   match Eta_http.Transport.Dispatch.decide_alpn alpn with
 7:   | Ok Eta_http.Transport.Dispatch.Use_h1 -> Ok (use_h1 ())
 8:   | Ok Eta_http.Transport.Dispatch.Use_h2 -> Ok (use_h2 ())
 9:   | Error protocol ->
10:       close ();
11:       Error { protocol }
````

## File: lib/http_eio/alpn_server.mli
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: (** Server-side ALPN dispatch helpers. *)
 4: 
 5: type unsupported = { protocol : string }
 6: 
 7: val dispatch :
 8:   close:(unit -> unit) ->
 9:   use_h1:(unit -> 'a) ->
10:   use_h2:(unit -> 'a) ->
11:   string option ->
12:   ('a, unsupported) result
13: (** Dispatch a negotiated ALPN value to a server protocol branch.
14: 
15:     [None] and ["http/1.1"] route to H1, ["h2"] routes to H2. Unsupported
16:     protocols close the underlying transport and return [Error]. *)
````
