## File: http-testsuite/lib/eta_server.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** In-process Eta server lifecycle for testsuite server-mode runs. *)
  2: 
  3: open Types
  4: 
  5: type t = Eta_http_eio.Server.t
  6: 
  7: let header_list entries =
  8:   match Eta_http.Core.Header.of_list entries with
  9:   | Ok headers -> headers
 10:   | Error _ -> invalid_arg "Eta_server.header_list: invalid fixture header"
 11: 
 12: let text ?(status = 200) ?(headers = []) body =
 13:   Eta_http.Server.Response.text ~status ~headers:(header_list headers) body
 14: 
 15: let empty ?(headers = []) status =
 16:   Eta_http.Server.Response.empty ~status ~headers:(header_list headers) ()
 17: 
 18: let fixed ?(status = 200) ?(headers = []) body =
 19:   Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status ~headers:(header_list headers)
 20:     ~body:(Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.string body)
 21:     ()
 22: 
 23: let redirect status =
 24:   empty ~headers:[ ("Location", "/healthz") ] status
 25: 
 26: let read_file path =
 27:   let input = open_in_bin path in
 28:   Fun.protect
 29:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_in_noerr input)
 30:     (fun () ->
 31:       let len = in_channel_length input in
 32:       really_input_string input len)
 33: 
 34: let static_response ~temp_dir path =
 35:   let prefix = "/static/" in
 36:   if not (String.starts_with ~prefix path) then None
 37:   else
 38:     let name =
 39:       String.sub path (String.length prefix)
 40:         (String.length path - String.length prefix)
 41:     in
 42:     let file = Filename.concat temp_dir name in
 43:     if Sys.file_exists file then Some (fixed (read_file file)) else None
 44: 
 45: let status_response = function
 46:   | "/status204" -> Some (empty 204)
 47:   | "/status206" -> Some (text ~status:206 "partial")
 48:   | "/status400" -> Some (empty 400)
 49:   | "/status401" -> Some (empty 401)
 50:   | "/status413" -> Some (empty 413)
 51:   | "/status429" -> Some (empty 429)
 52:   | "/status500" -> Some (empty 500)
 53:   | "/status502" -> Some (empty 502)
 54:   | "/status503" -> Some (empty 503)
 55:   | "/status504" -> Some (empty 504)
 56:   | _ -> None
 57: 
 58: let redirect_response = function
 59:   | "/redirect301" -> Some (redirect 301)
 60:   | "/redirect302" -> Some (redirect 302)
 61:   | "/redirect307" -> Some (redirect 307)
 62:   | "/redirect308" -> Some (redirect 308)
 63:   | _ -> None
 64: 
 65: let user_id_response path =
 66:   let prefix = "/user/" in
 67:   if String.starts_with ~prefix path then
 68:     Some (text (String.sub path (String.length prefix)
 69:                   (String.length path - String.length prefix)))
 70:   else None
 71: 
 72: let echo_response request =
 73:   Eta_http.Server.Body.read_all request.Eta_http.Server.Request.body
 74:   |> Eta.Effect.map (fun body ->
 75:          fixed ~headers:[ ("content-type", "text/plain") ] (Bytes.to_string body))
 76:   |> Eta.Effect.catch (fun _error -> Eta.Effect.pure (empty 500))
 77: 
 78: let empty_after_body request =
 79:   Eta_http.Server.Body.read_all request.Eta_http.Server.Request.body
 80:   |> Eta.Effect.map (fun _body -> empty 200)
 81: 
 82: let trailer_response () =
 83:   Eta.Effect.pure
 84:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200
 85:        ~headers:(header_list [ ("trailer", "x-trailer") ])
 86:        ~trailers:(fun () ->
 87:          Eta.Effect.pure (header_list [ ("x-trailer", "eta-trailer") ]))
 88:        ~body:
 89:          (Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.string "body-with-trailer")
 90:        ())
 91: 
 92: let handler ~temp_dir request =
 93:   match request.Eta_http.Server.Request.path with
 94:   | "/" -> Eta.Effect.pure (empty 200)
 95:   | "/user" when String.equal request.Eta_http.Server.Request.method_ "POST" ->
 96:       empty_after_body request
 97:   | "/healthz" -> Eta.Effect.pure (text "ok\n")
 98:   | "/echo" | "/reflect" -> echo_response request
 99:   | "/trailer" -> trailer_response ()
100:   | path -> (
101:       match user_id_response path with
102:       | Some response -> Eta.Effect.pure response
103:       | None -> (
104:           match static_response ~temp_dir path with
105:           | Some response -> Eta.Effect.pure response
106:           | None -> (
107:               match redirect_response path with
108:               | Some response -> Eta.Effect.pure response
109:               | None -> (
110:                   match status_response path with
111:                   | Some response -> Eta.Effect.pure response
112:                   | None -> Eta.Effect.pure (empty 404)))))
113: 
114: let config =
115:   let body_limit = 128 * 1024 * 1024 in
116:   let server =
117:     {
118:       Eta_http.Server.Config.default with
119:       unread_body_policy = Drain_up_to body_limit;
120:       limits =
121:         {
122:           Eta_http.Server.Config.default.limits with
123:           max_request_body_bytes = Some body_limit;
124:         };
125:     }
126:   in
127:   {
128:     Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default with
129:     server;
130:     h2_config =
131:       {
132:         Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default.h2_config with
133:         max_concurrent_streams = 4096l;
134:       };
135:   }
136: 
137: let tls_config cert_dir protocol =
138:   let alpn_protocols =
139:     match protocol with H1 -> [ "http/1.1" ] | H2 -> [ "h2"; "http/1.1" ]
140:   in
141:   Eta_http.Tls.Config.default_server
142:     ~certificate_chain_file:(Certs.cert_path cert_dir)
143:     ~private_key_file:(Certs.key_path cert_dir) ~alpn_protocols ()
144: 
145: let start ~sw ~env ~port ~temp_dir ?cert_dir ~protocol ~transport () =
146:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
147:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
148:   let addr = `Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port) in
149:   let handler = handler ~temp_dir in
150:   match (protocol, transport) with
151:   | H1, Plain ->
152:       Ok
153:         (Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1 ~sw ~net ~clock ~config ~addr handler)
154:   | H2, Plain ->
155:       Ok
156:         (Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h2c ~sw ~net ~clock ~config ~addr handler)
157:   | (H1 | H2), TLS -> (
158:       match cert_dir with
159:       | None -> Error "eta TLS server requires cert_dir"
160:       | Some cert_dir ->
161:           let tls_config = tls_config cert_dir protocol in
162:           Ok
163:             (Eta_http_eio.Server.start_https ~sw ~net ~clock ~config
164:                ~tls_config ~addr handler))
165: 
166: let stop t =
167:   Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown t Immediate;
168:   Ok ()
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/util.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** Shared utilities. *)
  2: 
  3: let ( let* ) = Result.bind
  4: let quote = Filename.quote
  5: 
  6: let run_cmd ?(env = []) cmd =
  7:   let full = String.concat " " (List.map (fun (k, v) -> Printf.sprintf "%s=%s" k (quote v)) env @ [ cmd ]) in
  8:   match Sys.command full with
  9:   | 0 -> Ok ()
 10:   | code -> Error (Printf.sprintf "command failed code=%d cmd=%s" code full)
 11: 
 12: let run_cmd_out ?(env = []) cmd =
 13:   let full = String.concat " " (List.map (fun (k, v) -> Printf.sprintf "%s=%s" k (quote v)) env @ [ cmd ]) in
 14:   let ic = Unix.open_process_in full in
 15:   Fun.protect
 16:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_in_noerr ic)
 17:     (fun () ->
 18:        let rec loop acc =
 19:          match input_line ic with
 20:          | line -> loop (line :: acc)
 21:          | exception End_of_file -> List.rev acc
 22:        in
 23:        let lines = loop [] in
 24:        match Unix.close_process_in ic with
 25:        | WEXITED 0 -> Ok lines
 26:        | WEXITED code ->
 27:            Error
 28:              (Printf.sprintf "exit %d: %s\n%s" code cmd
 29:                 (String.concat "\n" lines))
 30:        | WSIGNALED n ->
 31:            Error
 32:              (Printf.sprintf "signal %d: %s\n%s" n cmd
 33:                 (String.concat "\n" lines))
 34:        | WSTOPPED n ->
 35:            Error
 36:              (Printf.sprintf "stopped %d: %s\n%s" n cmd
 37:                 (String.concat "\n" lines)))
 38: 
 39: let write_file path contents =
 40:   let out = open_out path in
 41:   Fun.protect
 42:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr out)
 43:     (fun () -> output_string out contents)
 44: 
 45: let read_file path =
 46:   let ic = open_in path in
 47:   Fun.protect
 48:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_in_noerr ic)
 49:     (fun () ->
 50:        let len = in_channel_length ic in
 51:        really_input_string ic len)
 52: 
 53: let mkdir_p path =
 54:   ignore (Sys.command ("mkdir -p " ^ quote path))
 55: 
 56: let absolute_path path =
 57:   if Filename.is_relative path then Filename.concat (Sys.getcwd ()) path else path
 58: 
 59: let sha256_of_string = Sha256.hex
 60: 
 61: let sha256_of_file path =
 62:   read_file path |> sha256_of_string
 63: 
 64: let body_to_string stream =
 65:   (* Match the interop/bench client body cap (128 MiB) so large-body scenarios
 66:      such as static_100m are not rejected by the default 1 MiB read_all cap. *)
 67:   Eta_http.Body.Stream.read_all ~max_bytes:(128 * 1024 * 1024) stream
 68:   |> Eta.Effect.map Bytes.unsafe_to_string
 69: 
 70: let now_ms () =
 71:   Unix.gettimeofday () *. 1000.0
 72: 
 73: let gc_stat () =
 74:   let s = Gc.quick_stat () in
 75:   (s.Gc.minor_words, s.Gc.major_words, s.Gc.promoted_words, s.Gc.top_heap_words)
 76: 
 77: let rss_kb () =
 78:   try
 79:     let ic = open_in "/proc/self/status" in
 80:     Fun.protect
 81:       ~finally:(fun () -> close_in_noerr ic)
 82:       (fun () ->
 83:          let rec loop () =
 84:            match input_line ic with
 85:            | line when String.starts_with ~prefix:"VmRSS:" line ->
 86:                (match String.split_on_char ' ' line |> List.filter (( <> ) "") with
 87:                 | _ :: value :: _ -> int_of_string value
 88:                 | _ -> 0)
 89:            | _ -> loop ()
 90:            | exception End_of_file -> 0
 91:          in
 92:          loop ())
 93:   with _ -> 0
 94: 
 95: let fd_count () =
 96:   try Array.length (Sys.readdir "/proc/self/fd") with _ -> 0
 97: 
 98: let version_of_cmd cmd =
 99:   match run_cmd_out cmd with
100:   | Ok (line :: _) ->
101:       (match String.split_on_char ' ' line with
102:        | _ :: ver :: _ -> String.trim ver
103:        | _ -> String.trim line)
104:   | _ -> "unknown"
105: 
106: let hostname () =
107:   match run_cmd_out "hostname" with
108:   | Ok (h :: _) -> String.trim h
109:   | _ -> "unknown"
110: 
111: let utc_timestamp () =
112:   let now = Unix.gettimeofday () in
113:   let tm = Unix.gmtime now in
114:   Printf.sprintf "%04d-%02d-%02dT%02d:%02d:%02dZ"
115:     (tm.tm_year + 1900) (tm.tm_mon + 1) tm.tm_mday
116:     tm.tm_hour tm.tm_min tm.tm_sec
117: 
118: let git_sha () =
119:   match run_cmd_out "git rev-parse --short HEAD" with
120:   | Ok (sha :: _) -> String.trim sha
121:   | _ -> "unknown"
122: 
123: let random_port () =
124:   let s = Unix.socket Unix.PF_INET Unix.SOCK_STREAM 0 in
125:   Fun.protect
126:     ~finally:(fun () -> Unix.close s)
127:     (fun () ->
128:        Unix.setsockopt s Unix.SO_REUSEADDR true;
129:        Unix.bind s (Unix.ADDR_INET (Unix.inet_addr_loopback, 0));
130:        match Unix.getsockname s with
131:        | Unix.ADDR_INET (_, port) -> port
132:        | _ -> failwith "unexpected socket address")
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/red_probes/h2_server_streams/run.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** h2_server_streams: HTTP/2 server-side stream scheduling and request-body
  2:     interleaving red probes.
  3: 
  4:     These probes start an Eta H2C server and then drive it with pathological
  5:     client behaviour across many concurrent streams: interleaved DATA frames,
  6:     RST_STREAM after partial bodies, peer SETTINGS lowering max concurrent
  7:     streams, PRIORITY manipulation, tiny DATA chunks, and HEADERS floods. A hang
  8:     past the deadline is recorded as a finding, not a test failure. *)
  9: 
 10: open Eta_http_testsuite
 11: 
 12: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 13:    Raw HTTP/2 helpers
 14:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 15: 
 16: let h2_preface = Adversarial.h2_client_preface
 17: let write_string flow s = Eio.Flow.write flow [ Cstruct.of_string s ]
 18: 
 19: let read_exact flow buf len =
 20:   let rec loop off =
 21:     if off >= len then ()
 22:     else
 23:       let n = Eio.Flow.single_read flow (Cstruct.sub buf off (len - off)) in
 24:       if n = 0 then raise End_of_file else loop (off + n)
 25:   in
 26:   loop 0
 27: 
 28: let read_frame_header flow =
 29:   let buf = Cstruct.create 9 in
 30:   read_exact flow buf 9;
 31:   let len =
 32:     (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 0 lsl 16)
 33:     lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 1 lsl 8)
 34:     lor Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 2
 35:   in
 36:   let ty = Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 3 in
 37:   let flags = Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 4 in
 38:   let stream_id =
 39:     ((Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 5 land 0x7F) lsl 24)
 40:     lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 6 lsl 16)
 41:     lor (Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 7 lsl 8)
 42:     lor Cstruct.get_uint8 buf 8
 43:   in
 44:   (len, ty, flags, stream_id)
 45: 
 46: let read_payload flow len =
 47:   if len = 0 then ""
 48:   else
 49:     let buf = Cstruct.create len in
 50:     read_exact flow buf len;
 51:     Cstruct.to_string buf
 52: 
 53: let settings_ack = Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []
 54: 
 55: let h2_handshake ?(settings = []) flow =
 56:   write_string flow h2_preface;
 57:   write_string flow (Malicious_h2.settings_frame settings);
 58:   let rec loop () =
 59:     let len, ty, _flags, _sid = read_frame_header flow in
 60:     let payload = read_payload flow len in
 61:     if ty = 0x04 then (
 62:       write_string flow settings_ack;
 63:       payload)
 64:     else loop ()
 65:   in
 66:   loop ()
 67: 
 68: let parse_frames s =
 69:   let rec loop off acc =
 70:     if off + 9 > String.length s then List.rev acc
 71:     else
 72:       let len =
 73:         (Char.code s.[off] lsl 16)
 74:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 1] lsl 8)
 75:         lor Char.code s.[off + 2]
 76:       in
 77:       let ty = Char.code s.[off + 3] in
 78:       let flags = Char.code s.[off + 4] in
 79:       let stream_id =
 80:         ((Char.code s.[off + 5] land 0x7F) lsl 24)
 81:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 6] lsl 16)
 82:         lor (Char.code s.[off + 7] lsl 8)
 83:         lor Char.code s.[off + 8]
 84:       in
 85:       if off + 9 + len > String.length s then List.rev acc
 86:       else loop (off + 9 + len) ((ty, flags, stream_id) :: acc)
 87:   in
 88:   loop 0 []
 89: 
 90: let count_response_headers frames =
 91:   List.fold_left
 92:     (fun acc (ty, flags, sid) ->
 93:       if ty = 0x01 && flags land 0x04 <> 0 && sid land 1 = 1 then acc + 1
 94:       else acc)
 95:     0 frames
 96: 
 97: let has_goaway frames = List.exists (fun (ty, _, _) -> ty = 0x07) frames
 98: let has_rst_stream frames = List.exists (fun (ty, _, _) -> ty = 0x03) frames
 99: 
100: (** Read until the server stops sending data for [seconds]. *)
101: let drain_available ~clock ~seconds flow =
102:   let buffer = Buffer.create 256 in
103:   let scratch = Cstruct.create 1024 in
104:   try
105:     Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock seconds (fun () ->
106:         let rec loop () =
107:           match Eio.Flow.single_read flow scratch with
108:           | 0 -> ()
109:           | n ->
110:               Buffer.add_string buffer
111:                 (Cstruct.to_string (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 n));
112:               loop ()
113:           | exception End_of_file -> ()
114:         in
115:         loop ();
116:         `Drained (Buffer.contents buffer))
117:   with
118:   | Eio.Time.Timeout -> `Timeout (Buffer.contents buffer)
119:   | exn -> `Error (Printexc.to_string exn, Buffer.contents buffer)
120: 
121: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122:    Server handlers
123:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
124: 
125: let immediate_handler _request = Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.text "ok\n")
126: 
127: let echo_handler request =
128:   Eta_http.Server.Body.read_all request.Eta_http.Server.Request.body
129:   |> Eta.Effect.map (fun body ->
130:          Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200
131:            ~body:(Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.fixed [ body ])
132:            ())
133: 
134: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135:    Probe harness
136:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
137: 
138: type status = PASS | FAIL | HANG | CRASH | POLICY_GAP
139: 
140: let string_of_status = function
141:   | PASS -> "PASS"
142:   | FAIL -> "FAIL"
143:   | HANG -> "HANG"
144:   | CRASH -> "CRASH"
145:   | POLICY_GAP -> "POLICY_GAP"
146: 
147: let print_probe name status detail =
148:   Printf.printf "probe %s %s%s\n%!" name (string_of_status status)
149:     (match detail with None -> "" | Some d -> " [" ^ d ^ "]")
150: 
151: type outcome =
152:   | Closed of string
153:   | Read_timeout of string
154:   | Probe_timeout
155:   | Errored of string
156: 
157: let tcp_port = function
158:   | `Tcp (_, port) -> port
159:   | `Unix _ -> failwith "expected TCP listener"
160: 
161: let h2_config_with_max_streams n =
162:   let base = Adversarial.h2_adversarial_config () in
163:   {
164:     base with
165:     Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config =
166:       { base.Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config with
167:         H2.Config.max_concurrent_streams = Int32.of_int n
168:       };
169:   }
170: 
171: let run_h2c_probe ~env ~name ~deadline_sec ~read_deadline_sec ?config ~handler
172:     client_fn =
173:   let outcome =
174:     try
175:       Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
176:       let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
177:       let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
178:       let socket =
179:         Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
180:           (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
181:       in
182:       let port = tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
183:       let config = Option.value config ~default:(Adversarial.h2_adversarial_config ()) in
184:       Eio.Fiber.fork_daemon ~sw (fun () ->
185:           Eio.Switch.run @@ fun conn_sw ->
186:           let flow, peer = Eio.Net.accept ~sw:conn_sw socket in
187:           let runtime_factory ~sw ~connection:_ () = Eta_eio.Runtime.create ~sw ~clock () in
188:           Eta_http_eio.H2.Server_connection.run_h2c ~sw:conn_sw ~clock
189:             ~flow:(flow :> Eta_http_eio.H2.Server_connection.flow)
190:             ~peer ~config ~runtime_factory handler;
191:           `Stop_daemon);
192:       let flow =
193:         Eio.Net.connect ~sw net (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
194:       in
195:       Fun.protect
196:         ~finally:(fun () -> try Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `All with _ -> ())
197:         (fun () ->
198:           try
199:             Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock deadline_sec (fun () ->
200:                 client_fn ~clock flow;
201:                 match drain_available ~clock ~seconds:read_deadline_sec flow with
202:                 | `Drained bytes -> Closed bytes
203:                 | `Timeout bytes -> Read_timeout bytes
204:                 | `Error (msg, bytes) ->
205:                     Errored (msg ^ "; drained=" ^ string_of_int (String.length bytes)))
206:           with
207:           | Eio.Time.Timeout -> Probe_timeout
208:           | exn -> Errored (Printexc.to_string exn))
209:     with exn -> Errored (Printexc.to_string exn)
210:   in
211:   (name, outcome)
212: 
213: let assess_responses name ~expected outcome =
214:   match outcome with
215:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
216:   | Errored msg ->
217:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
218:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
219:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
220:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
221:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
222:       let responses = count_response_headers frames in
223:       if responses >= expected then
224:         print_probe name PASS
225:           (Some
226:              (Printf.sprintf "%d/%d responses (bytes=%d)" responses expected
227:                 (String.length bytes)))
228:       else if has_goaway frames || has_rst_stream frames then
229:         print_probe name FAIL
230:           (Some
231:              (Printf.sprintf "only %d/%d responses, error frame seen (bytes=%d)"
232:                 responses expected (String.length bytes)))
233:       else
234:         print_probe name HANG
235:           (Some
236:              (Printf.sprintf "only %d/%d responses, connection still open"
237:                 responses expected))
238: 
239: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
240:    Probe implementations
241:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
242: 
243: let probe_data_interleaved ~env () =
244:   let streams = 20 in
245:   let rounds = 4 in
246:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
247:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
248:     for i = 1 to streams do
249:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
250:       write_string flow
251:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
252:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ())
253:     done;
254:     for round = 1 to rounds do
255:       for i = 1 to streams do
256:         let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
257:         let chunk = Printf.sprintf "r%02d" round in
258:         write_string flow
259:           (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id chunk)
260:       done
261:     done;
262:     for i = 1 to streams do
263:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
264:       write_string flow (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id "")
265:     done;
266:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
267:   in
268:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_data_interleaved"
269:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0 ~handler:echo_handler client_logic
270:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:streams outcome
271: 
272: let probe_rst_stream_during_bodies ~env () =
273:   let streams = 10 in
274:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
275:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
276:     for i = 1 to streams do
277:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
278:       write_string flow
279:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
280:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ())
281:     done;
282:     (* First round of DATA on every stream. *)
283:     for i = 1 to streams do
284:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
285:       write_string flow
286:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id "first")
287:     done;
288:     (* Reset stream 1 while its body is still incomplete and other streams are
289:        active. *)
290:     write_string flow (Malicious_h2.rst_stream_frame ~stream_id:1 8);
291:     (* Continue completing the remaining streams. *)
292:     for i = 2 to streams do
293:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
294:       write_string flow
295:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id "last")
296:     done;
297:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
298:   in
299:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_rst_during_bodies"
300:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0 ~handler:echo_handler client_logic
301:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:(streams - 1) outcome
302: 
303: let probe_settings_lower_max_concurrent_streams ~env () =
304:   let streams = 10 in
305:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
306:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
307:     for i = 1 to streams do
308:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
309:       write_string flow
310:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
311:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ())
312:     done;
313:     (* Peer tells the server it may not open more than one concurrent
314:        server-initiated stream. This must not affect the already-open client
315:        streams. *)
316:     write_string flow (Malicious_h2.settings_frame [ (0x3, 1) ]);
317:     let rec wait_ack () =
318:       let len, ty, flags, _sid = read_frame_header flow in
319:       if ty = 0x04 && flags land 0x01 <> 0 then ()
320:       else (
321:         ignore (read_payload flow len : string);
322:         wait_ack ())
323:     in
324:     wait_ack ();
325:     for i = 1 to streams do
326:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
327:       write_string flow
328:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id "done")
329:     done;
330:     (* Open one more stream after the peer setting took effect. *)
331:     let stream_id = (streams * 2) + 1 in
332:     write_string flow
333:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"GET" ~stream_id
334:          ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ());
335:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
336:   in
337:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_settings_lower_max_concurrent"
338:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0
339:     ~config:(h2_config_with_max_streams 100)
340:     ~handler:echo_handler client_logic
341:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:(streams + 1) outcome
342: 
343: let probe_priority_self_dependency ~env () =
344:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
345:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
346:     write_string flow
347:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:true
348:          ~end_headers:true ());
349:     (* PRIORITY frame making stream 3 depend exclusively on itself. This is a
350:        protocol violation and should be rejected on stream 3. *)
351:     let priority_payload = "\x80\x00\x00\x03\x10" in
352:     write_string flow
353:       (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x02 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:3 priority_payload);
354:     write_string flow
355:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:3 ~end_stream:true
356:          ~end_headers:true ());
357:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
358:   in
359:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_priority_self_dependency"
360:     ~deadline_sec:3.0 ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 ~handler:immediate_handler
361:     client_logic
362:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
363:   match outcome with
364:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
365:   | Errored msg ->
366:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
367:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
368:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
369:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
370:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
371:       let stream1_ok = List.exists (fun (ty, f, sid) -> ty = 0x01 && f land 0x04 <> 0 && sid = 1) frames in
372:       let stream3_error =
373:         List.exists
374:           (fun (ty, _, sid) -> (ty = 0x03 || ty = 0x07) && sid = 3)
375:           frames
376:       in
377:       if stream1_ok && stream3_error then
378:         print_probe name PASS (Some "stream 1 responded, stream 3 rejected")
379:       else if stream1_ok then
380:         print_probe name POLICY_GAP
381:           (Some "stream 1 responded but self-dependency was ignored")
382:       else
383:         print_probe name FAIL
384:           (Some
385:              (Printf.sprintf "stream 1 did not respond (bytes=%d)"
386:                 (String.length bytes)))
387: 
388: let probe_tiny_data_chunks ~env () =
389:   let streams = 40 in
390:   let chunks_per_stream = 4 in
391:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
392:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
393:     for i = 1 to streams do
394:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
395:       write_string flow
396:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
397:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ())
398:     done;
399:     for chunk = 1 to chunks_per_stream do
400:       for i = 1 to streams do
401:         let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
402:         let end_stream = chunk = chunks_per_stream in
403:         write_string flow
404:           (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream ~stream_id
405:              (String.make 1 (Char.chr (0x60 + chunk))))
406:       done
407:     done;
408:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
409:   in
410:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_tiny_data_chunks"
411:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0 ~handler:echo_handler client_logic
412:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:streams outcome
413: 
414: let probe_headers_flood_no_data ~env () =
415:   let streams = 80 in
416:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
417:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
418:     for i = 1 to streams do
419:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
420:       write_string flow
421:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"GET" ~stream_id
422:            ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ())
423:     done;
424:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
425:   in
426:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_headers_flood_no_data"
427:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0
428:     ~config:(h2_config_with_max_streams 128)
429:     ~handler:immediate_handler client_logic
430:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:streams outcome
431: 
432: let probe_unread_bodies_interleaved ~env () =
433:   let streams = 30 in
434:   let rounds = 3 in
435:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
436:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
437:     for i = 1 to streams do
438:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
439:       write_string flow
440:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
441:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ())
442:     done;
443:     for round = 1 to rounds do
444:       for i = 1 to streams do
445:         let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
446:         let end_stream = round = rounds in
447:         write_string flow
448:           (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream ~stream_id
449:              (Printf.sprintf "round%d" round))
450:       done
451:     done;
452:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
453:   in
454:   (* Handler returns a response without reading the request bodies, so the
455:      server must reset/discard each stream without stalling. *)
456:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_unread_bodies_interleaved"
457:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0 ~handler:immediate_handler
458:     client_logic
459:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:streams outcome
460: 
461: let probe_stalled_body_not_blocking ~env () =
462:   let streams = 10 in
463:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
464:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
465:     (* Stream 1 starts a POST body but never sends DATA. *)
466:     write_string flow
467:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id:1
468:          ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ());
469:     (* Other streams complete fully. *)
470:     for i = 2 to streams do
471:       let stream_id = (i * 2) - 1 in
472:       write_string flow
473:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id
474:            ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ());
475:       write_string flow
476:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id "done")
477:     done;
478:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
479:   in
480:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_stalled_body_not_blocking"
481:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 ~read_deadline_sec:2.0 ~handler:echo_handler client_logic
482:   |> fun (name, outcome) -> assess_responses name ~expected:(streams - 1) outcome
483: 
484: let probe_empty_data_flood_many_streams ~env () =
485:   let flood_count = 101 in
486:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
487:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
488:     write_string flow
489:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id:1
490:          ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ());
491:     (* Spread empty DATA frames across several open/idle streams. The per-
492:        connection empty-data limit should fire after the 100th frame. *)
493:     for i = 1 to flood_count do
494:       let stream_id = 1 + ((i mod 5) * 2) in
495:       write_string flow (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id "")
496:     done;
497:     (* A later valid request should not be processed after the flood. *)
498:     write_string flow
499:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"GET" ~stream_id:11
500:          ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ());
501:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
502:   in
503:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_empty_data_flood"
504:     ~deadline_sec:3.0 ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 ~handler:immediate_handler
505:     client_logic
506:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
507:   match outcome with
508:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
509:   | Errored msg ->
510:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
511:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
512:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
513:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
514:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
515:       if has_goaway frames || has_rst_stream frames then
516:         print_probe name PASS (Some "server rejected empty-data flood")
517:       else
518:         print_probe name FAIL
519:           (Some
520:              (Printf.sprintf "server accepted %d empty DATA frames (bytes=%d)"
521:                 flood_count (String.length bytes)))
522: 
523: let probe_priority_on_stream_zero ~env () =
524:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
525:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
526:     write_string flow
527:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ~end_stream:true
528:          ~end_headers:true ());
529:     (* PRIORITY frames must not be sent on stream 0. *)
530:     write_string flow
531:       (Malicious_h2.frame ~ty:0x02 ~flags:0x00 ~stream_id:0
532:          "\x00\x00\x00\x00\x10");
533:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
534:   in
535:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_priority_on_stream_zero"
536:     ~deadline_sec:3.0 ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 ~handler:immediate_handler
537:     client_logic
538:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
539:   match outcome with
540:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
541:   | Errored msg ->
542:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
543:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
544:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
545:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
546:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
547:       if has_goaway frames then
548:         print_probe name PASS (Some "server closed with GOAWAY")
549:       else
550:         print_probe name POLICY_GAP
551:           (Some
552:              (Printf.sprintf "server ignored PRIORITY on stream 0 (bytes=%d)"
553:                 (String.length bytes)))
554: 
555: let probe_settings_flood_mid_stream ~env () =
556:   let settings_count = 11 in
557:   let client_logic ~clock:_ flow =
558:     ignore (h2_handshake flow : string);
559:     write_string flow
560:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~stream_id:1
561:          ~end_stream:false ~end_headers:true ());
562:     for _i = 1 to settings_count do
563:       write_string flow (Malicious_h2.settings_frame [ (0x3, 10) ])
564:     done;
565:     write_string flow
566:       (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"GET" ~stream_id:3
567:          ~end_stream:true ~end_headers:true ());
568:     Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send
569:   in
570:   run_h2c_probe ~env ~name:"h2_streams_settings_flood_mid_stream"
571:     ~deadline_sec:3.0 ~read_deadline_sec:1.0 ~handler:immediate_handler
572:     client_logic
573:   |> fun (name, outcome) ->
574:   match outcome with
575:   | Probe_timeout -> print_probe name HANG (Some "probe deadline exceeded")
576:   | Errored msg ->
577:       if String.starts_with ~prefix:"Eio.Time.Timeout" msg then
578:         print_probe name HANG (Some "deadline exceeded")
579:       else print_probe name CRASH (Some msg)
580:   | Read_timeout bytes | Closed bytes ->
581:       let frames = parse_frames bytes in
582:       if has_goaway frames then
583:         print_probe name PASS (Some "server closed with GOAWAY")
584:       else
585:         print_probe name FAIL
586:           (Some
587:              (Printf.sprintf "server accepted %d SETTINGS frames (bytes=%d)"
588:                 settings_count (String.length bytes)))
589: 
590: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
591:    Main
592:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
593: 
594: let () =
595:   Eio_main.run @@ fun env ->
596:   let probes =
597:     [
598:       probe_data_interleaved;
599:       probe_rst_stream_during_bodies;
600:       probe_settings_lower_max_concurrent_streams;
601:       probe_priority_self_dependency;
602:       probe_tiny_data_chunks;
603:       probe_headers_flood_no_data;
604:       probe_unread_bodies_interleaved;
605:       probe_stalled_body_not_blocking;
606:       probe_empty_data_flood_many_streams;
607:       probe_priority_on_stream_zero;
608:       probe_settings_flood_mid_stream;
609:     ]
610:   in
611:   List.iter (fun f -> f ~env ()) probes;
612:   Printf.printf "h2_server_streams done\n%!"
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/red_probes/handler_fail/run.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Red probe: handler failure phases.
  2:    Each probe installs a misbehaving Eta handler and drives the server with a
  3:    minimal raw client. The goal is to surface hangs, crashes, or protocol-level
  4:    divergence, not to assert correctness. *)
  5: 
  6: open Eta_http_testsuite
  7: 
  8: type probe_status = Pass | Fail | Hang | Crash | Policy_gap
  9: 
 10: let string_of_status = function
 11:   | Pass -> "PASS"
 12:   | Fail -> "FAIL"
 13:   | Hang -> "HANG"
 14:   | Crash -> "CRASH"
 15:   | Policy_gap -> "POLICY_GAP"
 16: 
 17: let print_probe name status detail =
 18:   match detail with
 19:   | None -> Printf.printf "probe %s %s\n%!" name (string_of_status status)
 20:   | Some d ->
 21:       Printf.printf "probe %s %s %s\n%!" name (string_of_status status) d
 22: 
 23: let header_list entries =
 24:   match Eta_http.Core.Header.of_list entries with
 25:   | Ok headers -> headers
 26:   | Error _ -> invalid_arg "handler_fail: invalid header list"
 27: 
 28: let h1_get_request =
 29:   "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: example.test\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"
 30: 
 31: let h2_request =
 32:   Adversarial.h2_client_preface
 33:   ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []
 34:   ^ Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()
 35: 
 36: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 37:    H1 driver
 38:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
 39: 
 40: let run_h1_probe ~env ~handler ~deadline_sec ~judge () =
 41:   try
 42:     Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
 43:     let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
 44:     let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
 45:     let socket =
 46:       Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
 47:         (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
 48:     in
 49:     let port = Adversarial.tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
 50:     let config = Adversarial.h1_adversarial_config () in
 51:     let server =
 52:       Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~config ~socket handler
 53:     in
 54:     let flow =
 55:       Eio.Net.connect ~sw net (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
 56:     in
 57:     Fun.protect
 58:       ~finally:(fun () ->
 59:         (try Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `All with _ -> ());
 60:         Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate)
 61:       (fun () ->
 62:         try
 63:           let response =
 64:             Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock deadline_sec (fun () ->
 65:                 Eio.Flow.copy_string h1_get_request flow;
 66:                 Adversarial.read_h1_response flow)
 67:           in
 68:           judge response
 69:         with
 70:         | Eio.Time.Timeout -> (Hang, Some "deadline exceeded")
 71:         | exn -> (Crash, Some (Printexc.to_string exn)))
 72:   with exn -> (Crash, Some (Printexc.to_string exn))
 73: 
 74: let h1_body_after_headers response =
 75:   let len = String.length response in
 76:   let rec find off =
 77:     if off + 4 > len then ""
 78:     else if String.sub response off 4 = "\r\n\r\n" then
 79:       String.sub response (off + 4) (len - off - 4)
 80:     else find (off + 1)
 81:   in
 82:   find 0
 83: 
 84: let judge_h1_handler_raise response =
 85:   match Adversarial.h1_status response with
 86:   | Some 500 -> (Pass, Some "status 500")
 87:   | Some s -> (Fail, Some (Printf.sprintf "status %d" s))
 88:   | None -> (Crash, Some "no status line")
 89: 
 90: let judge_h1_body_thunk response =
 91:   match Adversarial.h1_status response with
 92:   | Some 500 -> (Pass, Some "recovered with 500")
 93:   | Some s ->
 94:       ( Policy_gap,
 95:         Some (Printf.sprintf "status %d before connection closed" s) )
 96:   | None -> (Crash, Some "no response")
 97: 
 98: let judge_h1_stream_partial response =
 99:   match Adversarial.h1_status response with
100:   | Some 500 -> (Pass, Some "recovered with 500")
101:   | Some 200 ->
102:       let body = h1_body_after_headers response in
103:       let body_len = String.length body in
104:       if body_len < 10 then
105:         ( Policy_gap,
106:           Some
107:             (Printf.sprintf "200 with truncated body len=%d (declared 10)"
108:                body_len) )
109:       else (Pass, Some "200 with full body")
110:   | Some s -> (Policy_gap, Some (Printf.sprintf "status %d" s))
111:   | None -> (Crash, Some "no response")
112: 
113: let judge_h1_trailers response =
114:   match Adversarial.h1_status response with
115:   | Some 500 -> (Pass, Some "recovered with 500")
116:   | Some 200 when String.ends_with ~suffix:"0\r\n\r\n" response ->
117:       (Pass, Some "chunked response completed")
118:   | Some 200 ->
119:       (Policy_gap, Some "200 without terminal chunk after trailer failure")
120:   | Some s -> (Policy_gap, Some (Printf.sprintf "status %d" s))
121:   | None -> (Crash, Some "no response")
122: 
123: let judge_h1_cancel response =
124:   match Adversarial.h1_status response with
125:   | Some 500 -> (Pass, Some "status 500")
126:   | Some s -> (Policy_gap, Some (Printf.sprintf "status %d" s))
127:   | None -> (Policy_gap, Some "connection closed without response")
128: 
129: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
130:    H2C driver
131:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
132: 
133: let h2_has_frame bytes ty =
134:   let len = String.length bytes in
135:   let rec scan off =
136:     if off + 9 > len then false
137:     else
138:       let frame_type = Char.code (String.get bytes (off + 3)) in
139:       if frame_type = ty then true
140:       else
141:         let length =
142:           (Char.code (String.get bytes off) lsl 16)
143:           lor (Char.code (String.get bytes (off + 1)) lsl 8)
144:           lor Char.code (String.get bytes (off + 2))
145:         in
146:         scan (off + 9 + length)
147:   in
148:   scan 0
149: 
150: let h2_summary bytes =
151:   Printf.sprintf "len=%d headers=%b rst=%b" (String.length bytes)
152:     (h2_has_frame bytes 0x01) (h2_has_frame bytes 0x03)
153: 
154: let read_h2_until ?(max_bytes = 64 * 1024) ~stop_when flow =
155:   let buffer = Buffer.create 256 in
156:   let scratch = Cstruct.create 1024 in
157:   let rec loop total =
158:     let bytes = Buffer.contents buffer in
159:     if stop_when bytes || total >= max_bytes then bytes
160:     else
161:       let len = min (Cstruct.length scratch) (max_bytes - total) in
162:       match Eio.Flow.single_read flow (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 len) with
163:       | 0 -> Buffer.contents buffer
164:       | read ->
165:           Buffer.add_string buffer
166:             (Cstruct.to_string (Cstruct.sub scratch 0 read));
167:           loop (total + read)
168:       | exception End_of_file -> Buffer.contents buffer
169:   in
170:   loop 0
171: 
172: let h2_judge_with_rst detail bytes =
173:   let has_headers = h2_has_frame bytes 0x01 in
174:   let has_rst = h2_has_frame bytes 0x03 in
175:   let summary = h2_summary bytes in
176:   match (has_headers, has_rst) with
177:   | true, true -> (Pass, Some ("headers + rst_stream: " ^ detail ^ " (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
178:   | true, false ->
179:       ( Fail,
180:         Some ("headers without rst_stream: " ^ detail ^ " (" ^ summary ^ ")") )
181:   | false, true -> (Fail, Some ("rst_stream without headers (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
182:   | false, false -> (Crash, Some ("no h2 frames received (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
183: 
184: let h2_judge_response detail bytes =
185:   let has_headers = h2_has_frame bytes 0x01 in
186:   let has_rst = h2_has_frame bytes 0x03 in
187:   let summary = h2_summary bytes in
188:   if has_headers && not has_rst then
189:     (Pass, Some ("headers response: " ^ detail ^ " (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
190:   else if has_rst then
191:     (Policy_gap, Some ("unexpected rst_stream: " ^ detail ^ " (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
192:   else (Crash, Some ("no h2 frames received (" ^ summary ^ ")"))
193: 
194: let run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler ~deadline_sec ~stop_when ~judge () =
195:   try
196:     Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
197:     let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
198:     let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
199:     let socket =
200:       Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
201:         (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
202:     in
203:     let port = Adversarial.tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
204:     let config =
205:       Adversarial.h2_adversarial_config
206:         ~idle_timeout:(Some (Eta.Duration.ms 300))
207:         ()
208:     in
209:     let server =
210:       Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h2c_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~config ~socket handler
211:     in
212:     let flow =
213:       Eio.Net.connect ~sw net (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
214:     in
215:     Fun.protect
216:       ~finally:(fun () ->
217:         (try Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `All with _ -> ());
218:         Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate)
219:       (fun () ->
220:         try
221:           let result =
222:             Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock deadline_sec (fun () ->
223:                 Eio.Flow.copy_string h2_request flow;
224:                 Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send;
225:                 read_h2_until ~stop_when flow)
226:           in
227:           judge result
228:         with
229:         | Eio.Time.Timeout -> (Hang, Some "deadline exceeded")
230:         | exn -> (Crash, Some (Printexc.to_string exn)))
231:   with exn -> (Crash, Some (Printexc.to_string exn))
232: 
233: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234:    Misbehaving handlers
235:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
236: 
237: let handler_raise _request = failwith "handler raised before returning effect"
238: 
239: let read_raises () = failwith "response body thunk raised"
240: 
241: let handler_body_thunk_h1 _request =
242:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read_raises in
243:   let headers = header_list [ ("Content-Length", "10") ] in
244:   Eta.Effect.pure
245:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~headers ~body ())
246: 
247: let handler_body_thunk_h2 _request =
248:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read_raises in
249:   Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~body ())
250: 
251: let handler_stream_partial_h1 _request =
252:   let counter = ref 0 in
253:   let read () =
254:     incr counter;
255:     if !counter = 1 then Eta.Effect.pure (Some (Bytes.of_string "abc"))
256:     else failwith "stream read raised after partial body"
257:   in
258:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read in
259:   let headers = header_list [ ("Content-Length", "10") ] in
260:   Eta.Effect.pure
261:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~headers ~body ())
262: 
263: let handler_stream_partial_h2 _request =
264:   let counter = ref 0 in
265:   let read () =
266:     incr counter;
267:     if !counter = 1 then Eta.Effect.pure (Some (Bytes.of_string "abc"))
268:     else failwith "stream read raised after partial body"
269:   in
270:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read in
271:   Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~body ())
272: 
273: let handler_trailers_h1 _request =
274:   let counter = ref 0 in
275:   let read () =
276:     incr counter;
277:     if !counter = 1 then Eta.Effect.pure (Some (Bytes.of_string "hello"))
278:     else Eta.Effect.pure None
279:   in
280:   let trailers () = failwith "trailers construction raised" in
281:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream read in
282:   let headers =
283:     header_list
284:       [ ("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked"); ("Trailer", "X-Trailer") ]
285:   in
286:   Eta.Effect.pure
287:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~headers ~body ~trailers ())
288: 
289: let handler_trailers_h2 _request =
290:   let counter = ref 0 in
291:   let read () =
292:     incr counter;
293:     if !counter = 1 then Eta.Effect.pure (Some (Bytes.of_string "hello"))
294:     else Eta.Effect.pure None
295:   in
296:   let trailers () = failwith "trailers construction raised" in
297:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream read in
298:   Eta.Effect.pure
299:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~body ~trailers ())
300: 
301: let handler_cancel_h1 _request =
302:   let read () =
303:     Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
304:         Eio.Cancel.sub (fun ctx ->
305:             Eio.Cancel.cancel ctx (Failure "probe cancellation during response");
306:             Eio.Cancel.check ctx;
307:             assert false))
308:   in
309:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read in
310:   let headers = header_list [ ("Content-Length", "10") ] in
311:   Eta.Effect.pure
312:     (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~headers ~body ())
313: 
314: let handler_cancel_h2 _request =
315:   let read () =
316:     Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
317:         Eio.Cancel.sub (fun ctx ->
318:             Eio.Cancel.cancel ctx (Failure "probe cancellation during response");
319:             Eio.Cancel.check ctx;
320:             assert false))
321:   in
322:   let body = Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream ~length:10 read in
323:   Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.make ~status:200 ~body ())
324: 
325: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
326:    Orchestration
327:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
328: 
329: let () =
330:   Eio_main.run @@ fun env ->
331:   let probes =
332:     [
333:       ( "h1_handler_raise_before_effect",
334:         run_h1_probe ~env ~handler:handler_raise ~deadline_sec:2.0
335:           ~judge:judge_h1_handler_raise );
336:       ( "h1_response_body_thunk_raise",
337:         run_h1_probe ~env ~handler:handler_body_thunk_h1 ~deadline_sec:2.0
338:           ~judge:judge_h1_body_thunk );
339:       ( "h1_stream_read_raise_after_partial",
340:         run_h1_probe ~env ~handler:handler_stream_partial_h1 ~deadline_sec:2.0
341:           ~judge:judge_h1_stream_partial );
342:       ( "h1_trailers_construction_raise",
343:         run_h1_probe ~env ~handler:handler_trailers_h1 ~deadline_sec:2.0
344:           ~judge:judge_h1_trailers );
345:       ( "h1_cancellation_during_response",
346:         run_h1_probe ~env ~handler:handler_cancel_h1 ~deadline_sec:2.0
347:           ~judge:judge_h1_cancel );
348:       ( "h2_handler_raise_before_effect",
349:         run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler:handler_raise ~deadline_sec:2.0
350:           ~stop_when:(fun bytes -> h2_has_frame bytes 0x01)
351:           ~judge:(h2_judge_response "expected 500 headers") );
352:       ( "h2_response_body_thunk_raise",
353:         run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler:handler_body_thunk_h2 ~deadline_sec:2.0
354:           ~stop_when:(fun bytes -> h2_has_frame bytes 0x03)
355:           ~judge:(h2_judge_with_rst "body thunk raise") );
356:       ( "h2_stream_read_raise_after_partial",
357:         run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler:handler_stream_partial_h2 ~deadline_sec:2.0
358:           ~stop_when:(fun bytes -> h2_has_frame bytes 0x03)
359:           ~judge:(h2_judge_with_rst "partial body then raise") );
360:       ( "h2_trailers_construction_raise",
361:         run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler:handler_trailers_h2 ~deadline_sec:2.0
362:           ~stop_when:(fun bytes -> h2_has_frame bytes 0x03)
363:           ~judge:(h2_judge_with_rst "trailers raise") );
364:       ( "h2_cancellation_during_response",
365:         run_h2c_probe ~env ~handler:handler_cancel_h2 ~deadline_sec:2.0
366:           ~stop_when:(fun bytes -> h2_has_frame bytes 0x03)
367:           ~judge:(h2_judge_with_rst "cancellation") );
368:     ]
369:   in
370:   List.iter
371:     (fun (name, run) ->
372:       let status, detail = run () in
373:       print_probe name status detail)
374:     probes
````

## File: lib/http/body/chunked.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: open Eta
  4: 
  5: module Error = Error
  6: module Header = Header
  7: 
  8: type context = {
  9:   protocol : Error.protocol;
 10:   method_ : string;
 11:   uri : string;
 12: }
 13: 
 14: type reader = {
 15:   read_exact : (int -> (bytes, Error.t) Effect.t);
 16:   read_line : (limit:int -> (string, Error.t) Effect.t);
 17: }
 18: 
 19: type t = {
 20:   context : context;
 21:   reader : reader;
 22:   max_decoded_bytes : int;
 23:   max_trailer_bytes : int;
 24:   max_trailers : int;
 25:   mutable decoded_bytes : int;
 26:   mutable done_ : bool;
 27:   mutable trailers : Header.t;
 28: }
 29: 
 30: let default_line_limit = 8 * 1024
 31: let default_max_decoded_bytes = Stream.default_max_bytes
 32: let default_max_trailer_bytes = 8 * 1024
 33: let default_max_trailers = 64
 34: 
 35: let create ?(max_decoded_bytes = default_max_decoded_bytes)
 36:     ?(max_trailer_bytes = default_max_trailer_bytes)
 37:     ?(max_trailers = default_max_trailers) ~context ~reader () =
 38:   if max_decoded_bytes < 0 then
 39:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.Body.Chunked.create: max_decoded_bytes must be >= 0";
 40:   if max_trailer_bytes < 0 then
 41:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.Body.Chunked.create: max_trailer_bytes must be >= 0";
 42:   if max_trailers < 0 then
 43:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.Body.Chunked.create: max_trailers must be >= 0";
 44:   {
 45:     context;
 46:     reader;
 47:     max_decoded_bytes;
 48:     max_trailer_bytes;
 49:     max_trailers;
 50:     decoded_bytes = 0;
 51:     done_ = false;
 52:     trailers = Header.empty;
 53:   }
 54: 
 55: let decode_error t message =
 56:   Error.make ~protocol:t.context.protocol ~method_:t.context.method_
 57:     ~uri:t.context.uri (Decode_error { codec = "chunked"; message })
 58: 
 59: let body_too_large t length =
 60:   Error.make ~protocol:t.context.protocol ~method_:t.context.method_
 61:     ~uri:t.context.uri
 62:     (Body_too_large { limit = t.max_decoded_bytes; length })
 63: 
 64: let is_hex = function
 65:   | '0' .. '9' | 'a' .. 'f' | 'A' .. 'F' -> true
 66:   | _ -> false
 67: 
 68: let hex_value = function
 69:   | '0' .. '9' as c -> Char.code c - Char.code '0'
 70:   | 'a' .. 'f' as c -> 10 + Char.code c - Char.code 'a'
 71:   | 'A' .. 'F' as c -> 10 + Char.code c - Char.code 'A'
 72:   | _ -> -1
 73: 
 74: let[@zero_alloc] chunk_size_stop line =
 75:   match String.index_opt line ';' with
 76:   | None -> String.length line
 77:   | Some index -> index
 78: 
 79: let invalid_chunk_size line start stop =
 80:   Error ("invalid chunk size " ^ String.escaped (String.sub line start (stop - start)))
 81: 
 82: let parse_size line =
 83:   let raw_stop = chunk_size_stop line in
 84:   if raw_stop = 0 then Error "empty chunk size"
 85:   else if not (is_hex (String.unsafe_get line 0)) then
 86:     invalid_chunk_size line 0 raw_stop
 87:   else
 88:     let rec loop index acc =
 89:       if index = raw_stop then Ok acc
 90:       else
 91:         let c = String.unsafe_get line index in
 92:         if not (is_hex c) then
 93:           invalid_chunk_size line 0 raw_stop
 94:         else
 95:           let digit = hex_value c in
 96:           if acc > (max_int - digit) / 16 then Error "chunk size overflow"
 97:           else loop (index + 1) ((acc * 16) + digit)
 98:     in
 99:     loop 0 0
100: 
101: let parse_trailer line =
102:   match String.index_opt line ':' with
103:   | None -> Error ("invalid trailer " ^ String.escaped line)
104:   | Some 0 -> Error ("invalid trailer " ^ String.escaped line)
105:   | Some index ->
106:       let name = String.sub line 0 index in
107:       let value_start =
108:         Eta.String_helpers.trim_left line (index + 1) (String.length line)
109:       in
110:       let value_stop =
111:         Eta.String_helpers.trim_right line value_start (String.length line)
112:       in
113:       let value = String.sub line value_start (value_stop - value_start) in
114:       Ok (name, value)
115: 
116: let[@zero_alloc] bytes_is_crlf bytes =
117:   Bytes.length bytes = 2
118:   && Char.equal (Bytes.unsafe_get bytes 0) '\r'
119:   && Char.equal (Bytes.unsafe_get bytes 1) '\n'
120: 
121: let invalid_trailer_message = function
122:   | Error.Header_invalid { reason } -> "invalid trailer header: " ^ reason
123:   | kind -> "invalid trailer header: " ^ Error.kind_name kind
124: 
125: (* RFC 7230 6919/7230 4.1.2: framing, routing, and authentication fields must
126:    not appear in trailers, since a recipient merging them into the header set
127:    could be misled about body length/framing or connection handling. *)
128: let forbidden_trailer_name name =
129:   match String.lowercase_ascii (String.trim name) with
130:   | "connection" | "keep-alive" | "proxy-authenticate" | "proxy-authorization"
131:   | "te" | "trailer" | "transfer-encoding" | "upgrade" | "host"
132:   | "content-length" ->
133:       true
134:   | _ -> false
135: 
136: let store_trailers t trailers =
137:   match Header.of_list (List.rev trailers) with
138:   | Ok trailers -> (
139:       match
140:         List.find_opt (fun (name, _) -> forbidden_trailer_name name) trailers
141:       with
142:       | Some (name, _) ->
143:           Effect.fail (decode_error t ("forbidden trailer field: " ^ name))
144:       | None ->
145:           t.trailers <- trailers;
146:           t.done_ <- true;
147:           Effect.pure None)
148:   | Error kind -> Effect.fail (decode_error t (invalid_trailer_message kind))
149: 
150: let trailer_section_too_large t length =
151:   decode_error t
152:     (Printf.sprintf "trailer section too large: limit=%d length=%d"
153:        t.max_trailer_bytes length)
154: 
155: let too_many_trailers t count =
156:   decode_error t
157:     (Printf.sprintf "too many trailers: limit=%d count=%d" t.max_trailers count)
158: 
159: let rec read_trailers t acc bytes count =
160:   t.reader.read_line ~limit:default_line_limit
161:   |> Effect.bind (fun line ->
162:          let next_bytes = bytes + String.length line + 2 in
163:          if next_bytes < bytes || next_bytes > t.max_trailer_bytes then
164:            Effect.fail (trailer_section_too_large t next_bytes)
165:          else if String.equal line "" then store_trailers t acc
166:          else if count >= t.max_trailers then
167:            Effect.fail (too_many_trailers t (count + 1))
168:          else
169:            match parse_trailer line with
170:            | Error message -> Effect.fail (decode_error t message)
171:            | Ok trailer -> read_trailers t (trailer :: acc) next_bytes (count + 1))
172: 
173: let read t =
174:   if t.done_ then Effect.pure None
175:   else
176:     t.reader.read_line ~limit:default_line_limit
177:     |> Effect.bind (fun line ->
178:            match parse_size line with
179:            | Error message -> Effect.fail (decode_error t message)
180:            | Ok 0 -> read_trailers t [] 0 0
181:            | Ok size ->
182:                let next_total = t.decoded_bytes + size in
183:                if next_total < t.decoded_bytes || next_total > t.max_decoded_bytes then
184:                  Effect.fail (body_too_large t next_total)
185:                else
186:                  t.reader.read_exact size
187:                  |> Effect.bind (fun chunk ->
188:                         t.reader.read_exact 2
189:                         |> Effect.bind (fun crlf ->
190:                                if not (bytes_is_crlf crlf) then
191:                                  Effect.fail (decode_error t "chunk data missing CRLF")
192:                                else (
193:                                  t.decoded_bytes <- next_total;
194:                                  Effect.pure (Some chunk)))))
195: 
196: let trailers t = t.trailers
197: 
198: let[@zero_alloc] hex_digit_count value =
199:   let count = ref 1 in
200:   let value = ref (value lsr 4) in
201:   while !value > 0 do
202:     incr count;
203:     value := !value lsr 4
204:   done;
205:   !count
206: 
207: let chunk_header_bytes length =
208:   let digits = hex_digit_count length in
209:   let out = Bytes.create (digits + 2) in
210:   let value = ref length in
211:   for index = digits - 1 downto 0 do
212:     Bytes.unsafe_set out index
213:       (Eta.String_helpers.lower_hex_digit (!value land 0xf));
214:     value := !value lsr 4
215:   done;
216:   Bytes.unsafe_set out digits '\r';
217:   Bytes.unsafe_set out (digits + 1) '\n';
218:   out
219: 
220: let crlf_bytes () =
221:   let out = Bytes.create 2 in
222:   Bytes.unsafe_set out 0 '\r';
223:   Bytes.unsafe_set out 1 '\n';
224:   out
225: 
226: let encode_chunk chunk =
227:   if Bytes.length chunk = 0 then []
228:   else
229:     [
230:       chunk_header_bytes (Bytes.length chunk);
231:       Bytes.copy chunk;
232:       crlf_bytes ();
233:     ]
234: 
235: let encode_last_chunk ?(trailers = Header.empty) () =
236:   (match Header.validate trailers with
237:   | None -> ()
238:   | Some _ ->
239:       invalid_arg
240:         "Eta_http.Body.Chunked.encode_last_chunk: invalid trailer header");
241:   (match
242:      List.find_opt (fun (name, _) -> forbidden_trailer_name name) trailers
243:    with
244:   | None -> ()
245:   | Some (name, _) ->
246:       invalid_arg
247:         ("Eta_http.Body.Chunked.encode_last_chunk: forbidden trailer field "
248:        ^ name));
249:   let buffer = Buffer.create 32 in
250:   Buffer.add_string buffer "0\r\n";
251:   let rec add_trailers = function
252:     | [] -> ()
253:     | (name, value) :: rest ->
254:       Buffer.add_string buffer name;
255:       Buffer.add_string buffer ": ";
256:       Buffer.add_string buffer value;
257:       Buffer.add_string buffer "\r\n";
258:       add_trailers rest
259:   in
260:   add_trailers (List.rev trailers);
261:   Buffer.add_string buffer "\r\n";
262:   Bytes.of_string (Buffer.contents buffer)
````

## File: lib/http/body/chunked.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer coding.
 2: 
 3:     The decoder is stateful and pull-based. It owns only transfer-coding
 4:     framing; callers own the byte source and map transport failures into the
 5:     eta-http error context. *)
 6: 
 7: type context = {
 8:   protocol : Error.protocol;
 9:   method_ : string;
10:   uri : string;
11: }
12: 
13: type reader = {
14:   read_exact : (int -> (bytes, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t);
15:   read_line : (limit:int -> (string, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t);
16: }
17: 
18: type t
19: 
20: val default_line_limit : int
21: val default_max_decoded_bytes : int
22: (** Default maximum decoded bytes for chunked bodies. This matches
23:     {!Stream.default_max_bytes}. *)
24: 
25: val create :
26:   ?max_decoded_bytes:int ->
27:   ?max_trailer_bytes:int ->
28:   ?max_trailers:int ->
29:   context:context ->
30:   reader:reader ->
31:   unit ->
32:   t
33: 
34: val read : t -> (bytes option, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
35: val trailers : t -> Header.t
36: 
37: val forbidden_trailer_name : string -> bool
38: 
39: val encode_chunk : bytes -> bytes list
40: val encode_last_chunk : ?trailers:Header.t -> unit -> bytes
````

## File: lib/http/server_body.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: open Eta
  4: 
  5: module Error = Server_error
  6: 
  7: type t = {
  8:   read_next : unit -> (bytes option, Error.t) Effect.t;
  9:   release : unit -> (unit, Error.t) Effect.t;
 10:   discard_unread : drain:bool -> (unit, Error.t) Effect.t;
 11:   mutable released : bool;
 12:   active : bool Atomic.t;
 13: }
 14: 
 15: let concurrent_use () =
 16:   Error.make ~method_:"*" ~target:"*"
 17:     (Protocol_error
 18:        {
 19:          kind = "body_concurrent_use";
 20:          message = "concurrent server body operation";
 21:        })
 22: 
 23: let with_operation t eff =
 24:   if not (Atomic.compare_and_set t.active false true) then
 25:     Effect.fail (concurrent_use ())
 26:   else
 27:     eff |> Effect.finally (Effect.sync (fun () -> Atomic.set t.active false))
 28: 
 29: let release_once t =
 30:   if t.released then Effect.unit
 31:   else (
 32:     t.released <- true;
 33:     t.release ())
 34: 
 35: let empty () =
 36:   {
 37:     read_next = (fun () -> Effect.pure None);
 38:     release = (fun () -> Effect.unit);
 39:     discard_unread = (fun ~drain:_ -> Effect.unit);
 40:     released = false;
 41:     active = Atomic.make false;
 42:   }
 43: 
 44: let of_reader ?(release = fun () -> Effect.unit)
 45:     ?(discard = fun ~drain:_ -> Effect.unit) read_next =
 46:   {
 47:     read_next;
 48:     release;
 49:     discard_unread = discard;
 50:     released = false;
 51:     active = Atomic.make false;
 52:   }
 53: 
 54: let read_unlocked t =
 55:   if t.released then Effect.pure None
 56:   else
 57:     Effect.sync (fun () -> ref true)
 58:     |> Effect.bind (fun release_needed ->
 59:            let release_if_needed =
 60:              Effect.sync (fun () -> !release_needed)
 61:              |> Effect.bind (fun needed ->
 62:                     if needed then release_once t else Effect.unit)
 63:            in
 64:            t.read_next ()
 65:            |> Effect.bind (function
 66:                 | None -> Effect.pure None
 67:                 | Some chunk ->
 68:                     release_needed := false;
 69:                     Effect.pure (Some chunk))
 70:            |> Effect.finally release_if_needed)
 71: 
 72: let read t = with_operation t (read_unlocked t)
 73: 
 74: let body_too_large ~limit ~length =
 75:   Error.make ~method_:"*" ~target:"*"
 76:     (Request_body_too_large { limit; length })
 77: 
 78: let read_all ?(max_bytes = Stream.default_max_bytes) t =
 79:   if max_bytes < 0 then
 80:     invalid_arg "Eta_http.Server.Body.read_all: max_bytes must be >= 0";
 81:   let rec loop acc total =
 82:     read_unlocked t
 83:     |> Effect.bind (function
 84:          | None ->
 85:              let out = Bytes.create total in
 86:              let _ =
 87:                List.fold_left
 88:                  (fun off chunk ->
 89:                    let len = Bytes.length chunk in
 90:                    Bytes.blit chunk 0 out off len;
 91:                    off + len)
 92:                  0 (List.rev acc)
 93:              in
 94:              Effect.pure out
 95:          | Some chunk ->
 96:              let length = total + Bytes.length chunk in
 97:              if length < total || length > max_bytes then
 98:                Effect.fail (body_too_large ~limit:max_bytes ~length)
 99:              else loop (chunk :: acc) length)
100:   in
101:   with_operation t
102:     (Effect.scoped
103:        (Effect.acquire_release ~acquire:Effect.unit
104:           ~release:(fun () -> release_once t)
105:        |> Effect.bind (fun () -> loop [] 0)))
106: 
107: let discard ?(drain = false) t =
108:   with_operation t
109:     (if t.released then Effect.unit
110:      else (
111:        t.released <- true;
112:        t.discard_unread ~drain))
````

## File: lib/http/server_config.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Backend-neutral HTTP server configuration.
 2: 
 3:     These values describe HTTP semantics that every server backend must honor.
 4:     Runtime adapters keep transport-owned knobs, such as socket backlog and
 5:     read-buffer sizing, in their own configuration records. *)
 6: 
 7: type unread_body_policy =
 8:   | Reset
 9:   | Drain_up_to of int
10: 
11: type limits = {
12:   max_request_line_bytes : int;
13:   max_request_header_bytes : int;
14:   max_request_headers : int;
15:   max_request_body_bytes : int option;
16:   max_response_header_bytes : int;
17:   max_response_headers : int;
18:   max_trailer_bytes : int;
19:   max_trailers : int;
20: }
21: 
22: type timeouts = {
23:   request_header_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
24:   request_body_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
25:   response_write_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
26:   response_body_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
27:   idle_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
28:   handler_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
29: }
30: 
31: type t = {
32:   limits : limits;
33:   timeouts : timeouts;
34:   unread_body_policy : unread_body_policy;
35:   enable_otel : bool;
36:   emit_url_full : bool;
37: }
38: 
39: val default_limits : limits
40: val default_timeouts : timeouts
41: val default : t
42: val validate : t -> unit
````

## File: lib/http/server_handler.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: module Error = Server_error
 4: module Request = Server_request
 5: module Response = Server_response
 6: 
 7: type t = Request.t -> (Response.t, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
 8: 
 9: let map_error f handler request = handler request |> Eta.Effect.map_error f
10: 
11: let default_reason = function
12:   | 400 -> "bad request\n"
13:   | 408 -> "request timeout\n"
14:   | 413 -> "request body too large\n"
15:   | 417 -> "expectation failed\n"
16:   | 503 -> "service unavailable\n"
17:   | _ -> "internal server error\n"
18: 
19: let default_error_response error =
20:   let status = Option.value ~default:500 (Error.to_status error) in
21:   Response.text ~status (default_reason status)
22: 
23: let with_default_error_response ?(renderer = default_error_response) handler request =
24:   handler request
25:   |> Eta.Effect.catch (fun error -> Eta.Effect.pure (renderer error))
26: 
27: let route_not_found _request = Eta.Effect.pure (Response.text ~status:404 "not found\n")
````

## File: lib/http/server_request.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type peer = {
 4:   address : string option;
 5:   port : int option;
 6: }
 7: 
 8: type t = {
 9:   id : string;
10:   version : Version.t;
11:   scheme : string;
12:   authority : string option;
13:   method_ : string;
14:   target : string;
15:   path : string;
16:   query : string option;
17:   headers : Header.t;
18:   body : Server_body.t;
19:   trailers : unit -> (Header.t, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
20:   peer : peer;
21:   tls : bool;
22:   alpn_protocol : string option;
23:   stream_id : int option;
24:   connection_id : string;
25: }
26: 
27: let split_target target =
28:   match String.index_opt target '?' with
29:   | None -> (target, None)
30:   | Some query_start ->
31:       let path = String.sub target 0 query_start in
32:       let query =
33:         String.sub target (query_start + 1)
34:           (String.length target - query_start - 1)
35:       in
36:       (path, Some query)
37: 
38: let header name t = Header.get name t.headers
39: let body t = t.body
40: let trailers t = t.trailers ()
41: let trace_context t = Eta.Trace_context.extract (Header.to_list t.headers)
````

````ocaml
 1: (** Backend-neutral HTTP server request model. *)

 3: type peer = {
````
