## File: lib/http/server_response.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: module Error = Server_error
 4: 
 5: module Body = struct
 6:   type stream = {
 7:     length : int option;
 8:     read : unit -> (bytes option, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
 9:     release : unit -> (unit, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
10:   }
11: 
12:   type t =
13:     | Empty
14:     | Fixed of bytes list
15:     | Stream of stream
16: 
17:   let empty = Empty
18:   let fixed chunks = Fixed (List.map Bytes.copy chunks)
19:   let string value = fixed [ Bytes.of_string value ]
20: 
21:   let stream ?length ?(release = fun () -> Eta.Effect.unit) read =
22:     (match length with
23:     | Some length when length < 0 ->
24:         invalid_arg "Eta_http.Server.Response.Body.stream: length must be >= 0"
25:     | None | Some _ -> ());
26:     Stream { length; read; release }
27: end
28: 
29: type t = {
30:   status : int;
31:   headers : Header.t;
32:   body : Body.t;
33:   trailers : unit -> (Header.t, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
34: }
35: 
36: let validate_status status =
37:   match Status.of_int status with
38:   | Some status -> Status.to_int status
39:   | None ->
40:       invalid_arg
41:         "Eta_http.Server.Response.make: status must be in the range 100..599"
42: 
43: let validate_headers where headers =
44:   match Header.validate headers with
45:   | None -> headers
46:   | Some _ ->
47:       invalid_arg
48:         ("Eta_http.Server.Response." ^ where ^ ": invalid response header")
49: 
50: let make ?(headers = Header.empty)
51:     ?(trailers = fun () -> Eta.Effect.pure Header.empty) ~status ~body () =
52:   {
53:     status = validate_status status;
54:     headers = validate_headers "make" headers;
55:     body;
56:     trailers;
57:   }
58: 
59: let empty ?headers ~status () = make ?headers ~status ~body:Body.empty ()
60: let text ?headers ?(status = 200) value = make ?headers ~status ~body:(Body.string value) ()
61: let status t = t.status
62: let headers t = t.headers
63: let body t = t.body
64: let trailers t = t.trailers
````

## File: lib/http/server_response.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Backend-neutral HTTP server response model. *)
 2: 
 3: module Body : sig
 4:   type stream = {
 5:     length : int option;
 6:     read : unit -> (bytes option, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
 7:     release : unit -> (unit, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t;
 8:   }
 9: 
10:   type t =
11:     | Empty
12:     | Fixed of bytes list
13:     | Stream of stream
14: 
15:   val empty : t
16:   val fixed : bytes list -> t
17:   val string : string -> t
18: 
19:   val stream :
20:     ?length:int ->
21:     ?release:(unit -> (unit, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
22:     (unit -> (bytes option, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
23:     t
24: end
25: 
26: type t
27: 
28: val make :
29:   ?headers:Header.t ->
30:   ?trailers:(unit -> (Header.t, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t) ->
31:   status:int ->
32:   body:Body.t ->
33:   unit ->
34:   t
35: 
36: val empty :
37:   ?headers:Header.t ->
38:   status:int ->
39:   unit ->
40:   t
41: 
42: val text :
43:   ?headers:Header.t ->
44:   ?status:int ->
45:   string ->
46:   t
47: 
48: val status : t -> int
49: val headers : t -> Header.t
50: val body : t -> Body.t
51: val trailers : t -> unit -> (Header.t, Server_error.t) Eta.Effect.t
````

## File: lib/http_eio/server_stats.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: let add_non_negative current amount =
  4:   if amount < 0 then
  5:     invalid_arg "Eta_http_eio.Server_stats: counter increment must be >= 0";
  6:   current + amount
  7: 
  8: module Listener = struct
  9:   type snapshot = {
 10:     active_connections : int;
 11:     opened_connections : int;
 12:     closed_connections : int;
 13:     tls_handshakes : int;
 14:     tls_handshake_failures : int;
 15:     alpn_h1 : int;
 16:     alpn_h2 : int;
 17:     alpn_rejected : int;
 18:     listener_errors : int;
 19:   }
 20: 
 21:   type t = {
 22:     mutable opened_connections : int;
 23:     mutable closed_connections : int;
 24:     mutable tls_handshakes : int;
 25:     mutable tls_handshake_failures : int;
 26:     mutable alpn_h1 : int;
 27:     mutable alpn_h2 : int;
 28:     mutable alpn_rejected : int;
 29:     mutable listener_errors : int;
 30:   }
 31: 
 32:   let create () =
 33:     {
 34:       opened_connections = 0;
 35:       closed_connections = 0;
 36:       tls_handshakes = 0;
 37:       tls_handshake_failures = 0;
 38:       alpn_h1 = 0;
 39:       alpn_h2 = 0;
 40:       alpn_rejected = 0;
 41:       listener_errors = 0;
 42:     }
 43: 
 44:   let opened_connection t =
 45:     t.opened_connections <- t.opened_connections + 1
 46: 
 47:   let closed_connection t =
 48:     t.closed_connections <- t.closed_connections + 1
 49: 
 50:   let tls_handshake t = t.tls_handshakes <- t.tls_handshakes + 1
 51: 
 52:   let tls_handshake_failure t =
 53:     t.tls_handshake_failures <- t.tls_handshake_failures + 1
 54: 
 55:   let alpn_h1 t = t.alpn_h1 <- t.alpn_h1 + 1
 56:   let alpn_h2 t = t.alpn_h2 <- t.alpn_h2 + 1
 57:   let alpn_rejected t = t.alpn_rejected <- t.alpn_rejected + 1
 58:   let listener_error t = t.listener_errors <- t.listener_errors + 1
 59: 
 60:   let snapshot t ~active_connections : snapshot =
 61:     {
 62:       active_connections;
 63:       opened_connections = t.opened_connections;
 64:       closed_connections = t.closed_connections;
 65:       tls_handshakes = t.tls_handshakes;
 66:       tls_handshake_failures = t.tls_handshake_failures;
 67:       alpn_h1 = t.alpn_h1;
 68:       alpn_h2 = t.alpn_h2;
 69:       alpn_rejected = t.alpn_rejected;
 70:       listener_errors = t.listener_errors;
 71:     }
 72: end
 73: 
 74: module H1 = struct
 75:   type snapshot = {
 76:     active_requests : int;
 77:     completed_requests : int;
 78:     request_bytes : int;
 79:     response_bytes : int;
 80:     protocol_errors : int;
 81:   }
 82: 
 83:   type t = {
 84:     mutable active_requests : int;
 85:     mutable completed_requests : int;
 86:     mutable request_bytes : int;
 87:     mutable response_bytes : int;
 88:     mutable protocol_errors : int;
 89:   }
 90: 
 91:   let create () =
 92:     {
 93:       active_requests = 0;
 94:       completed_requests = 0;
 95:       request_bytes = 0;
 96:       response_bytes = 0;
 97:       protocol_errors = 0;
 98:     }
 99: 
100:   let request_started t = t.active_requests <- t.active_requests + 1
101: 
102:   let request_completed t =
103:     if t.active_requests > 0 then t.active_requests <- t.active_requests - 1;
104:     t.completed_requests <- t.completed_requests + 1
105: 
106:   let add_request_bytes t amount =
107:     t.request_bytes <- add_non_negative t.request_bytes amount
108: 
109:   let add_response_bytes t amount =
110:     t.response_bytes <- add_non_negative t.response_bytes amount
111: 
112:   let protocol_error t = t.protocol_errors <- t.protocol_errors + 1
113: 
114:   let snapshot t : snapshot =
115:     {
116:       active_requests = t.active_requests;
117:       completed_requests = t.completed_requests;
118:       request_bytes = t.request_bytes;
119:       response_bytes = t.response_bytes;
120:       protocol_errors = t.protocol_errors;
121:     }
122: end
123: 
124: module H2 = struct
125:   type snapshot = {
126:     active_streams : int;
127:     opened_streams : int;
128:     completed_streams : int;
129:     reset_streams : int;
130:     request_bytes : int;
131:     response_bytes : int;
132:     protocol_errors : int;
133:   }
134: 
135:   type t = {
136:     mutable opened_streams : int;
137:     mutable completed_streams : int;
138:     mutable reset_streams : int;
139:     mutable request_bytes : int;
140:     mutable response_bytes : int;
141:     mutable protocol_errors : int;
142:   }
143: 
144:   let create () =
145:     {
146:       opened_streams = 0;
147:       completed_streams = 0;
148:       reset_streams = 0;
149:       request_bytes = 0;
150:       response_bytes = 0;
151:       protocol_errors = 0;
152:     }
153: 
154:   let stream_opened t = t.opened_streams <- t.opened_streams + 1
155:   let stream_completed t = t.completed_streams <- t.completed_streams + 1
156:   let stream_reset t = t.reset_streams <- t.reset_streams + 1
157: 
158:   let add_reset_streams t amount =
159:     t.reset_streams <- add_non_negative t.reset_streams amount
160: 
161:   let add_request_bytes t amount =
162:     t.request_bytes <- add_non_negative t.request_bytes amount
163: 
164:   let add_response_bytes t amount =
165:     t.response_bytes <- add_non_negative t.response_bytes amount
166: 
167:   let protocol_error t = t.protocol_errors <- t.protocol_errors + 1
168: 
169:   let snapshot t ~active_streams : snapshot =
170:     {
171:       active_streams;
172:       opened_streams = t.opened_streams;
173:       completed_streams = t.completed_streams;
174:       reset_streams = t.reset_streams;
175:       request_bytes = t.request_bytes;
176:       response_bytes = t.response_bytes;
177:       protocol_errors = t.protocol_errors;
178:     }
179: end
````

 3: module Listener : sig

 5:     active_connections : int;

 7:     closed_connections : int;
 8:     tls_handshakes : int;

12:     alpn_rejected : int;
13:     listener_errors : int;

20:   val closed_connection : t -> unit
21:   val tls_handshake : t -> unit

24:   val alpn_h2 : t -> unit
25:   val alpn_rejected : t -> unit
26:   val listener_error : t -> unit
27:   val snapshot : t -> active_connections:int -> snapshot
28: end
29: 
30: module H1 : sig
31:   type snapshot = {
32:     active_requests : int;
33:     completed_requests : int;
34:     request_bytes : int;
35:     response_bytes : int;
36:     protocol_errors : int;
37:   }
38: 
39:   type t
40: 
41:   val create : unit -> t
42:   val request_started : t -> unit
43:   val request_completed : t -> unit
44:   val add_request_bytes : t -> int -> unit
45:   val add_response_bytes : t -> int -> unit
46:   val protocol_error : t -> unit

48: end

## File: test/http/test_eta_http_h2_security.ml
````ocaml
  1: open Test_eta_http_support
  2: open Test_eta_http_h2_support
  3: 
  4: let raw_h2_frame ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id payload =
  5:   h2_frame_header ~length:(String.length payload) ~frame_type ~flags ~stream_id
  6:   ^ payload
  7: 
  8: let expect_h2_security_error label frame =
  9:   match h2_observe_security frame with
 10:   | Some (Eta_http.Error.Connection_protocol_violation _) -> ()
 11:   | Some kind ->
 12:       Alcotest.failf "%s: unexpected security error: %s" label
 13:         (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
 14:   | None -> Alcotest.failf "%s: malformed frame passed H2.Security.observe" label
 15: 
 16: let expect_h2_security_ok label frame =
 17:   match h2_observe_security frame with
 18:   | None -> ()
 19:   | Some kind ->
 20:       Alcotest.failf "%s: unexpected security error: %s" label
 21:         (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
 22: 
 23: let h2_settings_pair id value =
 24:   let bytes = Bytes.create 6 in
 25:   Bytes.set bytes 0 (Char.chr ((id lsr 8) land 0xff));
 26:   Bytes.set bytes 1 (Char.chr (id land 0xff));
 27:   Bytes.blit_string (h2_uint32 value) 0 bytes 2 4;
 28:   Bytes.unsafe_to_string bytes
 29: 
 30: let h2_settings_payload payload =
 31:   Eta_http.H2.Frame.header ~length:(String.length payload)
 32:     ~frame_type:Eta_http.H2.Frame.Settings ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0
 33:   ^ payload
 34: 
 35: let observe_with security data =
 36:   let bs = Bigstringaf.of_string ~off:0 ~len:(String.length data) data in
 37:   Eta_http.H2.Security.observe security bs ~off:0 ~len:(String.length data)
 38: 
 39: let test_h2_multiplexer_buffer_full_is_security_error () =
 40:   let client =
 41:     H2.Client_connection.create
 42:       ~error_handler:(fun _ -> Alcotest.fail "unexpected h2 client error")
 43:       ()
 44:   in
 45:   let reader = Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.create_client_reader ~buffer_size:8 client in
 46:   let partial_headers =
 47:     String.sub
 48:       (h2_frame_header ~length:64 ~frame_type:0x1 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1)
 49:       0 8
 50:   in
 51:   let source =
 52:     Eio.Flow.cstruct_source (h2_cstruct_chunks ~chunk_size:8 partial_headers)
 53:   in
 54:   match Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.read_client_once ~flow:source reader with
 55:   | Security_error
 56:       (Connection_protocol_violation
 57:         { kind = "h2_read_buffer_exhausted"; message = _ }) -> ()
 58:   | Security_error kind ->
 59:       Alcotest.failf "unexpected security error: %s"
 60:         (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
 61:   | Read _ | Eof _ | Close ->
 62:       Alcotest.fail "buffer-full read did not surface a typed security error"
 63: 
 64: let test_h2_security_settings_churn_reader () =
 65:   let client =
 66:     H2.Client_connection.create
 67:       ~error_handler:(fun _ -> Alcotest.fail "unexpected h2 client error")
 68:       ()
 69:   in
 70:   let reader = Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.create_client_reader ~buffer_size:64 client in
 71:   let source =
 72:     Eio.Flow.cstruct_source
 73:       (h2_cstruct_chunks ~chunk_size:11
 74:          (String.concat "" (List.init 11 (fun _ -> h2_settings_frame))))
 75:   in
 76:   let rec loop attempts =
 77:     if attempts = 0 then Alcotest.fail "settings churn was not detected"
 78:     else
 79:       match Eta_http_eio.H2.Multiplexer.read_client_once ~flow:source reader with
 80:       | Security_error
 81:           (Settings_count_exceeded { observed_count; limit }) ->
 82:           Alcotest.(check int) "observed" 11 observed_count;
 83:           Alcotest.(check int) "limit" 10 limit
 84:       | Security_error kind ->
 85:           Alcotest.failf "unexpected security error: %s"
 86:             (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
 87:       | Read _ | Eof _ -> loop (attempts - 1)
 88:       | Close -> Alcotest.fail "client closed before settings churn detection"
 89:   in
 90:   loop 32
 91: 
 92: let test_h2_security_rejects_invalid_control_frame_envelopes () =
 93:   expect_h2_security_error "PING wrong length"
 94:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x6 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "1234567");
 95:   expect_h2_security_error "PING nonzero stream"
 96:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x6 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "12345678");
 97:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS nonzero stream"
 98:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x4 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "");
 99:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS ACK with payload"
100:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x4 ~flags:0x1 ~stream_id:0 "123456");
101:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS invalid length"
102:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x4 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "12345");
103:   expect_h2_security_error "RST_STREAM zero stream"
104:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x3 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "1234");
105:   expect_h2_security_error "RST_STREAM wrong length"
106:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x3 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "123");
107:   expect_h2_security_error "GOAWAY nonzero stream"
108:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x7 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "12345678");
109:   expect_h2_security_error "GOAWAY too short"
110:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x7 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "1234567")
111: 
112: let test_h2_security_rejects_invalid_stream_frame_envelopes () =
113:   expect_h2_security_error "DATA stream 0"
114:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x0 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "x");
115:   expect_h2_security_error "HEADERS stream 0"
116:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x1 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:0 "");
117:   expect_h2_security_error "PRIORITY wrong length"
118:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x2 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1
119:        "\000\000\000\001");
120:   expect_h2_security_error "PRIORITY stream 0"
121:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x2 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0
122:        "\000\000\000\000\001");
123:   expect_h2_security_error "PUSH_PROMISE stream 0"
124:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x5 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:0
125:        "\000\000\000\002");
126:   expect_h2_security_error "PUSH_PROMISE too short"
127:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x5 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:1
128:        "\000\000\000");
129:   expect_h2_security_error "CONTINUATION stream 0"
130:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x9 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:0 "")
131: 
132: let test_h2_security_rejects_invalid_continuation_envelopes () =
133:   expect_h2_security_error "CONTINUATION without HEADERS"
134:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x9 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:1 "");
135:   expect_h2_security_error "CONTINUATION wrong stream"
136:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x1 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "a"
137:     ^ raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x9 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:3 "b");
138:   expect_h2_security_error "non-CONTINUATION during header block"
139:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x1 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "a"
140:     ^ raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x4 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:0 "")
141: 
142: let test_h2_security_accepts_valid_split_header_block () =
143:   expect_h2_security_ok "split HEADERS/CONTINUATION"
144:     (raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x1 ~flags:0 ~stream_id:1 "a"
145:     ^ raw_h2_frame ~frame_type:0x9 ~flags:0x4 ~stream_id:1 "b")
146: 
147: let test_h2_security_rejects_invalid_settings_payload_values () =
148:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH=2"
149:     (h2_settings_payload (h2_settings_pair 0x2 2));
150:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS_MAX_FRAME_SIZE too small"
151:     (h2_settings_payload (h2_settings_pair 0x5 1));
152:   expect_h2_security_error "SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE too large"
153:     (h2_settings_payload (h2_settings_pair 0x4 0x80000000));
154:   expect_h2_security_ok "unknown SETTINGS identifier"
155:     (h2_settings_payload (h2_settings_pair 0xbeef 0x80000000))
156: 
157: let test_h2_security_allows_graceful_repeated_goaway () =
158:   let security = Eta_http.H2.Security.create () in
159:   let first = Eta_http.H2.Frame.goaway_no_error ~last_stream_id:3 in
160:   let second = Eta_http.H2.Frame.goaway_no_error ~last_stream_id:1 in
161:   Alcotest.(check bool) "first GOAWAY accepted" true
162:     (Option.is_none (observe_with security first));
163:   match observe_with security second with
164:   | None -> ()
165:   | Some kind ->
166:       Alcotest.failf "valid repeated GOAWAY rejected: %s"
167:         (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
168: 
169: let test_h2_security_rejects_increasing_goaway_last_stream_id () =
170:   let security = Eta_http.H2.Security.create () in
171:   let first = Eta_http.H2.Frame.goaway_no_error ~last_stream_id:1 in
172:   let second = Eta_http.H2.Frame.goaway_no_error ~last_stream_id:3 in
173:   Alcotest.(check bool) "first GOAWAY accepted" true
174:     (Option.is_none (observe_with security first));
175:   match observe_with security second with
176:   | Some
177:       (Eta_http.Error.Connection_protocol_violation
178:         { kind = "goaway_last_stream_id_increase"; _ }) ->
179:       ()
180:   | Some kind ->
181:       Alcotest.failf "unexpected GOAWAY sequence error: %s"
182:         (Eta_http.Error.kind_name kind)
183:   | None -> Alcotest.fail "GOAWAY last_stream_id increase was accepted"
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/json.ml
````ocaml
  1: (** JSON report serialization. *)
  2: 
  3: open Types
  4: 
  5: let yojson_of_string s = `String s
  6: let yojson_of_int i = `Int i
  7: let yojson_of_float f = `Float f
  8: let yojson_of_int64 i = `Intlit (Int64.to_string i)
  9: let yojson_of_bool b = `Bool b
 10: let yojson_of_pair f g (a, b) = `List [ f a; g b ]
 11: let yojson_of_list f xs = `List (List.map f xs)
 12: let yojson_of_option f = function
 13:   | None -> `Null
 14:   | Some x -> f x
 15: 
 16: let yojson_of_protocol = function
 17:   | H1 -> `String "h1"
 18:   | H2 -> `String "h2"
 19: 
 20: let yojson_of_transport = function
 21:   | Plain -> `String "plain"
 22:   | TLS -> `String "tls"
 23: 
 24: let yojson_of_server_kind = function
 25:   | Nginx -> `String "nginx"
 26:   | Caddy -> `String "caddy"
 27:   | Eta -> `String "eta"
 28:   | Node -> `String "node"
 29:   | Go -> `String "go"
 30: 
 31: let yojson_of_scenario_status = function
 32:   | Pass -> `String "PASS"
 33:   | Divergent -> `String "DIVERGENT"
 34:   | Fail -> `String "FAIL"
 35:   | Skip reason -> `Assoc [ ("status", `String "SKIP"); ("reason", `String reason) ]
 36: 
 37: let yojson_of_normalized_result (r : normalized_result) =
 38:   `Assoc [
 39:     ("status", yojson_of_int r.status);
 40:     ("body_sha256", yojson_of_string r.body_sha256);
 41:     ("body_length", yojson_of_int r.body_length);
 42:     ("headers_normalized", yojson_of_list (yojson_of_pair yojson_of_string yojson_of_string) r.headers_normalized);
 43:     ("trailers_normalized", yojson_of_list (yojson_of_pair yojson_of_string yojson_of_string) r.trailers_normalized);
 44:   ]
 45: 
 46: let yojson_of_scenario_result (r : scenario_result) =
 47:   `Assoc [
 48:     ("name", yojson_of_string r.name);
 49:     ("server", yojson_of_server_kind r.server);
 50:     ("protocol", yojson_of_protocol r.protocol);
 51:     ("transport", yojson_of_transport r.transport);
 52:     ("status", yojson_of_scenario_status r.status);
 53:     ("eta_result", yojson_of_option yojson_of_normalized_result r.eta_result);
 54:     ("curl_result", yojson_of_option yojson_of_normalized_result r.curl_result);
 55:     ("eta_error", yojson_of_option yojson_of_string r.eta_error);
 56:     ("curl_error", yojson_of_option yojson_of_string r.curl_error);
 57:     ("duration_ms", yojson_of_float r.duration_ms);
 58:   ]
 59: 
 60: let yojson_of_bench_iteration (i : bench_iteration) =
 61:   `Assoc [
 62:     ("scenario", yojson_of_string i.scenario);
 63:     ("client", yojson_of_string i.client);
 64:     ("iteration", yojson_of_int i.iteration);
 65:     ("duration_ns", yojson_of_int64 i.duration_ns);
 66:     ("minor_words", yojson_of_float i.minor_words);
 67:     ("major_words", yojson_of_float i.major_words);
 68:     ("promoted_words", yojson_of_float i.promoted_words);
 69:     ("top_heap_words", yojson_of_int i.top_heap_words);
 70:     ("rss_kb", yojson_of_int i.rss_kb);
 71:   ]
 72: 
 73: let yojson_of_adversarial_result (r : adversarial_result) =
 74:   `Assoc [
 75:     ("name", yojson_of_string r.name);
 76:     ("passed", yojson_of_bool r.passed);
 77:     ("skipped", yojson_of_option yojson_of_string r.skipped);
 78:     ("deadline_respected", yojson_of_bool r.deadline_respected);
 79:     ("peak_rss_kb", yojson_of_int r.peak_rss_kb);
 80:     ("error_variant", yojson_of_option yojson_of_string r.error_variant);
 81:     ("eta_error", yojson_of_option yojson_of_string r.eta_error);
 82:     ("duration_ms", yojson_of_float r.duration_ms);
 83:     ("fd_baseline", yojson_of_int r.fd_baseline);
 84:     ("fd_after", yojson_of_int r.fd_after);
 85:     ("minor_words_during", yojson_of_float r.minor_words_during);
 86:     ("major_words_during", yojson_of_float r.major_words_during);
 87:   ]
 88: 
 89: let yojson_of_run_manifest (m : run_manifest) =
 90:   `Assoc [
 91:     ("run_id", yojson_of_string m.run_id);
 92:     ("git_sha", yojson_of_string m.git_sha);
 93:     ("ocaml_version", yojson_of_string m.ocaml_version);
 94:     ("nginx_version", yojson_of_string m.nginx_version);
 95:     ("caddy_version", yojson_of_string m.caddy_version);
 96:     ("curl_version", yojson_of_string m.curl_version);
 97:     ("nghttp2_version", yojson_of_string m.nghttp2_version);
 98:     ("eta_http_sha", yojson_of_string m.eta_http_sha);
 99:     ("host", yojson_of_string m.host);
100:     ("started_at", yojson_of_string m.started_at);
101:   ]
102: 
103: let write_json ~path json =
104:   let oc = open_out path in
105:   Fun.protect
106:     ~finally:(fun () -> close_out_noerr oc)
107:     (fun () -> Yojson.Safe.to_channel oc json)
108: 
109: let write_manifest ~path manifest =
110:   write_json ~path (yojson_of_run_manifest manifest)
111: 
112: let write_interop ~path results =
113:   write_json ~path (yojson_of_list yojson_of_scenario_result results)
114: 
115: let write_cve ~path results =
116:   write_json ~path (yojson_of_list yojson_of_adversarial_result results)
117: 
118: let write_bench ~path iterations =
119:   write_json ~path (yojson_of_list yojson_of_bench_iteration iterations)
````

## File: http-testsuite/lib/types.ml
````ocaml
 1: (** Core types shared across the test suite. *)
 2: 
 3: type protocol = H1 | H2
 4: type transport = Plain | TLS
 5: type server_kind = Nginx | Caddy | Eta | Node | Go
 6: 
 7: type server_config = {
 8:   kind : server_kind;
 9:   protocol : protocol;
10:   transport : transport;
11:   port : int;
12:   temp_dir : string;
13:   cert_dir : string option;
14: }
15: 
16: type scenario_status =
17:   | Pass
18:   | Divergent
19:   | Fail
20:   | Skip of string
21: 
22: type normalized_result = {
23:   status : int;
24:   body_sha256 : string;
25:   body_length : int;
26:   headers_normalized : (string * string) list;
27:   trailers_normalized : (string * string) list;
28: }
29: 
30: type scenario_result = {
31:   name : string;
32:   server : server_kind;
33:   protocol : protocol;
34:   transport : transport;
35:   status : scenario_status;
36:   eta_result : normalized_result option;
37:   curl_result : normalized_result option;
38:   eta_error : string option;
39:   curl_error : string option;
40:   duration_ms : float;
41: }
42: 
43: type bench_iteration = {
44:   scenario : string;
45:   client : string;
46:   iteration : int;
47:   duration_ns : int64;
48:   minor_words : float;
49:   major_words : float;
50:   promoted_words : float;
51:   top_heap_words : int;
52:   rss_kb : int;
53: }
54: 
55: type adversarial_result = {
56:   name : string;
57:   passed : bool;
58:   skipped : string option;
59:   deadline_respected : bool;
60:   peak_rss_kb : int;
61:   error_variant : string option;
62:   eta_error : string option;
63:   duration_ms : float;
64:   fd_baseline : int;
65:   fd_after : int;
66:   minor_words_during : float;
67:   major_words_during : float;
68: }
69: 
70: type run_manifest = {
71:   run_id : string;
72:   git_sha : string;
73:   ocaml_version : string;
74:   nginx_version : string;
75:   caddy_version : string;
76:   curl_version : string;
77:   nghttp2_version : string;
78:   eta_http_sha : string;
79:   host : string;
80:   started_at : string;
81: }
````

## File: http-testsuite/test/red_probes/tls_frag/run.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* TLS/H2 fragmentation red probes for eta_http / eta_http_eio.
  2:    These probes intentionally fragment TLS records and HTTP/2 frames to look
  3:    for hangs, crashes, or resource leaks in the Eta TLS and H2 paths. *)
  4: 
  5: open Eta_http_testsuite
  6: open Eio.Std
  7: 
  8: let backend_name () =
  9:   match Sys.getenv_opt "EIO_BACKEND" with
 10:   | Some "posix" -> "posix"
 11:   | Some other -> "forced_" ^ other
 12:   | None -> "default"
 13: 
 14: let status_to_string = function
 15:   | `Pass -> "PASS"
 16:   | `Fail -> "FAIL"
 17:   | `Hang -> "HANG"
 18:   | `Crash -> "CRASH"
 19:   | `Policy_gap -> "POLICY_GAP"
 20: 
 21: let emit_probe name status detail =
 22:   Printf.printf "probe %s_%s %s %s\n%!"
 23:     (backend_name ())
 24:     name
 25:     (status_to_string status)
 26:     detail
 27: 
 28: type 'a probe_result =
 29:   | Ok of 'a
 30:   | Hang
 31:   | Crash of string
 32: 
 33: let run_with_deadline ~clock ~deadline_sec f =
 34:   try
 35:     Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock deadline_sec (fun () -> Ok (f ()))
 36:   with
 37:   | Eio.Time.Timeout -> Hang
 38:   | exn -> Crash (Printexc.to_string exn)
 39: 
 40: let close_flow flow =
 41:   try Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `All with _ -> ()
 42: 
 43: let close_server server =
 44:   try Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate with _ -> ()
 45: 
 46: let start_https_server ~sw ~env ~protocol () =
 47:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
 48:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
 49:   let temp_dir =
 50:     Filename.concat (Filename.get_temp_dir_name ())
 51:       ("eta_tls_frag_" ^ backend_name ())
 52:   in
 53:   Util.mkdir_p temp_dir;
 54:   let cert_dir =
 55:     match Certs.prepare ~temp_dir with
 56:     | Ok d -> d
 57:     | Error e -> failwith e
 58:   in
 59:   let socket =
 60:     Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
 61:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
 62:   in
 63:   let port = Adversarial.tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
 64:   let tls_config = Eta_server.tls_config cert_dir protocol in
 65:   let server =
 66:     Eta_http_eio.Server.start_https_on_socket ~sw ~clock
 67:       ~config:Eta_server.config ~tls_config ~socket
 68:       (Eta_server.handler ~temp_dir)
 69:   in
 70:   (server, port, cert_dir)
 71: 
 72: let host_peer_name s =
 73:   match Domain_name.of_string s with
 74:   | Ok dn -> Domain_name.host_exn dn
 75:   | Error (`Msg e) -> failwith ("invalid peer name: " ^ e)
 76: 
 77: let client_tls_config ~cert_dir ~alpn_protocols =
 78:   Eta_http.Tls.Config.default_client
 79:     ~peer_name:(host_peer_name "localhost")
 80:     ~alpn_protocols
 81:     ~ca_file:(Certs.ca_path cert_dir)
 82:     ()
 83: 
 84: let connect_tls ~sw ~env ~port ~cert_dir ~alpn_protocols () =
 85:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
 86:   let tcp =
 87:     Eio.Net.connect ~sw net
 88:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
 89:   in
 90:   let flow =
 91:     (tcp :> [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t)
 92:   in
 93:   let config = client_tls_config ~cert_dir ~alpn_protocols in
 94:   Eta_http_eio.Tls.Eio.client_of_flow config flow
 95: 
 96: let write_byte_by_byte ?(delay_sec = 0.0) ~clock flow s =
 97:   for i = 0 to String.length s - 1 do
 98:     Eio.Flow.copy_string (String.make 1 s.[i]) flow;
 99:     if delay_sec > 0.0 then Eio.Time.sleep clock delay_sec
100:   done
101: 
102: let read_h1_status ~clock flow =
103:   Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock 2.0 (fun () ->
104:       let response = Adversarial.read_h1_response flow in
105:       match Adversarial.h1_status response with
106:       | Some status -> Printf.sprintf "status=%d" status
107:       | None -> "malformed_response")
108: 
109: let read_h2_response ~clock flow =
110:   Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock 2.0 (fun () ->
111:       let rec loop bytes =
112:         let len, ty, _flags, sid = Malicious_h2.read_frame_header flow in
113:         Malicious_h2.skip_frame_payload flow len;
114:         let bytes = bytes + 9 + len in
115:         match ty with
116:         | 0x01 -> Printf.sprintf "headers stream=%d bytes=%d" sid bytes
117:         | 0x03 -> Printf.sprintf "rst_stream stream=%d bytes=%d" sid bytes
118:         | 0x07 -> Printf.sprintf "goaway bytes=%d" bytes
119:         | _ -> loop bytes
120:       in
121:       loop 0)
122: 
123: let run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol ~alpn_protocols ~name ~deadline_sec
124:     client_fn =
125:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
126:   match
127:     run_with_deadline ~clock ~deadline_sec (fun () ->
128:         Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
129:         let server, port, cert_dir =
130:           start_https_server ~sw ~env ~protocol ()
131:         in
132:         Fun.protect
133:           ~finally:(fun () -> close_server server)
134:           (fun () ->
135:             let flow =
136:               connect_tls ~sw ~env ~port ~cert_dir ~alpn_protocols ()
137:             in
138:             Fun.protect
139:               ~finally:(fun () -> close_flow flow)
140:               (fun () -> client_fn ~clock flow)))
141:   with
142:   | Ok msg -> emit_probe name `Pass msg
143:   | Hang -> emit_probe name `Hang "deadline_exceeded"
144:   | Crash msg -> emit_probe name `Crash msg
145: 
146: (* Raw-TCP probes: speak plaintext to the TLS port. *)
147: let run_raw_tcp_probe ~env ~protocol ~name ~deadline_sec client_fn =
148:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
149:   let result =
150:     run_with_deadline ~clock ~deadline_sec (fun () ->
151:         Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
152:         let server, port, _cert_dir =
153:           start_https_server ~sw ~env ~protocol ()
154:         in
155:         let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
156:         let flow =
157:           Eio.Net.connect ~sw net
158:             (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, port))
159:         in
160:         Fun.protect
161:           ~finally:(fun () ->
162:             close_flow flow;
163:             close_server server)
164:           (fun () -> client_fn ~clock flow))
165:   in
166:   match result with
167:   | Ok msg -> emit_probe name `Pass msg
168:   | Hang -> emit_probe name `Hang "deadline_exceeded"
169:   | Crash msg -> emit_probe name `Crash msg
170: 
171: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
172:    Individual probes
173:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
174: 
175: let h1_byte_records ~env =
176:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H1 ~alpn_protocols:[ "http/1.1" ]
177:     ~name:"h1_byte_records" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
178:       let request =
179:         "GET /healthz HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"
180:       in
181:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow request;
182:       read_h1_status ~clock flow)
183: 
184: let h2_byte_frames ~env =
185:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
186:     ~name:"h2_byte_frames" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
187:       let request =
188:         Adversarial.h2_client_preface
189:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []
190:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []
191:         ^ Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()
192:       in
193:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow request;
194:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
195: 
196: let h2_tiny_writes ~env =
197:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
198:     ~name:"h2_tiny_writes" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
199:       (* Valid H2 POST with DATA, but every TLS write is one byte. *)
200:       let request =
201:         Adversarial.h2_client_preface
202:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []
203:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []
204:         ^ Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~end_stream:false
205:             ~stream_id:1 ()
206:         ^ Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:1 "hello"
207:         ^ Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:1 ""
208:       in
209:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow request;
210:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
211: 
212: let h2_slow_preface ~env =
213:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
214:     ~name:"h2_slow_preface" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
215:       (* Send the 24-byte preface one byte every 100ms, then finish quickly. *)
216:       write_byte_by_byte ~delay_sec:0.1 ~clock flow Adversarial.h2_client_preface;
217:       let rest =
218:         Malicious_h2.settings_frame []
219:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []
220:         ^ Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()
221:       in
222:       Eio.Flow.copy_string rest flow;
223:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
224: 
225: let h2_data_payload_byte ~env =
226:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
227:     ~name:"h2_data_payload_byte" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
228:       (* All H2 frame headers sent normally; only the DATA payload bytes are
229:          delivered as one byte per TLS record. *)
230:       Eio.Flow.copy_string Adversarial.h2_client_preface flow;
231:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
232:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []) flow;
233:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
234:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~end_stream:false
235:            ~stream_id:1 ())
236:         flow;
237:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
238:         (Adversarial.h2_frame_header ~length:5 ~ty:0x00 ~flags:0x00
239:            ~stream_id:1)
240:         flow;
241:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow "hello";
242:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
243:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:1 "")
244:         flow;
245:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
246: 
247: let h2_data_frame_byte ~env =
248:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
249:     ~name:"h2_data_frame_byte" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
250:       (* Preface/SETTINGS/HEADERS sent normally; the DATA frame (header+payload)
251:          is sent one byte per TLS record. *)
252:       Eio.Flow.copy_string Adversarial.h2_client_preface flow;
253:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
254:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []) flow;
255:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
256:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~end_stream:false
257:            ~stream_id:1 ())
258:         flow;
259:       let data_frame =
260:         Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:false ~stream_id:1 "hello"
261:       in
262:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow data_frame;
263:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
264:         (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:1 "")
265:         flow;
266:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
267: 
268: let h1_body_byte_records ~env =
269:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H1 ~alpn_protocols:[ "http/1.1" ]
270:     ~name:"h1_body_byte_records" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
271:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
272:         "POST /echo HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nContent-Length: 5\r\n\
273:          Connection: close\r\n\r\n"
274:         flow;
275:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow "hello";
276:       read_h1_status ~clock flow)
277: 
278: let h1_body_ignored_byte_records ~env =
279:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H1 ~alpn_protocols:[ "http/1.1" ]
280:     ~name:"h1_body_ignored_byte_records" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
281:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
282:         "POST /healthz HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nContent-Length: 5\r\n\
283:          Connection: close\r\n\r\n"
284:         flow;
285:       write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow "hello";
286:       read_h1_status ~clock flow)
287: 
288: let h2c_data_payload_byte ~env =
289:   (* Isolation probe: same fragmented DATA payload over cleartext H2C to show
290:      the hang is not specific to TLS framing. *)
291:   let result =
292:     Adversarial.run_eta_h2c_adversarial_client ~env
293:       ~name:"h2c_data_payload_byte"
294:       ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun flow ->
295:         Eio.Flow.copy_string
296:           (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~end_stream:false
297:              ~stream_id:1 ())
298:           flow;
299:         Eio.Flow.copy_string
300:           (Adversarial.h2_frame_header ~length:5 ~ty:0x00 ~flags:0x00
301:              ~stream_id:1)
302:           flow;
303:         let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
304:         write_byte_by_byte ~clock flow "hello";
305:         Eio.Flow.copy_string
306:           (Malicious_h2.data_frame ~end_stream:true ~stream_id:1 "")
307:           flow;
308:         Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send)
309:   in
310:   emit_probe "h2c_data_payload_byte"
311:     (if result.deadline_respected then `Pass else `Hang)
312:     (Option.value result.error_variant ~default:"ok")
313: 
314: let shutdown_during_handshake ~env =
315:   run_raw_tcp_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~name:"shutdown_during_handshake"
316:     ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
317:       (* Send plaintext HTTP bytes to the TLS port and half-close. *)
318:       Eio.Flow.copy_string "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\n" flow;
319:       Eio.Flow.shutdown flow `Send;
320:       (* Drain any alert/response bytes until EOF. *)
321:       let buf = Cstruct.create 4096 in
322:       (try
323:          while true do
324:            let n = Eio.Flow.single_read flow buf in
325:            if n = 0 then raise Exit
326:          done
327:        with End_of_file | Exit -> ());
328:       "connection_closed")
329: 
330: let shutdown_during_headers ~env =
331:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
332:     ~name:"shutdown_during_headers" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
333:       Eio.Flow.copy_string Adversarial.h2_client_preface flow;
334:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
335:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []) flow;
336:       (* Only the first five bytes of a HEADERS frame, then close TLS. *)
337:       let partial_headers =
338:         String.sub (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()) 0 5
339:       in
340:       Eio.Flow.copy_string partial_headers flow;
341:       close_flow flow;
342:       "connection_closed")
343: 
344: let shutdown_during_data ~env =
345:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
346:     ~name:"shutdown_during_data" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
347:       Eio.Flow.copy_string Adversarial.h2_client_preface flow;
348:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame []) flow;
349:       Eio.Flow.copy_string (Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []) flow;
350:       Eio.Flow.copy_string
351:         (Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~method_:"POST" ~end_stream:false
352:            ~stream_id:1 ())
353:         flow;
354:       (* DATA header declares 100 bytes but only send five, then close TLS. *)
355:       let partial_data =
356:         Adversarial.h2_frame_header ~length:100 ~ty:0x00 ~flags:0x00
357:           ~stream_id:1
358:         ^ "hello"
359:       in
360:       Eio.Flow.copy_string partial_data flow;
361:       close_flow flow;
362:       "connection_closed")
363: 
364: let shutdown_during_trailers ~env =
365:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H1 ~alpn_protocols:[ "http/1.1" ]
366:     ~name:"shutdown_during_trailers" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock:_ flow ->
367:       let request =
368:         "POST /echo HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\n"
369:         ^ "Transfer-Encoding: chunked\r\n\r\n"
370:         ^ "5\r\nhello\r\n0\r\nX-Trailer:"
371:       in
372:       Eio.Flow.copy_string request flow;
373:       close_flow flow;
374:       "connection_closed")
375: 
376: let alpn_h2_only ~env =
377:   run_tls_client_probe ~env ~protocol:H2 ~alpn_protocols:[ "h2" ]
378:     ~name:"alpn_h2_only" ~deadline_sec:5.0 (fun ~clock flow ->
379:       let request =
380:         Adversarial.h2_client_preface
381:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame []
382:         ^ Malicious_h2.settings_frame ~ack:true []
383:         ^ Adversarial.h2_request_headers ~stream_id:1 ()
384:       in
385:       Eio.Flow.copy_string request flow;
386:       read_h2_response ~clock flow)
387: 
388: (* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
389:    Runner
390:    --------------------------------------------------------------------------- *)
391: 
392: let () =
393:   Printf.printf "tls_frag backend=%s\n%!" (backend_name ());
394:   let selected =
395:     Sys.argv |> Array.to_list |> function
396:     | _ :: names -> names
397:     | [] -> []
398:   in
399:   let selected name = selected = [] || List.mem name selected in
400:   Eio_main.run @@ fun env ->
401:   let probes =
402:     [
403:       ("h1_byte_records", h1_byte_records);
404:       ("h1_body_byte_records", h1_body_byte_records);
405:       ("h1_body_ignored_byte_records", h1_body_ignored_byte_records);
406:       ("h2_byte_frames", h2_byte_frames);
407:       ("h2_data_payload_byte", h2_data_payload_byte);
408:       ("h2_data_frame_byte", h2_data_frame_byte);
409:       ("h2_tiny_writes", h2_tiny_writes);
410:       ("h2_slow_preface", h2_slow_preface);
411:       ("h2c_data_payload_byte", h2c_data_payload_byte);
412:       ("shutdown_during_handshake", shutdown_during_handshake);
413:       ("shutdown_during_headers", shutdown_during_headers);
414:       ("shutdown_during_data", shutdown_during_data);
415:       ("shutdown_during_trailers", shutdown_during_trailers);
416:       ("alpn_h2_only", alpn_h2_only);
417:     ]
418:   in
419:   List.iter (fun (name, probe) -> if selected name then probe ~env) probes;
420:   Printf.printf "tls_frag done\n%!"
````

## File: lib/http/h2/security.ml
````ocaml
  1: module Error = Error
  2: 
  3: type config = {
  4:   max_settings_per_connection : int;
  5:   max_goaway_per_connection : int;
  6:   max_rst_stream_per_connection : int;
  7:   max_ping_per_connection : int;
  8:   max_empty_data_frames_per_connection : int;
  9:   max_window_update_per_connection : int;
 10:   max_hpack_block_bytes : int;
 11:   max_continuation_accumulator_bytes : int;
 12:   max_response_headers_per_connection : int;
 13:   max_header_name_bytes : int;
 14:   max_header_value_bytes : int;
 15: }
 16: 
 17: let default_config =
 18:   {
 19:     max_settings_per_connection = 10;
 20:     max_goaway_per_connection = 8;
 21:     max_rst_stream_per_connection = 100;
 22:     max_ping_per_connection = 100;
 23:     max_empty_data_frames_per_connection = 100;
 24:     max_window_update_per_connection = 10_000;
 25:     max_hpack_block_bytes = 256 * 1024;
 26:     max_continuation_accumulator_bytes = 64 * 1024;
 27:     max_response_headers_per_connection = 32;
 28:     max_header_name_bytes = 8 * 1024;
 29:     max_header_value_bytes = 64 * 1024;
 30:   }
 31: 
 32: type t = {
 33:   config : config;
 34:   header : Bytes.t;
 35:   mutable header_len : int;
 36:   mutable payload_remaining : int;
 37:   mutable settings_seen : int;
 38:   mutable goaway_seen : int;
 39:   mutable last_goaway_stream_id : int option;
 40:   mutable rst_stream_seen : int;
 41:   mutable ping_seen : int;
 42:   mutable empty_data_seen : int;
 43:   mutable window_update_seen : int;
 44:   response_headers_seen_by_stream : (int, int) Hashtbl.t;
 45:   mutable header_block_bytes : int;
 46:   mutable header_block_frames : int;
 47:   mutable open_header_stream : int option;
 48:   mutable payload_observer : payload_observer;
 49: }
 50: 
 51: and payload_observer =
 52:   | Skip_payload
 53:   | Window_update_payload of {
 54:       stream_id : int;
 55:       payload : Bytes.t;
 56:       mutable payload_len : int;
 57:     }
 58:   | Settings_payload of {
 59:       payload : Bytes.t;
 60:       mutable payload_len : int;
 61:     }
 62:   | Goaway_payload of {
 63:       payload : Bytes.t;
 64:       mutable payload_len : int;
 65:     }
 66: 
 67: let create ?(config = default_config) () =
 68:   {
 69:     config;
 70:     header = Bytes.create Frame.header_size;
 71:     header_len = 0;
 72:     payload_remaining = 0;
 73:     settings_seen = 0;
 74:     goaway_seen = 0;
 75:     last_goaway_stream_id = None;
 76:     rst_stream_seen = 0;
 77:     ping_seen = 0;
 78:     empty_data_seen = 0;
 79:     window_update_seen = 0;
 80:     response_headers_seen_by_stream = Hashtbl.create 32;
 81:     header_block_bytes = 0;
 82:     header_block_frames = 0;
 83:     open_header_stream = None;
 84:     payload_observer = Skip_payload;
 85:   }
 86: 
 87: let has_open_header_block t = Option.is_some t.open_header_stream
 88: 
 89: let end_headers flags = flags land 0x4 <> 0
 90: 
 91: let reset_header_block t =
 92:   t.header_block_bytes <- 0;
 93:   t.header_block_frames <- 0;
 94:   t.open_header_stream <- None
 95: 
 96: let account_settings t =
 97:   t.settings_seen <- t.settings_seen + 1;
 98:   if t.settings_seen > t.config.max_settings_per_connection then
 99:     Some
100:       (Error.Settings_count_exceeded
101:          {
102:            observed_count = t.settings_seen;
103:            limit = t.config.max_settings_per_connection;
104:          })
105:   else None
106: 
107: let account_goaway t =
108:   t.goaway_seen <- t.goaway_seen + 1;
109:   if t.goaway_seen > t.config.max_goaway_per_connection then
110:     Some (Error.Connection_closed { during = Error.Http_response })
111:   else None
112: 
113: let account_rst_stream t =
114:   t.rst_stream_seen <- t.rst_stream_seen + 1;
115:   if t.rst_stream_seen > t.config.max_rst_stream_per_connection then
116:     Some
117:       (Error.Rst_count_exceeded
118:          {
119:            observed_count = t.rst_stream_seen;
120:            limit = t.config.max_rst_stream_per_connection;
121:          })
122:   else None
123: 
124: let account_ping t =
125:   t.ping_seen <- t.ping_seen + 1;
126:   if t.ping_seen > t.config.max_ping_per_connection then
127:     Some
128:       (Error.Ping_count_exceeded
129:          {
130:            observed_count = t.ping_seen;
131:            limit = t.config.max_ping_per_connection;
132:          })
133:   else None
134: 
135: let account_empty_data t =
136:   t.empty_data_seen <- t.empty_data_seen + 1;
137:   if t.empty_data_seen > t.config.max_empty_data_frames_per_connection then
138:     Some
139:       (Error.Empty_data_frame_count_exceeded
140:          {
141:            observed_count = t.empty_data_seen;
142:            limit = t.config.max_empty_data_frames_per_connection;
143:          })
144:   else None
145: 
146: let account_window_update t =
147:   t.window_update_seen <- t.window_update_seen + 1;
148:   if t.window_update_seen > t.config.max_window_update_per_connection then
149:     Some
150:       (Error.Window_update_count_exceeded
151:          {
152:            observed_count = t.window_update_seen;
153:            limit = t.config.max_window_update_per_connection;
154:          })
155:   else None
156: 
157: let account_response_headers t stream_id =
158:   let seen =
159:     Option.value
160:       (Hashtbl.find_opt t.response_headers_seen_by_stream stream_id)
161:       ~default:0
162:     + 1
163:   in
164:   Hashtbl.replace t.response_headers_seen_by_stream stream_id seen;
165:   if seen > t.config.max_response_headers_per_connection then
166:     Some
167:       (Error.Response_header_count_exceeded
168:          {
169:            observed_count = seen;
170:            limit = t.config.max_response_headers_per_connection;
171:          })
172:   else None
173: 
174: let saturating_add left right =
175:   if left > max_int - right then max_int else left + right
176: 
177: let continuation_flood t =
178:   Some
179:     (Error.Continuation_flood
180:        {
181:          accumulated_bytes = t.header_block_bytes;
182:          limit_bytes = t.config.max_continuation_accumulator_bytes;
183:          frames = t.header_block_frames;
184:        })
185: 
186: let account_header_bytes t ~frame_type ~flags ~length ~stream_id =
187:   match frame_type with
188:   | 0x1 | 0x5 ->
189:       t.open_header_stream <-
190:         (if end_headers flags then None else Some stream_id);
191:       t.header_block_bytes <- length;
192:       t.header_block_frames <- 1;
193:       (match
194:          if frame_type = 0x1 then account_response_headers t stream_id else None
195:        with
196:       | Some error -> Some error
197:       | None when length > t.config.max_hpack_block_bytes ->
198:         Some
199:           (Error.Hpack_decode_overflow
200:              {
201:                decoded_bytes = length;
202:                limit_bytes = t.config.max_hpack_block_bytes;
203:              })
204:       | None
205:         when (not (end_headers flags))
206:              && length > t.config.max_continuation_accumulator_bytes ->
207:         continuation_flood t
208:       | None when end_headers flags -> (
209:         reset_header_block t;
210:         None)
211:       | None -> None)
212:   | 0x9 ->
213:       t.header_block_bytes <- saturating_add t.header_block_bytes length;
214:       t.header_block_frames <- t.header_block_frames + 1;
215:       if t.header_block_bytes > t.config.max_continuation_accumulator_bytes then
216:         continuation_flood t
217:       else if end_headers flags then (
218:         reset_header_block t;
219:         None)
220:       else None
221:   | _ -> None
222: 
223: let connection_protocol_violation ~kind ~message =
224:   Error.Connection_protocol_violation { kind; message }
225: 
226: let protocol_violationf ~kind format =
227:   Printf.ksprintf
228:     (fun message -> Some (connection_protocol_violation ~kind ~message))
229:     format
230: 
231: let byte_at bytes index = Char.code (Bytes.unsafe_get bytes index)
232: 
233: let window_update_increment payload =
234:   ((byte_at payload 0 land 0x7f) lsl 24)
235:   lor (byte_at payload 1 lsl 16)
236:   lor (byte_at payload 2 lsl 8)
237:   lor (byte_at payload 3)
238: 
239: let validate_window_update_payload ~stream_id payload =
240:   let increment = window_update_increment payload in
241:   if increment = 0 then
242:     Some
243:       (connection_protocol_violation ~kind:"window_update_increment_zero"
244:          ~message:
245:            (Printf.sprintf
246:               "WINDOW_UPDATE stream_id=%d has zero flow-control increment"
247:               stream_id))
248:   else None
249: 
250: let uint16_at payload off =
251:   (byte_at payload off lsl 8) lor (byte_at payload (off + 1))
252: 
253: let uint32_at payload off =
254:   (byte_at payload off lsl 24)
255:   lor (byte_at payload (off + 1) lsl 16)
256:   lor (byte_at payload (off + 2) lsl 8)
257:   lor (byte_at payload (off + 3))
258: 
259: let validate_settings_payload payload =
260:   let id = uint16_at payload 0 in
261:   let value = uint32_at payload 2 in
262:   match id with
263:   | 0x2 when value <> 0 && value <> 1 ->
264:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_enable_push"
265:         "SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH value=%d, expected 0 or 1" value
266:   | 0x4 when value > 0x7fff_ffff ->
267:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_initial_window_size"
268:         "SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE value=%d exceeds 2^31-1" value
269:   | 0x5 when value < 16_384 || value > 16_777_215 ->
270:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_max_frame_size"
271:         "SETTINGS_MAX_FRAME_SIZE value=%d outside 16384..16777215" value
272:   | _ -> None
273: 
274: let goaway_last_stream_id payload = uint32_at payload 0 land 0x7fff_ffff
275: 
276: let validate_goaway_payload t payload =
277:   let last_stream_id = goaway_last_stream_id payload in
278:   match t.last_goaway_stream_id with
279:   | Some previous when last_stream_id > previous ->
280:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"goaway_last_stream_id_increase"
281:         "GOAWAY last_stream_id increased from %d to %d" previous last_stream_id
282:   | _ ->
283:       t.last_goaway_stream_id <- Some last_stream_id;
284:       None
285: 
286: let validate_settings_frame ~flags ~length ~stream_id =
287:   if stream_id <> 0 then
288:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_stream_id"
289:       "SETTINGS frame has stream_id=%d, expected 0" stream_id
290:   else if flags land 0x1 <> 0 && length <> 0 then
291:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_ack_length"
292:       "SETTINGS ACK payload length=%d, expected 0" length
293:   else if flags land 0x1 = 0 && length mod 6 <> 0 then
294:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"settings_length"
295:       "SETTINGS payload length=%d is not a multiple of 6" length
296:   else None
297: 
298: let validate_ping_frame ~length ~stream_id =
299:   if stream_id <> 0 || length <> 8 then
300:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"ping_envelope"
301:       "PING frame stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected stream_id=0 and \
302:        length=8"
303:       stream_id length
304:   else None
305: 
306: let validate_rst_stream_frame ~length ~stream_id =
307:   if stream_id = 0 || length <> 4 then
308:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"rst_stream_envelope"
309:       "RST_STREAM frame stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected nonzero \
310:        stream_id and length=4"
311:       stream_id length
312:   else None
313: 
314: let validate_goaway_frame ~length ~stream_id =
315:   if stream_id <> 0 || length < 8 then
316:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"goaway_envelope"
317:       "GOAWAY frame stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected stream_id=0 and \
318:        length>=8"
319:       stream_id length
320:   else None
321: 
322: let validate_data_frame ~stream_id =
323:   if stream_id = 0 then
324:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"data_stream_id"
325:       "DATA frame has stream_id=0, expected nonzero stream_id"
326:   else None
327: 
328: let validate_headers_frame ~stream_id =
329:   if stream_id = 0 then
330:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"headers_stream_id"
331:       "HEADERS frame has stream_id=0, expected nonzero stream_id"
332:   else None
333: 
334: let validate_priority_frame ~length ~stream_id =
335:   if stream_id = 0 || length <> 5 then
336:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"priority_envelope"
337:       "PRIORITY frame stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected nonzero \
338:        stream_id and length=5"
339:       stream_id length
340:   else None
341: 
342: let validate_push_promise_frame ~length ~stream_id =
343:   if stream_id = 0 || length < 4 then
344:     protocol_violationf ~kind:"push_promise_envelope"
345:       "PUSH_PROMISE frame stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected nonzero \
346:        stream_id and length>=4"
347:       stream_id length
348:   else None
349: 
350: let validate_continuation_frame t ~stream_id =
351:   match t.open_header_stream with
352:   | None ->
353:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"continuation_without_headers"
354:         "CONTINUATION frame stream_id=%d has no open header block" stream_id
355:   | Some expected when stream_id <> expected ->
356:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"continuation_stream_mismatch"
357:         "CONTINUATION frame stream_id=%d, expected stream_id=%d" stream_id
358:         expected
359:   | Some _ -> None
360: 
361: let validate_header_block_order t ~frame_type ~stream_id =
362:   match t.open_header_stream with
363:   | Some expected when frame_type <> 0x9 ->
364:       protocol_violationf ~kind:"continuation_expected"
365:         "frame type=0x%02x stream_id=%d arrived while header block for \
366:          stream_id=%d is open"
367:         frame_type stream_id expected
368:   | _ -> None
369: 
370: let observe_payload_byte t byte =
371:   match t.payload_observer with
372:   | Skip_payload -> None
373:   | Window_update_payload state ->
374:       Bytes.set state.payload state.payload_len byte;
375:       state.payload_len <- state.payload_len + 1;
376:       if state.payload_len = Bytes.length state.payload then (
377:         t.payload_observer <- Skip_payload;
378:         validate_window_update_payload ~stream_id:state.stream_id state.payload)
379:       else None
380:   | Settings_payload state ->
381:       Bytes.set state.payload state.payload_len byte;
382:       state.payload_len <- state.payload_len + 1;
383:       if state.payload_len = Bytes.length state.payload then
384:         match validate_settings_payload state.payload with
385:         | Some error -> Some error
386:         | None ->
387:             state.payload_len <- 0;
388:             None
389:       else None
390:   | Goaway_payload state ->
391:       Bytes.set state.payload state.payload_len byte;
392:       state.payload_len <- state.payload_len + 1;
393:       if state.payload_len = Bytes.length state.payload then (
394:         t.payload_observer <- Skip_payload;
395:         validate_goaway_payload t state.payload)
396:       else None
397: 
398: let observe_payload t bs ~off ~len =
399:   match t.payload_observer with
400:   | Skip_payload -> None
401:   | Window_update_payload _ | Settings_payload _ | Goaway_payload _ ->
402:       let rec loop index =
403:         if index = len then None
404:         else
405:           match observe_payload_byte t (Bigstringaf.get bs (off + index)) with
406:           | Some error -> Some error
407:           | None -> loop (index + 1)
408:       in
409:       loop 0
410: 
411: let complete_stream t stream_id =
412:   Hashtbl.remove t.response_headers_seen_by_stream stream_id
413: 
414: let start_frame t =
415:   let open Frame in
416:   let { length; frame_type; flags; stream_id } =
417:     parse_header_bytes t.header ~off:0
418:   in
419:   t.header_len <- 0;
420:   t.payload_remaining <- length;
421:   t.payload_observer <- Skip_payload;
422:   match validate_header_block_order t ~frame_type ~stream_id with
423:   | Some error -> Some error
424:   | None -> (
425:       match frame_type with
426:       | 0x0 -> (
427:           match validate_data_frame ~stream_id with
428:           | Some error -> Some error
429:           | None when length = 0 -> account_empty_data t
430:           | None -> None)
431:       | 0x1 -> (
432:           match validate_headers_frame ~stream_id with
433:           | Some error -> Some error
434:           | None ->
435:               account_header_bytes t ~frame_type ~flags ~length ~stream_id)
436:       | 0x2 -> validate_priority_frame ~length ~stream_id
437:       | 0x3 -> (
438:           match validate_rst_stream_frame ~length ~stream_id with
439:           | Some error -> Some error
440:           | None -> account_rst_stream t)
441:       | 0x4 -> (
442:           match validate_settings_frame ~flags ~length ~stream_id with
443:           | Some error -> Some error
444:           | None -> (
445:               match account_settings t with
446:               | Some error -> Some error
447:               | None ->
448:                   if length > 0 && flags land 0x1 = 0 then
449:                     t.payload_observer <-
450:                       Settings_payload
451:                         { payload = Bytes.create 6; payload_len = 0 };
452:                   None))
453:       | 0x5 -> (
454:           match validate_push_promise_frame ~length ~stream_id with
455:           | Some error -> Some error
456:           | None ->
457:               account_header_bytes t ~frame_type ~flags ~length ~stream_id)
458:       | 0x6 -> (
459:           match validate_ping_frame ~length ~stream_id with
460:           | Some error -> Some error
461:           | None -> account_ping t)
462:       | 0x7 -> (
463:           match validate_goaway_frame ~length ~stream_id with
464:           | Some error -> Some error
465:           | None -> (
466:               match account_goaway t with
467:               | Some error -> Some error
468:               | None ->
469:                   t.payload_observer <-
470:                     Goaway_payload { payload = Bytes.create 4; payload_len = 0 };
471:                   None))
472:       | 0x8 -> (
473:           if length <> 4 then
474:             Some
475:               (connection_protocol_violation ~kind:"window_update_length"
476:                  ~message:
477:                    (Printf.sprintf
478:                       "WINDOW_UPDATE stream_id=%d payload length=%d, expected 4"
479:                       stream_id length))
480:           else
481:             match account_window_update t with
482:             | Some error -> Some error
483:             | None ->
484:                 t.payload_observer <-
485:                   Window_update_payload
486:                     { stream_id; payload = Bytes.create 4; payload_len = 0 };
487:                 None)
488:       | 0x9 -> (
489:           match validate_continuation_frame t ~stream_id with
490:           | Some error -> Some error
491:           | None ->
492:               account_header_bytes t ~frame_type ~flags ~length ~stream_id)
493:       | _ -> None)
494: 
495: let observe_byte t byte =
496:   if t.payload_remaining > 0 then (
497:     t.payload_remaining <- t.payload_remaining - 1;
498:     observe_payload_byte t byte)
499:   else (
500:     Bytes.set t.header t.header_len byte;
501:     t.header_len <- t.header_len + 1;
502:     if t.header_len = Frame.header_size then start_frame t else None)
503: 
504: let observe t bs ~off ~len =
505:   let stop = off + len in
506:   let rec loop i =
507:     if i >= stop then None
508:     else if t.payload_remaining > 0 then (
509:       let skipped = min t.payload_remaining (stop - i) in
510:       t.payload_remaining <- t.payload_remaining - skipped;
511:       match observe_payload t bs ~off:i ~len:skipped with
512:       | Some error -> Some error
513:       | None -> loop (i + skipped))
514:     else
515:       let needed = Frame.header_size - t.header_len in
516:       let take = min needed (stop - i) in
517:       for j = 0 to take - 1 do
518:         Bytes.set t.header (t.header_len + j) (Bigstringaf.get bs (i + j))
519:       done;
520:       t.header_len <- t.header_len + take;
521:       if t.header_len = Frame.header_size then
522:         match start_frame t with
523:         | Some error -> Some error
524:         | None -> loop (i + take)
525:       else loop (i + take)
526:   in
527:   loop off
528: 
529: let has_nul value =
530:   String.exists (Char.equal '\000') value
531: 
532: let has_uppercase value =
533:   String.exists
534:     (function
535:       | 'A' .. 'Z' -> true
536:       | _ -> false)
537:     value
538: 
539: let header_invalid reason = Error.Header_invalid { reason }
540: 
541: let validate_header t (name, value) =
542:   match Header.validate_header (name, value) with
543:   | Some error -> Some error
544:   | None when String.length name > t.config.max_header_name_bytes ->
545:     Some (header_invalid "header name exceeds 8192 bytes")
546:   | None when String.length value > t.config.max_header_value_bytes ->
547:     Some (header_invalid "header value exceeds 65536 bytes")
548:   | None when has_nul name || has_nul value ->
549:       Some (header_invalid "header contains NUL")
550:   | None when has_uppercase name ->
551:     Some (header_invalid "uppercase h2 header name")
552:   | None -> None
553: 
554: let rec validate_headers_with t = function
555:   | [] -> None
556:   | header :: rest -> (
557:       match validate_header t header with
558:       | Some error -> Some error
559:       | None -> validate_headers_with t rest)
560: 
561: let validate_headers headers = validate_headers_with (create ()) headers
````

## File: lib/http/h2/security.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Byte-level HTTP/2 security envelope checks.
 2: 
 3:     This scanner observes raw HTTP/2 frame bytes before they are handed to
 4:     [ocaml-h2]. It owns eta-http's cheap frame-envelope policy: SETTINGS is a
 5:     connection-lifetime counter, and GOAWAY is counted while allowing valid
 6:     graceful shutdown sequences with non-increasing [last_stream_id] values.
 7:     Response HEADERS are counted per stream: long-lived multiplexed connections
 8:     may legitimately see hundreds of normal responses, while repeated HEADERS
 9:     transitions on the same stream remain a churn signal. Empty DATA and
10:     WINDOW_UPDATE frames are counted as cheap DoS envelopes.
11:     Header-block/CONTINUATION byte caps protect incomplete or oversized HPACK
12:     envelopes; HPACK decoding remains owned by [ocaml-h2]. *)
13: 
14: type config = {
15:   max_settings_per_connection : int;
16:   max_goaway_per_connection : int;
17:   max_rst_stream_per_connection : int;
18:   max_ping_per_connection : int;
19:   max_empty_data_frames_per_connection : int;
20:   max_window_update_per_connection : int;
21:   max_hpack_block_bytes : int;
22:   max_continuation_accumulator_bytes : int;
23:   max_response_headers_per_connection : int;
24:   (** Per-stream response HEADERS limit. The field name is retained for API
25:       compatibility; it no longer counts normal responses across the whole
26:       connection lifetime. *)
27:   max_header_name_bytes : int;
28:   max_header_value_bytes : int;
29: }
30: 
31: val default_config : config
32: 
33: type t
34: 
35: val create : ?config:config -> unit -> t
36: 
37: val complete_stream : t -> int -> unit
38: (** Forget per-stream response-header accounting for a stream that has
39:     completed, reset, or otherwise been released. *)
40: 
41: val has_open_header_block : t -> bool
42: (** [true] when the scanner has observed HEADERS, PUSH_PROMISE, or
43:     CONTINUATION without END_HEADERS. EOF in this state is an incomplete HTTP/2
44:     header block. *)
45: 
46: val observe :
47:   t ->
48:   Bigstringaf.t ->
49:   off:int ->
50:   len:int ->
51:   Error.kind option
52: 
53: val validate_headers : (string * string) list -> Error.kind option
````

## File: lib/http/tls/config.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: let policy_version = (`TLS_1_2, `TLS_1_3)
 4: 
 5: let policy_ciphers =
 6:   [
 7:     "ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256";
 8:     "ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384";
 9:     "ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305";
10:     "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256";
11:     "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384";
12:     "ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305";
13:   ]
14: 
15: let policy_tls13_ciphers =
16:   [
17:     "TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256";
18:     "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384";
19:     "TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256";
20:   ]
21: 
22: let default_alpn = [ "h2"; "http/1.1" ]
23: 
24: type t = {
25:   peer_name : [ `host ] Domain_name.t option;
26:   ip : Ipaddr.t option;
27:   alpn_protocols : string list;
28:   ca_file : string option;
29: }
30: 
31: type server_certificate = {
32:   server_name : string;
33:   certificate_chain_file : string;
34:   private_key_file : string;
35: }
36: 
37: type server = {
38:   certificate_chain_file : string;
39:   private_key_file : string;
40:   server_certificates : server_certificate list;
41:   require_sni_match : bool;
42:   server_alpn_protocols : string list;
43: }
44: 
45: let default_client ?peer_name ?ip ?(alpn_protocols = default_alpn) ?ca_file () =
46:   { peer_name; ip; alpn_protocols; ca_file }
47: 
48: let server_certificate ~server_name ~certificate_chain_file ~private_key_file =
49:   { server_name; certificate_chain_file; private_key_file }
50: 
51: let default_server ?(alpn_protocols = default_alpn) ?(certificates = [])
52:     ?(require_sni_match = false) ~certificate_chain_file ~private_key_file () =
53:   {
54:     certificate_chain_file;
55:     private_key_file;
56:     server_certificates = certificates;
57:     require_sni_match;
58:     server_alpn_protocols = alpn_protocols;
59:   }
60: 
61: let peer_name t = t.peer_name
62: let ip t = t.ip
63: let alpn_protocols t = t.alpn_protocols
64: let ca_file t = t.ca_file
65: let server_certificate_name (t : server_certificate) = t.server_name
66: let server_certificate_chain_file (t : server_certificate) =
67:   t.certificate_chain_file
68: 
69: let server_certificate_private_key_file (t : server_certificate) =
70:   t.private_key_file
71: let certificate_chain_file t = t.certificate_chain_file
72: let private_key_file t = t.private_key_file
73: let server_certificates t = t.server_certificates
74: let require_sni_match t = t.require_sni_match
75: let server_alpn_protocols t = t.server_alpn_protocols
````

## File: lib/http/tls/config.mli
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: (** ADR 0002 TLS client configuration chokepoint. *)
 4: 
 5: val policy_version : [ `TLS_1_2 ] * [ `TLS_1_3 ]
 6: (** The v1 policy offers TLS 1.2 as the minimum and TLS 1.3 as the maximum on
 7:     the OpenSSL substrate. *)
 8: 
 9: val policy_ciphers : string list
10: (** OpenSSL cipher names for the allowed TLS 1.2 ECDHE RSA/ECDSA AEAD
11:     ciphers. *)
12: 
13: val policy_tls13_ciphers : string list
14: (** OpenSSL cipher names for the allowed TLS 1.3 AEAD ciphers. *)
15: 
16: val default_alpn : string list
17: (** Client ALPN offer order: HTTP/2 first, HTTP/1.1 fallback. *)
18: 
19: type t
20: (** Opaque client TLS configuration. *)
21: 
22: type server
23: (** Opaque server TLS configuration. *)
24: 
25: type server_certificate
26: (** A certificate chain and private key selected by SNI server name. *)
27: 
28: val default_client :
29:   ?peer_name:[ `host ] Domain_name.t ->
30:   ?ip:Ipaddr.t ->
31:   ?alpn_protocols:string list ->
32:   ?ca_file:string ->
33:   unit ->
34:   t
35: (** Build the only supported eta-http client TLS config.
36: 
37:     [ca_file] is an optional PEM file added to the trust store on top of
38:     the system roots. The API intentionally exposes no [~version] or
39:     [~ciphers] override. *)
40: 
41: val peer_name : t -> [ `host ] Domain_name.t option
42: val ip : t -> Ipaddr.t option
43: val alpn_protocols : t -> string list
44: val ca_file : t -> string option
45: 
46: val default_server :
47:   ?alpn_protocols:string list ->
48:   ?certificates:server_certificate list ->
49:   ?require_sni_match:bool ->
50:   certificate_chain_file:string ->
51:   private_key_file:string ->
52:   unit ->
53:   server
54: (** Build the supported eta-http server TLS config. The certificate chain
55:     and private key must be PEM files accepted by OpenSSL. [certificates] are
56:     selected by TLS SNI. [require_sni_match] aborts handshakes that omit SNI or
57:     send a name absent from [certificates]; leave it [false] when the default
58:     certificate should act as fallback. *)
59: 
60: val server_certificate :
61:   server_name:string ->
62:   certificate_chain_file:string ->
63:   private_key_file:string ->
64:   server_certificate
65: (** Build an SNI certificate entry. [server_name] is matched
66:     case-insensitively against the client's TLS SNI hostname. *)
67: 
68: val server_certificate_name : server_certificate -> string
69: val server_certificate_chain_file : server_certificate -> string
70: val server_certificate_private_key_file : server_certificate -> string
71: val certificate_chain_file : server -> string
72: val private_key_file : server -> string
73: val server_certificates : server -> server_certificate list
74: val require_sni_match : server -> bool
75: val server_alpn_protocols : server -> string list
````

## File: lib/http/server_config.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type unread_body_policy =
  4:   | Reset
  5:   | Drain_up_to of int
  6: 
  7: type limits = {
  8:   max_request_line_bytes : int;
  9:   max_request_header_bytes : int;
 10:   max_request_headers : int;
 11:   max_request_body_bytes : int option;
 12:   max_response_header_bytes : int;
 13:   max_response_headers : int;
 14:   max_trailer_bytes : int;
 15:   max_trailers : int;
 16: }
 17: 
 18: type timeouts = {
 19:   request_header_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 20:   request_body_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 21:   response_write_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 22:   response_body_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 23:   idle_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 24:   handler_timeout : Eta.Duration.t option;
 25: }
 26: 
 27: type t = {
 28:   limits : limits;
 29:   timeouts : timeouts;
 30:   unread_body_policy : unread_body_policy;
 31:   enable_otel : bool;
 32:   emit_url_full : bool;
 33: }
 34: 
 35: let default_limits =
 36:   {
 37:     max_request_line_bytes = 8 * 1024;
 38:     max_request_header_bytes = 32 * 1024;
 39:     max_request_headers = 256;
 40:     max_request_body_bytes = Some Stream.default_max_bytes;
 41:     max_response_header_bytes = 32 * 1024;
 42:     max_response_headers = 256;
 43:     max_trailer_bytes = 8 * 1024;
 44:     max_trailers = 64;
 45:   }
 46: 
 47: let default_timeouts =
 48:   {
 49:     request_header_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 30);
 50:     request_body_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 30);
 51:     response_write_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 30);
 52:     response_body_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 30);
 53:     idle_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 60);
 54:     handler_timeout = Some (Eta.Duration.seconds 30);
 55:   }
 56: 
 57: let default =
 58:   {
 59:     limits = default_limits;
 60:     timeouts = default_timeouts;
 61:     unread_body_policy = Reset;
 62:     enable_otel = true;
 63:     emit_url_full = false;
 64:   }
 65: 
 66: let require_positive field value =
 67:   if value <= 0 then
 68:     invalid_arg ("Eta_http.Server.Config." ^ field ^ " must be > 0")
 69: 
 70: let require_non_negative field value =
 71:   if value < 0 then
 72:     invalid_arg ("Eta_http.Server.Config." ^ field ^ " must be >= 0")
 73: 
 74: let require_positive_duration field value =
 75:   if Eta.Duration.is_zero value then
 76:     invalid_arg ("Eta_http.Server.Config." ^ field ^ " must be > 0")
 77: 
 78: let validate_limits limits =
 79:   require_positive "max_request_line_bytes" limits.max_request_line_bytes;
 80:   require_positive "max_request_header_bytes" limits.max_request_header_bytes;
 81:   require_positive "max_request_headers" limits.max_request_headers;
 82:   Option.iter
 83:     (require_non_negative "max_request_body_bytes")
 84:     limits.max_request_body_bytes;
 85:   require_positive "max_response_header_bytes" limits.max_response_header_bytes;
 86:   require_positive "max_response_headers" limits.max_response_headers;
 87:   require_positive "max_trailer_bytes" limits.max_trailer_bytes;
 88:   require_positive "max_trailers" limits.max_trailers
 89: 
 90: let validate_unread_body_policy = function
 91:   | Reset -> ()
 92:   | Drain_up_to limit -> require_non_negative "Drain_up_to" limit
 93: 
 94: let validate_timeouts timeouts =
 95:   Option.iter
 96:     (require_positive_duration "request_header_timeout")
 97:     timeouts.request_header_timeout;
 98:   Option.iter
 99:     (require_positive_duration "request_body_timeout")
100:     timeouts.request_body_timeout;
101:   Option.iter
102:     (require_positive_duration "response_write_timeout")
103:     timeouts.response_write_timeout;
104:   Option.iter
105:     (require_positive_duration "response_body_timeout")
106:     timeouts.response_body_timeout;
107:   Option.iter (require_positive_duration "idle_timeout")
108:     timeouts.idle_timeout;
109:   Option.iter (require_positive_duration "handler_timeout")
110:     timeouts.handler_timeout
111: 
112: let validate t =
113:   validate_limits t.limits;
114:   validate_unread_body_policy t.unread_body_policy;
115:   validate_timeouts t.timeouts
````
