## File: lib/http/server.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Backend-neutral HTTP server surface. *)
 2: 
 3: module Error = Server_error
 4: module Body = Server_body
 5: module Config = Server_config
 6: module Request = Server_request
 7: module Validation = Server_request_validation
 8: module Response = Server_response
 9: 
10: type handler = Request.t -> (Response.t, Error.t) Eta.Effect.t
11: 
12: module Handler : sig
13:   val map_error :
14:     ('err -> Error.t) ->
15:     (Request.t -> (Response.t, 'err) Eta.Effect.t) ->
16:     handler
17: 
18:   val with_default_error_response :
19:     ?renderer:(Error.t -> Response.t) ->
20:     handler ->
21:     handler
22: 
23:   val default_error_response : Error.t -> Response.t
24:   val route_not_found : handler
25: end
````

## File: lib/http_eio/tls/tls_eio.mli
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: (** Eio adapter for the OpenSSL TLS backend. *)
 4: 
 5: type config = Config.t
 6: (** Client configuration. *)
 7: 
 8: type server_config = Config.server
 9: (** Server configuration. *)
10: 
11: type server_context
12: (** Compiled server-side TLS context. It owns the OpenSSL [SSL_CTX] built from
13:     certificate files and can create per-connection TLS flows without reloading
14:     PEM material for every accepted connection. *)
15: 
16: type epoch = {
17:   alpn_protocol : string option;
18:   sni : string option;
19:   peer_certificate_verified : bool;
20: }
21: (** Post-handshake metadata. *)
22: 
23: type flow =
24:   [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty | `Eta_tls ] Eio.Resource.t
25: (** An [Eio.Flow.two_way] backed by OpenSSL over an underlying flow. *)
26: 
27: module type EIO_FLOW = Eta_eio.Host.FLOW
28: (** Minimal host module shape needed by TLS flow hooks. *)
29: 
30: val client_of_flow :
31:   ?host_eio:Eta_eio.Host.t ->
32:   config ->
33:   ?host:[ `host ] Domain_name.t ->
34:   [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t ->
35:   flow
36: (** Wrap an existing TCP flow in TLS. Performs the handshake
37:     synchronously (blocking the fiber) before returning. *)
38: 
39: val server_of_flow :
40:   ?host_eio:Eta_eio.Host.t ->
41:   server_config ->
42:   [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t ->
43:   flow * epoch
44: (** Wrap an accepted TCP flow in server-side TLS. Performs the handshake
45:     synchronously (blocking the fiber) before returning the TLS flow and
46:     negotiated epoch. *)
47: 
48: val server_context : server_config -> server_context
49: (** Compile a server TLS configuration once. Use this for listeners so
50:     certificate chains, SNI certificates, ALPN policy, and strict-SNI policy are
51:     loaded once instead of once per connection. *)
52: 
53: val server_of_flow_with_context :
54:   ?host_eio:Eta_eio.Host.t ->
55:   server_context ->
56:   [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t ->
57:   flow * epoch
58: (** Wrap an accepted TCP flow using a precompiled server TLS context. *)
59: 
60: val epoch : flow -> (epoch, unit) result
61: (** Extract the negotiated epoch. [Error ()] if the handshake has not
62:     completed or the flow is not a TLS flow. *)
63: 
64: val alpn_protocol : flow -> string option
65: (** Convenience: the negotiated ALPN protocol, if any. *)
````

## File: test/http/test_eta_http_server_config.ml
````ocaml
  1: open Test_eta_http_support
  2: 
  3: let check_invalid label message config =
  4:   Alcotest.check_raises label (Invalid_argument message) (fun () ->
  5:       Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.validate config)
  6: 
  7: let test_server_config_validation () =
  8:   let config = Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default in
  9:   Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.validate config;
 10:   check_invalid "max connections"
 11:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.max_connections must be > 0"
 12:     { config with max_connections = 0 };
 13:   check_invalid "backlog"
 14:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.backlog must be > 0"
 15:     { config with backlog = 0 };
 16:   check_invalid "read buffer"
 17:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.read_buffer_size must be > 0"
 18:     { config with read_buffer_size = 0 };
 19:   check_invalid "command queue"
 20:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.command_queue_capacity must be > 0"
 21:     { config with command_queue_capacity = 0 };
 22:   check_invalid "tls handshake"
 23:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.tls_handshake_timeout must be > 0"
 24:     { config with tls_handshake_timeout = Eta.Duration.zero };
 25:   let server =
 26:     {
 27:       config.server with
 28:       limits = { config.server.limits with max_request_headers = 0 };
 29:     }
 30:   in
 31:   check_invalid "backend-neutral server config"
 32:     "Eta_http.Server.Config.max_request_headers must be > 0"
 33:     { config with server };
 34:   let h2_config =
 35:     { config.h2_config with read_buffer_size = 1024 }
 36:   in
 37:   check_invalid "h2 frame size"
 38:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config.read_buffer_size must be between 16384 and 16777215"
 39:     { config with h2_config };
 40:   let h2_config =
 41:     { config.h2_config with request_body_buffer_size = 0 }
 42:   in
 43:   check_invalid "h2 request body buffer"
 44:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config.request_body_buffer_size must be > 0"
 45:     { config with h2_config };
 46:   let h2_config =
 47:     { config.h2_config with max_concurrent_streams = 0l }
 48:   in
 49:   check_invalid "h2 concurrent streams"
 50:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config.max_concurrent_streams must be > 0"
 51:     { config with h2_config };
 52:   let h2_config =
 53:     { config.h2_config with max_concurrent_streams = 4097l }
 54:   in
 55:   check_invalid "h2 concurrent streams upper bound"
 56:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config.max_concurrent_streams must be <= 4096"
 57:     { config with h2_config };
 58:   let h2_config =
 59:     { config.h2_config with initial_window_size = -1l }
 60:   in
 61:   check_invalid "h2 initial window"
 62:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_config.initial_window_size must be >= 0"
 63:     { config with h2_config };
 64:   let h2_security_config =
 65:     {
 66:       Eta_http.H2.Security.default_config with
 67:       max_ping_per_connection = 0;
 68:     }
 69:   in
 70:   check_invalid "h2 security"
 71:     "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.h2_security_config.max_ping_per_connection must be > 0"
 72:     { config with h2_security_config = Some h2_security_config }
 73: 
 74: let test_server_config_default_h2_max_concurrent_streams_is_bounded () =
 75:   let config = Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default in
 76:   Alcotest.(check int32)
 77:     "default h2 max concurrent streams" 128l
 78:     config.h2_config.H2.Config.max_concurrent_streams
 79: 
 80: let test_start_h1_validates_config_before_fork () =
 81:   run_eio @@ fun env ->
 82:   Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
 83:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
 84:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
 85:   let socket =
 86:     Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
 87:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
 88:   in
 89:   let config =
 90:     { Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.default with max_connections = 0 }
 91:   in
 92:   let handler _request =
 93:     Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.text "unexpected\n")
 94:   in
 95:   Alcotest.check_raises "start validates"
 96:     (Invalid_argument
 97:        "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config.max_connections must be > 0")
 98:     (fun () ->
 99:       ignore
100:         (Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~config ~socket
101:            handler
102:           : Eta_http_eio.Server.t))
103: 
104: 
105: let tcp_port = function
106:   | `Tcp (_, port) -> port
107:   | `Unix _ -> Alcotest.fail "expected TCP listener"
108: 
109: let test_server_start_desired_port () =
110:   run_eio @@ fun env ->
111:   Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
112:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
113:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
114:   let desired_port =
115:     Eio.Switch.run @@ fun probe_sw ->
116:     let probe =
117:       Eio.Net.listen ~sw:probe_sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
118:         (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
119:     in
120:     tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr probe)
121:   in
122:   let socket =
123:     Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
124:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, desired_port))
125:   in
126:   let port = tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
127:   Alcotest.(check int) "desired port bound" desired_port port;
128:   let handler _request =
129:     Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.text "ok")
130:   in
131:   let server =
132:     Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~socket handler
133:   in
134:   Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate;
135:   Alcotest.(check unit) "shutdown completes" () ()
136: 
137: let test_server_start_available_port () =
138:   run_eio @@ fun env ->
139:   Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
140:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
141:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
142:   let socket =
143:     Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
144:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
145:   in
146:   let port = tcp_port (Eio.Net.listening_addr socket) in
147:   Alcotest.(check bool) "available port assigned" true (port <> 0);
148:   let handler _request =
149:     Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.text "ok")
150:   in
151:   let server =
152:     Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~socket handler
153:   in
154:   Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate;
155:   Alcotest.(check unit) "shutdown completes" () ()
156: 
157: let test_server_shutdown_before_connections () =
158:   run_eio @@ fun env ->
159:   Eio.Switch.run @@ fun sw ->
160:   let net = Eio.Stdenv.net env in
161:   let clock = Eio.Stdenv.clock env in
162:   let socket =
163:     Eio.Net.listen ~sw ~reuse_addr:true ~backlog:1 net
164:       (`Tcp (Eio.Net.Ipaddr.V4.loopback, 0))
165:   in
166:   let handler _request =
167:     Eta.Effect.pure (Eta_http.Server.Response.text "ok")
168:   in
169:   let server =
170:     Eta_http_eio.Server.start_h1_on_socket ~sw ~clock ~socket handler
171:   in
172:   Eta_http_eio.Server.shutdown server Immediate;
173:   (* zio-http allows providing a Server layer without starting it; Eta's
174:      equivalent is calling shutdown immediately after start_h1_on_socket,
175:      before any connection is accepted. *)
176:   Alcotest.(check unit) "shutdown before connections completes" () ()
````

## File: lib/http/tls/openssl.ml
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: type ctx
 4: type ssl
 5: type session
 6: 
 7: type server_certificate = {
 8:   server_name : string;
 9:   certificate_chain_file : string;
10:   private_key_file : string;
11: }
12: 
13: type handshake_result =
14:   | Handshake_ok
15:   | Handshake_error of int
16: 
17: external create_ctx : unit -> ctx = "eta_openssl_ctx_create"
18: external ctx_load_ca : ctx -> string -> unit = "eta_openssl_ctx_load_ca"
19: external create_server_ctx_raw :
20:   string -> string -> server_certificate list -> bool -> string list -> ctx
21:   = "eta_openssl_server_ctx_create"
22: external create_server_ssl : ctx -> ssl = "eta_openssl_server_ssl_create"
23: external create_ssl_raw : ctx -> string option -> string option -> string list -> ssl
24:   = "eta_openssl_ssl_create"
25: external handshake_raw : ssl -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_handshake"
26: external read_raw : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_read"
27: external write_raw : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_write"
28: external shutdown_raw : ssl -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_shutdown"
29: external bio_read_raw : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int = "eta_openssl_bio_read"
30: external bio_write_raw : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int = "eta_openssl_bio_write"
31: external bio_write_pending_raw : ssl -> int = "eta_openssl_bio_write_pending"
32: external ssl_pending_raw : ssl -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_pending"
33: external get_alpn_selected_raw : ssl -> string option = "eta_openssl_ssl_get_alpn_selected"
34: external get_version_raw : ssl -> string = "eta_openssl_ssl_get_version"
35: external get_servername_raw : ssl -> string option = "eta_openssl_ssl_get_servername"
36: external get_verify_result_raw : ssl -> int = "eta_openssl_ssl_get_verify_result"
37: external get1_session_raw : ssl -> session option = "eta_openssl_ssl_get1_session"
38: external set_session_raw : ssl -> session -> unit = "eta_openssl_ssl_set_session"
39: external session_reused_raw : ssl -> bool = "eta_openssl_ssl_session_reused"
40: external err_peek_error_raw : unit -> string option = "eta_openssl_err_peek_error"
41: external err_clear_error_raw : unit -> unit = "eta_openssl_err_clear_error"
42: external random_bytes_into : bytes -> int -> int -> unit = "eta_openssl_random_bytes"
43: external sha1 : string -> string = "eta_openssl_sha1"
44: 
45: let random_bytes len =
46:   if len < 0 then invalid_arg "OpenSSL.random_bytes: negative length";
47:   let bytes = Bytes.create len in
48:   random_bytes_into bytes 0 len;
49:   bytes
50: 
51: let create_ssl ctx ~hostname ~ip ~alpn_protocols =
52:   create_ssl_raw ctx hostname ip alpn_protocols
53: 
54: let server_certificate ~server_name ~certificate_chain_file ~private_key_file =
55:   { server_name; certificate_chain_file; private_key_file }
56: 
57: let create_server_ctx ?(certificates = []) ?(require_sni_match = false)
58:     ~certificate_chain_file ~private_key_file ~alpn_protocols () =
59:   create_server_ctx_raw certificate_chain_file private_key_file certificates
60:     require_sni_match alpn_protocols
61: 
62: let handshake ssl =
63:   match handshake_raw ssl with
64:   | 0 -> Handshake_ok
65:   | code -> Handshake_error code
66: 
67: let read ssl buf off len = read_raw ssl buf off len
68: let write ssl buf off len = write_raw ssl buf off len
69: let shutdown ssl = shutdown_raw ssl
70: let bio_read ssl buf off len = bio_read_raw ssl buf off len
71: let bio_write ssl buf off len = bio_write_raw ssl buf off len
72: let bio_write_pending ssl = bio_write_pending_raw ssl
73: let ssl_pending ssl = ssl_pending_raw ssl
74: let get_alpn_selected ssl = get_alpn_selected_raw ssl
75: let get_version ssl = get_version_raw ssl
76: let get_servername ssl = get_servername_raw ssl
77: let get_verify_result ssl = get_verify_result_raw ssl
78: let get_session ssl = get1_session_raw ssl
79: let set_session ssl session = set_session_raw ssl session
80: let session_reused ssl = session_reused_raw ssl
81: let err_peek_error () = err_peek_error_raw ()
82: let err_clear_error () = err_clear_error_raw ()
````

## File: lib/http/tls/openssl.mli
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: (** Low-level OpenSSL 3.x binding. No I/O — pure state-machine driver. *)
  4: 
  5: type ctx
  6: (** Opaque SSL_CTX wrapper. *)
  7: 
  8: type ssl
  9: (** Opaque SSL wrapper with memory BIO pair. *)
 10: 
 11: type session
 12: (** Opaque TLS session reference for explicit client-side resumption. *)
 13: 
 14: type server_certificate
 15: (** Certificate entry selected by SNI on server handshakes. *)
 16: 
 17: val create_ctx : unit -> ctx
 18: (** Create a client SSL_CTX with Eta's TLS 1.2/1.3 policy, default system
 19:     trust store, and peer verification enabled. *)
 20: 
 21: val ctx_load_ca : ctx -> string -> unit
 22: (** [ctx_load_ca ctx path] adds [path] (a PEM CA file) to the trust
 23:     store of [ctx]. The default system trust store remains in eff.
 24:     Raises [Failure] if the file cannot be loaded. *)
 25: 
 26: val create_server_ctx :
 27:   ?certificates:server_certificate list ->
 28:   ?require_sni_match:bool ->
 29:   certificate_chain_file:string ->
 30:   private_key_file:string ->
 31:   alpn_protocols:string list ->
 32:   unit ->
 33:   ctx
 34: (** Create a server SSL_CTX with Eta's TLS policy, certificate chain,
 35:     private key, ALPN selection callback, and OpenSSL session cache enabled
 36:     for TLS resumption. Raises [Failure] when the certificate or private key
 37:     cannot be loaded or do not match. *)
 38: 
 39: val server_certificate :
 40:   server_name:string ->
 41:   certificate_chain_file:string ->
 42:   private_key_file:string ->
 43:   server_certificate
 44: (** Build a certificate entry for [create_server_ctx ~certificates]. *)
 45: 
 46: val create_server_ssl : ctx -> ssl
 47: (** Create a server SSL connection with memory BIOs in accept state. *)
 48: 
 49: val create_ssl :
 50:   ctx ->
 51:   hostname:string option ->
 52:   ip:string option ->
 53:   alpn_protocols:string list ->
 54:   ssl
 55: (** Create an SSL connection with memory BIOs. [hostname] sets SNI and,
 56:     when [ip] is absent, DNS peer certificate identity verification. [ip]
 57:     sets IP peer certificate identity verification. [alpn_protocols] are sent
 58:     in wire order. *)
 59: 
 60: type handshake_result =
 61:   | Handshake_ok
 62:   | Handshake_error of int
 63: 
 64: val handshake : ssl -> handshake_result
 65: (** Drive one step of the TLS handshake. Returns [Handshake_ok] on
 66:     completion, or [Handshake_error code] where [code] is the raw
 67:     [SSL_get_error] value. *)
 68: 
 69: val read : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int
 70: (** [read ssl buf off len] attempts to read up to [len] decrypted bytes
 71:     into [buf] at offset [off]. Returns bytes read on success, or a
 72:     negated [SSL_get_error] code on failure. *)
 73: 
 74: val write : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int
 75: (** [write ssl buf off len] attempts to write [len] decrypted bytes
 76:     from [buf] at offset [off]. Returns bytes written on success, or a
 77:     negated [SSL_get_error] code on failure. *)
 78: 
 79: val shutdown : ssl -> int
 80: (** Initiate SSL shutdown. Returns [SSL_shutdown] result. *)
 81: 
 82: val bio_read : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int
 83: (** Read encrypted bytes from OpenSSL's write BIO (data to send over
 84:     the wire). *)
 85: 
 86: val bio_write : ssl -> Cstruct.buffer -> int -> int -> int
 87: (** Write encrypted bytes into OpenSSL's read BIO (data received from
 88:     the wire). *)
 89: 
 90: val bio_write_pending : ssl -> int
 91: (** Number of encrypted bytes pending in the write BIO. *)
 92: 
 93: val ssl_pending : ssl -> int
 94: (** Number of decrypted bytes readable from SSL without touching the
 95:     network. *)
 96: 
 97: val get_alpn_selected : ssl -> string option
 98: (** The ALPN protocol selected by the server, if any. *)
 99: 
100: val get_version : ssl -> string
101: (** The negotiated TLS protocol version, such as ["TLSv1.3"]. *)
102: 
103: val get_servername : ssl -> string option
104: (** The client SNI server name observed by a server SSL, if any. *)
105: 
106: val get_verify_result : ssl -> int
107: (** [X509_V_OK] (0) on success, or an [X509_V_ERR_*] code. *)
108: 
109: val get_session : ssl -> session option
110: (** Return a retained TLS session after a completed handshake, when OpenSSL
111:     made one available. *)
112: 
113: val set_session : ssl -> session -> unit
114: (** Attach a previous client session before driving the next client
115:     handshake. Raises [Failure] if OpenSSL rejects the session. *)
116: 
117: val session_reused : ssl -> bool
118: (** [true] when the completed handshake reused a previously supplied session. *)
119: 
120: val err_peek_error : unit -> string option
121: (** Peek at the top OpenSSL error string, if any. *)
122: 
123: val err_clear_error : unit -> unit
124: (** Clear the OpenSSL error stack. *)
125: 
126: val random_bytes : int -> bytes
127: (** [random_bytes len] returns [len] bytes from OpenSSL [RAND_bytes].
128:     Raises [Failure] if OpenSSL cannot provide random bytes. *)
129: 
130: val sha1 : string -> string
131: (** [sha1 input] returns the 20-byte SHA-1 digest from OpenSSL EVP. Eta uses
132:     this for protocol-mandated WebSocket accept keys, not as a security
133:     primitive exposed to applications. *)
````

## File: lib/http/tls/tls_openssl_stubs.c
````c
  1: /* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT */
  2: 
  3: #include <stdlib.h>
  4: #include <string.h>
  5: #include <strings.h>
  6: #include <openssl/ssl.h>
  7: #include <openssl/err.h>
  8: #include <openssl/bio.h>
  9: #include <openssl/evp.h>
 10: #include <openssl/rand.h>
 11: #include <openssl/x509v3.h>
 12: 
 13: #include <caml/mlvalues.h>
 14: #include <caml/memory.h>
 15: #include <caml/alloc.h>
 16: #include <caml/fail.h>
 17: #include <caml/custom.h>
 18: #include <caml/bigarray.h>
 19: 
 20: /* Cipher policy: TLS 1.2 ECDHE RSA/ECDSA AEAD only, no FFDHE. */
 21: #define ETA_CIPHER_LIST \
 22:   "ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:" \
 23:   "ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:" \
 24:   "ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:" \
 25:   "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:" \
 26:   "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:" \
 27:   "ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305"
 28: 
 29: #define ETA_TLS13_CIPHER_LIST \
 30:   "TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:" \
 31:   "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:" \
 32:   "TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"
 33: 
 34: static const unsigned char ETA_SESSION_ID_CONTEXT[] = "eta-http";
 35: 
 36: /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
 37: /* SSL_CTX — custom block; finalizer frees the context.                */
 38: 
 39: typedef struct {
 40:   unsigned char *wire;
 41:   unsigned int len;
 42: } eta_alpn_config;
 43: 
 44: typedef struct {
 45:   char *name;
 46:   SSL_CTX *ctx;
 47: } eta_sni_entry;
 48: 
 49: typedef struct {
 50:   eta_sni_entry *entries;
 51:   size_t count;
 52:   int require_match;
 53: } eta_sni_config;
 54: 
 55: typedef struct {
 56:   SSL_CTX *ctx;
 57: } eta_ssl_ctx_box;
 58: 
 59: static int eta_alpn_ex_index = -1;
 60: static int eta_sni_ex_index = -1;
 61: 
 62: static void eta_alpn_ex_free(void *parent, void *ptr, CRYPTO_EX_DATA *ad,
 63:                              int idx, long argl, void *argp)
 64: {
 65:   (void)parent;
 66:   (void)ad;
 67:   (void)idx;
 68:   (void)argl;
 69:   (void)argp;
 70:   eta_alpn_config *alpn = (eta_alpn_config *)ptr;
 71:   if (alpn != NULL) {
 72:     free(alpn->wire);
 73:     free(alpn);
 74:   }
 75: }
 76: 
 77: static void eta_sni_ex_free(void *parent, void *ptr, CRYPTO_EX_DATA *ad,
 78:                             int idx, long argl, void *argp)
 79: {
 80:   (void)parent;
 81:   (void)ad;
 82:   (void)idx;
 83:   (void)argl;
 84:   (void)argp;
 85:   eta_sni_config *sni = (eta_sni_config *)ptr;
 86:   if (sni != NULL) {
 87:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sni->count; i++) {
 88:       free(sni->entries[i].name);
 89:       if (sni->entries[i].ctx != NULL) {
 90:         SSL_CTX_free(sni->entries[i].ctx);
 91:       }
 92:     }
 93:     free(sni->entries);
 94:     free(sni);
 95:   }
 96: }
 97: 
 98: static int eta_alpn_ex_index_get(void)
 99: {
100:   if (eta_alpn_ex_index < 0) {
101:     eta_alpn_ex_index =
102:         SSL_CTX_get_ex_new_index(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, eta_alpn_ex_free);
103:   }
104:   return eta_alpn_ex_index;
105: }
106: 
107: static int eta_sni_ex_index_get(void)
108: {
109:   if (eta_sni_ex_index < 0) {
110:     eta_sni_ex_index =
111:         SSL_CTX_get_ex_new_index(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, eta_sni_ex_free);
112:   }
113:   return eta_sni_ex_index;
114: }
115: 
116: static int eta_ssl_ctx_cmp(value v1, value v2)
117: {
118:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *b1 = *((eta_ssl_ctx_box **)Data_custom_val(v1));
119:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *b2 = *((eta_ssl_ctx_box **)Data_custom_val(v2));
120:   return b1 != b2;
121: }
122: 
123: static intnat eta_ssl_ctx_hash(value v)
124: {
125:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *box = *((eta_ssl_ctx_box **)Data_custom_val(v));
126:   return (intnat)box;
127: }
128: 
129: static void eta_ssl_ctx_finalize(value v)
130: {
131:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *box = *((eta_ssl_ctx_box **)Data_custom_val(v));
132:   if (box != NULL) {
133:     if (box->ctx != NULL) {
134:       SSL_CTX_free(box->ctx);
135:     }
136:     free(box);
137:   }
138: }
139: 
140: static struct custom_operations eta_ssl_ctx_ops = {
141:   "eta.openssl.ctx",
142:   eta_ssl_ctx_finalize,
143:   eta_ssl_ctx_cmp,
144:   eta_ssl_ctx_hash,
145:   custom_serialize_default,
146:   custom_deserialize_default,
147:   custom_compare_ext_default,
148:   custom_fixed_length_default
149: };
150: 
151: static SSL_CTX *eta_ssl_ctx_val(value v)
152: {
153:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *box = *((eta_ssl_ctx_box **)Data_custom_val(v));
154:   return box->ctx;
155: }
156: 
157: static value eta_alloc_ssl_ctx(SSL_CTX *ctx)
158: {
159:   eta_ssl_ctx_box *box = malloc(sizeof(*box));
160:   if (box == NULL) {
161:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
162:     caml_failwith("malloc failed");
163:   }
164:   box->ctx = ctx;
165:   value v = caml_alloc_custom(&eta_ssl_ctx_ops, sizeof(void *), 0, 1);
166:   *((void **)Data_custom_val(v)) = box;
167:   return v;
168: }
169: 
170: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_random_bytes(value v_bytes, value v_off, value v_len)
171: {
172:   CAMLparam3(v_bytes, v_off, v_len);
173:   int off = Int_val(v_off);
174:   int len = Int_val(v_len);
175:   mlsize_t bytes_len = caml_string_length(v_bytes);
176:   if (off < 0 || len < 0 || (mlsize_t)off > bytes_len ||
177:       (mlsize_t)len > bytes_len - (mlsize_t)off) {
178:     caml_invalid_argument("OpenSSL RAND_bytes bounds");
179:   }
180:   if (RAND_bytes((unsigned char *)Bytes_val(v_bytes) + off, len) != 1) {
181:     caml_failwith("OpenSSL RAND_bytes failed");
182:   }
183:   CAMLreturn(Val_unit);
184: }
185: 
186: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_sha1(value v_input)
187: {
188:   CAMLparam1(v_input);
189:   unsigned char digest[20];
190:   unsigned int digest_len = 0;
191:   const EVP_MD *sha1 = EVP_sha1();
192:   if (sha1 == NULL ||
193:       EVP_Digest(String_val(v_input), caml_string_length(v_input), digest,
194:                  &digest_len, sha1, NULL) != 1 ||
195:       digest_len != sizeof(digest)) {
196:     caml_failwith("OpenSSL EVP SHA1 failed");
197:   }
198:   CAMLreturn(caml_alloc_initialized_string(sizeof(digest), (const char *)digest));
199: }
200: 
201: /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
202: /* SSL — custom block holds SSL*.  The SSL object holds an internal
203:    reference to its SSL_CTX (via SSL_new -> SSL_CTX_up_ref), so
204:    SSL_free decrements the ctx refcount automatically.                */
205: 
206: static void eta_ssl_finalize(value v)
207: {
208:   void **data = (void **)Data_custom_val(v);
209:   SSL *ssl = (SSL *)data[0];
210:   if (ssl != NULL) {
211:     SSL_free(ssl);
212:   }
213: }
214: 
215: static int eta_ssl_cmp(value v1, value v2)
216: {
217:   return *((void **)Data_custom_val(v1)) != *((void **)Data_custom_val(v2));
218: }
219: 
220: static intnat eta_ssl_hash(value v)
221: {
222:   return (intnat)*((void **)Data_custom_val(v));
223: }
224: 
225: static struct custom_operations eta_ssl_ops = {
226:   "eta.openssl.ssl",
227:   eta_ssl_finalize,
228:   eta_ssl_cmp,
229:   eta_ssl_hash,
230:   custom_serialize_default,
231:   custom_deserialize_default,
232:   custom_compare_ext_default,
233:   custom_fixed_length_default
234: };
235: 
236: static value eta_alloc_ssl(SSL *ssl)
237: {
238:   value v = caml_alloc_custom(&eta_ssl_ops, sizeof(void *), 0, 1);
239:   void **data = (void **)Data_custom_val(v);
240:   data[0] = ssl;
241:   return v;
242: }
243: 
244: static SSL *eta_ssl_val(value v)
245: {
246:   return ((SSL **)Data_custom_val(v))[0];
247: }
248: 
249: /* SSL_SESSION — custom block owns one SSL_SESSION reference. */
250: 
251: static void eta_ssl_session_finalize(value v)
252: {
253:   SSL_SESSION *session = *((SSL_SESSION **)Data_custom_val(v));
254:   if (session != NULL) {
255:     SSL_SESSION_free(session);
256:   }
257: }
258: 
259: static int eta_ssl_session_cmp(value v1, value v2)
260: {
261:   return *((void **)Data_custom_val(v1)) != *((void **)Data_custom_val(v2));
262: }
263: 
264: static intnat eta_ssl_session_hash(value v)
265: {
266:   return (intnat)*((void **)Data_custom_val(v));
267: }
268: 
269: static struct custom_operations eta_ssl_session_ops = {
270:   "eta.openssl.session",
271:   eta_ssl_session_finalize,
272:   eta_ssl_session_cmp,
273:   eta_ssl_session_hash,
274:   custom_serialize_default,
275:   custom_deserialize_default,
276:   custom_compare_ext_default,
277:   custom_fixed_length_default
278: };
279: 
280: static value eta_alloc_ssl_session(SSL_SESSION *session)
281: {
282:   value v = caml_alloc_custom(&eta_ssl_session_ops, sizeof(void *), 0, 1);
283:   *((void **)Data_custom_val(v)) = session;
284:   return v;
285: }
286: 
287: static SSL_SESSION *eta_ssl_session_val(value v)
288: {
289:   return ((SSL_SESSION **)Data_custom_val(v))[0];
290: }
291: 
292: /* Build ALPN protocol list in wire format.  Returns a freshly
293:    allocated buffer (or NULL on failure).  *out_len receives the
294:    total wire length. */
295: static unsigned char *eta_build_alpn(const char *const *protos, size_t count,
296:                                       size_t *out_len)
297: {
298:   size_t i, len = 0;
299:   for (i = 0; i < count; i++) {
300:     size_t plen = strlen(protos[i]);
301:     if (plen > 255) {
302:       return NULL;
303:     }
304:     len += 1 + plen;
305:   }
306:   unsigned char *buf = malloc(len);
307:   if (buf == NULL) {
308:     return NULL;
309:   }
310:   size_t off = 0;
311:   for (i = 0; i < count; i++) {
312:     size_t plen = strlen(protos[i]);
313:     buf[off++] = (unsigned char)plen;
314:     memcpy(buf + off, protos[i], plen);
315:     off += plen;
316:   }
317:   *out_len = len;
318:   return buf;
319: }
320: 
321: static unsigned char *eta_build_alpn_ocaml(value v_alpn, size_t *out_len)
322: {
323:   CAMLparam1(v_alpn);
324:   CAMLlocal1(v_item);
325:   size_t count = 0;
326:   value v_tail = v_alpn;
327:   while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
328:     count++;
329:     v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
330:   }
331:   if (count == 0) {
332:     *out_len = 0;
333:     CAMLreturnT(unsigned char *, NULL);
334:   }
335:   const char **protos = calloc(count, sizeof(char *));
336:   if (protos == NULL) {
337:     caml_failwith("calloc failed");
338:   }
339:   size_t i = 0;
340:   v_tail = v_alpn;
341:   while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
342:     v_item = Field(v_tail, 0);
343:     protos[i++] = String_val(v_item);
344:     v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
345:   }
346:   unsigned char *alpn_buf = eta_build_alpn(protos, count, out_len);
347:   free(protos);
348:   if (alpn_buf == NULL) {
349:     caml_failwith("ALPN encode failed");
350:   }
351:   CAMLreturnT(unsigned char *, alpn_buf);
352: }
353: 
354: static int eta_alpn_select_cb(SSL *ssl, const unsigned char **out,
355:                               unsigned char *outlen,
356:                               const unsigned char *in, unsigned int inlen,
357:                               void *arg)
358: {
359:   (void)ssl;
360:   eta_alpn_config *config = (eta_alpn_config *)arg;
361:   if (config == NULL || config->wire == NULL || config->len == 0) {
362:     return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK;
363:   }
364:   int rc = SSL_select_next_proto((unsigned char **)out, outlen,
365:                                  config->wire, config->len, in, inlen);
366:   if (rc == OPENSSL_NPN_NEGOTIATED) {
367:     return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_OK;
368:   }
369:   return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK;
370: }
371: 
372: static int eta_install_alpn(SSL_CTX *ctx, const unsigned char *wire, size_t len)
373: {
374:   if (len == 0) {
375:     return 1;
376:   }
377:   eta_alpn_config *alpn = malloc(sizeof(*alpn));
378:   if (alpn == NULL) {
379:     return 0;
380:   }
381:   alpn->wire = malloc(len);
382:   if (alpn->wire == NULL) {
383:     free(alpn);
384:     return 0;
385:   }
386:   memcpy(alpn->wire, wire, len);
387:   alpn->len = (unsigned int)len;
388:   int idx = eta_alpn_ex_index_get();
389:   if (idx < 0 || SSL_CTX_set_ex_data(ctx, idx, alpn) != 1) {
390:     free(alpn->wire);
391:     free(alpn);
392:     return 0;
393:   }
394:   SSL_CTX_set_alpn_select_cb(ctx, eta_alpn_select_cb, alpn);
395:   return 1;
396: }
397: 
398: static SSL_CTX *eta_new_server_ctx(const char *cert, const char *key,
399:                                    const unsigned char *alpn_wire,
400:                                    size_t alpn_len)
401: {
402:   const SSL_METHOD *method = TLS_server_method();
403:   if (method == NULL) {
404:     caml_failwith("TLS_server_method failed");
405:   }
406:   SSL_CTX *ctx = SSL_CTX_new(method);
407:   if (ctx == NULL) {
408:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_new failed");
409:   }
410: 
411:   if (!SSL_CTX_set_min_proto_version(ctx, TLS1_2_VERSION) ||
412:       !SSL_CTX_set_max_proto_version(ctx, TLS1_3_VERSION)) {
413:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
414:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_proto_version failed");
415:   }
416: 
417:   if (SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list(ctx, ETA_CIPHER_LIST) != 1) {
418:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
419:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list failed");
420:   }
421:   if (SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites(ctx, ETA_TLS13_CIPHER_LIST) != 1) {
422:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
423:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites failed");
424:   }
425: 
426:   SSL_CTX_set_session_cache_mode(ctx, SSL_SESS_CACHE_SERVER);
427:   if (SSL_CTX_set_session_id_context(
428:           ctx, ETA_SESSION_ID_CONTEXT,
429:           (unsigned int)(sizeof(ETA_SESSION_ID_CONTEXT) - 1)) != 1) {
430:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
431:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_session_id_context failed");
432:   }
433: 
434:   if (SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_file(ctx, cert) != 1) {
435:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
436:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_file failed");
437:   }
438: 
439:   if (SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey_file(ctx, key, SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) != 1) {
440:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
441:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey_file failed");
442:   }
443: 
444:   if (SSL_CTX_check_private_key(ctx) != 1) {
445:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
446:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_check_private_key failed");
447:   }
448: 
449:   if (!eta_install_alpn(ctx, alpn_wire, alpn_len)) {
450:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
451:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_ex_data failed");
452:   }
453: 
454:   return ctx;
455: }
456: 
457: static int eta_sni_select_cb(SSL *ssl, int *ad, void *arg)
458: {
459:   eta_sni_config *config = (eta_sni_config *)arg;
460:   const char *name = SSL_get_servername(ssl, TLSEXT_NAMETYPE_host_name);
461:   if (config == NULL || name == NULL || name[0] == '\0') {
462:     if (config != NULL && config->require_match) {
463:       *ad = SSL_AD_UNRECOGNIZED_NAME;
464:       return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_ALERT_FATAL;
465:     }
466:     return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK;
467:   }
468: 
469:   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->count; i++) {
470:     if (strcasecmp(config->entries[i].name, name) == 0) {
471:       SSL_set_SSL_CTX(ssl, config->entries[i].ctx);
472:       return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_OK;
473:     }
474:   }
475: 
476:   if (config->require_match) {
477:     *ad = SSL_AD_UNRECOGNIZED_NAME;
478:     return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_ALERT_FATAL;
479:   }
480:   return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK;
481: }
482: 
483: /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
484: /* OCaml externals                                                    */
485: 
486: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ctx_create(value v_unit)
487: {
488:   CAMLparam1(v_unit);
489:   const SSL_METHOD *method = TLS_client_method();
490:   if (method == NULL) {
491:     caml_failwith("TLS_client_method failed");
492:   }
493:   SSL_CTX *ctx = SSL_CTX_new(method);
494:   if (ctx == NULL) {
495:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_new failed");
496:   }
497: 
498:   /* TLS 1.2 minimum; TLS 1.3 preferred when the peer supports it. */
499:   if (!SSL_CTX_set_min_proto_version(ctx, TLS1_2_VERSION) ||
500:       !SSL_CTX_set_max_proto_version(ctx, TLS1_3_VERSION)) {
501:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
502:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_proto_version failed");
503:   }
504: 
505:   /* Cipher policy. */
506:   if (SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list(ctx, ETA_CIPHER_LIST) != 1) {
507:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
508:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list failed");
509:   }
510:   if (SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites(ctx, ETA_TLS13_CIPHER_LIST) != 1) {
511:     SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
512:     caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites failed");
513:   }
514: 
515:   SSL_CTX_set_session_cache_mode(ctx, SSL_SESS_CACHE_CLIENT);
516: 
517:   /* Default system trust store. */
518:   SSL_CTX_set_default_verify_paths(ctx);
519:   SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER, NULL);
520: 
521:   CAMLreturn(eta_alloc_ssl_ctx(ctx));
522: }
523: 
524: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ctx_load_ca(value v_ctx, value v_path)
525: {
526:   CAMLparam2(v_ctx, v_path);
527:   SSL_CTX *ctx = eta_ssl_ctx_val(v_ctx);
528:   const char *path = String_val(v_path);
529:   if (SSL_CTX_load_verify_locations(ctx, path, NULL) != 1) {
530:     char buf[256];
531:     unsigned long e = ERR_peek_error();
532:     if (e != 0) {
533:       ERR_error_string_n(e, buf, sizeof(buf));
534:       ERR_clear_error();
535:       caml_failwith(buf);
536:     } else {
537:       caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_load_verify_locations failed");
538:     }
539:   }
540:   CAMLreturn(Val_unit);
541: }
542: 
543: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_server_ctx_create(value v_cert, value v_key,
544:                                              value v_sni_certs,
545:                                              value v_require_sni_match,
546:                                              value v_alpn)
547: {
548:   CAMLparam5(v_cert, v_key, v_sni_certs, v_require_sni_match, v_alpn);
549:   CAMLlocal2(v_tail, v_entry);
550:   size_t alpn_len = 0;
551:   unsigned char *alpn_wire = eta_build_alpn_ocaml(v_alpn, &alpn_len);
552:   SSL_CTX *ctx =
553:       eta_new_server_ctx(String_val(v_cert), String_val(v_key), alpn_wire,
554:                          alpn_len);
555: 
556:   size_t count = 0;
557:   v_tail = v_sni_certs;
558:   while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
559:     count++;
560:     v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
561:   }
562: 
563:   if (count > 0 || Bool_val(v_require_sni_match)) {
564:     eta_sni_config *sni = calloc(1, sizeof(*sni));
565:     if (sni == NULL) {
566:       free(alpn_wire);
567:       SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
568:       caml_failwith("calloc failed");
569:     }
570:     sni->count = count;
571:     sni->require_match = Bool_val(v_require_sni_match);
572:     if (count > 0) {
573:       sni->entries = calloc(count, sizeof(*sni->entries));
574:       if (sni->entries == NULL) {
575:         free(sni);
576:         free(alpn_wire);
577:         SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
578:         caml_failwith("calloc failed");
579:       }
580:     }
581: 
582:     size_t i = 0;
583:     v_tail = v_sni_certs;
584:     while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
585:       v_entry = Field(v_tail, 0);
586:       const char *name = String_val(Field(v_entry, 0));
587:       const char *cert = String_val(Field(v_entry, 1));
588:       const char *key = String_val(Field(v_entry, 2));
589:       sni->entries[i].name = strdup(name);
590:       if (sni->entries[i].name == NULL) {
591:         free(alpn_wire);
592:         SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
593:         eta_sni_ex_free(NULL, sni, NULL, 0, 0, NULL);
594:         caml_failwith("strdup failed");
595:       }
596:       sni->entries[i].ctx = eta_new_server_ctx(cert, key, alpn_wire, alpn_len);
597:       i++;
598:       v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
599:     }
600: 
601:     int idx = eta_sni_ex_index_get();
602:     if (idx < 0 || SSL_CTX_set_ex_data(ctx, idx, sni) != 1) {
603:       free(alpn_wire);
604:       SSL_CTX_free(ctx);
605:       eta_sni_ex_free(NULL, sni, NULL, 0, 0, NULL);
606:       caml_failwith("SSL_CTX_set_ex_data failed");
607:     }
608:     SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_callback(ctx, eta_sni_select_cb);
609:     SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_arg(ctx, sni);
610:   }
611: 
612:   free(alpn_wire);
613:   CAMLreturn(eta_alloc_ssl_ctx(ctx));
614: }
615: 
616: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_server_ssl_create(value v_ctx)
617: {
618:   CAMLparam1(v_ctx);
619: 
620:   SSL_CTX *ctx = eta_ssl_ctx_val(v_ctx);
621:   SSL *ssl = SSL_new(ctx);
622:   if (ssl == NULL) {
623:     caml_failwith("SSL_new failed");
624:   }
625: 
626:   BIO *rbio = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());
627:   BIO *wbio = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());
628:   if (rbio == NULL || wbio == NULL) {
629:     if (rbio) BIO_free(rbio);
630:     if (wbio) BIO_free(wbio);
631:     SSL_free(ssl);
632:     caml_failwith("BIO_new failed");
633:   }
634:   SSL_set_bio(ssl, rbio, wbio);
635:   SSL_set_accept_state(ssl);
636: 
637:   CAMLreturn(eta_alloc_ssl(ssl));
638: }
639: 
640: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_create(value v_ctx, value v_hostname,
641:                                       value v_ip, value v_alpn)
642: {
643:   CAMLparam4(v_ctx, v_hostname, v_ip, v_alpn);
644:   CAMLlocal1(v_item);
645: 
646:   SSL_CTX *ctx = eta_ssl_ctx_val(v_ctx);
647:   SSL *ssl = SSL_new(ctx);
648:   if (ssl == NULL) {
649:     caml_failwith("SSL_new failed");
650:   }
651: 
652:   /* Memory BIO pair.  SSL_set_bio takes ownership. */
653:   BIO *rbio = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());
654:   BIO *wbio = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());
655:   if (rbio == NULL || wbio == NULL) {
656:     if (rbio) BIO_free(rbio);
657:     if (wbio) BIO_free(wbio);
658:     SSL_free(ssl);
659:     caml_failwith("BIO_new failed");
660:   }
661:   SSL_set_bio(ssl, rbio, wbio);
662:   SSL_set_connect_state(ssl);
663: 
664:   /* SNI hostname. */
665:   if (Is_some(v_hostname)) {
666:     const char *host = String_val(Some_val(v_hostname));
667:     if (!SSL_set_tlsext_host_name(ssl, host)) {
668:       SSL_free(ssl);
669:       caml_failwith("SSL_set_tlsext_host_name failed");
670:     }
671:   }
672: 
673:   /* Peer certificate identity.  SNI stays DNS-only; IP literals use OpenSSL's
674:      IP SAN matcher instead of falling through to hostname-less verification. */
675:   {
676:     X509_VERIFY_PARAM *param = SSL_get0_param(ssl);
677:     if (param == NULL) {
678:       SSL_free(ssl);
679:       caml_failwith("SSL_get0_param failed");
680:     }
681:     if (Is_some(v_ip)) {
682:       const char *ip = String_val(Some_val(v_ip));
683:       if (X509_VERIFY_PARAM_set1_ip_asc(param, ip) != 1) {
684:         SSL_free(ssl);
685:         caml_failwith("X509_VERIFY_PARAM_set1_ip_asc failed");
686:       }
687:     } else if (Is_some(v_hostname)) {
688:       const char *host = String_val(Some_val(v_hostname));
689:       X509_VERIFY_PARAM_set_hostflags(
690:           param, X509_CHECK_FLAG_NO_PARTIAL_WILDCARDS);
691:       if (X509_VERIFY_PARAM_set1_host(param, host, 0) != 1) {
692:         SSL_free(ssl);
693:         caml_failwith("X509_VERIFY_PARAM_set1_host failed");
694:       }
695:     }
696:   }
697: 
698:   /* ALPN.  v_alpn is an OCaml string list. */
699:   {
700:     size_t count = 0;
701:     value v_tail = v_alpn;
702:     while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
703:       count++;
704:       v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
705:     }
706:     if (count > 0) {
707:       const char **protos = calloc(count, sizeof(char *));
708:       if (protos == NULL) {
709:         SSL_free(ssl);
710:         caml_failwith("calloc failed");
711:       }
712:       size_t i = 0;
713:       v_tail = v_alpn;
714:       while (Is_block(v_tail)) {
715:         v_item = Field(v_tail, 0);
716:         protos[i++] = String_val(v_item);
717:         v_tail = Field(v_tail, 1);
718:       }
719:       size_t alpn_len = 0;
720:       unsigned char *alpn_buf = eta_build_alpn(protos, count, &alpn_len);
721:       free(protos);
722:       if (alpn_buf == NULL) {
723:         SSL_free(ssl);
724:         caml_failwith("ALPN encode failed");
725:       }
726:       int rc = SSL_set_alpn_protos(ssl, alpn_buf, (unsigned int)alpn_len);
727:       free(alpn_buf);
728:       if (rc != 0) {
729:         SSL_free(ssl);
730:         caml_failwith("SSL_set_alpn_protos failed");
731:       }
732:     }
733:   }
734: 
735:   CAMLreturn(eta_alloc_ssl(ssl));
736: }
737: 
738: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_handshake(value v_ssl)
739: {
740:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
741:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
742:   int rc = SSL_do_handshake(ssl);
743:   if (rc == 1) {
744:     CAMLreturn(Val_int(0)); /* Ok */
745:   }
746:   int err = SSL_get_error(ssl, rc);
747:   CAMLreturn(Val_int(err));
748: }
749: 
750: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_read(value v_ssl, value v_buf,
751:                                     value v_off, value v_len)
752: {
753:   CAMLparam4(v_ssl, v_buf, v_off, v_len);
754:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
755:   char *data = Caml_ba_data_val(v_buf) + Int_val(v_off);
756:   size_t readbytes = 0;
757:   int rc = SSL_read_ex(ssl, data, Int_val(v_len), &readbytes);
758:   if (rc > 0) {
759:     CAMLreturn(Val_long((long)readbytes));
760:   }
761:   int err = SSL_get_error(ssl, rc);
762:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(-err));
763: }
764: 
765: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_write(value v_ssl, value v_buf,
766:                                      value v_off, value v_len)
767: {
768:   CAMLparam4(v_ssl, v_buf, v_off, v_len);
769:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
770:   const char *data = Caml_ba_data_val(v_buf) + Int_val(v_off);
771:   size_t written = 0;
772:   int rc = SSL_write_ex(ssl, data, Int_val(v_len), &written);
773:   if (rc > 0) {
774:     CAMLreturn(Val_long((long)written));
775:   }
776:   int err = SSL_get_error(ssl, rc);
777:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(-err));
778: }
779: 
780: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_shutdown(value v_ssl)
781: {
782:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
783:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
784:   int rc = SSL_shutdown(ssl);
785:   CAMLreturn(Val_int(rc));
786: }
787: 
788: /* Read encrypted bytes from the write BIO (data OpenSSL wants to send). */
789: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_bio_read(value v_ssl, value v_buf,
790:                                     value v_off, value v_len)
791: {
792:   CAMLparam4(v_ssl, v_buf, v_off, v_len);
793:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
794:   BIO *wbio = SSL_get_wbio(ssl);
795:   if (wbio == NULL) {
796:     CAMLreturn(Val_long(0));
797:   }
798:   char *data = Caml_ba_data_val(v_buf) + Int_val(v_off);
799:   int n = BIO_read(wbio, data, Int_val(v_len));
800:   if (n < 0) {
801:     n = 0;
802:   }
803:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(n));
804: }
805: 
806: /* Write encrypted bytes into the read BIO (data received from network). */
807: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_bio_write(value v_ssl, value v_buf,
808:                                      value v_off, value v_len)
809: {
810:   CAMLparam4(v_ssl, v_buf, v_off, v_len);
811:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
812:   BIO *rbio = SSL_get_rbio(ssl);
813:   if (rbio == NULL) {
814:     CAMLreturn(Val_long(0));
815:   }
816:   const char *data = Caml_ba_data_val(v_buf) + Int_val(v_off);
817:   int n = BIO_write(rbio, data, Int_val(v_len));
818:   if (n < 0) {
819:     n = 0;
820:   }
821:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(n));
822: }
823: 
824: /* Number of bytes pending in the write BIO. */
825: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_bio_write_pending(value v_ssl)
826: {
827:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
828:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
829:   BIO *wbio = SSL_get_wbio(ssl);
830:   if (wbio == NULL) {
831:     CAMLreturn(Val_long(0));
832:   }
833:   int n = BIO_ctrl_pending(wbio);
834:   if (n < 0) {
835:     n = 0;
836:   }
837:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(n));
838: }
839: 
840: /* Number of bytes readable from SSL (decrypted). */
841: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_pending(value v_ssl)
842: {
843:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
844:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
845:   int n = SSL_pending(ssl);
846:   if (n < 0) {
847:     n = 0;
848:   }
849:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(n));
850: }
851: 
852: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_get_alpn_selected(value v_ssl)
853: {
854:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
855:   CAMLlocal1(v_result);
856:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
857:   const unsigned char *data = NULL;
858:   unsigned int len = 0;
859:   SSL_get0_alpn_selected(ssl, &data, &len);
860:   if (data == NULL || len == 0) {
861:     CAMLreturn(Val_none);
862:   }
863:   v_result = caml_alloc_string(len);
864:   memcpy((char *)String_val(v_result), data, len);
865:   CAMLreturn(caml_alloc_some(v_result));
866: }
867: 
868: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_get_version(value v_ssl)
869: {
870:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
871:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
872:   CAMLreturn(caml_copy_string(SSL_get_version(ssl)));
873: }
874: 
875: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_get_servername(value v_ssl)
876: {
877:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
878:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
879:   const char *name = SSL_get_servername(ssl, TLSEXT_NAMETYPE_host_name);
880:   if (name == NULL || name[0] == '\0') {
881:     CAMLreturn(Val_none);
882:   }
883:   CAMLreturn(caml_alloc_some(caml_copy_string(name)));
884: }
885: 
886: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_get_verify_result(value v_ssl)
887: {
888:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
889:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
890:   long rc = SSL_get_verify_result(ssl);
891:   CAMLreturn(Val_long(rc));
892: }
893: 
894: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_get1_session(value v_ssl)
895: {
896:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
897:   CAMLlocal1(v_session);
898:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
899:   SSL_SESSION *session = SSL_get1_session(ssl);
900:   if (session == NULL) {
901:     CAMLreturn(Val_none);
902:   }
903:   v_session = eta_alloc_ssl_session(session);
904:   CAMLreturn(caml_alloc_some(v_session));
905: }
906: 
907: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_set_session(value v_ssl, value v_session)
908: {
909:   CAMLparam2(v_ssl, v_session);
910:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
911:   SSL_SESSION *session = eta_ssl_session_val(v_session);
912:   if (SSL_set_session(ssl, session) != 1) {
913:     caml_failwith("SSL_set_session failed");
914:   }
915:   CAMLreturn(Val_unit);
916: }
917: 
918: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_ssl_session_reused(value v_ssl)
919: {
920:   CAMLparam1(v_ssl);
921:   SSL *ssl = eta_ssl_val(v_ssl);
922:   CAMLreturn(Val_bool(SSL_session_reused(ssl) == 1));
923: }
924: 
925: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_err_peek_error(value v_unit)
926: {
927:   CAMLparam1(v_unit);
928:   unsigned long e = ERR_peek_error();
929:   if (e == 0) {
930:     CAMLreturn(Val_none);
931:   }
932:   char buf[256];
933:   ERR_error_string_n(e, buf, sizeof(buf));
934:   CAMLreturn(caml_alloc_some(caml_copy_string(buf)));
935: }
936: 
937: CAMLprim value eta_openssl_err_clear_error(value v_unit)
938: {
939:   CAMLparam1(v_unit);
940:   ERR_clear_error();
941:   CAMLreturn(Val_unit);
942: }
````

## File: lib/http_eio/h2_server_connection.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Eio-owned h2c server connection loop. *)
 2: 
 3: type t
 4: 
 5: type flow = [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t
 6: 
 7: type time = Server_types.time = {
 8:   sleep : Eta.Duration.t -> unit;
 9:   with_timeout : 'a. Eta.Duration.t -> (unit -> 'a) -> 'a;
10: }
11: 
12: type stats = Server_stats.H2.snapshot = {
13:   active_streams : int;
14:   opened_streams : int;
15:   completed_streams : int;
16:   reset_streams : int;
17:   request_bytes : int;
18:   response_bytes : int;
19:   protocol_errors : int;
20: }
21: 
22: val run :
23:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
24:   clock:[> float Eio.Time.clock_ty ] Eio.Std.r ->
25:   ?time:time ->
26:   flow:flow ->
27:   connection:Server_types.Connection_info.t ->
28:   config:Server_types.Config.t ->
29:   runtime_factory:Server_types.runtime_factory ->
30:   ?on_start:(t -> unit) ->
31:   ?on_close:(stats -> unit) ->
32:   Eta_http.Server.handler ->
33:   unit
34: 
35: val run_h2c :
36:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
37:   clock:[> float Eio.Time.clock_ty ] Eio.Std.r ->
38:   ?time:time ->
39:   flow:flow ->
40:   peer:Eio.Net.Sockaddr.stream ->
41:   config:Server_types.Config.t ->
42:   runtime_factory:Server_types.runtime_factory ->
43:   ?on_start:(t -> unit) ->
44:   ?on_close:(stats -> unit) ->
45:   Eta_http.Server.handler ->
46:   unit
47: 
48: val stats : t -> stats
49: val shutdown : t -> Server_types.shutdown -> unit
````

## File: lib/http/server_error.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type protocol = H1 | H2 | H2c | Unknown
  4: 
  5: type layer =
  6:   | Accept
  7:   | Connection
  8:   | Request_headers
  9:   | Request_body
 10:   | Handler
 11:   | Response_headers
 12:   | Response_body
 13:   | Shutdown
 14: 
 15: type kind =
 16:   | Bad_request of { message : string }
 17:   | Header_invalid of { reason : string }
 18:   | Expectation_failed of { expectation : string }
 19:   | Request_body_too_large of { limit : int; length : int }
 20:   | Request_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 21:   | Stream_admission_rejected of { limit : int }
 22:   | Stream_reset of { code : string; message : string }
 23:   | Connection_closed of { during : layer }
 24:   | Protocol_error of { kind : string; message : string }
 25:   | Handler_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 26:   | Handler_failed of { message : string }
 27:   | Response_body_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
 28:   | Response_write_failed of { message : string }
 29: 
 30: type context = {
 31:   method_ : string;
 32:   target : string;
 33:   protocol : protocol;
 34:   stream_id : int option;
 35: }
 36: 
 37: type t = {
 38:   context : context;
 39:   kind : kind;
 40: }
 41: 
 42: let make ?(protocol = Unknown) ?stream_id ~method_ ~target kind =
 43:   { context = { method_; target; protocol; stream_id }; kind }
 44: 
 45: let protocol_to_string = function
 46:   | H1 -> "h1"
 47:   | H2 -> "h2"
 48:   | H2c -> "h2c"
 49:   | Unknown -> "unknown"
 50: 
 51: let layer_to_string = function
 52:   | Accept -> "accept"
 53:   | Connection -> "connection"
 54:   | Request_headers -> "request_headers"
 55:   | Request_body -> "request_body"
 56:   | Handler -> "handler"
 57:   | Response_headers -> "response_headers"
 58:   | Response_body -> "response_body"
 59:   | Shutdown -> "shutdown"
 60: 
 61: let kind_name = function
 62:   | Bad_request _ -> "Bad_request"
 63:   | Header_invalid _ -> "Header_invalid"
 64:   | Expectation_failed _ -> "Expectation_failed"
 65:   | Request_body_too_large _ -> "Request_body_too_large"
 66:   | Request_timeout _ -> "Request_timeout"
 67:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ -> "Stream_admission_rejected"
 68:   | Stream_reset _ -> "Stream_reset"
 69:   | Connection_closed _ -> "Connection_closed"
 70:   | Protocol_error _ -> "Protocol_error"
 71:   | Handler_timeout _ -> "Handler_timeout"
 72:   | Handler_failed _ -> "Handler_failed"
 73:   | Response_body_timeout _ -> "Response_body_timeout"
 74:   | Response_write_failed _ -> "Response_write_failed"
 75: 
 76: let layer t =
 77:   match t.kind with
 78:   | Bad_request _ | Header_invalid _ | Expectation_failed _
 79:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ ->
 80:       Request_headers
 81:   | Request_body_too_large _ | Request_timeout _ -> Request_body
 82:   | Stream_reset _ | Protocol_error _ -> Connection
 83:   | Connection_closed { during } -> during
 84:   | Handler_timeout _ -> Handler
 85:   | Handler_failed _ -> Handler
 86:   | Response_body_timeout _ -> Response_body
 87:   | Response_write_failed _ -> Response_body
 88: 
 89: let error_class t =
 90:   match t.kind with
 91:   | Bad_request _ -> "bad_request"
 92:   | Header_invalid _ -> "header_invalid"
 93:   | Expectation_failed _ -> "expectation_failed"
 94:   | Request_body_too_large _ -> "request_body_too_large"
 95:   | Request_timeout _ -> "request_timeout"
 96:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ -> "stream_admission_rejected"
 97:   | Stream_reset _ -> "stream_reset"
 98:   | Connection_closed _ -> "connection_closed"
 99:   | Protocol_error _ -> "protocol_error"
100:   | Handler_timeout _ -> "handler_timeout"
101:   | Handler_failed _ -> "handler_failed"
102:   | Response_body_timeout _ -> "response_body_timeout"
103:   | Response_write_failed _ -> "response_write_failed"
104: 
105: let to_status t =
106:   match t.kind with
107:   | Bad_request _ | Header_invalid _ -> Some 400
108:   | Expectation_failed _ -> Some 417
109:   | Request_body_too_large _ -> Some 413
110:   | Request_timeout _ -> Some 408
111:   | Stream_admission_rejected _ -> Some 503
112:   | Handler_timeout _ -> Some 503
113:   | Response_body_timeout _ -> Some 503
114:   | Handler_failed _ | Response_write_failed _ -> Some 500
115:   | Stream_reset _ | Connection_closed _ | Protocol_error _ -> None
116: 
117: let http_protocol = function
118:   | H1 -> Error.H1
119:   | H2 | H2c -> Error.H2
120:   | Unknown -> Error.Unknown
121: 
122: let to_http_layer = function
123:   | Accept | Connection -> Error.Tcp
124:   | Request_headers -> Error.Http_request
125:   | Request_body -> Error.Body_decode
126:   | Handler | Response_headers | Response_body -> Error.Http_response
127:   | Shutdown -> Error.Cancellation
128: 
129: let to_http_error t =
130:   let protocol = http_protocol t.context.protocol in
131:   let uri = t.context.target in
132:   let make kind = Error.make ~protocol ~method_:t.context.method_ ~uri kind in
133:   match t.kind with
134:   | Bad_request { message } ->
135:       make (Error.Connection_protocol_violation { kind = "bad_request"; message })
136:   | Header_invalid { reason } -> make (Error.Header_invalid { reason })
137:   | Expectation_failed { expectation } ->
138:       make
139:         (Error.Connection_protocol_violation
140:            {
141:              kind = "expectation_failed";
142:              message = "unsupported Expect header: " ^ expectation;
143:            })
144:   | Request_body_too_large { limit; length } ->
145:       make (Error.Body_too_large { limit; length })
146:   | Request_timeout { timeout_ms } ->
147:       make (Error.Total_request_timeout { timeout_ms })
148:   | Stream_admission_rejected { limit } ->
149:       make (Error.Stream_admission_rejected { limit })
150:   | Stream_reset { code; message } ->
151:       make (Error.Connection_protocol_violation { kind = code; message })
152:   | Connection_closed { during } ->
153:       make (Error.Connection_closed { during = to_http_layer during })
154:   | Protocol_error { kind; message } ->
155:       make (Error.Connection_protocol_violation { kind; message })
156:   | Handler_timeout { timeout_ms } ->
157:       make (Error.Total_request_timeout { timeout_ms })
158:   | Handler_failed { message } ->
159:       make (Error.Decode_error { codec = "eta-http-server-handler"; message })
160:   | Response_body_timeout { timeout_ms } ->
161:       make (Error.Response_body_idle_timeout { timeout_ms })
162:   | Response_write_failed { message } ->
163:       make
164:         (Error.Connection_protocol_violation
165:            { kind = "response_write_failed"; message })
166: 
167: let pp fmt t =
168:   Format.fprintf fmt
169:     "eta-http-server error=%s method=%s target=%s protocol=%s layer=%s \
170:      error_class=%s"
171:     (kind_name t.kind) t.context.method_ (Redaction.uri t.context.target)
172:     (protocol_to_string t.context.protocol)
173:     (layer_to_string (layer t))
174:     (error_class t);
175:   (match t.context.stream_id with
176:   | None -> ()
177:   | Some stream_id -> Format.fprintf fmt " stream_id=%d" stream_id);
178:   match to_status t with
179:   | None -> ()
180:   | Some status -> Format.fprintf fmt " status=%d" status
181: 
182: let to_string t = Format.asprintf "%a" pp t
````

## File: lib/http/server_error.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Typed eta-http server errors. *)
 2: 
 3: type protocol = H1 | H2 | H2c | Unknown
 4: 
 5: type layer =
 6:   | Accept
 7:   | Connection
 8:   | Request_headers
 9:   | Request_body
10:   | Handler
11:   | Response_headers
12:   | Response_body
13:   | Shutdown
14: 
15: type kind =
16:   | Bad_request of { message : string }
17:   | Header_invalid of { reason : string }
18:   | Expectation_failed of { expectation : string }
19:   | Request_body_too_large of { limit : int; length : int }
20:   | Request_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
21:   | Stream_admission_rejected of { limit : int }
22:   | Stream_reset of { code : string; message : string }
23:   | Connection_closed of { during : layer }
24:   | Protocol_error of { kind : string; message : string }
25:   | Handler_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
26:   | Handler_failed of { message : string }
27:   | Response_body_timeout of { timeout_ms : int option }
28:   | Response_write_failed of { message : string }
29: 
30: type context = {
31:   method_ : string;
32:   target : string;
33:   protocol : protocol;
34:   stream_id : int option;
35: }
36: 
37: type t = {
38:   context : context;
39:   kind : kind;
40: }
41: 
42: val make :
43:   ?protocol:protocol ->
44:   ?stream_id:int ->
45:   method_:string ->
46:   target:string ->
47:   kind ->
48:   t
49: 
50: val protocol_to_string : protocol -> string
51: val layer_to_string : layer -> string
52: val kind_name : kind -> string
53: val layer : t -> layer
54: val error_class : t -> string
55: val to_status : t -> int option
56: val to_http_error : t -> Error.t
57: val pp : Format.formatter -> t -> unit
58: val to_string : t -> string
````

## File: lib/http/server_request_validation.mli
````ocaml
 1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
 2: 
 3: (** Shared server-side request validation for transport adapters. *)
 4: 
 5: type authority = {
 6:   value : string;
 7:   scheme : Url.scheme;
 8:   host : string;
 9:   port : int;
10: }
11: 
12: val connection_scheme : tls:bool -> Url.scheme
13: val valid_authority : string -> bool
14: val parse_authority : scheme:Url.scheme -> string -> authority option
15: 
16: val normalize_h1_target :
17:   connection_scheme:Url.scheme ->
18:   method_:string ->
19:   target:string ->
20:   (string * authority option, string) result
21: 
22: val validate_h1_authority :
23:   connection_scheme:Url.scheme ->
24:   version:Version.t ->
25:   method_:string ->
26:   target:string ->
27:   target_authority:authority option ->
28:   headers:Header.t ->
29:   (unit, string) result
30: 
31: val validate_h2_request :
32:   connection_scheme:Url.scheme ->
33:   method_:string ->
34:   scheme:string ->
35:   target:string ->
36:   authority:string option ->
37:   (unit, string) result
38: 
39: val validate_response_headers :
40:   limits:Server_config.limits -> Header.t -> (unit, string) result
41: 
42: val validate_response_trailers :
43:   limits:Server_config.limits -> Header.t -> (unit, string) result
44: 
45: val validate_h2_request_headers :
46:   limits:Server_config.limits -> (string * string) list -> (unit, string) result
47: 
48: val h2_request_content_length :
49:   (string * string) list -> (int option, string) result
50: 
51: val validate_h2_response_headers :
52:   limits:Server_config.limits -> Header.t -> (unit, string) result
53: 
54: val validate_h2_response_trailers :
55:   limits:Server_config.limits -> Header.t -> (unit, string) result
````

## File: lib/http_eio/eta_http_eio.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: module Client = Client
  4: module Server = Server
  5: module Server_stats = Server_stats
  6: 
  7: let runtime_service ~sw ~net () =
  8:   let clients = Hashtbl.create 4 in
  9:   let mutex = Eio.Mutex.create () in
 10:   let with_lock f =
 11:     Eio.Mutex.lock mutex;
 12:     Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> Eio.Mutex.unlock mutex) f
 13:   in
 14:   let selected_protocol options = options.Eta_http.Client.selected_protocol in
 15:   let max_response_body_bytes options =
 16:     options.Eta_http.Client.max_response_body_bytes
 17:   in
 18:   let ca_file options = options.Eta_http.Client.ca_file in
 19:   let key options =
 20:     (selected_protocol options, max_response_body_bytes options, ca_file options)
 21:   in
 22:   let protocol_unsupported request =
 23:     Eta_http.Error.make ~method_:request.Eta_http.Request.method_
 24:       ~uri:request.uri
 25:       (Connection_protocol_violation
 26:          {
 27:            kind = "eio_http_service";
 28:            message =
 29:              "eta_http_eio runtime service does not implement forced HTTP/2; use Auto for ALPN dispatch";
 30:          })
 31:   in
 32:   let make_client options =
 33:     let max_response_body_bytes = max_response_body_bytes options in
 34:     let ca_file = ca_file options in
 35:     match selected_protocol options with
 36:     | H1 ->
 37:         Some
 38:           (Client.make_h1 ~sw ~net ~max_response_body_bytes ?ca_file ())
 39:     | Auto ->
 40:         Some (Client.make ~sw ~net ~max_response_body_bytes ?ca_file ())
 41:     | H2 -> None
 42:   in
 43:   let client_for options request =
 44:     Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
 45:         let key = key options in
 46:         with_lock (fun () ->
 47:             match Hashtbl.find_opt clients key with
 48:             | Some client -> Ok client
 49:             | None -> (
 50:                 match make_client options with
 51:                 | Some client ->
 52:                     Hashtbl.add clients key client;
 53:                     Ok client
 54:                 | None -> Error (protocol_unsupported request))))
 55:     |> Eta.Effect.bind (function
 56:          | Ok client -> Eta.Effect.pure client
 57:          | Error error -> Eta.Effect.fail error)
 58:   in
 59:   let stats_for options =
 60:     Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
 61:         match Hashtbl.find_opt clients (key options) with
 62:         | Some client -> Some client
 63:         | None -> None)
 64:     |> Eta.Effect.bind (function
 65:          | Some client -> Client.stats client
 66:          | None ->
 67:              Eta.Effect.pure
 68:                {
 69:                  Eta_http.Client.protocol = selected_protocol options;
 70:                  active = 0;
 71:                  idle = 0;
 72:                  capacity = 0;
 73:                  opened = 0;
 74:                  released = 0;
 75:                })
 76:   in
 77:   let shutdown_for options =
 78:     Eta.Effect.sync (fun () ->
 79:         let key = key options in
 80:         with_lock (fun () ->
 81:             match Hashtbl.find_opt clients key with
 82:             | None -> None
 83:             | Some client ->
 84:                 Hashtbl.remove clients key;
 85:                 Some client))
 86:     |> Eta.Effect.bind (function
 87:          | None -> Eta.Effect.unit
 88:          | Some client -> Client.shutdown client)
 89:   in
 90:   Eta_http.Client.runtime_service
 91:     {
 92:       Eta_http.Client.request =
 93:         (fun options request ->
 94:           client_for options request
 95:           |> Eta.Effect.bind (fun client -> Client.request client request));
 96:       stats = stats_for;
 97:       shutdown = shutdown_for;
 98:     }
 99: 
100: module Tls = struct
101:   module Config = Eta_http.Tls.Config
102:   module Eio = Tls_eio
103: end
104: 
105: module Transport = struct
106:   module Alpn = Eta_http.Transport.Alpn
107:   module Alpn_server = Alpn_server
108:   module Connect = Connect
109:   module Dispatch = Eta_http.Transport.Dispatch
110: end
111: 
112: module H1 = struct
113:   module Client = H1_client
114:   module Parse = Eta_http.H1.Parse
115:   module Server_connection = H1_server_connection
116:   module Write = Write
117: end
118: 
119: module H2 = struct
120:   module Admission = Eta_http.H2.Admission
121:   module Connection = Connection
122:   module Frame = Eta_http.H2.Frame
123:   module Informational_filter = Eta_http.H2.Informational_filter
124:   module Multiplexer = Multiplexer
125:   module Server_connection = H2_server_connection
126:   module Security = Eta_http.H2.Security
127:   module Stream_state = Eta_http.H2.Stream_state
128:   module Writer = Writer
129: end
130: 
131: module Ws = struct
132:   module Client = Ws_client
133:   module Codec = Eta_http.Ws.Codec
134: end
````

## File: lib/http_eio/eta_http_eio.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Eio transport adapter for eta-http. *)
 2: 
 3: module Client = Client
 4: module Server = Server
 5: module Server_stats = Server_stats
 6: 
 7: val runtime_service :
 8:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
 9:   net:_ Eio.Net.t ->
10:   unit ->
11:   Eta.Runtime_contract.service
12: (** Eio HTTP client service for {!Eta_http.Client.make_runtime}. Attach this to
13:     an {!Eta_eio.Runtime.create} call with [~services]. *)
14: 
15: module Tls : sig
16:   module Config = Eta_http.Tls.Config
17:   module Eio = Tls_eio
18: end
19: 
20: module Transport : sig
21:   module Alpn = Eta_http.Transport.Alpn
22:   module Alpn_server = Alpn_server
23:   module Connect = Connect
24:   module Dispatch = Eta_http.Transport.Dispatch
25: end
26: 
27: module H1 : sig
28:   module Client = H1_client
29:   module Parse = Eta_http.H1.Parse
30:   module Server_connection = H1_server_connection
31:   module Write = Write
32: end
33: 
34: module H2 : sig
35:   module Admission = Eta_http.H2.Admission
36:   module Connection = Connection
37:   module Frame = Eta_http.H2.Frame
38:   module Informational_filter = Eta_http.H2.Informational_filter
39:   module Multiplexer = Multiplexer
40:   module Server_connection = H2_server_connection
41:   module Security = Eta_http.H2.Security
42:   module Stream_state = Eta_http.H2.Stream_state
43:   module Writer = Writer
44: end
45: 
46: module Ws : sig
47:   module Client = Ws_client
48:   module Codec = Eta_http.Ws.Codec
49: end
````
