## File: lib/http/server_request_validation.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type authority = {
  4:   value : string;
  5:   scheme : Url.scheme;
  6:   host : string;
  7:   port : int;
  8: }
  9: 
 10: let connection_scheme ~tls = if tls then Url.Https else Url.Http
 11: 
 12: let is_hexdig = function
 13:   | '0' .. '9' | 'A' .. 'F' | 'a' .. 'f' -> true
 14:   | _ -> false
 15: 
 16: let is_reg_name_char = function
 17:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | '-' | '.' | '_' | '~' | '!'
 18:   | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' ->
 19:       true
 20:   | _ -> false
 21: 
 22: let is_ipvfuture_tail_char = function
 23:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | ':' | '.' | '-' | '_' | '~'
 24:   | '!' | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' ->
 25:       true
 26:   | _ -> false
 27: 
 28: let valid_port value start finish =
 29:   start < finish
 30:   &&
 31:   let rec loop index acc =
 32:     if index = finish then acc >= 1 && acc <= 65535
 33:     else
 34:       match String.unsafe_get value index with
 35:       | '0' .. '9' as c ->
 36:           let next = (acc * 10) + Char.code c - Char.code '0' in
 37:           next <= 65535 && loop (index + 1) next
 38:       | _ -> false
 39:   in
 40:   loop start 0
 41: 
 42: let valid_reg_name value start finish =
 43:   start < finish
 44:   &&
 45:   let rec loop index =
 46:     if index = finish then true
 47:     else
 48:       match String.unsafe_get value index with
 49:       | '%' ->
 50:           index + 2 < finish
 51:           && is_hexdig (String.unsafe_get value (index + 1))
 52:           && is_hexdig (String.unsafe_get value (index + 2))
 53:           && loop (index + 3)
 54:       | c -> is_reg_name_char c && loop (index + 1)
 55:   in
 56:   loop start
 57: 
 58: let valid_ipvfuture value start finish =
 59:   let rec version_loop index =
 60:     if index >= finish then false
 61:     else
 62:       match String.unsafe_get value index with
 63:       | '.' when index = start + 1 -> false
 64:       | '.' -> tail_loop (index + 1)
 65:       | char -> is_hexdig char && version_loop (index + 1)
 66:   and tail_loop index =
 67:     index < finish
 68:     &&
 69:     let rec tail_chars_loop index =
 70:       index = finish
 71:       ||
 72:       (is_ipvfuture_tail_char (String.unsafe_get value index)
 73:       && tail_chars_loop (index + 1))
 74:     in
 75:     tail_chars_loop index
 76:   in
 77:   start + 2 < finish
 78:   && (match String.unsafe_get value start with 'v' | 'V' -> true | _ -> false)
 79:   && version_loop (start + 1)
 80: 
 81: let valid_ip_literal value start finish =
 82:   start < finish
 83:   &&
 84:   match String.unsafe_get value start with
 85:   | 'v' | 'V' -> valid_ipvfuture value start finish
 86:   | _ -> (
 87:       match Ipaddr.V6.of_string (String.sub value start (finish - start)) with
 88:       | Ok _ -> true
 89:       | Error _ -> false)
 90: 
 91: let rec find_char_string value index finish char =
 92:   if index >= finish then None
 93:   else if Char.equal (String.unsafe_get value index) char then Some index
 94:   else find_char_string value (index + 1) finish char
 95: 
 96: let valid_authority value =
 97:   let len = String.length value in
 98:   if len = 0 then false
 99:   else if Char.equal (String.unsafe_get value 0) '[' then
100:     match find_char_string value 1 len ']' with
101:     | None -> false
102:     | Some close ->
103:         valid_ip_literal value 1 close
104:         &&
105:         if close + 1 = len then true
106:         else
107:           close + 2 < len
108:           && Char.equal (String.unsafe_get value (close + 1)) ':'
109:           && valid_port value (close + 2) len
110:   else
111:     let host_finish =
112:       Option.value ~default:len (find_char_string value 0 len ':')
113:     in
114:     valid_reg_name value 0 host_finish
115:     &&
116:     if host_finish = len then true
117:     else valid_port value (host_finish + 1) len
118: 
119: let parse_authority ~scheme value =
120:   if not (valid_authority value) then None
121:   else
122:     let raw = Url.scheme_to_string scheme ^ "://" ^ value in
123:     match Url.parse raw with
124:     | Error _ -> None
125:     | Ok url ->
126:         Some
127:           {
128:             value = Url.authority url;
129:             scheme;
130:             host = Url.host url;
131:             port = Url.effective_port url;
132:           }
133: 
134: let target_has_fragment target = Option.is_some (String.index_opt target '#')
135: 
136: let normalize_h1_target ~connection_scheme ~method_ ~target =
137:   if String.equal method_ "CONNECT" then
138:     Error "CONNECT is not supported by this server"
139:   else if String.equal target "*" then
140:     if String.equal method_ "OPTIONS" then Ok (target, None)
141:     else Error "asterisk-form request target is only valid with OPTIONS"
142:   else if String.starts_with ~prefix:"/" target then
143:     if target_has_fragment target then
144:       Error "request target must not include fragment"
145:     else Ok (target, None)
146:   else
147:     match Url.parse target with
148:     | Error _ -> Error "invalid request target form"
149:     | Ok url ->
150:         if Option.is_some (Url.fragment url) then
151:           Error "request target must not include fragment"
152:         else if Url.scheme url <> connection_scheme then
153:           Error "absolute-form request target scheme does not match connection"
154:         else
155:           Ok
156:             ( Url.origin_form url,
157:               Some
158:                 {
159:                   value = Url.authority url;
160:                   scheme = Url.scheme url;
161:                   host = Url.host url;
162:                   port = Url.effective_port url;
163:                 } )
164: 
165: let validate_h1_authority ~connection_scheme ~version ~method_:_ ~target:_
166:     ~target_authority ~headers =
167:   match Header.get_all "host" headers with
168:   | [] when version = Version.H1_1 ->
169:       Error "HTTP/1.1 request is missing Host header"
170:   | [] -> Ok ()
171:   | [ host ] ->
172:       let scheme =
173:         match target_authority with
174:         | Some authority -> authority.scheme
175:         | None -> connection_scheme
176:       in
177:       (match parse_authority ~scheme host with
178:       | None -> Error "invalid Host header"
179:       | Some host_authority -> (
180:           match target_authority with
181:           | Some authority
182:             when (not (String.equal host_authority.host authority.host))
183:                  || host_authority.port <> authority.port ->
184:               Error
185:                 "absolute-form request target authority conflicts with Host \
186:                  header"
187:           | None | Some _ -> Ok ()))
188:   | _ -> Error "multiple Host headers"
189: 
190: let scheme_of_h2_string = function
191:   | "http" -> Some Url.Http
192:   | "https" -> Some Url.Https
193:   | _ -> None
194: 
195: let valid_method_token method_ =
196:   let len = String.length method_ in
197:   len > 0
198:   &&
199:   let rec loop index =
200:     index = len
201:     ||
202:     match String.unsafe_get method_ index with
203:     | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | '!' | '#' | '$' | '%' | '&'
204:     | '\'' | '*' | '+' | '-' | '.' | '^' | '_' | '`' | '|' | '~' ->
205:         loop (index + 1)
206:     | _ -> false
207:   in
208:   loop 0
209: 
210: let valid_origin_form_char = function
211:   | 'A' .. 'Z' | 'a' .. 'z' | '0' .. '9' | '-' | '.' | '_' | '~' | '!'
212:   | '$' | '&' | '\'' | '(' | ')' | '*' | '+' | ',' | ';' | '=' | ':' | '@'
213:   | '/' ->
214:       true
215:   | _ -> false
216: 
217: let valid_query_char = function
218:   | '?' -> true
219:   | char -> valid_origin_form_char char
220: 
221: let valid_pct_encoded value index finish =
222:   index + 2 < finish
223:   && is_hexdig (String.unsafe_get value (index + 1))
224:   && is_hexdig (String.unsafe_get value (index + 2))
225: 
226: let valid_h2_origin_form target =
227:   let len = String.length target in
228:   len > 0
229:   && Char.equal (String.unsafe_get target 0) '/'
230:   &&
231:   let rec loop in_query index =
232:     if index = len then true
233:     else
234:       match String.unsafe_get target index with
235:       | '%' -> valid_pct_encoded target index len && loop in_query (index + 3)
236:       | '?' -> loop true (index + 1)
237:       | char ->
238:           (if in_query then valid_query_char char else valid_origin_form_char char)
239:           && loop in_query (index + 1)
240:   in
241:   loop false 1
242: 
243: let validate_h2_path ~method_ ~target =
244:   if String.equal method_ "CONNECT" then
245:     Error "CONNECT is not supported by this server"
246:   else if String.equal target "*" then
247:     if String.equal method_ "OPTIONS" then Ok ()
248:     else Error "asterisk-form request target is only valid with OPTIONS"
249:   else if String.equal target "" then Error "missing HTTP/2 :path"
250:   else if not (String.starts_with ~prefix:"/" target) then
251:     Error "invalid HTTP/2 :path"
252:   else if target_has_fragment target then
253:     Error "request target must not include fragment"
254:   else if not (valid_h2_origin_form target) then Error "invalid HTTP/2 :path"
255:   else Ok ()
256: 
257: let validate_h2_request ~connection_scheme ~method_ ~scheme ~target ~authority =
258:   if not (valid_method_token method_) then Error "invalid HTTP/2 :method"
259:   else
260:     match scheme_of_h2_string scheme with
261:     | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 :scheme"
262:     | Some scheme when scheme <> connection_scheme ->
263:         Error "HTTP/2 :scheme does not match connection"
264:     | Some scheme -> (
265:         match authority with
266:         | None -> Error "missing HTTP/2 :authority"
267:         | Some authority -> (
268:             match parse_authority ~scheme authority with
269:             | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 :authority"
270:             | Some _ -> validate_h2_path ~method_ ~target))
271: 
272: let header_block_bytes headers =
273:   List.fold_left
274:     (fun total (name, value) ->
275:       total + String.length name + String.length value + 4)
276:     0 headers
277: 
278: let validate_header_block ~max_bytes ~max_headers ~kind headers =
279:   match Header.validate headers with
280:   | Some _ -> Error ("invalid " ^ kind ^ " header")
281:   | None ->
282:       let count = List.length headers in
283:       if count > max_headers then
284:         Error
285:           (Printf.sprintf "%s header count exceeds %d" kind max_headers)
286:       else
287:         let bytes = header_block_bytes headers in
288:         if bytes > max_bytes then
289:           Error
290:             (Printf.sprintf "%s header section exceeds %d bytes" kind max_bytes)
291:         else Ok ()
292: 
293: let validate_response_headers ~(limits : Server_config.limits) headers =
294:   validate_header_block ~max_bytes:limits.max_response_header_bytes
295:     ~max_headers:limits.max_response_headers ~kind:"response" headers
296: 
297: let validate_response_trailers ~(limits : Server_config.limits) trailers =
298:   validate_header_block ~max_bytes:limits.max_trailer_bytes
299:     ~max_headers:limits.max_trailers ~kind:"response trailer" trailers
300: 
301: let has_uppercase value =
302:   String.exists
303:     (function
304:       | 'A' .. 'Z' -> true
305:       | _ -> false)
306:     value
307: 
308: let h2_pseudo_header = function
309:   | ":method" -> Some `Method
310:   | ":scheme" -> Some `Scheme
311:   | ":authority" -> Some `Authority
312:   | ":path" -> Some `Path
313:   | _ -> None
314: 
315: let is_h2_pseudo_header name =
316:   String.length name > 0 && Char.equal (String.unsafe_get name 0) ':'
317: 
318: let validate_h2_header_value kind value =
319:   match Header.validate_value value with
320:   | Some _ -> Error ("invalid " ^ kind ^ " value")
321:   | None -> Ok ()
322: 
323: let validate_h2_pseudo_header_value name kind value =
324:   match validate_h2_header_value name value with
325:   | Error _ as error -> error
326:   | Ok () -> (
327:       match kind with
328:       | `Method ->
329:           if valid_method_token value then Ok ()
330:           else Error "invalid HTTP/2 :method"
331:       | `Path ->
332:           if valid_h2_origin_form value || String.equal value "*" then Ok ()
333:           else Error "invalid HTTP/2 :path"
334:       | `Scheme | `Authority -> Ok ())
335: 
336: let h2_forbidden_connection_header = function
337:   | "connection" | "keep-alive" | "proxy-connection" | "transfer-encoding"
338:   | "upgrade" ->
339:       true
340:   | _ -> false
341: 
342: let validate_h2_request_connection_header name value =
343:   if h2_forbidden_connection_header name then
344:     Error ("HTTP/2 connection-specific header " ^ name ^ " is forbidden")
345:   else if
346:     String.equal name "te"
347:     && not (Eta.String_helpers.trim_equal_ascii_ci value "trailers")
348:   then Error "HTTP/2 TE header may only contain trailers"
349:   else Ok ()
350: 
351: let validate_h2_response_connection_header name =
352:   if h2_forbidden_connection_header name || String.equal name "te" then
353:     Error ("HTTP/2 response header " ^ name ^ " is forbidden")
354:   else Ok ()
355: 
356: let digit = function
357:   | '0' .. '9' as c -> Char.code c - Char.code '0'
358:   | _ -> -1
359: 
360: let parse_content_length value =
361:   let trimmed = Eta.String_helpers.trim value in
362:   let len = String.length trimmed in
363:   if len = 0 then Error ("invalid HTTP/2 content-length " ^ value)
364:   else
365:     let rec loop index acc =
366:       if index = len then Ok acc
367:       else
368:         let digit = digit (String.unsafe_get trimmed index) in
369:         if digit < 0 then Error ("invalid HTTP/2 content-length " ^ value)
370:         else if
371:           acc > max_int / 10 || (acc = max_int / 10 && digit > max_int mod 10)
372:         then Error ("invalid HTTP/2 content-length " ^ value)
373:         else loop (index + 1) ((acc * 10) + digit)
374:     in
375:     loop 0 0
376: 
377: let h2_request_content_length headers =
378:   let values =
379:     headers
380:     |> List.filter_map (fun (name, value) ->
381:            if Eta.String_helpers.trim_equal_ascii_ci "content-length" name then
382:              Some value
383:            else None)
384:   in
385:   match values with
386:   | [] -> Ok None
387:   | [ value ] -> Result.map Option.some (parse_content_length value)
388:   | _ -> Error "multiple HTTP/2 content-length headers"
389: 
390: let validate_h2_host_authority ~scheme authority host =
391:   match scheme_of_h2_string scheme with
392:   | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 :scheme"
393:   | Some scheme -> (
394:       match parse_authority ~scheme authority with
395:       | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 :authority"
396:       | Some authority -> (
397:           match parse_authority ~scheme host with
398:           | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 host header"
399:           | Some host ->
400:               if
401:                 String.equal host.host authority.host
402:                 && host.port = authority.port
403:               then Ok ()
404:               else Error "HTTP/2 host header conflicts with :authority"))
405: 
406: let validate_h2_request_header_values headers =
407:   let rec loop regular_seen method_seen scheme authority path_seen host =
408:     function
409:     | [] ->
410:         if not method_seen then Error "missing HTTP/2 :method pseudo-header"
411:         else (
412:           match (scheme, authority) with
413:           | None, _ -> Error "missing HTTP/2 :scheme pseudo-header"
414:           | Some _, None ->
415:               Error "missing HTTP/2 :authority pseudo-header"
416:           | Some scheme, Some authority ->
417:               if not path_seen then Error "missing HTTP/2 :path pseudo-header"
418:               else (
419:                 match host with
420:                 | None -> Ok ()
421:                 | Some host -> validate_h2_host_authority ~scheme authority host))
422:     | (name, value) :: rest -> (
423:         if is_h2_pseudo_header name then
424:           if regular_seen then
425:             Error "HTTP/2 pseudo-header appears after regular header"
426:           else
427:             match h2_pseudo_header name with
428:             | None -> Error "invalid HTTP/2 pseudo-header"
429:             | Some kind ->
430:                 let duplicate =
431:                   match kind with
432:                   | `Method -> method_seen
433:                   | `Scheme -> Option.is_some scheme
434:                   | `Authority -> Option.is_some authority
435:                   | `Path -> path_seen
436:                 in
437:                 if duplicate then
438:                   Error ("duplicate HTTP/2 " ^ name ^ " pseudo-header")
439:                 else
440:                   match validate_h2_pseudo_header_value name kind value with
441:                   | Error _ as error -> error
442:                   | Ok () ->
443:                       let method_seen = method_seen || kind = `Method in
444:                       let scheme =
445:                         match kind with `Scheme -> Some value | _ -> scheme
446:                       in
447:                       let authority =
448:                         match kind with
449:                         | `Authority -> Some value
450:                         | _ -> authority
451:                       in
452:                       let path_seen = path_seen || kind = `Path in
453:                       loop regular_seen method_seen scheme authority path_seen
454:                         host rest
455:         else
456:           match Header.validate_header (name, value) with
457:           | Some _ -> Error "invalid request header"
458:           | None when has_uppercase name ->
459:               Error "uppercase HTTP/2 request header name"
460:           | None -> (
461:               match validate_h2_request_connection_header name value with
462:               | Error _ as error -> error
463:               | Ok () ->
464:                   if String.equal name "host" then
465:                     match host with
466:                     | Some _ -> Error "multiple HTTP/2 host headers"
467:                     | None ->
468:                         loop true method_seen scheme authority path_seen
469:                           (Some value) rest
470:                   else
471:                     loop true method_seen scheme authority path_seen host rest))
472:   in
473:   loop false false None None false None headers
474: 
475: let validate_h2_request_headers ~(limits : Server_config.limits) headers =
476:   match validate_h2_request_header_values headers with
477:   | Error _ as error -> error
478:   | Ok () -> (
479:       match h2_request_content_length headers with
480:       | Error _ as error -> error
481:       | Ok _ ->
482:           let count = List.length headers in
483:           if count > limits.max_request_headers then
484:             Error
485:               (Printf.sprintf "request header count exceeds %d"
486:                  limits.max_request_headers)
487:           else
488:             let bytes = header_block_bytes headers in
489:             if bytes > limits.max_request_header_bytes then
490:               Error
491:                 (Printf.sprintf "request header section exceeds %d bytes"
492:                    limits.max_request_header_bytes)
493:             else Ok ())
494: 
495: let validate_h2_response_header_values ?(trailers = false) ~kind headers =
496:   let rec loop = function
497:     | [] -> Ok ()
498:     | (name, value) :: rest -> (
499:         match Header.validate_header (name, value) with
500:         | Some _ -> Error ("invalid " ^ kind ^ " header")
501:         | None when is_h2_pseudo_header name ->
502:             Error ("HTTP/2 " ^ kind ^ " pseudo-header is not user controlled")
503:         | None when has_uppercase name ->
504:             Error ("uppercase HTTP/2 " ^ kind ^ " header name")
505:         | None -> (
506:             let name = Header.normalize_name name in
507:             if trailers && Chunked.forbidden_trailer_name name then
508:               Error ("forbidden HTTP/2 response trailer " ^ name)
509:             else (
510:               match validate_h2_response_connection_header name with
511:               | Error _ as error -> error
512:               | Ok () -> loop rest)))
513:   in
514:   loop headers
515: 
516: let validate_h2_response_header_block ?(trailers = false) ~max_bytes
517:     ~max_headers ~kind headers =
518:   let headers = Header.to_list headers in
519:   match validate_h2_response_header_values ~trailers ~kind headers with
520:   | Error _ as error -> error
521:   | Ok () ->
522:       let count = List.length headers in
523:       if count > max_headers then
524:         Error
525:           (Printf.sprintf "%s header count exceeds %d" kind max_headers)
526:       else
527:         let bytes = header_block_bytes headers in
528:         if bytes > max_bytes then
529:           Error
530:             (Printf.sprintf "%s header section exceeds %d bytes" kind max_bytes)
531:         else Ok ()
532: 
533: let validate_h2_response_headers ~(limits : Server_config.limits) headers =
534:   validate_h2_response_header_block
535:     ~max_bytes:limits.max_response_header_bytes
536:     ~max_headers:limits.max_response_headers ~kind:"response" headers
537: 
538: let validate_h2_response_trailers ~(limits : Server_config.limits) trailers =
539:   validate_h2_response_header_block ~trailers:true
540:     ~max_bytes:limits.max_trailer_bytes
541:     ~max_headers:limits.max_trailers ~kind:"response trailer" trailers
````

````
 1: (test
````

## File: lib/http_eio/tls/tls_eio.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type config = Config.t
  4: type server_config = Config.server
  5: type server_context = { ctx : Openssl.ctx }
  6: 
  7: type epoch = {
  8:   alpn_protocol : string option;
  9:   sni : string option;
 10:   peer_certificate_verified : bool;
 11: }
 12: 
 13: type flow =
 14:   [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty | `Eta_tls ] Eio.Resource.t
 15: 
 16: module type EIO_FLOW = Eta_eio.Host.FLOW
 17: 
 18: module Default_eio_flow : EIO_FLOW = Eio.Flow
 19: 
 20: type 'a t_rec = {
 21:   ssl : Openssl.ssl;
 22:   flow : 'a;
 23:   eio_flow : (module EIO_FLOW);
 24:   ssl_mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 25:   handshake_mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 26:   read_mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 27:   write_mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 28:   progress_mutex : Eio.Mutex.t;
 29:   progress_condition : Eio.Condition.t;
 30:   sni : string option;
 31:   mutable peer_certificate_verified : bool;
 32:   mutable handshake_done : bool;
 33:   mutable closed : bool;
 34:   mutable rbio_epoch : int;
 35: }
 36: 
 37: type t = [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t t_rec
 38: 
 39: type tls_state = St : t -> tls_state
 40: 
 41: type (_, _, _) Eio.Resource.pi += Tls_state : ('a, 'a -> tls_state, [> `Eta_tls ]) Eio.Resource.pi
 42: 
 43: let with_ssl t f = Eio.Mutex.use_rw ~protect:false t.ssl_mutex f
 44: let with_flow_read t f = Eio.Mutex.use_ro t.read_mutex f
 45: let with_flow_write t f = Eio.Mutex.use_ro t.write_mutex f
 46: 
 47: (* Post-handshake TLS ownership invariants:
 48:    - only [feed_bio] reads ciphertext from the raw flow and feeds rbio;
 49:    - [single_write] never performs raw reads, even when SSL_write wants input;
 50:    - [single_read] drains pending plaintext before blocking on raw input;
 51:    - every rbio feed or close wakes writers parked on SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ. *)
 52: 
 53: let notify_rbio_progress t =
 54:   Eio.Mutex.use_rw ~protect:false t.progress_mutex (fun () ->
 55:       t.rbio_epoch <- t.rbio_epoch + 1);
 56:   Eio.Condition.broadcast t.progress_condition
 57: 
 58: let notify_closed t = Eio.Condition.broadcast t.progress_condition
 59: 
 60: let rbio_epoch t = Eio.Mutex.use_ro t.progress_mutex (fun () -> t.rbio_epoch)
 61: 
 62: let wait_for_rbio_progress t epoch =
 63:   Eio.Mutex.lock t.progress_mutex;
 64:   Fun.protect
 65:     ~finally:(fun () -> Eio.Mutex.unlock t.progress_mutex)
 66:     (fun () ->
 67:       while (not t.closed) && t.rbio_epoch = epoch do
 68:         Eio.Condition.await t.progress_condition t.progress_mutex
 69:       done;
 70:       if t.closed then raise End_of_file)
 71: 
 72: let debug_io direction storage ~storage_off ~display_off ~len =
 73:   match Sys.getenv_opt "ETA_TLS_DEBUG" with
 74:   | None -> ()
 75:   | Some _ ->
 76:       let dump_n = min len 32 in
 77:       let hex = Buffer.create (dump_n * 3) in
 78:       for i = 0 to dump_n - 1 do
 79:         let value = Char.code (Bigarray.Array1.get storage (storage_off + i)) in
 80:         Buffer.add_char hex (Eta.String_helpers.lower_hex_digit (value lsr 4));
 81:         Buffer.add_char hex (Eta.String_helpers.lower_hex_digit (value land 0xf));
 82:         Buffer.add_char hex ' '
 83:       done;
 84:       Printf.eprintf "[tls] %s rc=%d off=%d head: %s\n%!" direction len
 85:         display_off (Buffer.contents hex)
 86: 
 87: (* Helper: read encrypted data from underlying flow into OpenSSL's read BIO. *)
 88: let feed_bio t =
 89:   let module Flow = (val t.eio_flow : EIO_FLOW) in
 90:   let buf = Cstruct.create 32768 in
 91:   with_flow_read t (fun () ->
 92:       let n = Flow.single_read t.flow buf in
 93:       if n = 0 then raise End_of_file;
 94:       let rec write_all off len =
 95:         if len > 0 then (
 96:           let written =
 97:             with_ssl t (fun () ->
 98:                 Openssl.bio_write t.ssl (Cstruct.to_bigarray buf) off len)
 99:           in
100:           if written <= 0 then failwith "TLS BIO write failed";
101:           write_all (off + written) (len - written))
102:       in
103:       write_all 0 n;
104:       notify_rbio_progress t)
105: 
106: (* Helper: drain OpenSSL's write BIO to the underlying flow. *)
107: let drain_bio t =
108:   let module Flow = (val t.eio_flow : EIO_FLOW) in
109:   with_flow_write t (fun () ->
110:       let drained = ref false in
111:       let rec loop () =
112:         let pending = with_ssl t (fun () -> Openssl.bio_write_pending t.ssl) in
113:         if pending > 0 then (
114:           let buf = Cstruct.create pending in
115:           let n =
116:             with_ssl t (fun () ->
117:                 Openssl.bio_read t.ssl (Cstruct.to_bigarray buf) 0 pending)
118:           in
119:           if n > 0 then (
120:             drained := true;
121:             Flow.write t.flow [ Cstruct.sub buf 0 n ];
122:             loop ()))
123:       in
124:       loop ();
125:       !drained)
126: 
127: (* Drive the TLS handshake to completion. *)
128: let do_handshake t =
129:   Eio.Mutex.use_rw ~protect:false t.handshake_mutex (fun () ->
130:       if not t.handshake_done then
131:         let rec loop () =
132:           let rc = with_ssl t (fun () -> Openssl.handshake t.ssl) in
133:           match rc with
134:           | Openssl.Handshake_ok ->
135:               ignore (drain_bio t);
136:               t.handshake_done <- true
137:           | Openssl.Handshake_error code -> (
138:               ignore (drain_bio t);
139:               if code = 2 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ *) then (
140:                 feed_bio t;
141:                 loop ())
142:               else if code = 3 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE *) then (
143:                 if not (drain_bio t) then Eio.Fiber.yield ();
144:                 loop ())
145:               else (
146:                 match Openssl.err_peek_error () with
147:                 | Some msg ->
148:                     Openssl.err_clear_error ();
149:                     failwith ("TLS handshake: " ^ msg)
150:                 | None ->
151:                     failwith
152:                       ("TLS handshake failed (code " ^ string_of_int code ^ ")")
153:                 ))
154:         in
155:         loop ())
156: 
157: let shutdown_send t =
158:   if not t.closed then (
159:     let _ = with_ssl t (fun () -> Openssl.shutdown t.ssl) in
160:     ignore (drain_bio t);
161:     t.closed <- true;
162:     notify_closed t)
163: 
164: let close_underlying t = try Eio.Flow.close t.flow with _ -> ()
165: 
166: let close t =
167:   t.closed <- true;
168:   notify_closed t;
169:   close_underlying t
170: 
171: module Flow_impl = struct
172:   type nonrec t = t
173: 
174:   let read_methods = []
175: 
176:   let single_read t buf =
177:     (* No coarse read/write lock here: reads and writes on a TLS flow must be
178:        able to proceed concurrently for full-duplex protocols like HTTP/2. The
179:        shared SSL object is serialized by [ssl_mutex] (via [with_ssl]); socket
180:        reads are serialized by [read_mutex] in [feed_bio] and socket writes by
181:        [write_mutex] in [drain_bio]. A coarse per-direction lock here would let
182:        a reader parked in [feed_bio] starve the writer (and vice versa),
183:        deadlocking large H2 transfers. *)
184:     if t.closed then raise End_of_file;
185:     if not t.handshake_done then do_handshake t;
186:     let { Cstruct.off = display_off; len } = buf in
187:     let storage = Cstruct.to_bigarray buf in
188:     let rec loop () =
189:       let rc = with_ssl t (fun () -> Openssl.read t.ssl storage 0 len) in
190:       if rc > 0 then (
191:         debug_io "read" storage ~storage_off:0 ~display_off ~len:rc;
192:         rc)
193:       else if rc = 0 then (
194:         t.closed <- true;
195:         raise End_of_file)
196:       else (
197:         let code = -rc in
198:         ignore (drain_bio t);
199:         if code = 2 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ *) then (
200:           feed_bio t;
201:           loop ())
202:         else if code = 3 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE *) then (
203:           if not (drain_bio t) then Eio.Fiber.yield ();
204:           loop ())
205:         else if code = 6 (* SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN *) then (
206:           t.closed <- true;
207:           raise End_of_file)
208:         else (
209:           match Openssl.err_peek_error () with
210:           | Some msg ->
211:               Openssl.err_clear_error ();
212:               failwith ("TLS read: " ^ msg)
213:           | None ->
214:               failwith ("TLS read failed (code " ^ string_of_int code ^ ")")))
215:     in
216:     loop ()
217: 
218:   let single_write t bufs =
219:     if t.closed then 0
220:     else (
221:       if not t.handshake_done then do_handshake t;
222:       let total = ref 0 in
223:       List.iter
224:         (fun buf ->
225:           let { Cstruct.off = display_off; len } = buf in
226:           let storage = Cstruct.to_bigarray buf in
227:           let rec write_buf offset length =
228:             if length > 0 then (
229:               let epoch = rbio_epoch t in
230:               let rc =
231:                 with_ssl t (fun () -> Openssl.write t.ssl storage offset length)
232:               in
233:               if rc > 0 then (
234:                 debug_io "write" storage ~storage_off:offset
235:                   ~display_off:(display_off + offset) ~len:rc;
236:                 total := !total + rc;
237:                 ignore (drain_bio t);
238:                 if rc < length then write_buf (offset + rc) (length - rc))
239:               else (
240:                 let code = -rc in
241:                 ignore (drain_bio t);
242:                 if code = 2 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ *) then (
243:                   wait_for_rbio_progress t epoch;
244:                   write_buf offset length)
245:                 else if code = 3 (* SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE *) then (
246:                   if not (drain_bio t) then Eio.Fiber.yield ();
247:                   write_buf offset length)
248:                 else if code = 6 (* SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN *) then
249:                   raise End_of_file
250:                 else (
251:                   match Openssl.err_peek_error () with
252:                   | Some msg ->
253:                       Openssl.err_clear_error ();
254:                       failwith ("TLS write: " ^ msg)
255:                   | None ->
256:                       failwith
257:                         ("TLS write failed (code " ^ string_of_int code ^ ")"))))
258:           in
259:           write_buf 0 len)
260:         bufs;
261:       !total)
262: 
263:   let copy t ~src = Eio.Flow.Pi.simple_copy ~single_write t ~src
264: 
265:   let shutdown t cmd =
266:     match cmd with
267:     | `Send -> shutdown_send t
268:     | `All ->
269:         Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> close_underlying t) (fun () ->
270:             shutdown_send t)
271:     | `Receive -> ()
272: end
273: 
274: let ops =
275:   Eio.Resource.handler (
276:     Eio.Resource.H (Tls_state, (fun t -> St t))
277:     :: Eio.Resource.H (Eio.Resource.Close, close)
278:     :: Eio.Resource.bindings (Eio.Flow.Pi.two_way (module Flow_impl))
279:   )
280: 
281: let flow_module = function
282:   | None -> (module Default_eio_flow : EIO_FLOW)
283:   | Some host_eio ->
284:       let module Flow = (val Eta_eio.Host.flow host_eio : Eta_eio.Host.FLOW) in
285:       (module Flow : EIO_FLOW)
286: 
287: let make_tls_state ?host_eio ?sni ?(peer_certificate_verified = false) ssl flow =
288:   {
289:     ssl;
290:     flow;
291:     eio_flow = flow_module host_eio;
292:     ssl_mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
293:     handshake_mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
294:     read_mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
295:     write_mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
296:     progress_mutex = Eio.Mutex.create ();
297:     progress_condition = Eio.Condition.create ();
298:     sni;
299:     peer_certificate_verified;
300:     handshake_done = false;
301:     closed = false;
302:     rbio_epoch = 0;
303:   }
304: 
305: let epoch_of_state t =
306:   {
307:     alpn_protocol = Openssl.get_alpn_selected t.ssl;
308:     sni = t.sni;
309:     peer_certificate_verified = t.peer_certificate_verified;
310:   }
311: 
312: let client_of_flow ?host_eio (config : config) ?host
313:     (flow : [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t) :
314:     flow =
315:   let hostname =
316:     match host with
317:     | Some h -> Some (Domain_name.to_string h)
318:     | None -> Option.map Domain_name.to_string (Config.peer_name config)
319:   in
320:   let ip = Option.map Ipaddr.to_string (Config.ip config) in
321:   let ctx = Openssl.create_ctx () in
322:   (match Config.ca_file config with
323:    | Some path -> Openssl.ctx_load_ca ctx path
324:    | None -> ());
325:   let ssl =
326:     Openssl.create_ssl ctx ~hostname ~ip
327:       ~alpn_protocols:(Config.alpn_protocols config)
328:   in
329:   let t = make_tls_state ?host_eio ?sni:hostname ssl flow in
330:   do_handshake t;
331:   let verify = Openssl.get_verify_result ssl in
332:   if verify <> 0 then
333:     failwith ("TLS certificate verification failed (code " ^ string_of_int verify ^ ")");
334:   t.peer_certificate_verified <- true;
335:   (match Sys.getenv_opt "ETA_TLS_DEBUG" with
336:    | Some _ ->
337:        Printf.eprintf "[tls_eio] handshake done, alpn=%s\n%!"
338:          (match Openssl.get_alpn_selected ssl with Some p -> p | None -> "<none>")
339:    | None -> ());
340:   (Eio.Resource.T (t, ops) :> flow)
341: 
342: let server_context (config : server_config) =
343:   let certificates =
344:     Config.server_certificates config
345:     |> List.map (fun certificate ->
346:            Openssl.server_certificate
347:              ~server_name:(Config.server_certificate_name certificate)
348:              ~certificate_chain_file:
349:                (Config.server_certificate_chain_file certificate)
350:              ~private_key_file:
351:                (Config.server_certificate_private_key_file certificate))
352:   in
353:   {
354:     ctx =
355:       Openssl.create_server_ctx
356:         ~certificate_chain_file:(Config.certificate_chain_file config)
357:         ~private_key_file:(Config.private_key_file config)
358:         ~certificates
359:         ~require_sni_match:(Config.require_sni_match config)
360:         ~alpn_protocols:(Config.server_alpn_protocols config)
361:         ();
362:   }
363: 
364: let server_of_flow_with_context ?host_eio context
365:     (flow : [ Eio.Flow.two_way_ty | Eio.Resource.close_ty ] Eio.Resource.t) :
366:     flow * epoch =
367:   let ssl = Openssl.create_server_ssl context.ctx in
368:   let t = make_tls_state ?host_eio ssl flow in
369:   do_handshake t;
370:   let t = { t with sni = Openssl.get_servername ssl } in
371:   let epoch = epoch_of_state t in
372:   ((Eio.Resource.T (t, ops) :> flow), epoch)
373: 
374: let server_of_flow ?host_eio config flow =
375:   server_of_flow_with_context ?host_eio (server_context config) flow
376: 
377: let epoch flow =
378:   try
379:     let (Eio.Resource.T (t, handler)) = flow in
380:     let St t = Eio.Resource.get handler Tls_state t in
381:     if not t.handshake_done then Error ()
382:     else Ok (epoch_of_state t)
383:   with _ -> Error ()
384: 
385: let alpn_protocol flow =
386:   match epoch flow with
387:   | Ok e -> e.alpn_protocol
388:   | Error () -> None
````

## File: lib/http_eio/server_types.ml
````ocaml
  1: (* Copyright (c) 2026 Eta contributors. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT *)
  2: 
  3: type shutdown =
  4:   | Graceful of Eta.Duration.t
  5:   | Immediate
  6: 
  7: type domain_policy =
  8:   | Single_domain
  9:   | Recommended
 10:   | Additional of int
 11: 
 12: type time = {
 13:   sleep : Eta.Duration.t -> unit;
 14:   with_timeout : 'a. Eta.Duration.t -> (unit -> 'a) -> 'a;
 15: }
 16: 
 17: let live_time clock =
 18:   {
 19:     sleep =
 20:       (fun duration ->
 21:         Eio.Time.sleep clock (Eta.Duration.to_seconds_float duration));
 22:     with_timeout =
 23:       (fun duration f ->
 24:         Eio.Time.with_timeout_exn clock
 25:           (Eta.Duration.to_seconds_float duration)
 26:           f);
 27:   }
 28: 
 29: module Connection_info = struct
 30:   type t = {
 31:     id : string;
 32:     peer : Eta_http.Server.Request.peer;
 33:     protocol : Eta_http.Server.Error.protocol;
 34:     tls : bool;
 35:     alpn_protocol : string option;
 36:   }
 37: end
 38: 
 39: type runtime_factory =
 40:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
 41:   connection:Connection_info.t ->
 42:   unit ->
 43:   Eta_http.Server.Error.t Eta.Runtime.t
 44: 
 45: module Config = struct
 46:   type t = {
 47:     max_connections : int;
 48:     backlog : int;
 49:     read_buffer_size : int;
 50:     command_queue_capacity : int;
 51:     tls_handshake_timeout : Eta.Duration.t;
 52:     server : Eta_http.Server.Config.t;
 53:     h2_config : H2.Config.t;
 54:     h2_security_config : Eta_http.H2.Security.config option;
 55:   }
 56: 
 57:   let default =
 58:     {
 59:       max_connections = 1024;
 60:       backlog = 128;
 61:       read_buffer_size = 64 * 1024;
 62:       command_queue_capacity = 1024;
 63:       tls_handshake_timeout = Eta.Duration.seconds 10;
 64:       server = Eta_http.Server.Config.default;
 65:       h2_config = { H2.Config.default with max_concurrent_streams = 128l };
 66:       h2_security_config = None;
 67:     }
 68: 
 69:   let field name = "Eta_http_eio.Server.Config." ^ name
 70: 
 71:   let require_positive name value =
 72:     if value <= 0 then invalid_arg (field name ^ " must be > 0")
 73: 
 74:   let require_positive_duration name value =
 75:     if Eta.Duration.is_zero value then
 76:       invalid_arg (field name ^ " must be > 0")
 77: 
 78:   let require_positive_int32 name value =
 79:     if Int32.compare value 0l <= 0 then
 80:       invalid_arg (field name ^ " must be > 0")
 81: 
 82:   let require_non_negative_int32 name value =
 83:     if Int32.compare value 0l < 0 then
 84:       invalid_arg (field name ^ " must be >= 0")
 85: 
 86:   let max_h2_concurrent_streams = 4096l
 87: 
 88:   let validate_h2_max_concurrent_streams name value =
 89:     require_positive_int32 name value;
 90:     if Int32.compare value max_h2_concurrent_streams > 0 then
 91:       invalid_arg
 92:         (field name ^ " must be <= "
 93:         ^ Int32.to_string max_h2_concurrent_streams)
 94: 
 95:   let validate_h2_frame_size name value =
 96:     if value < 0x4000 || value > 0xffffff then
 97:       invalid_arg (field name ^ " must be between 16384 and 16777215")
 98: 
 99:   let validate_h2_config (config : H2.Config.t) =
100:     validate_h2_frame_size "h2_config.read_buffer_size"
101:       config.H2.Config.read_buffer_size;
102:     require_positive "h2_config.request_body_buffer_size"
103:       config.request_body_buffer_size;
104:     require_positive "h2_config.response_body_buffer_size"
105:       config.response_body_buffer_size;
106:     validate_h2_max_concurrent_streams "h2_config.max_concurrent_streams"
107:       config.max_concurrent_streams;
108:     require_non_negative_int32 "h2_config.initial_window_size"
109:       config.initial_window_size
110: 
111:   let validate_h2_security_config
112:       (config : Eta_http.H2.Security.config) =
113:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_settings_per_connection"
114:       config.max_settings_per_connection;
115:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_goaway_per_connection"
116:       config.max_goaway_per_connection;
117:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_rst_stream_per_connection"
118:       config.max_rst_stream_per_connection;
119:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_ping_per_connection"
120:       config.max_ping_per_connection;
121:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_empty_data_frames_per_connection"
122:       config.max_empty_data_frames_per_connection;
123:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_window_update_per_connection"
124:       config.max_window_update_per_connection;
125:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_hpack_block_bytes"
126:       config.max_hpack_block_bytes;
127:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_continuation_accumulator_bytes"
128:       config.max_continuation_accumulator_bytes;
129:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_response_headers_per_connection"
130:       config.max_response_headers_per_connection;
131:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_header_name_bytes"
132:       config.max_header_name_bytes;
133:     require_positive "h2_security_config.max_header_value_bytes"
134:       config.max_header_value_bytes
135: 
136:   let validate t =
137:     require_positive "max_connections" t.max_connections;
138:     require_positive "backlog" t.backlog;
139:     require_positive "read_buffer_size" t.read_buffer_size;
140:     require_positive "command_queue_capacity" t.command_queue_capacity;
141:     require_positive_duration "tls_handshake_timeout"
142:       t.tls_handshake_timeout;
143:     Eta_http.Server.Config.validate t.server;
144:     validate_h2_config t.h2_config;
145:     Option.iter validate_h2_security_config t.h2_security_config
146: end
147: 
148: module Stats = struct
149:   type t = Server_stats.Listener.snapshot = {
150:     active_connections : int;
151:     opened_connections : int;
152:     closed_connections : int;
153:     tls_handshakes : int;
154:     tls_handshake_failures : int;
155:     alpn_h1 : int;
156:     alpn_h2 : int;
157:     alpn_rejected : int;
158:     listener_errors : int;
159:   }
160: end
````

## File: lib/http_eio/server_types.mli
````ocaml
 1: (** Shared eta-http-eio server types. *)
 2: 
 3: type shutdown =
 4:   | Graceful of Eta.Duration.t
 5:   | Immediate
 6: 
 7: type domain_policy =
 8:   | Single_domain
 9:   | Recommended
10:   | Additional of int
11: 
12: type time = {
13:   sleep : Eta.Duration.t -> unit;
14:   with_timeout : 'a. Eta.Duration.t -> (unit -> 'a) -> 'a;
15: }
16: (** Time operations used by server connection deadlines. Tests can provide a
17:     controlled implementation; production entry points use {!live_time}. *)
18: 
19: val live_time : [> float Eio.Time.clock_ty ] Eio.Std.r -> time
20: (** [live_time clock] adapts an Eio clock to Eta HTTP server time. *)
21: 
22: module Connection_info : sig
23:   type t = {
24:     id : string;
25:     peer : Eta_http.Server.Request.peer;
26:     protocol : Eta_http.Server.Error.protocol;
27:     tls : bool;
28:     alpn_protocol : string option;
29:   }
30: end
31: 
32: type runtime_factory =
33:   sw:Eio.Switch.t ->
34:   connection:Connection_info.t ->
35:   unit ->
36:   Eta_http.Server.Error.t Eta.Runtime.t
37: 
38: module Config : sig
39:   type t = {
40:     max_connections : int;
41:     backlog : int;
42:     read_buffer_size : int;
43:     command_queue_capacity : int;
44:     tls_handshake_timeout : Eta.Duration.t;
45:     server : Eta_http.Server.Config.t;
46:     h2_config : H2.Config.t;
47:     h2_security_config : Eta_http.H2.Security.config option;
48:   }
49: 
50:   val default : t
51:   val validate : t -> unit
52: end
53: 
54: module Stats : sig
55:   type t = Server_stats.Listener.snapshot = {
56:     active_connections : int;
57:     opened_connections : int;
58:     closed_connections : int;
59:     tls_handshakes : int;
60:     tls_handshake_failures : int;
61:     alpn_h1 : int;
62:     alpn_h2 : int;
63:     alpn_rejected : int;
64:     listener_errors : int;
65:   }
66: end
````
